Creative-AI-Benchmark — exhaustive per-task mapping

Every one of the 66 tasks mapped against the framework: the input assets and how they're admission-scored (Phase 1), the golden trajectory (Phase 2), the expected outputs, and how each output is scored — the task-specific verifier checks (Layer 3), the operation-craft checklist (Layer 2), process & honesty (Layer 4), professional review (Layer 5) — rolled up into the K1–K7 capabilities (Layer 1), plus task-level metrics and the annotator flow.

The Layer-3 verifier checks are expert-authored and adversarially verified — one agent read each brief and wrote the exact checks (real counts, exact strings, precise dimensions), then a second agent audited each list to remove hallucinated requirements and lock the auto/expert + mandatory/dealbreaker/quality tags (1,077 checks across the 66 tasks). The other layers (admission, craft, process, review, capabilities) follow the Creative-AI-Benchmark doc. Brand names are the fictional, licensing-clean versions. Dealbreaker = a fatal miss that caps the score regardless of craft.

This page now also carries the Phase 0 — Task Validation panel (directly below) and a metadata-tag panel on every task (section “0 · Metadata tags”). Phase 0 = Gate A (task quality) + Gate B (metadata correctness); the asset files are validated separately in Phase 1.

Phase 0 — Task Validation

The first filter, run once per scraped task, before it is ever queued for an agent run or scored. Phase 0 judges only the task itself — the brief and its metadata. It does not open the asset files (those are checked in Phase 1 — Asset Validation, a separate stage) and it does not score the agent. A task must clear both gates below to be VALIDATED and move on to Phase 1.

Gate A — Task Quality

Is this a good, clear, doable task a real client would pay for?

9 dimensions, each resolving Pass / Fix / Reject, (code) where a script can decide, (expert) where it needs a human eye.

Gate B — Metadata Correctness

Are the labels describing this task right and consistent?

  • Presence — Every mandatory tag is filled (operation, family, modality, workflow, horizon, est_calls, domain, feasibility, exec-mode, capability-footprint).
  • Valid values — Each tag holds an allowed value (e.g. operation ∈ O1–O8; modality ∈ image/vector/document/video/audio/data).
  • Consistency with the brief — The labels actually describe this task — the operation, modality and deliverable match what the ask asks for.
  • Cross-tag coherence — Tags agree with each other — family is implied by operation; horizon is the right band for est_calls; exec-mode fits the operation.
  • De-duplication — The task is meaningfully distinct from tasks already accepted (corpus-level near-duplicate check).

Gate A — the 9 task-quality dimensions

#DimensionWhat it checks
A1Real-world briefReads like a genuine paid commission — concrete deliverable, plausible audience; not a toy/puzzle/“test-the-AI” prompt.
A2Clear &amp; unambiguousA reader lands on exactly one interpretation — no undefined “make it pop,” no contradictory directives.
A3Tool-feasibleEvery required action maps to a real connector. This is where not-doable tasks are caught — no text-to-image, no from-scratch logo/art with no source asset → feasibility = no, dropped.
A4Self-containedEvery essential input/parameter is in the brief or safely inferable — no “as we discussed” dependency.
A5Measurable outcomeAn objective pass/fail criterion can be written — success is checkable by observation, not pure taste.
A6Single coherent commissionOne unified job (may be multi-step), not several unrelated tasks bundled together.
A7Right difficulty for its horizonNot a one-tool one-liner, not unbounded — step count sits in the H-band for its est_calls.
A8Internally consistentThe brief doesn’t fight itself — counts, dims, dates, prices, references all agree.
A9Rubric-writableWe can draft a concrete, atomic acceptance checklist now — at least one objective verifier per task.

The “Good Task” definition

✓ INCLUDE a task if it…

  • Reads like a real commission someone would pay for
  • Has one clear goal, single obvious reading
  • Fully doable with the agent’s tools (edit, mask, retouch, vectorize, layout, data-merge, template-fill, stock, video/audio)
  • Self-contained — every essential input present or inferable
  • Has an objective acceptance criterion writable in advance
  • A single coherent commission toward one deliverable
  • Sits at the right difficulty for its horizon
  • Meaningfully distinct from accepted tasks

✗ FILTER OUT a task if it…

  • Needs text-to-image / generative image from a prompt (A3 → feasibility = no)
  • Needs a logo / illustration / artwork invented from scratch with no source asset (A3 → no)
  • Is a toy, puzzle, riddle or “test-the-AI” prompt
  • Is ambiguous (2+ readings) or self-contradictory
  • Can’t be done with our tools (banned capability / unreachable system)
  • Missing an essential input that can’t be recovered or defaulted
  • Has no objective success criterion (pure taste)
  • Bundles unrelated jobs
  • Too trivial (one-liner) or too sprawling to finish in its horizon
  • Is a near-duplicate of an accepted task

The metadata tags every task carries (validated at Gate B)

Each task below opens with its own “0 · Metadata tags” panel. The schema: operation (O1–O8) · family · output_modality · workflow_tag (Create/Edit/Analyze) · deliverable_type · horizon_tier (H1–H4) · est_calls (cost proxy) · domain · brand_persona · capability_footprint (which of the 7 scoring buckets the task exercises) · feasibility · execution_mode ([C]/[W]/[A]/[T]) · plus the five 1–5 difficulty axes (planning, brand-strictness, tool-diversity, visual-density, creative-freedom).

Verdict logic: a task is VALIDATED only when Gate A = Pass AND Gate B = Pass → it then passes to Phase 1 — Asset Validation. The verifiers we draft while gating (Gate A9) become the scoring contract — the exact Layer-3 checks run later against the agent’s output (shown per task below). So Phase 0 does double duty: validate the task now, define how it’ll be scored later.

AO-00   White-Background Ecommerce Hero Pack for an Automotive Aftermarket-Parts Marketplace (turbocharger listing: clean studio hero, transparent cutout, vector icon, and outpainted lifestyle banner)

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn a dealer's raw, off-color bench photo of an aftermarket turbocharger into a clean color-accurate #FFFFFF ecommerce hero plus a transparent cutout, a scalable vector icon, and an outpainted 16:9 lifestyle banner, then collect them on a review board.

Full brief

CONTEXT. We run a B2B ecommerce platform that lets automotive dealerships sell aftermarket performance parts online without carrying inventory. Our merchandising tone is operational, credible, and business-focused — NOT a flashy consumer ad. Dealers upload raw parts photos shot on a service-bay bench under mixed shop lighting; we need a repeatable Adobe pipeline that converts ONE such raw photo into a complete listing image pack that meets marketplace standards. SUPPLIED ASSET (client). raw_turbo_bench.jpg — a single hand-shot photo of a chrome/aluminium aftermarket turbocharger sitting on a cluttered, slightly tilted workshop bench under warm fluorescent light (visibly off-white-balanced, mild underexposure, blown chrome highlights). Plus brand_palette.json carrying our hex palette (anchor accent #C8102E "performance red", neutral #1A1A1A) and a one-line note that listing backgrounds MUST be pure #FFFFFF. WHAT WE NEED (deliverables). 1) PRIMARY HERO — a color-accurate, straightened, correctly exposed image of the turbo on a TRUE pure-white (#FFFFFF) background, cropped/resized to a 2048×2048 square (our listing-tile spec). 2) TRANSPARENT CUTOUT — the same corrected turbo as a background-free transparent PNG, for PDP hover-zoom and overlay use. 3) VECTOR ICON — a clean SVG line-art/silhouette derived from the cutout, for the spec-sheet header and UI category chips (must scale crisply). 4) LIFESTYLE BANNER — a 16:9 banner: license a real service-bay/garage stock backdrop and outpaint the corrected hero so the part sits naturally in a wider frame for the category landing page. 5) REVIEW BOARD — assemble the produced hero, cutout, icon, and banner onto a single shareable board so our merchandising lead can sign off in one link. CONSTRAINTS. Background on the hero must be genuine #FFFFFF (solid fill, not a gray studio sweep). Do not over-saturate — the part must read as credible/true-to-color, consistent with our B2B tone. Color correction must come BEFORE masking so the cutout and every downstream derivative inherit the corrected pixels. Keep the part's true geometry — straighten, don't warp. Deliver native formats where applicable (transparent PNG for the cutout, SVG for the icon).

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain automotiveDeliverable product-shotWorkflow EditOperation O2Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 17Feasibility partialExec mode [C]Brand generic
5Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Brand Alignment · Technical Quality · Process · Honesty. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
raw_turbo_bench.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal candid smartphone photo of a chrome-and-aluminium aftermarket automotive turbocharger sitting on a cluttered m
brand_palette.jsondatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a small JSON brand-palette file for a B2B automotive aftermarket-parts ecommerce platform. Include keys: brand_

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
turbo_hero_2048_white.pngimage2048x2048 square, true #FFFFFF background, straightened + tonally corrected aftermarket turbo, color-accurate (not oversaturated). Primary listing-tile hero.
turbo_cutout_transparent.pngimageBackground-free transparent PNG of the SAME corrected turbo (alpha channel), for PDP hover-zoom / overlays.
turbo_icon.svgvectorClean scalable SVG line-art / silhouette of the turbo derived from the transparent cutout, for spec-sheet header and UI category chips.
turbo_lifestyle_banner_16x9.pngimage16:9 category-landing banner: licensed service-bay/garage stock backdrop with the corrected hero AI-outpainted into a wider natural frame.
hero_pack_review_boardboardFirefly review board deep-link assembling hero, transparent cutout, vector icon, and lifestyle banner for one-link merchandising sign-off.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Primary hero deliverable turbo_hero_2048_white.png is exactly 2048x2048 pixelsautoImage dimensions read from turbo_hero_2048_white.png equal exactly 2048 px wide by 2048 px high (square listing-tile spec)K1mandatoryoutput
The hero background is genuine pure white #FFFFFF solid fill, not a gray studio sweepautoSampled background pixels in the non-subject region of turbo_hero_2048_white.png read RGB (255,255,255) / hex #FFFFFF as a flat solid fill, matching brand_palette listing_background_hex '#FFFFFF'; no gray gradient/sweepK1dealbreakerbrand
The turbocharger in the hero is straightened (bench tilt corrected) without warping its true geometryexpertThe ~4-degree tilt of the raw bench photo is corrected so the part sits level, and the turbo's proportions/geometry remain undistorted (straighten only, no warp), per the 'Keep the part's true geometry — straighten, don't warp' constraintK3mandatorybrief
The hero is tonally corrected: warm fluorescent cast neutralized, underexposure lifted, blown chrome highlights recoveredexpertChrome/aluminium reads neutral (no orange/yellow cast), overall brightness lifted to ecommerce-listing levels, and clipped specular highlights on the compressor housing show recovered detailK4mandatorybrief
The part is color-accurate and NOT oversaturated, consistent with the B2B operational toneexpertSaturation is restrained (gentle vibrance only); the turbo reads credible/true-to-color, honoring the brand_palette notes 'do not oversaturate parts; B2B operational tone'K4mandatorybrand
Transparent cutout deliverable turbo_cutout_transparent.png is a PNG with a real alpha channel and fully transparent backgroundautoturbo_cutout_transparent.png is PNG format, carries an alpha channel, and the non-subject region is fully transparent (background removed)K1mandatoryoutput
The cutout shows the SAME corrected turbo (inherits the color-corrected master pixels)expertThe turbo in turbo_cutout_transparent.png matches the corrected master tone/white-balance (correction done before masking), not the raw warm/underexposed pixelsK2mandatorybrief
The cutout mask edges cleanly follow the turbo silhouetteexpertMask edges hug the turbo outline with no leftover bench/clutter halo and no chunks of the part cut awayK3qualityoutput
Vector icon deliverable turbo_icon.svg is valid scalable SVG vector line-art/silhouetteautoturbo_icon.svg parses as valid SVG containing vector path geometry (not an embedded raster image), so it scales crisply for the spec-sheet header and UI category chipsK1mandatoryoutput
The vector icon is recognisably derived from the turbo cutout silhouetteexpertThe SVG line-art/silhouette reads clearly as the same turbocharger from turbo_cutout_transparent.png, clean enough to function as a UI iconK3qualityoutput
Lifestyle banner deliverable turbo_lifestyle_banner_16x9.png is a 16:9 aspect-ratio PNGautoturbo_lifestyle_banner_16x9.png is PNG with width:height equal to 16:9 (ratio 1.777...), per the category-landing banner specK1mandatoryoutput
The lifestyle banner places the corrected hero in a wider natural frame via AI outpaintingexpertThe corrected turbo sits naturally inside an AI-outpainted wider 16:9 frame with the part's pixels preserved and the extended canvas blending plausibly (generative_expand outpaint, per the banner deliverable)K4mandatoryoutput
All five expected deliverables are present in the hero packautoturbo_hero_2048_white.png, turbo_cutout_transparent.png, turbo_icon.svg, turbo_lifestyle_banner_16x9.png, and hero_pack_review_board are all producedK1mandatoryoutput
A single shareable Firefly review board collects the four image/vector derivatives for one-link sign-offautohero_pack_review_board is a Firefly board deep-link that assembles the hero (turbo_hero_2048_white.png), transparent cutout (turbo_cutout_transparent.png), vector icon (turbo_icon.svg), and lifestyle banner (turbo_lifestyle_banner_16x9.png) on one boardK6mandatoryoutput
Color correction was applied BEFORE masking so every derivative inherits the corrected pixelsexpertThe hero, cutout, icon, and banner all share the same corrected (white-balanced, exposed, highlight-recovered, saturation-restrained) appearance, confirming correction preceded the select/remove-background steps per the 'Color correction must come BEFORE masking' constraintK6mandatorybrief
Native delivery formats are honored: transparent PNG for the cutout and SVG for the iconautoThe cutout is delivered as a transparent PNG and the icon as an SVG (not flattened/rasterized substitutes), per the 'Deliver native formats where applicable (transparent PNG for the cutout, SVG for the icon)' constraintK1dealbreakerbrief

Layer 2 · Operation craft checklist — Photo & Image (expert marks Pass / Minor / Major vs reference images)

craft itemPassMinorMajor
Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost
Colour & white balanceneutral / on-brand; tones naturalslight castclear cast / wrong colour
Mask & edge qualityclean edges; fine detail (hair) keptsmall halo / jaggiesvisible halo / clipped subject
Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic
Texture & detail preservedrealistic micro-detailslightly softdetail smeared or lost
Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

Layer 4 · Process & honesty — from the agent's step-log (code counts + expert mark)

checkPassMinorMajorfeeds
Planningsensible order, deps ready firsta few wasted / out-of-order stepschaotic, no planK6
Tool useright tool & settingsoccasional wrong choice, self-correctedrepeatedly wrongK6
State managementalways uses latest filesone stale file usedwrong / old assets throughoutK6
Recoverydetects & fixes failuresrecovers slowly / clumsilyrepeats the same failureK6
Verificationchecks output before submitpartial checknever verifiesK6
Honesty (impossible asks)flags / refuses / discloseshedges but proceedsfabricates or hidesK7
Self-calibrationpredicted result matches realityslightly over/under-confidentconfidently wrongK7

Layer 5 · Professional review — the human verdict (each a fixed choice + one-line reason)

instrumentwhat the expert doesproduces
The callaccept / send-back / scrap (+ one-line reason)feeds the commission pass rate
Punch-listrevision notes tagged blocker / fix / nitpicka cost-to-ship number
Pin-the-flawclick the spot, pick the defect, tag severity (Critical/Major/Minor)localized, weighted defect labels
Blind face-offtwo outputs side by side: which would you send the client?stacks into craft ELO
ConfidenceHigh / Medium / Low on the call — one taplets buyers filter / down-weight

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-01   Premium Clinical Skincare Brand — Product-Shot Finishing, Logo Vectorization, Per-SKU Label Merge & Print-Ready Packaging Export

Brand/vertical Beauty, Cosmetics & Personal CareFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 18 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take our raw skincare hero shot, flat logo mark, texture plate and SKU copy sheet and produce a clinically clean white-background product image set, an editable vector logo, a recommended type system, packaging-safe outpainted canvases, a clinical monochrome variant, per-SKU print labels merged from our label template, and a print-ready packaging PDF — all assembled on one presentation board.

Full brief

We're launching DERMAVERA, a premium clinically-tested skincare line, and need a cohesive, credible visual identity executed across the product line (3 launch SKUs: a Vitamin-C Serum, a Barrier Repair Cream, and a Gentle Gel Cleanser). The look must be clean, minimalist, modern, science-driven and trustworthy — premium without being flashy, professional without looking pharmaceutical. We are supplying: (1) one raw hero photograph of the Serum bottle shot on a near-white sweep (slightly tilted, mixed lighting, cluttered edges); (2) a flat high-res raster of our chosen logo mark (a minimal molecule/droplet monogram + "DERMAVERA" wordmark) that we need delivered as a clean editable vector; (3) a soft lifestyle texture plate (matte stone surface) for collateral grounding; (4) a label-copy spreadsheet with one row per SKU (name, claim, volume, INCI line, batch placeholder, hex accent). We also supply our pre-authored print templates: an Illustrator label template (label.ai) with genuine Variables, and an InDesign unit-carton template (carton.indd). Deliverables: a flawless clinical white-background hero image (straightened, background swept to pure white, neutral clinical color, crisp product), a 1:1 e-commerce crop and an outpainted packaging-safe wide canvas, a clinical monochrome (single-accent duotone) brand variant of the hero, the logo delivered as an editable SVG, a recommended display+text type pairing, a print-ready per-SKU label set generated by merging our copy sheet into label.ai, a print-ready packaging PDF exported from carton.indd, and a single Firefly presentation board collecting the system for sign-off. Everything must be print-ready and leave us editable source (vector SVG + the authored .ai/.indd). Maintain consistent neutral, trustworthy color across every image.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 18 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.pngimageFinished clinical hero: auto-straightened, background swept to pure #FFFFFF, neutral-graded (auto-tone + neutralized color temp + lifted highlights), crisp product. Full-res ~4000x5000px PNG, sRGB, e-commerce white-bg standard.
dermavera_hero_1x1_ecom.pngimage1:1 square e-commerce crop of the finished hero, product centered with clinical whitespace, 2048x2048px PNG on pure white.
dermavera_hero_packaging_wide.pngimageOutpainted packaging-safe wide canvas of the finished hero (generative_expand extends the white sweep left/right), ~5000x2800px PNG, white field for carton/hero-plate placement.
dermavera_hero_mono_variant.pngimageClinical monochrome brand variant of the finished hero — single deep slate-teal (#16403B) duotone/monochromatic tint, for collateral. Same resolution as hero, PNG.
dermavera_logo.svgvectorEditable vector SVG of the supplied logo mark, clean single-color paths, transparent background — the editable source the brief demands.
dermavera_type_system.jsondataRecommended display + text typeface pairing (PostScript names + rationale) from font_recommend, to anchor the visual-identity typography spec.
dermavera_labels_merged.pdfpdfPrint-ready per-SKU label set: 3 label artboards (one per SKU row) produced by merging the copy CSV into label.ai. CMYK, 3mm bleed, crop marks. Vector + editable source = label.ai retained.
dermavera_carton_print.pdfpdfPrint-ready unit-carton PDF exported from carton.indd with the finished white-bg hero plate placed in HERO_PLATE, CMYK, 3mm bleed, registration/crop marks.
dermavera_brand_board_deeplinkurlFirefly Board deep-link assembling the finished hero, 1:1 crop, wide canvas, mono variant, vector logo and label proofs into one sign-off presentation board.

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All 9 named deliverables are present in the output setautoExactly these 9 deliverables exist: dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.png, dermavera_hero_1x1_ecom.png, dermavera_hero_packaging_wide.png, dermavera_hero_mono_variant.png, dermavera_logo.svg, dermavera_type_system.json, dermavera_labels_merged.pdf, dermavera_carton_print.pdf, and the dermavera_brand_board_deeplink URLK1mandatoryoutput
The clinical hero background is swept to pure whiteautoIn dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.png the sweep/background region sampled away from the product reads #FFFFFF (RGB 255,255,255) with no residual yellow-green castK1mandatoryoutput

The 1:1 e-commerce crop is exactly square at the specified pixel dimensionsautodermavera_hero_1x1_ecom.png is a PNG measuring exactly 2048x2048px on pure whiteK1mandatoryoutput
The packaging-safe wide canvas matches the specified outpainted dimensions with the white sweep extendedautodermavera_hero_packaging_wide.png is a PNG at approximately 5000x2800px (within ~5% on each side) with the white sweep extended left/right beyond the original hero frameK1mandatoryoutput
The monochrome variant is a single deep slate-teal duotone in the exact brand accent at hero resolutionexpertdermavera_hero_mono_variant.png reads as a single-color monochromatic/duotone tint keyed to slate-teal #16403B (not full color, not greyscale), at the same resolution as dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.pngK2mandatorybrand
The logo is delivered as an editable vector SVG with transparent backgroundautodermavera_logo.svg is a valid SVG containing vector <path> geometry (not an embedded raster bitmap) with a transparent/no background fillK1dealbreakeroutput
The vectorized logo preserves the supplied mark and wordmark faithfullyexpertdermavera_logo.svg reproduces the supplied molecule/droplet monogram glyph and the legible 'DERMAVERA' wordmark in a single slate-teal ink, with clean crisp paths and no regenerated/altered letterforms or invented elementsK2dealbreakerinput
The type system recommends a display + text pairing with PostScript names plus rationaleautodermavera_type_system.json contains a display typeface and a text typeface, each given by PostScript name, plus a rationale fieldK1mandatoryoutput
The recommended type pairing fits the clinical/premium identity briefexpertThe display+text pairing in dermavera_type_system.json reads as clean, minimalist, modern, science-driven and premium-not-luxurious with strong typography, consistent with the stated DERMAVERA aestheticK4qualitybrief
The merged label PDF contains exactly one label artboard per SKU rowautodermavera_labels_merged.pdf has exactly 3 label artboards/pages, one per CSV row (Vitamin-C Brightening Serum, Barrier Repair Cream, Gentle Gel Cleanser)K1mandatoryinput
Each merged label shows the correct per-SKU copy from the CSV verbatimautoVia OCR each of the 3 labels shows its row's sku_name, claim, volume and inci_line verbatim — e.g. the Serum label shows 'Vitamin-C Brightening Serum', '15% L-Ascorbic Acid — clinically tested', '30 ml e', and 'Aqua, Ascorbic Acid, Propylene Glycol, Ferulic Acid, Tocopherol'K1mandatoryinput
The merged label PDF is print-ready CMYK with 3mm bleed and crop marks on 70x90mm artboardsautodermavera_labels_merged.pdf is CMYK with 3mm bleed and crop marks present on 70x90mm label artboardsK1dealbreakeroutput
The carton PDF is exported from the authored carton.indd with the finished hero placed in HERO_PLATEexpertdermavera_carton_print.pdf is the 5-panel unit carton from carton.indd showing the finished white-bg hero plate in the HERO_PLATE front-panel frame and the placed DERMAVERA logo, exported (not a re-composed or regenerated layout)K6mandatoryoutput
The carton PDF is print-ready CMYK with bleed and registration/crop marksautodermavera_carton_print.pdf is CMYK with 3mm bleed and registration + crop marks presentK1dealbreakeroutput
Neutral, trustworthy color is consistent across every produced hero imageexpertThe clinical hero, the 1:1 crop and the wide canvas share one neutral clinical color balance with no warm/cool cast drift between themK4qualitybrief
The Firefly board deep-link assembles the full system for sign-offautodermavera_brand_board_deeplink is a working Firefly Board URL collecting the finished hero, 1:1 crop, wide canvas, mono variant, vector logo and label proofs onto one boardK1mandatoryoutput
The editable source files demanded by the brief are retained and deliveredautoThe delivery retains the editable sources alongside the print-ready PDFs: the vector dermavera_logo.svg plus the authored label.ai and carton.inddK1mandatorybrief

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-02   Performance-ready Meta ad product creatives: white-bg clean-up, brand color grade, multi-ratio outpaint set, and vectorized logo for paid-social testing

Brand/vertical E-commerce, Retail & ProductFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's raw product shot, lifestyle backdrop, and logo into a performance-ready Meta ad creative kit: a retouched pure-white-bg hero, a brand-graded variant, three platform-ratio outpainted canvases (1:1 / 4:5 / 9:16), a vectorized logo, and a Firefly review board.

Full brief

DONE is a DTC homeware brand launching paid-social tests for its matte-ceramic pour-over coffee dripper and needs a freelancer to build the imagery layer of a Meta (Facebook + Instagram) ad-creative kit, faithful to the brand guidelines (brand teal #0F6E64 on warm off-white #F4EFE7). You are supplied four client assets: (1) a raw studio product photo of the dripper on a near-white sweep that still shows seamless-paper shadows and a faint gray cast; (2) a lifestyle/kitchen backdrop plate for context variants; (3) the DONE wordmark logo as a flat PNG; and (4) the brand-guideline sheet as a PDF for reference. Build the production chain entirely on Adobe connector tools: ingest the assets into a working folder; auto-correct the studio shot's geometry and tone; isolate the product and force a true, pure-white e-commerce background (catalog-clean, no gray cast, no stray shadow halo); push a brand-consistent color grade so the ceramic reads warm and premium and the teal accents stay on-palette; then generate the platform canvases performance marketers actually test — a 1:1 feed square, a 4:5 portrait feed, and a 9:16 Stories/Reels vertical — using AI outpaint to extend the clean background into each frame instead of cropping the product. Separately, vectorize the supplied wordmark to a clean SVG so it stays crisp at any ad size. Finally, assemble the full variation set into a Firefly board as a single review deep-link the client can sign off on in Slack. Out of scope for this connector pass (hand off to the typesetting/Express step): baking headline/offer/CTA copy and the logo lockup onto the frames, and choosing final on-image type — though you should return a font recommendation to steer that. Deliverables: the pure-white hero, the brand-graded hero, the three ratio canvases, the vector logo, the font direction, and the Firefly review board link.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain ecommerce-retailDeliverable product-shotWorkflow Edit+CreateOperation O2Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 21Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand generic
5Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
done_hero_pure_white.pngimageRetouched product hero on a true pure-white (#FFFFFF) e-commerce background, product isolated with clean edge, no gray cast / no shadow halo, straightened and auto-toned, ~4000x3000px PNG.
done_hero_brand_graded.jpgimageBrand-consistent color-graded hero: warm premium ceramic, on-palette teal accents (HSL nudged toward #0F6E64), exposure/contrast/temperature tuned for paid-social punch, high-quality JPEG.
done_creative_1x1.jpgimage1:1 feed square (1080x1080) with the product centered and the clean background AI-outpainted to fill the square — product not cropped.
done_creative_4x5.jpgimage4:5 portrait feed (1080x1350) produced by outpainting the graded hero's background vertically; product preserved, brand whitespace extended.
done_creative_9x16.jpgimage9:16 Stories/Reels vertical (1080x1920) produced by outpainting top/bottom; generous brand negative space left for the handed-off headline/CTA copy.
done_logo_vector.svgvectorClean vectorized DONE wordmark + dripper glyph as SVG, scalable crisp for any ad size, solid brand-teal paths on transparent.
done_font_direction.jsondatafont_recommend output: geometric-sans headline + humanist-sans body pairing matching the brand-guideline type note, to steer the handed-off on-image typesetting step.
done_review_board_linkdataFirefly Boards deep-link assembling the hero, graded hero, and three ratio canvases as one client-review moodboard for Slack sign-off.

AO-03   E-commerce white-background product retouch suite + vectorized brand mark

Brand/vertical E-commerce, Retail & ProductFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn a client's raw, messy-background product photo into a pure-white-background marketplace catalog image and convert their rough hand-drawn logo sketch into a clean, brand-colored, scalable vector logo — then package everything for delivery.

Full brief

Our small skincare-and-homeware shop is scaling onto Amazon, Etsy and our own Shopify store and we badly need visual credibility — right now our listings use phone snaps shot on a kitchen table and they look amateur, which is killing conversion. We can't afford a studio day, so we're sending you what we have: RAW product captures (a 24MP amber-glass serum bottle shot handheld under window light on a cluttered wooden table, slightly tilted) and a rough logo: our founder hand-inked our brand mark "AURELIA BOTANICALS" on paper and we photographed it with a phone — it's wonky, grey-ish, on off-white paper. We need you to do the full product-photographer post-production pass and the logo cleanup: PRODUCT IMAGE (do this as a repeatable recipe — we'll send 10-15 more bottles and jars that all must match): - Level the horizon (the table edge is tilted). - Cut the bottle off the messy table and put it on a PURE WHITE (#FFFFFF) seamless background, which is the hard requirement for Amazon's main-image policy. - Deliver it as a square 2000×2000 px marketplace canvas with the product centered. - Make it look professional: balanced exposure and color, true-to-life pop on the amber glass, and recover the blown-out highlights/hot spots from the window light so the glass reads cleanly. LOGO: - Take our phone photo of the inked sketch and turn it into a CLEAN, infinitely scalable vector (SVG) so it works on a 16px favicon and a 3-metre trade-show banner alike. - Recolor the mark to our brand green (deep botanical green ~#1E5E3A). - Recommend 2-3 fonts that pair with the hand-drawn mark for the "AURELIA BOTANICALS" wordmark so we can set the lockup ourselves. DELIVERY: put the final catalog image, the transparent-background product cut-out, the vector logo and the recolored logo into a clean, clearly-named delivery folder we can pull straight into our listings and brand kit. Keep the white truly white — no grey cast — and keep the logo edges crisp at any size.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)

logo_sketch_phone_photo.jpgimagegemini-3-pro-imageA phone photograph of a rough hand-inked logo sketch on a piece of slightly off-white textured paper. The mark is a hand

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
AURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white.jpgimageSquare 2000×2000 px JPEG, product centered on pure #FFFFFF seamless background, leveled, auto-toned, vibrance-balanced, highlights recovered — Amazon/Etsy/Shopify main-image compliant catalog hero. Explicitly the template recipe for the remaining 10-15 SKUs.
AURELIA_serum_cutout_transparent.pngimageProduct cut-out on transparent background (alpha PNG), leveled and toned, for re-use on colored lifestyle backgrounds and brand-kit compositing by the client.
AURELIA_logo_vector.svgvectorClean scalable SVG traced from the hand-inked sketch — crisp paths at 16px favicon through 3m banner, neutral/original ink color.
AURELIA_logo_brandgreen.svgvectorRecolored vector logo in brand botanical green (~#1E5E3A) via solid color overlay, scalable SVG for the brand kit.
font_pairing_recommendations.txtdata2-3 recommended wordmark fonts that pair with the hand-drawn mark for the 'AURELIA BOTANICALS' lockup, returned by font_recommend.
AURELIA_delivery_folderdataA clearly-named CC asset delivery folder containing the catalog hero JPEG, the transparent cut-out PNG, both SVG logos and the font note — ready to pull into listings and the brand kit.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 17 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Catalog hero JPEG is exactly 2000x2000 px squareautoAURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white.jpg has width == 2000 px AND height == 2000 pxK1mandatoryoutput
Catalog hero is delivered in JPEG formatautoAURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white file is a valid JPEG (JPEG/JFIF magic bytes), not PNG/WEBP/otherK1mandatoryoutput
Catalog hero background is pure white #FFFFFF with no grey castautoSampled background pixels (corner and edge samples in the seamless region around the bottle) of AURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white.jpg read RGB (255,255,255) within JPEG noise tolerance of +/-2 on each channel — no grey cast, matching the brief's hard #FFFFFF Amazon main-image requirementK1dealbreakerbrief
Product is centered on the square canvasexpertIn AURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white.jpg the amber serum bottle's subject bounding-box centroid sits near the canvas center with balanced left/right and top/bottom margins, per the brief 'product centered'K3mandatorybrief
Bottle is leveled (the ~4-degree tilt is corrected)expertThe serum bottle stands vertically upright in AURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white.jpg and AURELIA_serum_cutout_transparent.png with no residual ~4-degree tilt and no diagonal table edge remaining, per the brief 'Level the horizon (the table edge is tilted)'K3mandatorybrief
Cluttered original background (tea towel, mug, power cord, wood table, dust) is fully removedexpertNo element of the original messy table scene (crumpled tea towel, coffee mug, power cord, wood grain, dust) is visible in AURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white.jpg; only the bottle on white remainsK3mandatorybrief
Background-removal mask edges around the bottle are cleanexpertThe cut-out edge of the amber bottle (including black dropper cap and curved glass) is crisp with no halo, fringe, leftover background pixels, or chewed-into-subject artifactsK3qualitybrief
Blown-out specular highlights on the amber glass are recoveredexpertThe bright window-light hot spots / specular blowout on the curved amber glass are tamed so the glass reads cleanly with recovered detail rather than pure clipped white, per the brief 'recover the blown-out highlights/hot spots'K4mandatorybrief
Exposure and color are balanced with true-to-life amber pop and no oversaturated whiteexpertAURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white.jpg shows balanced exposure, neutral color (no window-light cast), and natural vibrance/saturation pop on the amber glass without oversaturating or tinting the white sweepK4qualitybrief
Transparent cut-out PNG has a real alpha channel with genuinely transparent backgroundautoAURELIA_serum_cutout_transparent.png is a PNG with an alpha channel and alpha == 0 in the background region around the subject (genuinely transparent), not a flattened white/opaque backgroundK1mandatoryoutput
Cleaned logo is delivered as a vector SVG with real geometryautoAURELIA_logo_vector.svg is a valid SVG file containing vector <path>/<polygon> geometry (not merely an embedded raster <image> element)K1dealbreakeroutput
Vector logo is traced from the supplied hand-inked sketch, not regeneratedexpertAURELIA_logo_vector.svg reproduces the supplied sketch's mark (a single sprig of laurel/olive leaves curving into a small circle) with crisp clean paths and the stray pencil guide lines removed; it is a faithful trace of the supplied logo_sketch_phone_photo.jpg input, NOT a regenerated or redrawn logoK2dealbreakerinput
Neutral vector logo retains the original ink color (is not the green version)autoAURELIA_logo_vector.svg fills the mark in a neutral/original-ink color (dark grey-black, NOT brand green ~#1E5E3A), distinct from the recolored AURELIA_logo_brandgreen.svg, per the output spec 'neutral/original ink color'K1mandatoryoutput
Recolored logo SVG uses brand botanical green ~#1E5E3AautoAURELIA_logo_brandgreen.svg is a scalable vector file that fills the mark with brand botanical green approximately #1E5E3A (R~30, G~94, B~58) within a small toleranceK2mandatorybrand
Font pairing note recommends 2-3 fonts for the AURELIA BOTANICALS wordmarkautofont_pairing_recommendations.txt names between 2 and 3 specific fonts recommended to pair with the hand-drawn mark for the 'AURELIA BOTANICALS' wordmark lockupK1mandatoryoutput
Delivery folder is a clearly-named folder containing all four assets plus the font noteautoAURELIA_delivery_folder is a clearly-named delivery folder containing exactly these 5 items: AURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white.jpg, AURELIA_serum_cutout_transparent.png, AURELIA_logo_vector.svg, AURELIA_logo_brandgreen.svg and font_pairing_recommendations.txtK6mandatoryoutput
Brand name appears exactly as 'AURELIA BOTANICALS'autoWherever the brand name is referenced (font note, asset/folder naming, logo wordmark) it reads exactly 'AURELIA BOTANICALS' with no altered or misspelled wordingK2mandatorybrief

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-04   Silver Jewelry iPhone Shots to White-Background E-commerce Set (JPEG + transparent PNG)

Brand/vertical Fashion & ApparelFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn a handheld iPhone shot of a silver jewellery piece into a clean, colour-accurate, uniform-white e-commerce set: a 3000x3000 sRGB JPEG plus a matching transparent PNG, ready to slot straight into the online store.

Full brief

I run a silver jewellery line and need my iPhone-captured product frames turned into a polished, store-ready e-commerce set through AI-powered post-production. The look is minimalist and clean: crisp, shadow-controlled pieces on a pure or near-white backdrop, accurate metal tone and true gemstone colour, pin-sharp on every hero angle, with no colour cast — so they drop straight into my online store without further retouching. Work this one representative hero frame end-to-end as the master recipe, then I will reuse the exact same chain on the rest of the line (the full order is 20 pieces). Start from the original iPhone HEIC/RAW file I supply. First fix the small handheld tilt and set a neutral baseline exposure and white balance, because the iPhone leaves a warm cast that throws off the silver and the gemstone. Then isolate the jewellery itself, drive the background to a uniform pure white with no cast, and grade the piece so the metal highlights read clean-silver and the stone's colour is faithful. Deliverables for this frame: one finished JPEG at exactly 3000 x 3000 px, sRGB, on uniform white; and one matching PNG of the same piece on a fully transparent background for layering on coloured store sections. Reframe to a true 1:1 square by extending the white canvas (do not stretch or distort the piece), then export at the exact pixel spec. Finally, organise the outputs into a tidy delivery folder and assemble the graded pieces into a single review board so I can approve the look before you run the remaining frames. Acceptance: uniform white background, no colour cast; accurate metal tone and gemstone colour; pin-sharp focus on the hero angle; exact 3000 x 3000 sRGB JPEG plus matching transparent PNG.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
silver_ring_hero_3000.jpgimage3000x3000 px, 1:1 square, sRGB, JPEG under 2 MB, uniform pure-white background, neutral colour (no cast), accurate silver metal tone and faithful sapphire colour, pin-sharp on the band. Web-ready e-commerce hero.
silver_ring_hero_transparent.pngimageMatching PNG of the same graded piece on a fully transparent background (alpha), same grade as the JPEG, for layering on coloured store sections.
delivery_folderdataAsset folder structure containing the finished JPEG, the transparent PNG, and the original supplied iPhone file, named for client download.
review_board_deeplinkdataFirefly Board deep-link assembling the graded master frame(s) for one-click client approval of the look before the remaining 20 pieces are run.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The finished JPEG deliverable silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg has pixel dimensions of exactly 3000 x 3000 (true 1:1 square).autoImage width == 3000 px AND height == 3000 px as read from silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg.K1mandatoryoutput
silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg is encoded as JPEG in the sRGB colour space.autosilver_ring_hero_3000.jpg file format == JPEG AND the colour profile is sRGB (embedded sRGB profile, or untagged/assumed-sRGB with no conflicting profile such as Display P3 or Adobe RGB).K1dealbreakeroutput
The finished JPEG file size is under 2 MB.autoOn-disk size of silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg < 2,097,152 bytes (2 MB).K1mandatoryoutput
A matching PNG of the same piece, silver_ring_hero_transparent.png, exists with a genuinely transparent background.autosilver_ring_hero_transparent.png is PNG format AND has an alpha channel containing fully transparent (alpha == 0) pixels in the background region surrounding the jewellery.K1mandatoryoutput
The JPEG background is a uniform pure / near-white with no colour cast.autoSampled background pixels of silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg are near RGB 255/255/255 (each channel >= 250) AND the three channels are balanced (max channel spread <= 5) so there is no warm/cool tint.K1dealbreakeroutput
The PNG carries the identical grade as the JPEG (same colour-corrected graded master), differing only in background.expertWhen silver_ring_hero_transparent.png is composited on white, the silver metal tone and sapphire colour visually match silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg (both derive from the same graded master, not separately graded).K2mandatoryoutput
The warm iPhone white-balance cast is neutralised so the silver reads as clean, neutral silver.expertReviewer confirms the metal highlights/mid-tones read as neutral clean-silver with no residual warm/yellow cast carried from the input frame.K4mandatorybrief
The faceted deep-blue sapphire renders as a faithful, true blue gemstone colour.expertReviewer confirms the sapphire reads as an accurate deep-blue (selectively saturated correctly, not washed out, oversaturated, or hue-shifted).K4mandatorybrief
The ring band is pin-sharp on the hero angle.expertReviewer confirms the silver band is tack-sharp / in crisp focus with no softening, blur, or destructive artefacts introduced by the editing chain.K4mandatorybrief
The ~2-3 degree handheld tilt is corrected so the piece is geometrically straight.expertReviewer confirms the piece and the implied horizon are level, with no visible residual tilt.K3qualitybrief
The square 1:1 frame was reached by extending the white canvas, not by stretching or distorting the ring.expertReviewer confirms the ring retains its original undistorted proportions and the extra area is added uniform white canvas (no anamorphic stretch to fill the square).K3mandatorybrief
The cut-out edge between the jewellery and the white/transparent background is clean.expertReviewer confirms the subject mask edge is precise around the ring and stone with no white halo, fringing, or chewed-away metal/prongs.K3qualitybrief
A delivery folder is assembled containing the finished JPEG, the transparent PNG, and the original supplied iPhone file.autodelivery_folder contains exactly the three named assets: silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg, silver_ring_hero_transparent.png, and iphone_silver_ring_hero.heic.K6mandatoryoutput
A Firefly Board deep-link is produced assembling the graded master frame(s) for client approval.autoreview_board_deeplink resolves to a Firefly Board URL that contains the graded master and finished hero frame (silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg) for one-click client review.K6mandatoryoutput
The uniform white backdrop was produced non-generatively (select piece -> invert -> solid-white fill), not via generative fill or prompt-based background replacement.expertWorkflow evidence shows the background was driven to white via image_select_by_prompt -> image_invert_selection -> image_fill_area (solid white RGB 255/255/255), with no generative-fill or background-replace-by-prompt step used.K7mandatorybrief
The original supplied iphone_silver_ring_hero.heic is preserved and delivered unaltered alongside the edited outputs.autoThe original iPhone file delivered in delivery_folder is byte-identical to the supplied input iphone_silver_ring_hero.heic (not the straightened/graded version).K2mandatoryinput

AO-05   Weekly Meta Ad Creative Batch for a Hemp/Supplement E-Commerce Brand: White-BG Product Isolation, Compliance-Safe Color Grade, Multi-Placement Sizing, Vectorized Logo + Motion Variant

Brand/vertical Health, Wellness & MedicalFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 23)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take one hemp/CBD product SKU through a full Adobe pipeline to produce a Meta-ready ad creative set: a clean white-background hero, a tone-corrected lifestyle hero outpainted and cropped to the three Meta placements (1:1, 4:5, 9:16), a crisp vectorized logo lockup, and one motion variant reframed to 9:16 story.

Full brief

Our hemp/supplement e-commerce brand runs weekly Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ad campaigns and needs a reliable creative pipeline that turns raw product shots into a compliant, performance-ready creative set. For this cycle, work the new CBD tincture SKU. We're supplying: (1) a raw studio photo of the tincture bottle on a messy desk (uneven lighting, cluttered background) that we want as a clean white-background product hero; (2) a separate lifestyle photo of the bottle on a kitchen counter with morning light — but it's slightly crooked, a touch underexposed, and shot in portrait when we need it to fill landscape and story placements; (3) our brand wordmark logo as a flat PNG that we need as a clean scalable vector for crisp overlays at any size; and (4) a short 6-second clip of the dropper in use that we want as a vertical story/reel motion ad. Deliverables, all optimized for Meta and positioned to avoid compliance flags (clean studio product imagery, no exaggerated claims baked into the image): a pure-white-background product hero; a corrected, straightened, properly exposed lifestyle hero; that lifestyle hero expanded and re-cropped into the three standard Meta placements — 1:1 (1080×1080 feed square), 4:5 (1080×1350 feed portrait), and 9:16 (1080×1920 story/reel); a vector (SVG) version of the wordmark for overlay use; and one 9:16 motion variant of the dropper clip. Keep the product color accurate (CBD oil amber tone must read true) and the white background truly white. Package the final set for hand-off.

Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Technical Quality · Process · Brand Alignment. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 23 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
product_hero_white_1080.jpgimagePure white (#ffffff) background product hero, subject isolated from the cluttered desk and composited on flat white, auto-toned for accurate amber oil color, square 1080×1080 JPEG, Meta feed ready.
lifestyle_hero_corrected.jpgimageStraightened (horizon leveled), exposure-corrected, tone-balanced lifestyle hero of the kitchen shot at full source resolution, warm cast neutralized, ready to feed placement crops.
lifestyle_feed_1x1_1080.jpgimage1:1 Meta feed square, 1080×1080, subject-aware crop of the corrected lifestyle hero.
lifestyle_feed_4x5_1080x1350.jpgimage4:5 Meta feed portrait, 1080×1350, subject-aware crop of the corrected lifestyle hero.
lifestyle_story_9x16_1080x1920.jpgimage9:16 story/reel, 1080×1920, produced by outpainting the portrait lifestyle hero on the short axis then cropping to 9:16 so no product content is lost.
balance_wordmark.svgvectorClean scalable SVG of the BALANCE wordmark+leaf, single forest-green fill, transparent, for crisp overlay at any size.
dropper_story_9x16.mp4video9:16 vertical reframe of the 6-second dropper clip, same length (no trim), 1080×1920, story/reel motion ad.
balance_meta_creative_set.zipdataSingle zipped hand-off package containing all of the above static, vector, and motion deliverables plus a folder structure on Adobe storage.

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The single zip hand-off package contains all seven non-zip deliverablesautobalance_meta_creative_set.zip contains exactly these 7 assets: product_hero_white_1080.jpg, lifestyle_hero_corrected.jpg, lifestyle_feed_1x1_1080.jpg, lifestyle_feed_4x5_1080x1350.jpg, lifestyle_story_9x16_1080x1920.jpg, balance_wordmark.svg, and dropper_story_9x16.mp4K1mandatoryoutput
The white-bg product hero is a square 1080x1080 JPEGautoproduct_hero_white_1080.jpg is exactly 1080x1080 px and encoded as JPEGK1mandatoryoutput
The product hero background is pure whiteautoSampled background pixels (non-subject corners/edges) of product_hero_white_1080.jpg read #ffffff (truly white, no residual desk clutter or color cast)K1dealbreakerbrief
The tincture bottle is cleanly isolated from the original cluttered desk in the white-bg heroexpertproduct_hero_white_1080.jpg shows only the BALANCE bottle on flat white with no coffee mug, papers, pen, laptop edge or desk remnants, and mask edges around glass/dropper are cleanK3mandatorybrief
The amber CBD oil color reads true and accurate across the corrected outputsexpertThe amber oil in product_hero_white_1080.jpg and lifestyle_hero_corrected.jpg reads as a true amber tone (auto-toned / color-temperature corrected, no green/orange cast carried over from the desk or warm golden kitchen light)K4mandatorybrief
The lifestyle hero horizon is leveled (straightened)expertlifestyle_hero_corrected.jpg shows a level horizon with the marble counter line horizontal (the ~4-degree tilt of the source removed)K3mandatorybrief
The lifestyle hero is exposure-corrected and the warm golden cast neutralizedexpertlifestyle_hero_corrected.jpg is lifted from the underexposed source to a correct mid-tone and the warm golden cast is neutralized so the marble reads neutral and the amber reads trueK4mandatorybrief
The 1:1 feed crop is 1080x1080autolifestyle_feed_1x1_1080.jpg is exactly 1080x1080 px (JPEG)K1mandatoryoutput
The 4:5 feed portrait crop is 1080x1350autolifestyle_feed_4x5_1080x1350.jpg is exactly 1080x1350 px (JPEG)K1mandatoryoutput
The 9:16 story placement is 1080x1920autolifestyle_story_9x16_1080x1920.jpg is exactly 1080x1920 px (JPEG)K1mandatoryoutput
The three placement crops keep the tincture bottle in frame (subject-aware)expertThe BALANCE bottle is the kept, in-frame focal subject in lifestyle_feed_1x1_1080.jpg, lifestyle_feed_4x5_1080x1350.jpg, and lifestyle_story_9x16_1080x1920.jpg — not cut off or pushed out of frameK3mandatorybrief
The 9:16 story was produced by outpainting the portrait hero before cropping so no product content is lostexpertlifestyle_story_9x16_1080x1920.jpg shows generatively-expanded marble/scene filling the 9:16 frame with the bottle fully intact (not a hard crop that amputates product), consistent with an expand-then-crop chainK6qualitybrief
The wordmark deliverable is a true vector SVG with transparent backgroundautobalance_wordmark.svg is a valid SVG file containing vector path data (not a raster image embedded in an SVG wrapper) with a transparent backgroundK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized wordmark preserves the BALANCE wordmark, leaf glyph, and forest-green fillexpertbalance_wordmark.svg renders the word 'BALANCE' plus the small leaf glyph in a single deep forest-green fill, with crisp clean edges faithful to the supplied PNG (not regenerated or restyled)K2dealbreakerinput
The motion variant is a 9:16 vertical 1080x1920 video at the original 6-second lengthautodropper_story_9x16.mp4 is exactly 1080x1920 px, aspect 9:16, and approximately 6 seconds long (same length as the source, no trim)K1mandatoryoutput
The dropper motion variant is a visual reframe of the source clip with the dropper action kept in frameexpertdropper_story_9x16.mp4 shows the same hand/dropper/drops-into-water action reframed for vertical with the action centered/in-frame, same length and audio as source (resize is visual-only)K6qualitybrief
No exaggerated claims or non-compliant text are baked into any creative (compliance-safe positioning)expertNone of the static, story, or motion deliverables (product_hero_white_1080.jpg, lifestyle_hero_corrected.jpg, the three placement crops, dropper_story_9x16.mp4) have exaggerated health/CBD claims or text overlays baked into the image/video — clean studio/lifestyle imagery only, positioned to avoid Meta compliance flagsK7mandatorybrief

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-06   White-Background Catalog Cleanup for a Firearms/Optics/Outdoor Shopify Store

Brand/vertical Nonprofit, Religious & CommunityFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take a batch of inconsistent distributor-supplied product photos for a firearms/optics/outdoor Shopify store and turn them into clean, straightened, color-corrected, pure-white-background, square marketplace hero images (plus transparent-PNG tile variants and a vectorized listing badge), all organized into a review board and a tidy asset folder.

Full brief

I run a hands-off Shopify store selling thermal cameras, night-vision optics, firearms, ammunition, trail-camera mounts and hunting accessories. My distributor feeds dump product photos that are all over the place: shot on off-white or gray seamless, slightly tilted, warm/yellow color cast, and framed inconsistently — they look unprofessional next to each other on a collection page and several get rejected by marketplace channels that require a pure-white background. As part of the catalog cleanup I need a repeatable Adobe pipeline that takes one raw distributor photo and produces marketplace-ready listing assets, demonstrated end-to-end on my hero SKU (a handheld thermal weapon scope) and ready to repeat across the catalog. For the hero SKU, starting from the single raw distributor JPEG I provide, deliver: (1) a corrected master — auto-straightened, cropped to the product, exposure/tone normalized, and the warm color cast neutralized; (2) a pure #FFFFFF background version where the scope is isolated and the messy seamless is replaced with solid white per Shopify/Amazon hero spec; (3) a transparent-PNG cut-out of the same scope for collection tiles and bundle mock-ups; (4) a square 1:1 hero by outpainting the corrected master to fill a square canvas, then exported at 2048×2048. Also vectorize the small distributor brand badge I supply (a flat single-color logo mark) into a clean SVG so it scales on the listing, recommend a clean sans-serif catalog typeface, and assemble the finished hero set into a Firefly review board so I can approve it in one place. Finally, drop everything into a tidy, clearly named asset folder I can hand to the next batch. Constraints: backgrounds must be solid pure white (no gradient, no AI-invented scene), the product must not be distorted or recolored beyond honest color-cast correction, and nothing may be composited in or generatively painted onto the product itself. Keep my color/tone edits faithful to the real product (a firearm optic — accuracy matters to buyers).
