4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
scope_master_corrected.jpgimageStraightened, cropped-to-product, auto-toned, color-cast-neutralized master JPEG of the thermal scope, sRGB, high quality. The clean base every other deliverable derives from.
scope_whitebg_hero.jpgimagePure #FFFFFF solid-background hero of the isolated scope, marketplace-compliant (no gradient, no scene), sRGB JPEG.
scope_cutout.pngimageTransparent-background PNG cut-out of the scope for collection tiles / bundle mock-ups, edges clean, alpha channel.
scope_hero_square_2048.jpgimageSquare 1:1 Shopify/Amazon hero, generative-expanded to a square canvas then resized/exported at exactly 2048x2048, sRGB JPEG.
brand_badge.svgvectorClean scalable SVG of the distributor brand mark vectorized from the flat PNG, for crisp display at any listing size.
catalog_typeface_recommendationdataA recommended clean sans-serif catalog/listing typeface (name + rationale) from font_recommend.
hero_review_boarddataFirefly Board deep-link assembling the corrected master, white-bg hero, cut-out, and square hero for one-click owner approval.
Cleaned_Catalog_Assets/ folderdataA named CC asset folder containing all finished SKU deliverables, copied in and organized for handoff to the next batch.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
scope_master_corrected.jpg deliverable is present as an sRGB JPEGautoA file named scope_master_corrected.jpg exists in the deliverable set; format == JPEG and color space == sRGBK1mandatoryoutput
Corrected master is straightened, cropped to product, and exposure/tone normalizedexpertIn scope_master_corrected.jpg the thermal scope sits level (the original ~4-degree clockwise tilt is removed and the product axis is square to the frame), the loose/uneven empty margins are trimmed to the product bounds, and exposure/contrast is normalized (not under- or over-exposed)K3mandatorybrief
Warm tungsten color cast is neutralized toward neutral white balanceexpertThe warm yellow-orange tungsten cast present in the raw input is corrected toward a neutral white balance in scope_master_corrected.jpg (background reads neutral, no residual yellow-orange tint)K4mandatorybrief
scope_whitebg_hero.jpg has a pure #FFFFFF solid background with no gradientautoSampling the background region (outside the scope) of scope_whitebg_hero.jpg returns RGB (255,255,255) / hex #FFFFFF across the whole field, with no gradient and no off-white pixels; file is an sRGB JPEGK1dealbreakerbrief
White-bg hero background is solid white only — no AI-invented scene or composited elementexpertThe background of scope_whitebg_hero.jpg is a flat solid-white field with no painted scene, props, gradient, or composited objects; nothing has been generatively invented behind the productK7dealbreakerbrief
scope_cutout.png is a transparent-background PNG cut-out of the scopeautoscope_cutout.png exists, is PNG format with an alpha channel, and the background region is fully transparent (alpha == 0) while the scope pixels are opaque (alpha == 255)K1mandatoryoutput
Cut-out mask edges are clean around the scopeexpertIn scope_cutout.png the alpha edge follows the scope silhouette cleanly — no leftover seamless/halo fringe, and no chewed-away product detail (rail, eyepiece, control buttons intact)K3qualityoutput
scope_hero_square_2048.jpg is exactly 2048x2048 pixels, square 1:1, sRGB JPEGautoscope_hero_square_2048.jpg dimensions == 2048 x 2048 (1:1 square), format == JPEG, color space == sRGBK1dealbreakeroutput
Square hero was made by outpainting the white field only, not by painting onto the productexpertscope_hero_square_2048.jpg fills the square canvas by extending the pure-white field around the scope; the background remains solid white with no invented scene, and nothing is generatively painted onto or added to the product bodyK7mandatorybrief
brand_badge.svg is a true vector SVG (not an embedded raster image)autobrand_badge.svg exists, is valid SVG markup containing vector path/shape elements, and does NOT consist of (or merely wrap) an embedded raster <image> elementK2mandatoryoutput
Vectorized badge faithfully reproduces the supplied flat single-color brand markexpertbrand_badge.svg visually matches the supplied distributor logo from distributor_brand_badge.png — the reticle-and-mountain emblem with its wordmark beneath, in a single flat dark-charcoal color, same shapes, crisp edges that scale cleanly, with no added gradients, no photographic texture, and no invented elementsK2mandatoryinput
A clean sans-serif catalog typeface recommendation is providedexpertcatalog_typeface_recommendation names a specific sans-serif typeface (sourced from font_recommend) plus a rationale suited to a clean product-listing/catalog useK5mandatoryoutput
A Firefly review board assembling the hero set is deliveredautohero_review_board is a Firefly Board deep-link/URL that assembles the corrected master, white-bg hero, cut-out, and square hero for one-click owner approvalK6mandatoryoutput
Cleaned_Catalog_Assets/ folder contains the organized finished deliverablesautoA CC asset folder named 'Cleaned_Catalog_Assets/' exists and contains the finished SKU deliverables copied in for handoff: scope_master_corrected.jpg, scope_whitebg_hero.jpg, scope_cutout.png, scope_hero_square_2048.jpg, and brand_badge.svgK6mandatoryoutput
All four hero image deliverables depict the same single thermal scope SKU consistentlyexpertscope_master_corrected.jpg, scope_whitebg_hero.jpg, scope_cutout.png, and scope_hero_square_2048.jpg all show the same handheld thermal weapon scope from the supplied raw input — same product, consistent correction across the set, with no deliverable distorted in proportionK3mandatorybrief
No deliverable distorts, recolors, or composites onto the product itselfexpertAcross all image deliverables the scope is not stretched/warped, is not recolored beyond honest color-cast correction (true matte-black body and materials preserved), and has nothing composited or generatively painted onto the product body — only background and canvas operations were appliedK7dealbreakerbrief

AO-07   Men's pendant line: vectorize the 2D spec sketch + retouch and catalog-prep the sample studio shots

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 24)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

From the industrial designer's raster pendant sketch and two mixed-lighting workbench photos of a stainless-steel sample pendant and chain, produce a clean factory-ready vector spec asset plus retouched, isolated, color-accurate catalog hero shots (one expanded to a catalog canvas, one duotone print-screen variant) and gather everything on a Firefly board for the design team.

Full brief

Authored above in the full_brief field.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 24 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
pendant_spec_lineart.svgvectorClean scalable SVG of the deskewed, tight-cropped pendant line-art, crisp at any print size, suitable as a dimensional spec reference for the factory.
pendant_hero_catalog.pngimageRetouched, white-balanced, auto-toned sample pendant cut out from the bench onto a clean solid catalog background and outpainted/expanded to a roomy catalog hero canvas (e.g. ~4:5 or wider). PNG.
pendant_spec_screenprint.pngimageHigh-contrast duotone/halftone 'print-screen' variant of the hero pendant for the black-and-white spec sheet. PNG.
chain_hero_catalog.pngimageThe box-link chain color-matched to the pendant (same neutral white balance + tonal treatment) and isolated on the same clean catalog background, so pendant and chain read as a coordinated set. PNG.
pendant_line_review_boarddeep-linkA single Firefly board assembling the vector spec, the catalog hero, the screen-print variant and the chain hero as a moodboard/deep-link for the brand's design team to review during the refinements-and-iterations loop.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All five expected deliverables are present: pendant_spec_lineart.svg, pendant_hero_catalog.png, pendant_spec_screenprint.png, chain_hero_catalog.png, and the pendant_line_review_board Firefly board deep-link.autoAll five deliverables are delivered: the four files pendant_spec_lineart.svg, pendant_hero_catalog.png, pendant_spec_screenprint.png, chain_hero_catalog.png, plus the pendant_line_review_board Firefly board deep-link; missing any one of the five fails.K1mandatoryoutput
The pendant spec deliverable pendant_spec_lineart.svg is a valid scalable vector SVG (not a raster PNG/JPG renamed) containing vector path/shape line work.autoFile parses as well-formed SVG (MIME image/svg+xml, .svg extension) containing vector path/shape elements; it is not an embedded raster or a renamed bitmap.K1dealbreakeroutput
The three image deliverables (pendant_hero_catalog.png, pendant_spec_screenprint.png, chain_hero_catalog.png) are each valid PNG files.autoEach of the three image deliverables has a .png extension and a valid PNG file signature.K1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized pendant line-art in pendant_spec_lineart.svg is deskewed (square/upright), correcting the input scan's ~4-degree clockwise rotation.expertThe pendant outline sits square to the canvas axes with no residual ~4-degree clockwise skew from the original scan (auto_straighten applied before vectorize).K3mandatorybrief
The vectorized pendant line-art is tight-cropped to the pendant artwork, with the scanned white paper margin/border removed.expertpendant_spec_lineart.svg bounds hug the pendant line-art with no large surrounding white scan margin or paper border retained.K3mandatorybrief
pendant_spec_lineart.svg preserves the sketch's drawn features: rounded-square body, soldered top bail loop, interior compass-rose engraving lines, and edge dimension tick marks, as clean black line work.expertThe vector reproduces the rounded-square pendant outline, soldered top bail loop, fine interior compass-rose engraving, and edge dimension tick marks as clean black line work without fabricating or dropping any of these spec features.K2mandatoryinput
The pendant in pendant_hero_catalog.png is cleanly isolated from the busy machinist's bench background (no calipers, layout square, or metal shavings remaining).expertNo bench-clutter elements (calipers, layout square, metal shavings, workbench surface) are visible; the pendant is cleanly masked with no haloing or clipped edges.K3mandatorybrief
pendant_hero_catalog.png places the cut-out pendant on a clean solid light-gray catalog background (solid-color fill), not on a composited photographic scene.expertThe background behind the pendant is a single clean solid catalog fill (light gray, consistent with image_fill_area solid-color fill), not another photo composited in.K1mandatoryoutput
pendant_hero_catalog.png is outpainted/expanded to a roomy catalog hero canvas (~4:5 portrait or wider), not a tight subject-filling crop.autoImage aspect ratio is approximately 4:5 (portrait) or wider, with generous catalog space around the pendant, not a tight subject-filling crop.K1mandatoryoutput
The pendant's mixed warm-shop / cool-window yellow-green color cast is neutralized so the stainless steel reads as clean neutral white-balanced metal in pendant_hero_catalog.png.expertThe metal appears neutral / cool-neutral with no residual yellow-green cast; white balance reads corrected versus the off-neutral input photo.K4mandatorybrief
Brushed-steel highlight detail is retained (not clipped to pure white) in the toned pendant hero.expertBright areas on the pendant keep brushed-metal grain/texture rather than blowing out to flat clipped white.K4qualitybrief
pendant_spec_screenprint.png is a high-contrast duotone/halftone print-screen variant of the same pendant as the hero, suitable for the black-and-white spec sheet.expertThe image is a high-contrast halftone/duotone (effectively B&W print-screen) rendition of the same pendant shown in pendant_hero_catalog.png, not a full-color photo.K1mandatoryoutput
chain_hero_catalog.png shows the box-link chain isolated from the bench on the SAME clean solid light-gray catalog background used for the pendant hero.expertThe chain is cleanly cut out (no bench clutter) and placed on the identical solid light-gray catalog background fill as pendant_hero_catalog.png.K3mandatoryoutput
The chain in chain_hero_catalog.png is color-matched to the pendant: same neutralized white balance and tonal treatment so the pair reads as one coordinated set.expertThe chain's metal white-balance and tonal grade visibly match the pendant hero (same neutral steel cast), so pendant and chain read as a consistent coordinated pair, not two differently-graded shots.K4mandatorybrief
The pendant_line_review_board Firefly board assembles all four produced assets (vector spec, catalog hero, screen-print variant, chain hero) into one reviewable deep-link.expertThe Firefly board deep-link opens and contains all four deliverables (pendant_spec_lineart.svg, pendant_hero_catalog.png, pendant_spec_screenprint.png, chain_hero_catalog.png) gathered for the design team's refinements-and-iterations review.K6mandatoryoutput
No 3D/CAD model is fabricated and no deliverable is invented from scratch; the response delivers only the Adobe-doable 2D asset-prep slice using only generative_expand as the generative step.expertDeliverables are derived from the supplied sketch and two bench photos (deskew/crop/vectorize, grade/mask/solid-fill/outpaint/halftone); no invented 3D CAD render is presented as if produced, and image_generative_expand (the hero outpaint) is the ONLY generative step used.K7dealbreakerbrief

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-08   Signature Bracelet Launch: Studio Retouch, White-Background E-Comm Set, Graded Hero, Expanded Social Banner & Vectorized Mark

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 18 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the brand's raw studio prototype shots, wordmark, and lifestyle frame into a launch image kit: a clean white-background e-commerce set, a color-graded close-up hero, an expanded social banner, and a vectorized wordmark — then assemble everything on a Firefly board for sign-off.

Full brief

We are launching the signature bracelet for our new lifestyle and cultural brand (gathering, celebration, connection, memorable experiences). Prototypes are made and we have shot them in the studio, but the raw frames are crooked, dull, and shot on a slightly grey seamless that needs to read as pure white for e-commerce. We need you to produce the full launch image kit from the assets we supply — no new product design, just world-class post-production on what we shot. Specifically: 1) Clean the hero studio shot (DSLR raw of the woven-cord-and-gold-clasp bracelet on a slightly-tilted grey seamless): auto-straighten the horizon, crop to a clean square, and bring it to life with auto-tone plus exposure/contrast and a slight warm white-balance so the gold clasp glows. 2) Make it e-commerce-ready: select the bracelet subject, invert the selection, and fill the background to pure white (#FFFFFF) so it drops cleanly onto our product pages; boost only the gold's saturation so the clasp reads as real 18k without touching the cord color. 3) From that cleaned, graded master, produce a punchy color-graded CLOSE-UP HERO for the homepage: apply a cohesive Lightroom look/preset and a gentle lens blur so the macro clasp detail pops, then crop to a 4:5 portrait. 4) Produce a wide SOCIAL/WEB BANNER from the same graded master by outpainting (generative expand) the white studio sweep out to a 16:9 canvas so we have clean negative space for headline text on the website hero. 5) We also supply a flat PNG of our wordmark — vectorize it to a clean SVG so it scales for packaging, the website, and the bracelet's hang-tag. 6) License one premium lifestyle "table of friends gathered" stock backdrop from Adobe Stock at full res so our social team has an on-brand scene to drop the banner over. 7) Assemble the final graded master, the white-background e-comm shot, the close-up hero, the expanded banner, the vectorized wordmark, and the licensed backdrop onto a single Firefly board so we can review and approve the whole kit in one place. Deliverables: pure-white-background e-commerce JPEG, a 4:5 graded close-up hero, a 16:9 expanded banner with text-safe negative space, a scalable SVG wordmark, the licensed stock backdrop, and a shareable Firefly board link. Keep the look elevated, warm, and timeless to match the brand.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain jewelryDeliverable product-shotWorkflow EditOperation O4Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 21Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand Gather & Co.
5Planning3Brand strictness5Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Technical Quality · Brand Alignment · Process. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

brand_wordmark_flat.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageFlat raster PNG of a refined lifestyle-brand WORDMARK reading 'GATHER & CO.' in an elegant high-contrast serif, deep cha
gathering_lifestyle_frame.jpgimagegpt-image-2Warm candid documentary photograph of a group of friends gathered around a wooden table at golden hour — laughing, raise

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpgimageSquare ~2400x2400 JPEG, bracelet on pure #FFFFFF background, auto-toned + exposure/contrast corrected + warm WB, gold clasp saturation-boosted; drop-in ready for product detail pages.
homepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpgimage4:5 portrait (~2048x2560) color-graded close-up hero with a cohesive Lightroom preset look + gentle lens blur isolating the macro clasp detail.
social_banner_16x9.jpgimage16:9 (~2560x1440) banner produced by generative-expand/outpaint of the white studio sweep, leaving clean text-safe negative space for a website-hero headline.
wordmark_vector.svgvectorClean scalable SVG traced from the flat wordmark PNG; single-color charcoal, crisp paths for packaging / web / hang-tag.
licensed_gathering_backdrop.jpgimageFull-resolution licensed Adobe Stock 'friends gathered at a table' lifestyle backdrop (presigned download URL) for the social team to place the banner over.
launch_kit_firefly_boarddataFirefly Boards deep-link assembling the graded master, white-bg e-comm shot, 4:5 hero, 16:9 banner, vectorized wordmark, and licensed backdrop for one-place review/approval.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All six deliverables are present: ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg, homepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpg, social_banner_16x9.jpg, wordmark_vector.svg, licensed_gathering_backdrop.jpg, and the launch_kit_firefly_board link.autoExactly 6 deliverables exist matching the names ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg (JPEG), homepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpg (JPEG), social_banner_16x9.jpg (JPEG), wordmark_vector.svg (SVG), licensed_gathering_backdrop.jpg (JPEG), and one launch_kit_firefly_board URL; none missing.K1mandatoryoutput
The e-commerce shot background is pure white #FFFFFF.autoSampling the background region of ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg returns RGB exactly (255,255,255) / #FFFFFF across the seamless area surrounding the bracelet.K1dealbreakerbrief
The e-commerce shot is a square ~2400x2400 JPEG.autoecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg is JPEG format with width == height, dimensions approximately 2400x2400 (within ~5% tolerance).K1mandatoryoutput
The homepage close-up hero is a 4:5 portrait at ~2048x2560.autohomepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpg is JPEG with width:height ratio == 4:5 (portrait), dimensions approximately 2048x2560 (within ~5% tolerance).K1mandatoryoutput
The social banner is a 16:9 landscape at ~2560x1440.autosocial_banner_16x9.jpg is JPEG with width:height ratio == 16:9 (landscape), dimensions approximately 2560x1440 (within ~5% tolerance).K1mandatoryoutput
The wordmark deliverable is a valid scalable SVG vector file (not a raster).autowordmark_vector.svg parses as valid SVG containing vector path/shape elements, with no embedded raster bitmap (e.g. no <image>/base64 data URI) as the trace content.K1dealbreakeroutput
The licensed backdrop is delivered as a full-resolution Adobe Stock asset via a presigned download URL.autolicensed_gathering_backdrop.jpg resolves from an Adobe Stock license-and-download presigned URL and is full-resolution (not a watermarked comp/preview).K6mandatoryoutput
The Firefly board assembles exactly the graded master plus the five produced/licensed deliverables.autoThe launch_kit_firefly_board link opens to a shareable board containing all six elements: the graded master hero_warm_master, ecom_white_bg_bracelet, homepage_hero_closeup_4x5, social_banner_16x9, wordmark_vector, and licensed_gathering_backdrop.K6mandatoryoutput
The vectorized SVG wordmark reads 'GATHER & CO.' and preserves the knotted-cord-loop icon from the supplied flat PNG.expertThe SVG legibly renders the text 'GATHER & CO.' in the high-contrast serif and includes the minimal line-art knotted cord loop icon to the left of the text, matching the supplied brand_wordmark_flat.png; the brand mark is traced, not regenerated or restyled.K2dealbreakerinput
The wordmark vector is single-color charcoal, matching the supplied flat PNG.expertwordmark_vector.svg paths are a single deep-charcoal fill color with no gradients or introduced colors, faithful to the charcoal of brand_wordmark_flat.png.K2mandatoryinput
The hero studio shot horizon is straightened (level) and not left crooked.expertIn the e-comm and graded outputs the seamless sweep / contact-shadow line reads level (the few-degrees tilt of the raw bracelet_studio_hero.jpg is corrected), with no awkward post-straighten crop artifacts.K3mandatorybrief
Only the gold clasp saturation is boosted; the navy waxed-cord band color is unchanged.expertIn ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg the gold/yellow of the barrel clasp and charm reads as richer/more saturated 18k gold while the dark navy braided cord retains its original hue (single-color saturation, no global shift).K3mandatorybrief
The bracelet subject is cleanly masked against the white background with no grey-seamless fringe or clipped product edges.expertAround the bracelet in ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg the select-subject -> invert -> fill-white edge is clean: no residual grey halo, no white spill onto cord fibers or gold, and fine cord/clasp edges intact.K3mandatorybrief
The 16:9 social banner leaves clean text-safe negative space for a headline.expertThe generative-expand outpaint in social_banner_16x9.jpg extends the white studio sweep with an uncluttered, seamless region of negative space suitable for overlaying a website-hero headline; expansion has no visible seams or artifacts.K3mandatoryoutput
The close-up hero applies a cohesive Lightroom preset look plus a gentle lens blur, and the overall kit reads elevated, warm, and timeless.experthomepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpg shows a cohesive graded preset look with a soft lens blur over the macro clasp detail, and the e-comm / hero / banner set shares a warm, elevated, timeless tone consistent with the brand.K4qualitybrief
All edited pixels originate from the client-supplied photos/assets; nothing is generated from scratch or composited beyond the single allowed outpaint.expertecom, hero, banner and SVG all derive from bracelet_studio_hero.jpg and brand_wordmark_flat.png; the only generative content is the 16:9 outpaint negative space; no from-scratch product design, no prompt-based background replacement, no compositing of the bracelet onto a new scene.K7dealbreakerbrief

AO-10   Modern Silver Rosary Necklace — Commercial Product Package (Hero Retouch + Vector Spec + Screen-Print Tech Graphic + Data-Merge Spec Sheet)

Brand/vertical Fashion & ApparelFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's raw studio shot of a modern silver rosary necklace into a full commercial product package: a retouched hero image on a clean sweep, a vector line-art spec, a screen-print-style halftone tech graphic, and a print-ready data-merged spec sheet — all assembled into a review board for revision rounds.

Full brief

We sell handcrafted sterling silver religious jewelry and have just finalized a new MODERN-style rosary necklace (clean geometric links, minimal crucifix, brushed-silver finish). I shot it on my bench under mixed lighting and need a freelancer to build the full commercial product package we can use across our store listing, lookbook, a stamped/printed merch concept, and our wholesale line sheet. Deliverables: 1. A retouched HERO image of the rosary: straightened, auto-toned, exposure/highlight balanced so the brushed silver reads true (not yellow-cast), the subject isolated, dropped onto a clean neutral studio sweep, with the canvas extended to a roomy 4:5 e-commerce frame and a soft blurred-background depth look. Deliver a polished JPEG and the cut-out transparent PNG. 2. A clean VECTOR line-art / silhouette of the necklace (SVG) we can scale for stamping, embroidery, and the modern logo-lockup spec sheet. 3. A screen-print / halftone TECH GRAPHIC of the rosary (single-tone, print-screen look) for a tote/tee merch mockup and our stamping vendor. 4. A print-ready PRODUCT SPEC SHEET: take our authored InDesign spec-sheet template and our SKU CSV (material, chain length, crucifix size, finish, price tiers) and data-merge it into a per-variant PDF, plus a vector variant of the line sheet from our Illustrator template. 5. Assemble the hero, the cut-out, the vector spec, the halftone graphic and the spec-sheet preview into a single Firefly review board (deep-link) so we can run revision rounds. Keep everything modern, minimal, and silver-true. I will pick the final crop. Strong communication for revisions is essential.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
rosary_hero_4x5.jpgimageRetouched commercial hero, 4:5 (e.g. 2400x3000px) JPEG sRGB: straightened, auto-toned, neutral white balance (silver-true), subject isolated on a clean neutral sweep, canvas outpainted to a roomy 4:5 frame, soft lens-blur background depth.
rosary_cutout.pngimageTransparent-background PNG cut-out of the rosary (background removed), full-res, for listings and the spec-sheet image placeholder.
rosary_lineart.svgvectorClean vectorized line-art / silhouette SVG of the necklace for stamping/embroidery and the logo-lockup spec.
rosary_halftone_tech.pngimageSingle-tone screen-print / halftone tech graphic PNG of the rosary for tote/tee merch and the stamping vendor.
rosary_spec_sheet_merged.pdfpdfMulti-page print-ready PDF, one page per SKU variant, produced by data-merging the authored .indd template against sku_variants.csv (material/length/finish/price + hero image).
rosary_line_sheet.pngimageRendered wholesale line-sheet card(s) from the authored .ai after Illustrator-variable data-merge against the CSV.
rosary_review_board_linkdataFirefly Board deep-link assembling hero, cut-out, vector line-art, halftone graphic and spec-sheet preview for client revision rounds.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All 7 expected deliverables are present: rosary_hero_4x5.jpg, rosary_cutout.png, rosary_lineart.svg, rosary_halftone_tech.png, rosary_spec_sheet_merged.pdf, rosary_line_sheet.png, and rosary_review_board_link.autoExactly these 7 named outputs exist and are non-empty: a JPEG hero (rosary_hero_4x5.jpg), a PNG cut-out (rosary_cutout.png), an SVG line-art (rosary_lineart.svg), a PNG halftone (rosary_halftone_tech.png), a merged PDF spec sheet (rosary_spec_sheet_merged.pdf), a PNG line sheet (rosary_line_sheet.png), and a Firefly Board deep-link string (rosary_review_board_link).K1mandatoryoutput
The hero image rosary_hero_4x5.jpg is delivered as a JPEG in sRGB at a 4:5 portrait aspect ratio.autoFile is a valid JPEG in sRGB color space with width:height = 4:5 portrait (e.g. 2400x3000px); height/width == 1.25 within +/-1% tolerance.K1mandatoryoutput
The cut-out rosary_cutout.png is a PNG with a transparent (alpha) background and the rosary subject isolated.autoFile is a valid PNG containing an alpha channel with fully transparent pixels in the background region surrounding the necklace.K1mandatoryoutput
The line-art deliverable rosary_lineart.svg is a true scalable SVG vector file, not a rasterized image embedded in SVG.autoFile is well-formed SVG containing vector path/shape elements (e.g. <path>/<polygon>) that resolve to scalable line-art of the necklace, and does NOT consist solely of a single embedded raster <image> bitmap.K1mandatoryoutput
The halftone tech graphic rosary_halftone_tech.png is a single-tone screen-print / halftone-style PNG of the rosary.autoFile is a valid PNG exhibiting a single-tone halftone/screen-print dot pattern (one ink color), recognizable as the rosary silhouette.K1mandatoryoutput
The spec-sheet PDF rosary_spec_sheet_merged.pdf is a multi-page print-ready PDF with exactly one page per SKU variant from sku_variants.csv (6 rows).autoFile is a valid PDF with exactly 6 pages, each page populated from a distinct sku_variants.csv row.K1mandatoryoutput
The spec-sheet PDF was produced by binding the authored .indd merge fields so each page renders the actual per-variant CSV values rather than literal placeholder tokens.autoVia OCR/text extraction, no page contains literal merge-field tokens (e.g. '<<sku>>', '<<material>>', '<<chain_length>>', '<<finish>>', '<<wholesale_price>>', '<<retail_price>>'); each page's text matches its corresponding sku_variants.csv row values for material, chain_length, finish, wholesale_price and retail_price.K1dealbreakerbrief
The per-variant pages reflect the CSV's stated variation: chain lengths 45cm / 55cm / 65cm and finishes brushed silver / oxidized silver.autoAcross the 6 pages, the chain_length values collectively include 45cm, 55cm and 65cm, and the finish values include both 'brushed silver' and 'oxidized silver', matching sku_variants.csv exactly.K1mandatoryinput
The line sheet rosary_line_sheet.png is a PNG rendered from the authored .ai after Illustrator-variable data-merge, showing the bound CSV values (sku, product_name, material, wholesale_price).autoFile is a valid PNG; via OCR, visible text matches sku_variants.csv values for the bound variables sku, product_name, material and wholesale_price, and contains no literal Illustrator variable-name placeholders.K1mandatoryoutput
The review board deep-link assembles the hero, cut-out, vector line-art, halftone graphic and the line-sheet preview into one Firefly Board.expertrosary_review_board_link is a resolvable Firefly Board URL whose board contains all five assembled assets: the hero (rosary_hero_4x5.jpg), the cut-out (rosary_cutout.png), the line-art (rosary_lineart.svg), the halftone (rosary_halftone_tech.png) and a spec/line-sheet preview.K6mandatoryoutput
The hero retouch corrected the warm yellow desk-lamp cast so the brushed silver reads neutral/silver-true.expertOn the hero, the metal of the necklace reads as neutral brushed silver with no residual warm/yellow color cast; a neutral white-balance is evident versus the warm raw input.K4mandatorybrief
The hero capture was straightened so the necklace sits level (the raw input was shot crooked/tilted).expertThe necklace reads as level/upright in the hero, with the crooked tilt of the raw bench shot corrected.K3qualitybrief
The hero subject is isolated onto a clean neutral light-grey studio sweep (messy bench gone) with a soft blurred-background depth look.expertBackground is a clean neutral light-grey studio sweep with no bench clutter, the necklace sits cleanly on it, and a soft shallow-depth/lens-blur background quality is present.K3mandatorybrief
The subject-isolation mask/edge quality on the cut-out and hero is clean (no haloing, no clipped beads/links, no background fringe around the geometric links and crucifix).expertEdges of the necklace, beads, geometric links and crucifix are cleanly masked with no halo, fringe, or amputated detail.K3qualityoutput
The whole package holds the modern, minimal, silver-true (charcoal/silver) aesthetic stated in the brief across hero, line-art, halftone and spec/line sheets.expertAll deliverables present a consistent modern, minimal, charcoal/silver visual treatment true to the brief, with no clashing styling.K4qualitybrief
The brand wordmark 'ARGENT & FAITH' appears in the spec-sheet / line-sheet brand-lockup area and is not regenerated, re-typeset, or restyled.expertThe string 'ARGENT & FAITH' is legibly present in the spec-sheet / line-sheet wordmark lockup area in the supplied minimal charcoal sans-serif treatment, not re-typeset or re-generated into a different logo styling.K2mandatorybrand

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-11   Sterling-Silver Ruby Pendant — Retouched Hero Shots, Color-Matched Set, Vectorized Calligraphy Mark, Moodboard & Spec-Sheet Export

Brand/vertical Nonprofit, Religious &amp; CommunityFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 22)Tools 20 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's raw photos of a finished Sterling-Silver 925 ruby pendant into a retouched, color-consistent hero product-photo set with a clean white-background cut-out, an outpainted banner, a vectorized Arabic-calligraphy hallmark, a Firefly moodboard, and an exported lookbook/spec sheet.

Full brief

The client, Shady, has a finished custom pendant — Sterling Silver 925, opening double doors on hidden hinges, a ruby locking element, Islamic geometric openwork, and Arabic calligraphy engravings — and wants to present and market it ("share similar work, estimated cost, and timeline"). He supplies three studio photographs of the actual piece (a front-on hero, a three-quarter open-doors view showing the ruby latch, and a macro of the engraved calligraphy) plus a jeweler-authored InDesign spec/lookbook sheet (.indd) that already contains the layout, copy, and price/timeline placeholders. Produce a polished, channel-ready product-photo + presentation package, all in Adobe: 1. Ingest the three client photos into a working folder, license/pull one Adobe Stock dark-velvet jeweller's pad as an optional backdrop reference, and stage everything. 2. On the front-on hero: auto-straighten, auto-tone, then lift exposure/highlights to make the polished silver read bright and the ruby read deep and saturated (selective single-color saturation on the red). Warm the white balance slightly for a luxury feel. 3. Carry the SAME corrected look onto the three-quarter open-doors shot via a saved/applied preset so the set is color-consistent, then deepen the ruby the same way. 4. Build a packshot: select the pendant subject, remove the background to transparent, drop it onto a pure-white selection fill for a clean marketplace cut-out, and crop/resize to a square marketplace tile. 5. Outpaint (generative-expand) the hero into a wide hero-banner crop for the listing header. 6. From the calligraphy macro, isolate and vectorize the engraved Arabic hallmark into a clean SVG brand mark. 7. Assemble the finished shots into a Firefly Boards moodboard the client can review. 8. Export the jeweler-authored InDesign spec/lookbook .indd to a print-ready PDF and a web JPEG. Deliverables: corrected hero, color-matched open-doors shot, white-bg square packshot, wide hero-banner, ruby-deep variant, calligraphy SVG mark, Firefly board deep-link, and the exported spec-sheet PDF + JPEG.

hero_corrected.jpg shows the deliberate ~3-degree input tilt corrected (straightened) and the under-exposed baseline lifted (auto-toned, exposure raised) so the polished silver reads brightexpertPendant/chain appears level (the ~3deg input tilt is corrected) AND the silver is noticeably brighter/cleaner than the deliberately dark, under-exposed inputK1mandatorybrief
hero_corrected.jpg has a slightly warm white balance and a deep, saturated red ruby achieved via selective single-color saturation (ruby boosted without a global red cast on the silver)expertOverall white balance reads slightly warm (luxury feel) AND the ruby reads deep/saturated red while the silver stays neutral (no global red cast)K4qualitybrief
open_doors_matched.jpg is color-matched to hero_corrected.jpg via an applied preset, neutralizing the input's greener/cooler uneven cast so the two shots read as a consistent setexpertopen_doors_matched white balance and tone visibly match hero_corrected (the input's green/cool cast is removed); the pair looks shot/graded togetherK3mandatorybrief
open_doors_matched.jpg has the ruby latch deepened the same way the hero ruby was deepenedexpertThe ruby latch on the open-doors shot reads deep/saturated red consistent with the hero's ruby treatmentK4qualitybrief
packshot_white_square shows a clean subject cut-out: the pendant correctly isolated with crisp edges (chain and Islamic openwork preserved) against the white fillexpertPendant and chain are fully retained with clean, accurate mask edges (no background halo, no chopped chain/filigree, no eaten-away openwork)K3mandatorybrief
calligraphy_mark.svg reads as a clean, legible scalable brand mark with smooth traced edges (not noisy/jagged tracing artifacts from the macro)expertVectorized Arabic-calligraphy shapes are clean and recognizable, edges smooth, usable as a brand hallmark at scale, and correspond to the engraved strokes in pendant_calligraphy_macro.jpgK3qualitybrief
hero_banner_wide.jpg outpaint extends the hero plausibly left/right with seamless continuation of the charcoal-grey studio backdrop (no visible seam, repetition, or distortion of the pendant)expertGenerative-expanded regions blend seamlessly with the original; pendant unchanged/undistorted; no obvious seam or artifacting at the joinK4qualitybrief

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-12   Bright-and-Airy Lightroom Grade for a 38-Shot Food Photography Set (Flat-Lay, 45°, Side)

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Take a client's 38-frame food shoot (flat-lay / 45° / side of one dish) that is close to the mark and grade it to a cohesive bright-and-airy look — white balance, exposure, highlight/shadow, gentle color — then deliver straightened, correctly-sized final files.

Full brief

A food-photography client (single hero dish, shot three ways: an overhead flat-lay, a 45-degree three-quarter, and a side/profile shot, 38 frames total) hands you a set that is "close to the goal but needs tweaking." The goal is a consistent BRIGHT AND AIRY look across the whole set: clean neutral-to-slightly-warm white balance, lifted exposure, bright but not blown highlights on the plate and window light, open (not muddy) shadows, and gentle, appetising color — applied cohesively so the 38 frames read as one shoot. Quick turnaround / ASAP. Concretely, working in the Adobe connector entirely on the client's own pixels (no compositing, no generative fill, no background swap): 1. Ingest the three representative hero frames the recipe is tuned on, plus license one neutral linen/marble backdrop reference from Adobe Stock for the social teaser at the end. 2. On the flat-lay, square it up (auto-straighten the overhead so the plate edges are true) and crop to a clean composition. 3. Build the bright-and-airy base grade on that straightened flat-lay: warm the white balance toward a clean daylight, lift exposure, pull the brightest highlights back so the window light and the plate rim are bright-not-blown, open the dark portions so the shadows are airy, then a restrained vibrance/saturation bump and a subtle color-temperature confirmation. 4. Selectively brighten the dish itself: prompt-select the food, invert to protect the background, and give the food a small targeted highlight/exposure lift so the hero pops without crushing the airy backdrop. 5. Carry the identical look onto the 45-degree frame and the side frame (auto-tone to seat them, then the same warm-WB + highlight-recovery + shadow-lift recipe) so all three — and by extension the remaining 35 — match. 6. Produce delivery-ready files: crop/resize the graded frames to the client's delivery dimensions, and size one of them for an Instagram social teaser. Deliverables: the three fully-graded hero frames at delivery resolution, the selective-food-pop master, an Instagram-sized teaser, and a short edit recipe note describing the cohesive look so the client can see exactly how the set was unified.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
flatlay_bright_airy.jpgimageStraightened, cropped, fully bright-and-airy-graded flat-lay at client delivery resolution (3000px long edge, sRGB JPG).
food_pop_master.jpgimageThe flat-lay grade with a selective targeted lift on the dish (food prompt-selected, background protected) — the hero master, full resolution sRGB JPG.
angle45_bright_airy.jpgimage45-degree frame matched to the same bright-and-airy look (auto-tone + warm WB + highlight recovery + shadow lift), delivery resolution sRGB JPG.
side_bright_airy.jpgimageSide frame matched to the same look, delivery resolution sRGB JPG.
ig_teaser_1080.jpgimageInstagram teaser crop/resize of the food-pop master at 1080x1350 (4:5) sRGB JPG.
edit_recipe_note.txttextShort plain-text note documenting the cohesive bright-and-airy recipe (the ordered list of adjustments and their intent) so the client can see how the set was unified; compiled locally from the connector step log.

flatlay_bright_airy.jpg is delivered at 3000px on its long edge in sRGB JPGautoflatlay_bright_airy.jpg long edge == 3000px, format == JPEG, color space == sRGB (per output spec: '3000px long edge, sRGB JPG')K1mandatoryoutput
food_pop_master.jpg is delivered at full resolution as an sRGB JPGautofood_pop_master.jpg is JPEG, color space == sRGB, at full resolution (long edge >= 3000px, consistent with the flat-lay master it is built from)K1mandatoryoutput

ig_teaser_1080.jpg is derived from food_pop_master (the selective-food-pop master), not a raw or un-graded frameexpertThe Instagram teaser visibly shows the same bright-and-airy grade AND the selective food-pop lift present in food_pop_master.jpg (same dish framing, same tonal look), confirming it is a crop/resize of the food-pop masterK1mandatoryoutput
The flat-lay was straightened (overhead squared so plate edges are true) and cropped before gradingexpertIn flatlay_bright_airy.jpg the ~1-2 degree tilt of flatlay_raw.jpg is corrected — plate edges/table grain read true/square and the rotation margins are cropped out, vs the tilted rawK3mandatorybrief
The flat-lay grade applies the bright-and-airy base recipe: warm/clean white balance, lifted exposure, recovered (bright-not-blown) highlights, and opened shadowsexpertvs flatlay_raw.jpg, flatlay_bright_airy.jpg is warmer (the faintly-cool import neutralised toward clean daylight), brighter overall, with window light / honey / plate-rim highlights bright but not clipped/blown, and the under-plate shadows opened/airy (not muddy)K4mandatorybrief
The flat-lay color is given a restrained vibrance/saturation bump — appetising but not garishexpertColor in flatlay_bright_airy.jpg is more vivid/appetising than the raw yet still natural — no oversaturated or garish castK4qualitybrief
food_pop_master.jpg shows a selective targeted lift on the dish (ricotta-fig honey toast) while the airy background is protectedexpertIn food_pop_master.jpg the food (toast and figs) is brighter/popped relative to the flat-lay base grade, while the surrounding plate/table/background brightness is unchanged (background protected) — no global brightenK3mandatoryoutput
The selective food-pop edge is clean — the food mask does not bleed into or visibly halo the protected backgroundexpertNo visible brightness halo, fringe, or hard mask edge between the lifted food and the protected background in food_pop_master.jpgK3qualitybrief
angle45_bright_airy.jpg matches the same bright-and-airy look as the flat-lay (auto-tone seat + warm WB + highlight recovery + shadow lift)expertThe 45-degree frame reads as the same shoot as flatlay_bright_airy.jpg — comparable warm-clean white balance, brightness, highlight handling and open shadows; not a mismatched or differently-toned gradeK4mandatoryoutput
side_bright_airy.jpg matches the same bright-and-airy look, with the bright background window recovered and shadows openedexpertThe side/profile frame reads cohesively with the other two — warm-clean WB, lifted brightness, the bright background window bright-not-blown, and opened shadows; consistent with the setK4mandatoryoutput
All three graded frames read as one cohesive shoot (consistent white balance, exposure and color across flat-lay, 45-degree and side)expertViewed together, flatlay_bright_airy.jpg, angle45_bright_airy.jpg and side_bright_airy.jpg show a unified bright-and-airy grade with no frame standing out as cooler/warmer/darker/brighter than the othersK4mandatorybrief
All grades operate on the client's own pixels only — no generative fill, object removal, background replacement, or compositingexpertEach graded frame shows the identical dish/props/scene as its corresponding raw input (ricotta toast with figs, honey, microgreens on the matte off-white plate, pale oak table, linen napkin, espresso cup) with only tonal/color changes — no added, removed, replaced or composited contentK7dealbreakerbrief
edit_recipe_note.txt is a plain-text note documenting the ordered bright-and-airy recipe and its intentautoedit_recipe_note.txt is plain text and lists the ordered adjustments (straighten/crop, warm white balance, exposure lift, highlight recovery, shadow open, vibrance, selective food pop, angle matching) describing how the set was unified into one cohesive lookK5qualityoutput

Layer 2 · Operation craft checklist — Photo & Image (expert marks Pass / Minor / Major vs reference images)

craft itemPassMinorMajor
Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost
Colour & white balanceneutral / on-brand; tones naturalslight castclear cast / wrong colour
Mask & edge qualityclean edges; fine detail (hair) keptsmall halo / jaggiesvisible halo / clipped subject
Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic
Texture & detail preservedrealistic micro-detailslightly softdetail smeared or lost
Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

AO-13   Taco Tuesday & Lunch-Special Meta Ad Photo-Asset Prep — Retouch, Grade, Cutout, Multi-Ratio Outpaint & Delivery Pack for a West Palm Beach Mexican Restaurant

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 20)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Prep and deliver the campaign-ready photo + brand asset pack (retouched, warm/bold-graded hero food shots, color-splash and subject-pop scroll-stop variants, cut-out dishes, three Meta ad ratios via outpaint, a licensed backdrop texture, and a vectorized logo) for a West Palm Beach Mexican restaurant's Taco Tuesday & Lunch-Special Meta ad mockups.

Full brief

You are the production designer prepping the photo and brand assets that will be dropped into 12 static Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ad mockups for a family-owned, authentic Mexican restaurant in West Palm Beach, FL. The brand is warm, bold, family-oriented and community-focused — a local neighborhood favorite, NOT a corporate chain. The campaign covers two offers: Taco Tuesday (scroll-stop, urgent, recurring-promo feel) and Lunch Specials. The client has supplied three raw phone/DSLR food photos (a hero al-pastor taco platter, a carne-asada lunch plate, a margarita-and-chips table scene), plus their logo as a flat PNG. Your job, entirely within the Adobe connector: 1) Load the three supplied food photos and the logo PNG into the project. 2) Straighten and tonally fix the hero taco shot (auto-straighten the slightly tilted handheld frame, then auto-tone), so it reads clean and appetizing. 3) Push a warm, bold restaurant grade on the corrected hero: lift color temperature toward warm, boost vibrance/saturation so the salsa reds and lime greens pop, and recover highlight detail on the glossy meat — the "appetizing and bold" look the brand wants. 4) Build a scroll-stop "color-splash" variant from the graded hero: select the taco/food by prompt, invert the selection so everything BUT the food is selected, and desaturate that background so the food is the only color on screen (eye-catching scroll-stop power). 5) Apply an Adaptive "Subject Pop" preset to the graded hero to create a second high-contrast scroll-stop variant with the food made to stand forward. 6) Create a clean cut-out of the lunch plate: remove the background to a transparent PNG so the dish can float on any colored ad background the layout team chooses. 7) Outpaint the graded hero into the three Meta ad canvases the campaign needs — square 1:1 feed, 4:5 portrait feed, and 9:16 story — by generative-expand (extend the table/scene, do not crop the food). 8) License one on-brand backdrop texture (warm woven Mexican textile / rustic wood) from Adobe Stock at full res for the layout team to use behind cut-outs. 9) Vectorize the supplied logo PNG into a clean SVG so it scales crisply across every ad size. 10) Assemble all produced assets into a Firefly board as a labeled review deck for the client, and copy the final delivery set into a clearly named project folder. Deliverables: the corrected + warm-graded hero JPEG; the color-splash hero; the Subject-Pop hero; the transparent-PNG lunch-plate cut-out; three ratio-correct hero canvases (1:1, 4:5, 9:16); one licensed full-res backdrop texture; the logo SVG; a Firefly review board link; and a named delivery folder containing the set.

The graded hero shows a warm color-temperature shift versus the supplied raw photoautohero_warm_graded.jpg has a measurably warmer average tone than the input hero_taco_platter.jpg (higher mean red-to-blue ratio / warmer white balance), consistent with the brief's 'lift color temperature toward warm' stepK1mandatorybrief
The hero taco platter was straightened from its ~4-degree handheld tiltexpertIn hero_warm_graded.jpg the table edges read level (the ~4-degree tilt of the input hero_taco_platter.jpg is corrected), with no obvious residual rotationK3mandatorybrief
The color-splash variant keeps the food in full color while the rest of the frame is desaturatedexpertIn hero_color_splash.jpg only the taco/food region retains saturated color and everything outside it (table, background) is visibly desaturated toward grayscale, with the splash boundary following the food edgeK3mandatorybrief
The subject-pop variant is a distinct higher-contrast treatment of the graded heroexperthero_subject_pop.jpg is visibly different from hero_warm_graded.jpg, showing the higher-contrast / food-forward Adaptive 'Subject Pop' look (not an identical copy of the graded hero)K4mandatorybrief
The lunch-plate cut-out has clean, layout-ready edges around the carne asada dishexpertIn lunch_plate_cutout.png the carne asada plate is fully retained with the cluttered table background removed, and the cut edge is clean (no haloing, leftover background fringe, or chunks missing from the plate)K3mandatorybrief
The three ratio canvases extend the scene by outpainting without cropping the foodexpertIn hero_1x1_feed.jpg, hero_4x5_feed.jpg, and hero_9x16_story.jpg the original taco platter food is fully present and uncropped, and the added canvas is a plausible continuation of the table/scene (no visible seam or distorted duplication at the expansion boundary)K3mandatorybrief
The backdrop texture is a properly licensed full-res Adobe Stock asset matching the brief's directionexpertbackdrop_texture_fullres.jpg is a full-resolution Adobe Stock asset delivered with a valid license (not a watermarked comp), depicting a warm woven Mexican textile or rustic-wood surfaceK2mandatorybrief
The vectorized logo faithfully reproduces the supplied El Vecino Cocina logoexpertlogo_vector.svg visually matches restaurant_logo_flat.png — same 'El Vecino Cocina' wordmark, sombrero-and-chili-pepper emblem, and terracotta-red/deep-green palette — with shapes traced cleanly and nothing redrawn, renamed, or inventedK2dealbreakerinput
The vectorized logo color palette is preserved from the source PNGautologo_vector.svg uses the source logo's warm terracotta-red and deep-green colors (dominant fill colors match the input restaurant_logo_flat.png, not arbitrary substitute hues)K2mandatoryinput
The Firefly review board assembles the produced delivery set as a client-approval deckexpertThe Firefly board deep-link opens a board containing the produced campaign assets (graded hero, both scroll-stop variants, lunch-plate cut-out, three ratio canvases, backdrop texture, logo) presented as a labeled review deck for client approvalK6mandatoryoutput

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-14   Brickyard retro one-page menu: food-photo grade + logo vectorize + InDesign data-merge to print PDF/JPEG

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 25)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Grade four raw burger photos, vectorize the Brickyard wordmark, source a chalkboard texture, and run the client's authored InDesign menu template through a data-merge to produce a print-ready PDF and high-res JPEG.

Full brief

Brickyard, a retro street-food burger joint, needs its one-page menu finished for print. The owner has handed off an authored InDesign menu template (menu_template.indd) laid out in a rustic chalkboard style with four photo frames and real data-merge fields (<<section>>, <<item>>, <<desc>>, <<price>> across Classic Burgers, Specialty Burgers, Sides, Beverages), the brand wordmark as a flat PNG, four raw phone-shot food photos (a double Brickyard, a specialty bacon-jalapeño burger, a basket of fries, a craft cola), and a CSV of placeholder item names / descriptions / prices. The four food photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull/flat, cool white-balance, busy backgrounds. The job: (1) make every food photo look vibrant and appetizing — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a hot street-food look, push vibrance, apply a food-pop preset, and throw the cluttered background out of focus so the food reads as hero; consistently crop all four to the same frame ratio the template expects; (2) lift the hero double-smash shot cleanly onto a transparent background so it can sit over the chalkboard without a hard photographic edge; (3) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at large print size; (4) license a rustic chalkboard/blackboard texture from Adobe Stock to back the layout; (5) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the placeholder-prices CSV so each section/item/price binds into the template, and export the bound document to a print-ready PDF and a high-resolution JPEG; (6) assemble the produced assets onto a Firefly board so the owner can sign off on the photo treatment and final menu in one deep-link. Every section/price stays placeholder per the brief.

See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

photo_bev_craft_cola.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of a craft cola in a frosted glass bottle with condensation, on a wooden counter, dim warm bar

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 25 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Brickyard_Menu_print.pdfpdfPrint-ready one-page A4 portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document; placeholder copy bound from CSV, graded food photos + vectorized wordmark + chalkboard texture placed; ~300 DPI print quality.
Brickyard_Menu_highres.jpgimageHigh-resolution JPEG of the same bound one-page menu (A4 portrait, max-quality) for digital/social sharing.
graded_food_photos_x4imageFour color-graded, straightened, consistently 4:5-cropped food photos (classic burger / specialty burger / fries / cola) matching the template frames; the classic hero also delivered as a transparent-background PNG.
brickyard_wordmark.svgvectorClean vector SVG of the Brickyard wordmark for crisp scaling at large print size.
Brickyard_Menu_review_boarddataFirefly board deep-link assembling the graded photos, vectorized wordmark, and final menu render for one-click owner sign-off.

The four food photos are graded to a consistent vibrant, appetizing street-food lookexpertAll four photos are straightened, well-toned, warmed in white balance, and vibrance-pushed so the food reads as hero and appetizing, with a consistent look across the setK4qualitybrief
Cluttered backgrounds behind the hero and specialty burgers are thrown out of focusexpertThe classic double-smash and specialty bacon-jalapeno burger photos show a defocused/blurred background so the food stands out as heroK3qualitybrief

AO-15   Umaya Tri-Fold Menu Refresh — Food Photography Prep, Brand-Cohesive Color Grade, Logo Vectorization & Source Export

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 20 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Prep, color-grade, cut out, crop, and print-export the new dish photography, the vectorized logo, the textured background, and the existing Illustrator menu source for Umaya's tri-fold menu refresh, all matched to the dark/red Japanese brand look and ready for the designer to place.

Full brief

Umaya is a ramen + sushi restaurant refreshing its existing tri-fold dine-in menu (22.5"x12" unfolded, print-ready CMYK 300 DPI). They are adding several new ramen bowls and an entirely new Wagyu section, and the brand look is fixed: dark background, hero food photography, red accent elements, Japanese ramen/sushi aesthetic. The page layout itself is being hand-typeset by their designer in the existing Adobe Illustrator source; this engagement is the PHOTOGRAPHY + BRAND-ASSET production that feeds that layout. Deliver a clean, cohesive set of placement-ready assets, all chained off the client's supplied raws.\n\nWork to perform with Adobe connectors:\n1. Ingest the client-supplied assets: two newly-shot dish photos (a tonkotsu ramen bowl and the new Wagyu donburi hero), the restaurant's flat logo PNG, and the client's existing authored Illustrator menu source (.ai).\n2. The Wagyu hero was shot slightly off-level — auto-straighten it, then auto-tone it as a neutral starting point.\n3. Pull the whole photo set to ONE cohesive brand look: warm the white balance toward the existing menu's amber/dark feel, deepen the dark portions (the dark-background brand requirement), lift the highlights on the broth/garnish so the food still pops, and push vibrance so the red chili oil / red accents read strong against the dark plate. Both dishes must end up matching.\n4. For the crowded ramen section, the designer needs tight cut-outs: isolate the ramen bowl subject and remove its background to a transparent PNG so it can sit close to type without a hard photo edge; do the same selective treatment so the Wagyu marbling reads (boost just the warm/red tones on the Wagyu via a prompt selection).\n5. Crop each finished shot to the exact aspect ratios the menu cells need at 300 DPI (a tall ramen cell and a wide Wagyu banner).\n6. Vectorize the flat logo PNG to clean SVG for crisp red-accent printing.\n7. Prep the dark textured background plate: take a licensed dark slate/wood stock texture, darken its light portions and add fine grain so it reads as a premium dark menu background and not a flat fill.\n8. Export print-ready PNG renders of the client's existing authored Illustrator menu source so the team has a current proof to drop the new assets into.\n9. Assemble the final delivered set into a Firefly board so the client can review all placement-ready assets in one deep-link, and package the files.\n\nEverything must chain: each step consumes the previous step's output. No layout is composed by the connector — the designer hand-places these assets into the existing .ai. Deliverables: color-graded + brand-matched dish photos, transparent ramen cut-out, print-cropped shots at 300 DPI, vectorized logo SVG, dark textured background plate, print-ready menu-source proof renders, and a review board.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
ramen_brand_graded.pngimageTonkotsu ramen bowl, brand-matched look: warmed WB, deepened darks, lifted broth/garnish highlights, vibrance pushed so red chili oil reads. Full-res PNG.
wagyu_brand_graded.pngimageWagyu donburi hero, auto-straightened + auto-toned + same brand grade as the ramen, with red/warm marbling tones selectively boosted. Full-res PNG.
ramen_cutout_transparent.pngimageRamen bowl isolated, background removed to full transparency (alpha PNG) for tight placement in the crowded ramen section.
ramen_cell_300dpi.pngimageRamen shot cropped to the tall menu-cell aspect ratio, sized for 300 DPI print placement.
wagyu_banner_300dpi.pngimageWagyu hero cropped to the wide banner aspect ratio for the new Wagyu section, sized for 300 DPI print placement.
house_of_umami_logo.svgvectorVectorized logo as clean SVG, red (#C8302A) + near-black, crisp for print red-accent use.
dark_background_plate.pngimageLicensed dark slate texture darkened in its light portions + fine grain added = premium dark menu background plate. Full-res PNG.
menu_source_proof.pngimagePrint-ready PNG render(s) of the client's existing authored Illustrator menu source, exported as a current proof to drop new assets into.
umami_menu_assets_boarddataFirefly board deep-link assembling all placement-ready delivered assets for client review, plus a packaged asset folder/zip.
