Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 15 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All 9 expected deliverables are present in the delivered setautoThe delivered set contains all 9 outputs named in the spec: ramen_brand_graded.png, wagyu_brand_graded.png, ramen_cutout_transparent.png, ramen_cell_300dpi.png, wagyu_banner_300dpi.png, house_of_umami_logo.svg, dark_background_plate.png, menu_source_proof.png, and the umami_menu_assets_board Firefly board deep-linkK1mandatoryoutput
The ramen cut-out is a transparent alpha PNG with the background removedautoramen_cutout_transparent.png is a PNG with an alpha channel where the area outside the ramen-bowl subject is fully transparent (alpha == 0), produced via image_remove_background on the graded ramen shotK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo is delivered as a true SVG vector fileautohouse_of_umami_logo.svg is valid XML with an <svg> root containing path/vector geometry (not an embedded raster <image>), derived from house_of_umami_logo_flat.png via image_vectorizeK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo preserves the brand red and near-black color schemeautohouse_of_umami_logo.svg uses the red accent #C8302A plus near-black as its only accent colors (single red accent #C8302A + near-black per the brand kit; no other accent hues introduced)K2dealbreakerbrand
The two print-crop shots are exported at the correct orientation and 300 DPI sizingautoramen_cell_300dpi.png is cropped to a tall (portrait, height > width) menu-cell aspect ratio and wagyu_banner_300dpi.png to a wide (landscape, width > height) banner aspect ratio, each carrying DPI metadata / pixel dimensions consistent with 300 DPI print sizingK1mandatoryoutput
The Wagyu hero was auto-straightened before the tonal workexpertThe horizon/surface of wagyu_brand_graded.png reads level: the ~3-4 degree off-level tilt of wagyu_donburi_hero_raw.jpg is corrected (to within roughly +/-1 degree) as the first step before gradingK1mandatorybrief
The menu-source proof is an export of the supplied authored Illustrator source, not a connector-composed layoutautomenu_source_proof.png is a print-ready PNG produced from house_of_umami_menu_existing.ai via document_render_vector — its dark-background / red-accent panels and section labels (Small Plates / Sushi Bowls / Artisan Ramen / Donburi Rice Bowls / Sushi Rolls / Nigiri-Sashimi / Kids / Desserts) match the authored .ai, and no tri-fold layout was composed by the connectorK7dealbreakerbrief
The dark background plate is the licensed stock texture darkened with grain added, not a flat fillautodark_background_plate.png is derived from the licensed dark_slate_texture_stock.jpg with its light portions darkened (image_adjust_light_portions) and fine grain added (image_add_grain), showing non-uniform per-pixel variance rather than a single solid colorK1mandatoryoutput
Both dish photos share one cohesive brand gradeexpertramen_brand_graded.png and wagyu_brand_graded.png read as a matched set — consistent warmed white balance, deepened darks, lifted highlights, and pushed vibrance so they look edited togetherK4mandatorybrief
The graded photos match the dark-background / red-accent Japanese brand lookexpertBoth graded dish photos show a warm amber-leaning white balance, deep dark background, and red chili-oil / red accents reading strong against the dark plate, matching the existing menu's dark / red-accent Japanese ramen-sushi aestheticK4mandatorybrand
The Wagyu marbling has a selective warm/red tone boostexpertwagyu_brand_graded.png shows the seared Wagyu marbling / red meat tones selectively enriched (warmer / redder than the surrounding shot) so the marbling reads rich, applied via a prompt-selected mask rather than to the whole frameK4qualitybrief
The ramen cut-out has clean subject-edge qualityexpertThe isolated ramen bowl in ramen_cutout_transparent.png has clean, accurate edges (no haloing, no leftover background fringe, no clipped bowl/garnish) suitable for tight placement next to type in the crowded ramen sectionK3qualitybrief
The food still looks appetising and realistic after the gradeexpertBroth surface, onsen egg, garnish and Wagyu slices remain photoreal and appetising (highlights lifted without blowing out, darks deep without crushing detail), not over-processedK4qualitybrief
All delivered assets are assembled into a reviewable Firefly board deep-linkautoumami_menu_assets_board is a working Firefly board deep-link assembling the placement-ready assets for client review: ramen_cell_300dpi, wagyu_banner_300dpi, ramen_cutout_transparent, house_of_umami_logo.svg, dark_background_plate, menu_source_proof, wagyu_brand_graded, and the graded ramen (photos_brand_graded)K6mandatoryoutput
The final deliverables are packaged into a single delivery folder for hand-offautoA single delivery folder/zip (via asset_copy_assets) collects the final files: ramen_cell_300dpi, wagyu_banner_300dpi, ramen_cutout_transparent, house_of_umami_logo.svg, dark_background_plate, menu_source_proof, and wagyu_brand_gradedK6qualityoutput

craft itemPassMinorMajor
Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost

Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic
Texture & detail preservedrealistic micro-detailslightly softdetail smeared or lost
Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

checkPassMinorMajorfeeds

State managementalways uses latest filesone stale file usedwrong / old assets throughoutK6

The callaccept / send-back / scrap (+ one-line reason)feeds the commission pass rate

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-16   Restaurant Menu: Categorized Food Videos + Color-Graded Menu Still Package

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

From the restaurant's raw food clips and flat-lay dish photos, produce a categorized (appetizers / mains / desserts) menu-media package: color-graded, canvas-expanded hero stills, three category-cover cards, a vector dish icon, a Firefly moodboard, plus vertical-reframed short clips and a sizzle reel for the in-store menu screens.

Full brief

A restaurant is rebuilding its digital menu (tablet/QR ordering + in-store vertical menu screens) and hired a freelancer to "create short, high-quality videos of each food item, categorized into appetizers, main courses, and desserts." The kitchen has shot raw footage and flat-lay stills of nine signature dishes on a pass under mixed tungsten/window light, but the material is ungraded, slightly tilted, shot 16:9, and the framing is too tight for the portrait menu screens — and the client also needs matching still imagery (menu thumbnails, three category-cover cards, a small icon set, a pitch moodboard) to go with the clips, since a menu can't be all video. Deliverable is a unified, on-brand media kit. Work entirely inside Adobe. STILLS BACKBONE (headless): ingest the client's raw flat-lay dish photo and the supplied tilted appetizer/dessert frames; auto-straighten and crop the hero dish; correct exposure/white-balance for the mixed lighting; apply a warm, appetizing food preset and lift dish vibrance; expand the 16:9 frame to a tall 4:5 hero via AI outpaint so the plated food breathes on the portrait menu card; deepen the warm tone for a "mains" cover and shift it cool-clean for a "desserts" cover so all three category covers share one grade but read distinctly; isolate the signature plate to vectorize a clean menu icon; and gather the produced hero + covers into a Firefly board as the client pitch. VIDEO (async product track): reframe the client's 16:9 dish clip to vertical for the menu screens (same length) and assemble the short dish clips into a categorized sizzle reel. Every editing step must build on the previous output so the final covers, icon, board and clips all derive from the same corrected hero — no from-scratch art, no compositing, no generative fill beyond the single canvas-expand.

5Planning2Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_main_4x5.pngimageColor-graded, auto-straightened, AI canvas-expanded hero of the signature main course, 4:5 portrait (2048x2560), warm appetizing grade, ready as the top menu card / thumbnail.
cover_mains_warm.pngimageMain-courses category cover derived from the hero, deepened warm tone, 4:5 (2048x2560).
cover_appetizers.pngimageAppetizers category cover: straightened, exposure/WB-corrected, same-grade appetizer frame, 4:5 (2048x2560).
cover_desserts_cool.pngimageDesserts category cover: same shared grade shifted cool-clean for desserts, 4:5 (2048x2560).
dish_icon.svgvectorClean vector icon of the isolated signature plate, flat SVG for menu UI / favicons / category chips.
menu_pitch_boardurlFirefly board deep-link assembling the hero + three category covers as the client-facing menu look pitch.
dish_clip_vertical_4x5.mp4videoThe main-course clip reframed to vertical for portrait menu screens, SAME length as source (async product render).
menu_sizzle_reel.mp4videoCategorized sizzle reel assembled from the appetizer / main / dessert clips for the menu screen loop (async product render).

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom

hero_main_4x5.png is exactly 4:5 portrait at 2048x2560 pixelsautohero_main_4x5.png dimensions read exactly 2048 wide x 2560 tall (4:5 portrait)K1mandatoryoutput
All three category covers (cover_mains_warm.png, cover_appetizers.png, cover_desserts_cool.png) are delivered as PNGs at 4:5 portrait 2048x2560autocover_mains_warm.png, cover_appetizers.png and cover_desserts_cool.png all exist as PNGs and each measures exactly 2048x2560K1mandatoryoutput
dish_icon.svg is delivered as a valid vector SVG fileautoA file named dish_icon.svg exists and parses as valid SVG vector markup (paths/shapes), not a raster image embedded in an SVG wrapperK1mandatoryoutput
menu_pitch_board is delivered as a Firefly board deep-link URLautomenu_pitch_board is a URL (kind=url) that resolves to a Firefly boardK1mandatoryoutput
dish_clip_vertical_4x5.mp4 is delivered as a vertical 4:5 MP4 the same length as the source 16:9 clip (reframe only, no trim)autodish_clip_vertical_4x5.mp4 exists as a valid MP4 with a vertical 4:5 frame and its duration equals the duration of the source dish_clip_landscape_16x9.mp4K1mandatoryoutput
menu_sizzle_reel.mp4 is delivered as a multi-clip MP4 assembled from the appetizer, main and dessert clipsautomenu_sizzle_reel.mp4 exists as a valid MP4 whose duration and cut structure reflect assembly of the appetizer, main and dessert dish clips into a single reelK1mandatoryoutput
The Firefly pitch board assembles exactly the hero plus the three category covers as the client menu-look pitchexpertThe board contains hero_main_4x5, cover_mains_warm, cover_appetizers and cover_desserts_cool (exactly the four produced still assets) presented as the client-facing menu-look pitchK1mandatoryoutput
The 4:5 hero was produced by AI canvas-expand (outpaint) of the 16:9 source, not by croppingexpertCompared to the 16:9 source, hero_main_4x5 shows outpainted canvas above/below the original plated food (food breathes on the card) with seamless extension, consistent with image_generative_expand rather than a tight cropK3mandatorybrief
The hero shows the tilt corrected and white-balance/exposure repaired from the mixed-light camera-originalexpertCompared to hero_dish_flatlay_raw.jpg, hero_main_4x5 is level (no residual tilt), evenly exposed, and the mixed tungsten/window color cast is neutralized so the food color reads trueK3mandatorybrief
The hero and all three covers share one warm food family grade yet read as distinct categoriesexpertcover_mains_warm reads as a deepened-warm variant of the hero, cover_appetizers carries the same warm preset, and cover_desserts_cool is visibly cool-clean shifted, while all four still read as one consistent family gradeK4mandatorybrief
cover_appetizers is the appetizer frame straightened and exposure/white-balance-corrected under the same warm preset as the heroexpertcover_appetizers derives from appetizer_frame_raw.jpg with its tilt leveled and exposure/WB corrected, and carries the same warm appetizing preset as the hero so it reads as part of the shared gradeK4mandatoryoutput
cover_desserts_cool reads cool-clean relative to the warm mains/appetizers coversexpertcover_desserts_cool has a visibly cooler/cleaner color temperature than cover_mains_warm and cover_appetizers, sourced from the dessert frameK4qualityoutput
Dish vibrance is lifted on the hero so sauce/garnish pop without oversaturationexperthero_main_4x5 shows enhanced but natural vibrance/saturation (sauce and garnish read vivid yet realistic, not blown out or oversaturated)K4qualitybrief
The dish_icon depicts the isolated signature plate from the finished heroexpertdish_icon.svg is a clean flat representation of the same signature plate isolated from hero_main_4x5 (the plate subject, not the background or a different dish)K2mandatoryoutput
Every deliverable derives from the corrected hero or the supplied source frames/clips, with no from-scratch art, no compositing, and no generative fill beyond the single canvas-expandexpertCovers, icon, board and clips all trace to the corrected hero or the client-supplied frames/clips; the only generative step present is the lone hero outpaint; no invented imagery, composites, or extra generative fill appearsK7dealbreakerbrief

AO-17   Dark-Mode Restaurant Website Hero Food Photography Pack

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 22)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Retouch a restaurant's raw plated-dish photos into a cohesive dark-mode hero-image pack (hero + menu tiles + mobile crop) and assemble a Firefly moodboard for the agency's web designer.

Full brief

We are a Portugal-based boutique digital agency building a premium restaurant website. The design is high-end, clean, modern-minimalist, Dark Mode aesthetic, and focuses heavily on high-quality food photography across the Home and Interactive Food Menu sections, fully optimized for mobile. We do NOT need the site built here — our Wix Studio designer handles layout. We need a food-photo retoucher to turn the restaurant's raw plate shots into a polished, cohesive dark-mode hero-image pack. Supplied raw assets: (1) a hero shot of a signature seared-scallop dish on a dark slate plate, shot slightly tilted under warm mixed restaurant lighting; (2) a flat-lay of a chocolate dessert on a marble board; (3) a close-up of a cocktail with garnish; plus the restaurant's existing flat wordmark logo as a PNG. Deliverables: 1. A polished, perfectly level dark-mode HERO image — straightened, auto-toned, exposure/contrast set for a moody dark background, the plate subject made to "pop" off the dark surround, cooler-warmer balance dialed to a premium look, with the canvas extended on the left so the web designer has clean negative space for an overlaid headline. Export-ready full-bleed desktop hero. 2. Two square MENU-TILE images (dessert + cocktail) graded to match the hero's dark, moody, consistent look so the Interactive Food Menu feels cohesive. 3. One full-bleed 9:16 MOBILE crop of the hero, subject-aware, for the mobile Home section. 4. The flat wordmark logo cleaned to a transparent PNG and vectorized to crisp SVG for retina/responsive use on the dark site. 5. A Firefly Boards moodboard collecting the final hero, menu tiles, and logo as a single deep-link the Wix designer can reference for the dark-mode visual direction. The whole set must read as one premium, dark, cohesive system. Match grade across all food images. Keep skin/food tones appetizing — dark mode, not muddy.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 22 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_dark_desktop.pngimageFull-bleed desktop website hero, ~2880x1620 (16:9), straightened, dark-mode graded, subject popped off a moody dark surround, canvas extended on the left for headline negative space, sRGB PNG.
menu_tile_dessert.pngimageSquare 1:1 menu-tile image (~1200x1200), graded to MATCH the hero's dark moody look, sRGB PNG.
menu_tile_cocktail.pngimageSquare 1:1 menu-tile image (~1200x1200), graded to MATCH the hero's dark moody look, sRGB PNG.
hero_mobile_9x16.pngimageFull-bleed mobile Home crop, 1080x1920 (9:16), subject-aware crop of the graded hero, sRGB PNG.
logo_wordmark.svgvectorTransparent, cleanly vectorized wordmark logo as crisp SVG for retina/responsive use on the dark site (plus the transparent-PNG intermediate).
moodboard_deeplinkdataFirefly Boards deep-link URL assembling the final hero, both menu tiles, and the logo as one dark-mode visual-direction reference for the Wix designer.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 15 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All six required deliverables are present: hero_dark_desktop.png, menu_tile_dessert.png, menu_tile_cocktail.png, hero_mobile_9x16.png, logo_wordmark.svg, and a moodboard_deeplink URL.autoExactly these 6 named outputs exist (4 PNG images, 1 SVG vector, 1 Firefly Boards deep-link URL); any missing item fails.K1mandatoryoutput
The desktop hero (hero_dark_desktop.png) is a 16:9 full-bleed image at approximately 2880x1620.autoWidth:height ratio == 16:9 and dimensions are ~2880x1620 (within rounding); file is PNG.K1mandatoryoutput
Both menu tiles (menu_tile_dessert.png, menu_tile_cocktail.png) are square 1:1 images at approximately 1200x1200.autoEach tile has width == height (1:1) at ~1200x1200 px; both are PNG.K1mandatoryoutput
The mobile crop (hero_mobile_9x16.png) is a full-bleed 9:16 image at 1080x1920.autoDimensions == 1080x1920 (9:16 portrait); file is PNG.K1mandatoryoutput
All four exported raster deliverables are sRGB PNG files.autohero_dark_desktop.png, menu_tile_dessert.png, menu_tile_cocktail.png, and hero_mobile_9x16.png are all PNG format in sRGB color space.K1dealbreakeroutput
The logo deliverable is a valid vector SVG with a transparent background, accompanied by a transparent-PNG intermediate.autologo_wordmark.svg parses as SVG containing vector path data (not an embedded raster), has no opaque background fill, and a transparent-PNG intermediate of the wordmark also exists.K1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo preserves the original wordmark's letterforms, the thin serif typeface, the inter-word leaf glyph, and the single near-black charcoal ink color without regenerating, restyling, or recoloring the mark.expertlogo_wordmark.svg visually matches restaurant_wordmark_logo.png: same two words/letterforms, elegant thin serif, small leaf glyph between the two words, solid near-black charcoal color; no redrawn, restyled, or recolored logo.K2dealbreakerinput
The logo background was removed by dropping the flat white field to transparency (not by masking or erasing the wordmark itself), with no halo or fringe.expertThe transparent-PNG intermediate shows the charcoal wordmark and leaf glyph fully intact on full transparency, with the white background cleanly removed and no white halo, fringe, or matte ring around the letters or glyph.K2qualitybrief
The desktop hero horizon is level — the ~4-degree raw tilt has been corrected.expertThe plate/table horizon in hero_dark_desktop.png reads level with no residual tilt; the original ~4deg slant is removed.K3mandatorybrief
The desktop hero canvas is extended on the LEFT with clean dark negative space for an overlaid headline.expertThe left side of hero_dark_desktop.png shows added, clean, dark empty space (outpainted), leaving room for a headline; the scallop plate subject sits to the right of that negative space.K3mandatorybrief
The hero is rendered in a moody dark-mode look with the scallop plate subject popped off a dark surround.expertBackground/surround in hero_dark_desktop.png is dark and moody; the scallop plate subject is visibly brighter/more saturated and stands out from the dark background (selective subject pop), not muddy or flat.K4mandatorybrief
The mobile 9:16 crop keeps the scallop plate subject framed (subject-aware) rather than clipping it, and is taken from the graded hero.expertThe 1080x1920 hero_mobile_9x16.png is derived from the graded desktop hero with the scallop plate subject substantially in-frame and not awkwardly clipped.K3mandatorybrief
Both menu tiles are grade-matched to the hero's dark, moody look so the food set reads as one cohesive system.expertmenu_tile_dessert.png and menu_tile_cocktail.png share the hero's dark moody base, contrast, and warm-cool color balance; the originally cooler/brighter dessert and neutral-cool cocktail no longer look mismatched against the hero.K4mandatorybrief
Across all three food/drink images, the food tones remain appetizing and are not crushed into muddy darkness.expertScallops, chocolate dessert, and cocktail retain natural, appetizing color and visible detail in the shadows; the dark-mode grade has not flattened them into muddy/blocked tones.K4qualitybrief
The Firefly Boards moodboard deep-link assembles the final hero, both menu tiles, and the logo as the dark-mode visual-direction reference.expertThe moodboard_deeplink opens a Firefly board containing the finished hero_dark_desktop, menu_tile_dessert, menu_tile_cocktail, and the logo (4 final assets) presented together as one cohesive dark-mode reference.K6mandatoryoutput

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-18   Premium Dark-Mode Restaurant Food-Photography Asset Suite (Hero, Menu Tiles, Logo Vector)

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 26)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

From the client's raw food photos and logo, produce a dark-mode-ready food-photography asset suite — a color-graded wide hero banner, clean-backdrop menu tiles, a mobile crop, and a vector logo — for a premium restaurant website.

Full brief

We are a boutique digital agency building a premium, dark-mode restaurant website (Home, Interactive Food Menu, Contact/Booking) that focuses heavily on high-quality food photography and must be perfectly optimized for mobile. We are NOT asking you to build the site here — we have a Framer developer for that. What we need from YOU is the polished food-photography asset suite the design hangs on, all prepared from our raw shoot so it drops straight into the dark aesthetic. Deliverables, all derived from the assets we supply: 1) HERO BANNER — take our raw hero shot (a signature octopus dish, slightly crooked off the handheld shoot) and turn it into a wide, cinematic, dark-mode hero: straighten it, apply a cohesive warm-but-moody grade so it reads premium on a near-black background, then extend the canvas to a full ultra-wide banner ratio so our developer has bleed room for the headline. Mobile-optimized vertical crop of the same hero, too. 2) MENU TILES — for two menu items (the octopus and a steak plate), isolate the plated dish onto a clean, uniform near-black backdrop (so all menu tiles match), color-match them to the hero grade, and deliver square tiles. 3) LOGO MARK — our logo is supplied as a flat raster; we need a crisp vector version (SVG) for retina/responsive use across the dark site. House look: warm highlights, deep controlled shadows, rich but not cartoonish saturation, consistent across every tile so the menu grid feels like one shoot. Keep everything cohesive — the whole point of the dark-mode design is that the photography carries it.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain food-bevDeliverable food-photo-suiteWorkflow EditOperation O1Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 26Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[L]Brand Casa Polvo
4Planning3Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 26 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_banner_ultrawide.pngimage21:9 ultra-wide dark-mode hero banner, straightened + warm-moody graded + canvas-extended (outpainted) bleed, ~3440x1440 PNG, ready for headline overlay by the dev
hero_mobile_vertical.jpgimageMobile hero crop of the same graded hero, 4:5 portrait ~1080x1350 JPEG, subject-safe framing
menu_tile_octopus.jpgimageSquare menu tile, plated octopus isolated on uniform near-black (#0B0B0D) backdrop, grade-matched, ~1200x1200 JPEG
menu_tile_steak.jpgimageSquare menu tile, plated steak isolated on the SAME near-black (#0B0B0D) backdrop, color-matched to hero grade, ~1200x1200 JPEG
logo_mark.svgvectorClean 2-color vector logo mark (monoline octopus + wordmark), scalable SVG for retina/responsive use
restaurant_photo_suite.zipdataSingle zip bundling all five deliverables in a /hero, /menu, /logo folder structure for handoff

The hero carries the warm-but-moody dark-mode house grade (warm highlights, deep controlled shadows, rich-but-not-cartoonish saturation)expertHero shows warm highlights, deep controlled shadows, and rich-but-not-cartoonish saturation, reading premium on a near-black backgroundK4mandatorybrief
The two menu tiles and the hero are color-matched so the menu grid reads as one cohesive shootexpertOctopus and steak tiles share the same warm house grade as the hero (the steak's original tungsten cast corrected), with consistent warmth, shadow depth, and saturation across both tiles and the heroK4mandatorybrief
logo_mark.svg reproduces the supplied 2-color monoline CASA POLVO logo faithfully (octopus-in-ring mark + serif wordmark)expertThe vector logo preserves the monoline octopus-in-circular-ring mark and the 'CASA POLVO' serif wordmark from restaurant_logo_flat.png without redrawing, altering, or omitting elementsK2dealbreakerinput

AO-19   Print-ready promotional window decals for a pizza restaurant: enhance food photography, vectorize the logo, and data-merge multiple promo versions from an authored decal layout

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Take the client's raw pizza hero photo, logo, authored decal layout, and promo price list, and produce print-ready large-format window-decal PDFs — one per promotion — with professionally enhanced food photography and a vectorized logo.

Full brief

Our pizza restaurant needs several promotional window decals prepared for large-format storefront printing. We already have an approved sticker concept (the layout, spacing, and visual hierarchy are signed off) — we are NOT looking for a redesign, new logo, or rebranding. We need a designer to: (1) professionally enhance the raw food photography from our recent shoot so the pizza looks appetizing and prints cleanly at large size — fix the overall tone and exposure, warm it up so the cheese and crust look golden, boost the color of the toppings, and make the pizza itself pop against the background; (2) extend the photo so it fills the full decal panel including the print bleed, because the original crop is too tight; (3) take our actual logo file (currently a flat raster PNG) and convert it to a crisp scalable vector so it reproduces sharply at storefront size and replaces the placeholder; (4) using our approved decal layout (supplied as an InDesign template with merge fields for deal name, price, details and the hero image) and our price list, generate a print-ready PDF for EACH promotion in the same design style — Large 1-Topping Pizza Special, Pizza & Wings Deal, Family Feast, and one additional combo. Deliverables: the enhanced, bleed-extended hero photo (PNG); the vectorized logo (SVG); and one large-format print-ready PDF per promotion, all consistent in style.

5Planning4Brand strictness4Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
pizza_hero_enhanced.pngimagePrint-grade enhanced and bleed-extended pizza hero, PNG (lossless), color-corrected (auto-tone + exposure + warm temperature + boosted vibrance + masked subject-pop on the pizza) and outpainted to fill the full 24x36 decal panel plus 0.125in bleed.
restaurant_logo.svgvectorClean vectorized SVG of the restaurant logo, infinitely scalable for large-format storefront reproduction, solid-fill paths, replaces the modest-resolution raster placeholder.
decal_Large1Topping.pdfpdf24x36in, 300 DPI, 0.125in bleed, print-ready PDF for the Large 1-Topping Pizza Special, rendered from the authored decal layout with the enhanced hero + vectorized logo + merged copy.
decal_PizzaWings.pdfpdf24x36in, 300 DPI, 0.125in bleed, print-ready PDF for the Pizza & Wings Deal, same style.
decal_FamilyFeast.pdfpdf24x36in, 300 DPI, 0.125in bleed, print-ready PDF for the Family Feast, same style.
decal_GameDayCombo.pdfpdf24x36in, 300 DPI, 0.125in bleed, print-ready PDF for the Game Day Combo, same style.
pizza_decal_package.ziparchiveSingle ZIP bundling the enhanced hero PNG, logo SVG, and all four print-ready decal PDFs for handoff to the print vendor.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
pizza_decal_package.zip bundles exactly the six deliverable files (the enhanced hero PNG, logo SVG, and four decal PDFs)autopizza_decal_package.zip exists and contains exactly these 6 files and no others: pizza_hero_enhanced.png, restaurant_logo.svg, decal_Large1Topping.pdf, decal_PizzaWings.pdf, decal_FamilyFeast.pdf, decal_GameDayCombo.pdfK1mandatoryoutput
Exactly four print-ready decal PDFs exist, one per CSV promotion, with no missing or duplicate promoautoFour distinct decal PDFs are delivered, one for each of the four promotions in promotions.csv (Large 1-Topping Pizza Special, Pizza & Wings Deal, Family Feast, Game Day Combo); count == 4, each named decal_Large1Topping.pdf, decal_PizzaWings.pdf, decal_FamilyFeast.pdf, decal_GameDayCombo.pdfK1mandatoryoutput
Each decal PDF page is 24x36 inches portrait at 300 DPIautoAll four decal PDFs report a trim/page size of 24in wide x 36in tall (portrait orientation) at 300 DPI when read from the file metadataK1mandatoryoutput
Each decal PDF includes a 0.125in print bleedautoAll four decal PDFs carry a 0.125in bleed beyond the 24x36in trim (bleed box extends 0.125in on every side, i.e. ~24.25in x 36.25in)K1mandatoryoutput
Each promotion's exact deal name appears as the headline in its corresponding decal PDFautoOCR of each PDF shows the exact DEAL_NAME string from promotions.csv: 'Large 1-Topping Pizza Special', 'Pizza & Wings Deal', 'Family Feast', and 'Game Day Combo' respectivelyK1mandatoryinput
Each promotion's exact price from promotions.csv appears in its decal PDFautoOCR shows the exact PRICE per deal: $9.99 (Large 1-Topping Pizza Special), $19.99 (Pizza & Wings Deal), $29.99 (Family Feast), $24.99 (Game Day Combo)K1dealbreakerinput
Each promotion's exact details sub-line from promotions.csv appears in its decal PDFautoOCR shows the exact DETAILS string per deal: 'One large pizza, any single topping. Carryout only.' (Large 1-Topping), 'Large 2-topping pizza + 10 wings + 2L soda.' (Pizza & Wings), 'Two large pizzas, breadsticks, and a 2L soda.' (Family Feast), 'Large pizza, 20 wings, and 4 drinks.' (Game Day Combo)K1mandatoryinput
The enhanced hero deliverable is a lossless PNG sized to the 24x36 decal panel aspect (2:3) including bleedautopizza_hero_enhanced.png is a valid lossless PNG with a 2:3 width:height aspect ratio matching the 24x36 panel plus 0.125in bleedK1mandatoryoutput
The restaurant logo deliverable is a true vector SVG, not an embedded rasterautorestaurant_logo.svg is a valid SVG built from vector path/shape elements with solid fills; it does NOT merely embed the original PNG bitmap as an <image> data URIK2mandatoryoutput
The same single enhanced hero image is merged into all four decal PDFs (every CSV HERO_IMAGE row points at @enhanced_hero)autoThe hero image placed in all four decal PDFs is the same enhanced hero asset (pizza_hero_enhanced.png), consistent with every promotions.csv HERO_IMAGE cell being @enhanced_hero; it is not four different imagesK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo faithfully reproduces the supplied raster logo, preserving the chef-hat pizza-slice mascot, the 'TONY'S BRICK OVEN PIZZA' wordmark, and the red/cream paletteexpertrestaurant_logo.svg visually matches restaurant_logo.png: same chef-hat pizza-slice mascot, the wordmark reads 'TONY'S BRICK OVEN PIZZA', and the two-color red-and-cream palette is preserved; the logo is not redesigned or recoloredK2dealbreakerinput
The vectorized client logo (not the placeholder) appears in every decal PDF footer, crisply at large-format scaleexpertEach of the four decal PDFs displays the actual Tony's Brick Oven Pizza logo in the footer (placeholder replaced), reproduced crisply at storefront/large-format scaleK2mandatorybrief
The enhanced hero shows the full tonal grade the brief requested: corrected tone/exposure, warmer temperature, boosted topping color, and the pizza popping against the backgroundexpertCompared to pizza_hero_raw.jpg, pizza_hero_enhanced.png is brighter/better-exposed, visibly warmer (golden cheese and crust), has more saturated toppings, and the pizza subject stands out from the table/background; it no longer looks flat/cool/underexposedK4mandatorybrief
The hero photo is expanded to fill the full decal panel including bleed without a visible seam or warped edgeexpertThe enhanced hero fills the full 24x36 panel plus 0.125in bleed; the outpainted table/background extension blends seamlessly with the original tight crop, with no visible seam, repetition, or warped pizza edgeK3qualitybrief
The four decals read as one coherent series, consistent in layout, typography, color treatment, logo placement, and visual hierarchyexpertAll four decal PDFs share the same authored layout, typography, color treatment, logo placement, and visual hierarchy, differing only in the merged deal name, price, and details textK3qualitybrief
The work refines the existing approved concept rather than redesigning it — no new logo, rebrand, or altered layout structureexpertThe decals use the supplied authored InDesign layout and the client's actual logo unchanged in design; no new logo mark, no rebrand, and no restructured layout was introduced (scope = refine existing approved concept only)K7mandatorybrief

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-20   Premium corporate-website photo retouch: grade, declutter, and export a consistent responsive image set

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 20)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take two client-shot raw corporate images (a plant-floor engineer-by-equipment hero and an executive portrait), professionally retouch and color-grade them to one consistent premium look, declutter the busy backgrounds the allowed way, then export a web-optimized responsive set (desktop/tablet/mobile) plus a Firefly board for sign-off.

Full brief

We are launching a new corporate website for our industrial / CNC-machining business and need a Photoshop expert to professionally retouch a set of images so they look premium, modern, and consistent. Attached are two client-shot RAW-ish JPEGs straight off the camera: (1) HERO — a process engineer standing beside a CNC machining centre on the plant floor, shot under mixed fluorescent + window light so it is slightly underexposed, cool/green-cast, and the background is cluttered with parts bins, cables and a fire extinguisher; (2) a paired executive half-body portrait shot in the same facility, warmer but flat and low-contrast. We need you to: correct the lighting imbalance and exposure on the hero; pull the white balance neutral and add real contrast, color and sharpness so it reads premium; clean up the distracting background clutter behind the engineer (we don't want a fake studio look — keep it authentic, just calm it down) and gently throw the background out of focus so the subject pops; then carry the EXACT same finished look onto the executive portrait so the two images are visually consistent as a set. Once the look is locked, prepare web-optimized, responsive exports of the hero at desktop (1920px wide), tablet (1024px) and mobile (640px) sizes, all centred on the subject and fast-loading. Finally, collect the graded hero, the matching portrait and the three responsive crops into a single review board so our marketing lead can approve the style in one click before we roll it across the rest of the site library. Keep everything natural and realistic — this is a real factory, not a render.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain automotiveDeliverable responsive-photo-setWorkflow EditOperation O4Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 20Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand generic
4Planning2Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density3Creative freedom

See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 20 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_graded_master.pngimageFull-resolution retouched + color-graded hero master: exposure/WB corrected, contrast+color+sharpness enhanced, background decluttered (solid neutral fill on the cluttered backdrop region) and thrown out of focus with lens blur, subject sharp and authentic. ~6000x4000px PNG, the locked premium look.
portrait_graded_master.pngimageExecutive portrait carrying the IDENTICAL finished look (same preset) as the hero so the pair reads as one consistent set. ~6000x4000px PNG.
hero_desktop_1920.pngimageWeb-optimized desktop hero crop centred on the subject, 1920px wide (16:9), from the graded master.
hero_tablet_1024.pngimageResponsive tablet crop, 1024px wide, subject-centred, from the graded master.
hero_mobile_640.pngimageResponsive mobile crop, 640px wide, subject-centred, fast-loading, from the graded master.
corporate_retouch_review_boardurlFirefly Board deep-link assembling the graded hero, the matching portrait and the three responsive crops for one-click marketing sign-off.
corporate_website_image_set.ziparchiveSingle ZIP bundling the two graded masters and the three responsive crops for handoff to the web team.

The cluttered hero background (parts bins, cables, fire extinguisher) is calmed via a solid neutral fill on the inverted background mask, NOT AI object removal, with the subject untouchedexpertDistracting background clutter behind the engineer is suppressed by a neutral solid fill over the background region while the engineer remains intact and unaltered; no generative/AI object-removal artifacts presentK3mandatorybrief
The hero background is thrown out of focus (lens blur) so the subject popsexperthero_graded_master shows the calmed background softened/out of focus relative to the subjectK3qualitybrief
The executive portrait carries the identical finished look (same shared preset) as the hero so the pair reads as one consistent setexpertportrait_graded_master and hero_graded_master share the same tone/color/contrast grade so the originally warm-flat portrait and cool-underexposed hero now read as one visually consistent setK4mandatorybrief
The retouch stays natural and realistic — authentic factory subject, no fake studio/CGI lookexpertBoth graded images retain authentic skin texture, natural hands and a believable real-factory environment with no glossy/CGI/over-retouched appearanceK4mandatorybrief

AO-21   Creative Business Headshot — Studio Retouch, Color Grade & Brand-Profile Asset Pipeline

Brand/vertical General / Cross-Industry Branding & GraphicsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 20)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take the client's raw studio headshot frame and brand monogram and produce a retouched, color-graded, creatively recomposed professional-profile headshot set (color + monochrome + 16:9 dynamic + LinkedIn 1:1 + banner crops) plus a clean vector logomark, all delivered in one folder.

Full brief

I'm a founder who needs a creative, dynamic headshot for my professional business profile (LinkedIn + website "About"). My photographer shot me in-studio against a seamless mid-gray backdrop but only gave me the flat, un-retouched RAW-export JPEG plus my simple wordmark monogram as a PNG — I need the actual finished, polished delivery set. Please, working only from the files I provide: 1. Straighten and clean up the frame, then apply a professional studio tone so the exposure and contrast look intentional, not flat. 2. Warm the skin tone slightly (studio strobes left it a touch cool) and give the whole image a cohesive graded look — I want it to read "premium business portrait," not phone snapshot. 3. Do tasteful, believable retouching: brighten my eyes and teeth a little using selective adjustments (no plastic skin, no face reshaping). 4. Make it feel "dynamic": separate me from the background with a soft blur behind the subject, and extend the frame wider into a 16:9 environmental crop so it works as a hero image with breathing room for text. 5. Give me a tasteful monochrome alternate of the final color portrait for places that want B&W. 6. Produce the profile crops I'll actually upload: a 1:1 LinkedIn/avatar crop and a 1584x396 LinkedIn banner crop, both off the finished color portrait. 7. Vectorize my flat monogram PNG into a clean SVG logomark so it stays crisp on print and large web. 8. Put everything — color hero, mono alt, 1:1, banner, vector logo — into one delivery folder and give me a preview. Deliverable: a print/web-ready professional headshot package for my business profile.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain professional-servicesDeliverable headshotWorkflow EditOperation O4Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 20Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand generic
5Planning2Brand strictness5Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 20 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
headshot_hero_color_16x9.jpgimageFinal retouched + color-graded creative headshot, outpainted to a 16:9 environmental hero crop (e.g. 2560x1440), warm studio skin tone, brightened eyes/teeth, soft background separation blur. JPEG, sRGB.
headshot_hero_mono.jpgimageMonochrome (monochromatic-tint) alternate of the final color hero, same 16:9 framing. JPEG, sRGB.
headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpgimage1:1 avatar/profile crop (e.g. 1000x1000) derived from the finished color portrait, subject-centered. JPEG, sRGB.
headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpgimage1584x396 LinkedIn banner crop derived from the finished color portrait/hero, subject offset with text room. JPEG, sRGB.
monogram_logomark.svgvectorClean vectorized SVG of the 'AV' ring monogram, single-color paths, scalable for print + large web.
Headshot_Delivery/ (folder + preview)dataOne CC asset folder containing all of the above, plus an inline preview of the delivery set for client review.

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The color hero deliverable headshot_hero_color_16x9.jpg is present and exactly 16:9 aspect ratioautoA file named headshot_hero_color_16x9.jpg exists and its pixel width:height ratio equals 16:9 (1.7778 within rounding, e.g. 2560x1440)K1mandatoryoutput
The monochrome alternate headshot_hero_mono.jpg is present at the same 16:9 framing as the color heroautoA file named headshot_hero_mono.jpg exists and is 16:9 aspect ratio (1.7778 within rounding) matching the color hero dimensionsK1mandatoryoutput
The LinkedIn avatar crop headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg is present at exactly 1:1 aspect ratioautoA file named headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg exists with equal pixel width and height (1:1, e.g. 1000x1000)K1mandatoryoutput
The LinkedIn banner crop headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg is present at exactly 1584x396 pixelsautoA file named headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg exists with pixel dimensions exactly 1584x396 (the verbatim LinkedIn banner size in the brief)K1dealbreakeroutput
All four raster image deliverables are JPEG format in the sRGB color spaceautoheadshot_hero_color_16x9.jpg, headshot_hero_mono.jpg, headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg, and headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg are each encoded as JPEG and in the sRGB color space (no CMYK or wide-gamut/non-sRGB ICC profile)K1dealbreakeroutput
The vector logomark monogram_logomark.svg is present and is a valid scalable SVG containing vector paths (not an embedded raster)autoA file named monogram_logomark.svg exists, parses as valid SVG, and contains <path> vector geometry with no embedded bitmap (no <image> element or base64 raster data)K1dealbreakeroutput
The vectorized logomark renders the 'AV' two-letter monogram inside a thin circular ring as a single ink colorexpertRendered SVG visibly shows the serif letters 'AV' within a thin circular ring, traced as clean single-color paths faithful to the supplied monogram_flat.png (solid charcoal #2B2B2B, no gradients/texture)K2dealbreakerinput
All five deliverables are placed inside a single CC asset folder named Headshot_Delivery/autoA folder named Headshot_Delivery/ exists and contains exactly the five deliverables: headshot_hero_color_16x9.jpg, headshot_hero_mono.jpg, headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg, headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg, and monogram_logomark.svgK1mandatoryoutput
An inline preview of the populated delivery set is produced for client reviewautoAn inline preview of the populated Headshot_Delivery/ folder is rendered and returned to the userK6mandatoryoutput
The hero frame has been straightened from the raw ~1.5-degree tilt and shows a level horizon/verticalsexpertThe subject and seamless backdrop in the color hero appear level (no residual ~1.5-degree tilt) compared to the supplied tilted studio_headshot_raw.jpgK3qualitybrief
The color hero is tonally graded with intentional exposure/contrast and warmer skin tone than the flat, cool raw inputexpertCompared to studio_headshot_raw.jpg, the color hero shows added contrast/exposure grade and a warmer (less cool/blue) skin tone, reading as a premium business portrait rather than a flat phone snapshotK4mandatorybrief
Eyes and teeth are selectively brightened while skin texture is preserved and the face is not reshapedexpertEyes and teeth read brighter than the raw input, with natural skin pores/texture retained (no plastic-smooth skin) and no face/feature reshapingK7dealbreakerbrief
The background is softly blurred to separate the subject while the subject stays sharpexpertIn the color hero the backdrop shows a soft separation blur with clean subject edges while the subject (face/shoulders) remains in focusK3mandatorybrief
The 16:9 hero was extended wider via outpaint, adding seamless backdrop room for textexpertThe color hero shows a widened canvas with extended seamless backdrop (consistent outpainted continuation of the mid-gray studio backdrop) giving negative space for text, beyond the original portrait framingK3mandatorybrief
The monochrome alternate is a true B&W/monochromatic-tint version of the same finished color heroexpertheadshot_hero_mono.jpg shows the identical composition to the color hero rendered in monochrome/monochromatic tint with no residual color castK4mandatoryoutput
The 1:1 avatar and 1584x396 banner crops are derived from the finished color portrait, with the subject centered in the 1:1 and offset for text room in the bannerexpertheadshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg shows the subject centered and headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg shows the subject offset leaving clear text space, both clearly cropped from the same finished color heroK3qualityoutput

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-22   Production-finish a modern vibrant abstract logo: clean, color-tune, vectorize, and ship full-colour + single-colour + print variants with a style note

Brand/vertical Party, Events &amp; PromotionFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take my approved raster abstract logo concept and produce the full hand-off package — cleaned and brightened full-colour vector master (AI/SVG), a single-colour version, a single-colour print/screen variant, a wide social-header lockup, high-res PNG/JPEG exports, and a style note with hex/RGB values and recommended typography.

Full brief

I'm refreshing my brand identity and we've already landed on the strongest concept direction together: a bright, energetic, clean abstract mark — modern, no literal illustrations, no vintage flourishes. I'm handing you the approved raster rendering of that mark (PNG with some edge fringing and a slightly off-square crop from the concept render) plus a small swatch/reference image of the exact brand colours I want to lock to. Please turn this into the final production package. Concretely: (1) straighten and tightly crop the mark so it sits square and centered; (2) normalize the tone, then push the colours so they read as bright and energetic — lift vibrance/saturation and tune the individual hues until they hit the brand harmony, not muddy; (3) knock out the background to clean transparency so the mark drops onto any header, packaging, or profile photo; (4) from that clean mark, produce the FULL-COLOUR VECTOR MASTER (AI/SVG source) so it stays razor-sharp at any size; (5) produce the SINGLE-COLOUR version (one flat brand colour on transparent) for stamps, embossing and one-ink contexts; (6) produce a single-colour PRINT/SCREEN variant (halftone/duotone) for low-ink packaging and merch screen-printing; (7) build a wide social-header / website-header lockup by extending the canvas around the mark so it composes well as a banner and as a square profile photo; (8) export everything as high-resolution PNG and JPEG; and (9) give me a simple style note: the exact hex/RGB values pulled from the locked palette plus a recommended typography pairing that suits this modern, energetic personality. Bundle the finished full-colour, single-colour, halftone, vector and lockup deliverables together so I can review them in one place.

brand_palette_swatch.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA simple horizontal brand colour swatch strip on white, five flat solid rectangles edge to edge with no gaps: electric m

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
logo_fullcolour_master.svgvectorFull-colour vector master of the cleaned, brightened, background-removed abstract mark. Produced by image_vectorize from the finished raster. Razor-sharp at any size; this is the AI/SVG source file the brief contracts for.
logo_fullcolour_master.pngimageHigh-resolution transparent PNG of the full-colour mark, exported from the cleaned raster (post background-removal). >=2000 px on the long edge, alpha transparency, brand-accurate brightened colours.
logo_singlecolour.pngimageSingle-colour (one flat brand ink, magenta #E6007E) version of the mark on transparency, produced via subject selection -> invert -> solid fill. For one-ink stamping, embossing, foil and dark/light backgrounds.
logo_singlecolour_halftone_print.pngimageSingle-colour PRINT/SCREEN variant: halftone/duotone treatment of the mark for low-ink packaging and merch screen-printing. Crisp dot pattern, one ink.
logo_social_header_lockup.pngimageWide website/social-header lockup: the mark with canvas extended (outpainted) into a banner field that also crops cleanly to a square profile photo. High-res PNG/JPEG.
brand_style_note.txt/boarddataSimple style note: exact hex/RGB values for the locked palette (#E6007E, #FF5A36, #00C2D1, #16161D, #F4F2EC) plus a recommended typography pairing (from font_recommend) suited to a modern energetic brand. Delivered alongside a Firefly board that gathers all deliverables for one-place review.

5 · How the output is scored

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom

Single-colour version is one flat brand ink on transparencyautologo_singlecolour.png exists as a PNG with a transparent background where the mark is filled with a single flat colour matching brand magenta #E6007E (one hue only, no gradient, no second colour)K1mandatoryoutput
Single-colour print/screen variant uses a halftone one-ink screenexpertlogo_singlecolour_halftone_print.png exists and shows a halftone dot-screen pattern rendered in a single ink (one colour), suitable for low-ink packaging / merch screen-printingK1mandatoryoutput
Wide social/website-header lockup delivered as a banner that also crops squareexpertlogo_social_header_lockup.png exists as a wide landscape banner with the canvas extended (outpainted) around the mark, whose central region also composes cleanly as a square profile photoK1mandatoryoutput
Deliverables exported as high-resolution PNG and JPEG per the briefautoThe hand-off includes high-resolution exports in BOTH PNG and JPEG formats, as the brief requires ('export everything as high-resolution PNG and JPEG')K1mandatorybrief

Brand colours match the supplied palette swatch, not invented or muddy huesexpertThe tuned mark's colours read as the locked brand palette (electric magenta #E6007E, coral-orange #FF5A36, vivid cyan #00C2D1) matching brand_palette_swatch.png — clean and brand-accurate, not shifted or muddyK2dealbreakerinput
Supplied abstract mark is finished, not regenerated or replacedexpertThe delivered mark is the same approved abstract form from approved_abstract_mark.png (three overlapping rounded chevron/petal shapes) — production-finished, not a new or redrawn markK2dealbreakerinput
Mark sits square, level and tightly croppedexpertThe finished mark is straightened (no residual ~1.5deg tilt) and tightly cropped to a centered square bound with the uneven margin removedK3mandatorybrief
Background knocked out to clean transparency with no grey fringe/haloexpertThe full-colour master has a fully transparent background with the original grey background and edge fringe/halo removed cleanly at the shape edgesK3mandatorybrief
Colours read bright and energetic after the vibrance/saturation pushexpertCompared to the flat/dull input, the finished mark's colours are noticeably brighter and more energetic (vibrance/saturation lifted) while staying clean, not muddyK4qualitybrief
Mark is abstract/geometric with no literal illustration or vintage flourishesexpertThe delivered mark remains a modern clean abstract geometric form — no literal objects/illustrations and no vintage flourishes, per the brand directionK4mandatorybrief
Vector master stays razor-sharp at large scaleexpertlogo_fullcolour_master.svg renders crisp clean edges with no rasterized blur or jaggedness when scaled up to large sizesK3qualityoutput

AO-23   Teaser, Thumbnail and End-Card Content Package for a Luxury Manhattan Real-Estate YouTube Channel

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Produce the full per-video content package for one luxury Manhattan real-estate YouTube video — a retouched high-contrast thumbnail base plate, a podcast-style teaser cut (YouTube + LinkedIn sizes) with cleaned voice-over, and a branded end card — all from the broker's supplied portrait, penthouse still, raw walkthrough footage, end-card backplate, and wordmark logo.

Full brief

A top-producing Manhattan luxury real-estate broker runs a YouTube channel (market insights, property walkthroughs, deal-level expertise) at 3-4 videos/month and needs the full content package built around each video, with luxury-brand restraint and a 48-72h turnaround. For THIS video — a 12-minute Tribeca penthouse walkthrough that opens with a market-insight monologue — deliver three things. THUMBNAIL: a high-contrast, click-worthy, on-brand thumbnail base plate at 1280x720, NOT a generic headshot with a text box. Start from the broker's supplied studio portrait and the penthouse hero still. Straighten and auto-tone the portrait, isolate the broker from the background so the figure reads cleanly, and grade the penthouse hero into a moody high-contrast luxury look (deepened shadows, controlled highlights, warm prestige temperature, punchy local contrast). Crop/resize the hero to the 16:9 thumbnail frame and, where the source still is too tight, extend the canvas with AI outpainting to fill the full 1280x720 frame without stretching. Also vectorize the broker's wordmark logo to a clean SVG for crisp placement at any size. The on-thumbnail HEADLINE TEXT is the single step the image connector cannot do (it cannot set type), so it is finished in one local packaging step over the delivered base plate. TEASER: from the raw 1080p walkthrough footage (which carries the broker's lavalier voice-over), first summarize the footage to locate the single most compelling 60-90 seconds for a podcast-cold-open hook; produce an AI highlight cut to that target duration that hooks in the first 5 seconds and hard-cuts before the resolution; reframe that cut to a 1080x1080 square for LinkedIn cross-posting (same length, no trim); and clean/denoise the broker's voice-over so the spoken track is broadcast-clean. Deliver the YouTube-native teaser and the LinkedIn square, with prestige tone and no cheap transitions. END CARD: from the client's pre-shot branded end-card backplate footage (10-15s), reframe it to the exact 1920x1080 YouTube delivery dimensions so the subscribe prompt, suggested-video slot and CTA sit correctly and stay consistent across videos. All image work must be done on the connector (straighten, tone, mask, grade, crop, outpaint, vectorize); all video/audio work via the async media tools (summarize, quick-cut, resize, speech-clean). Only the final headline typesetting on the thumbnail base plate is local.
