3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
thumbnail_base_1280x720.pngimage1280x720 (16:9) high-contrast luxury thumbnail BASE plate: straightened + auto-toned broker figure isolated from background, moody graded penthouse hero, canvas outpainted to fill the full frame, no stretching. PNG, sRGB. Headline text added in the single local packaging step.
broker_portrait_isolated.pngimageTransparent-background PNG of the retouched broker (auto-straightened, auto-toned, subject-isolated) for flexible placement on the thumbnail and future videos.
logo_wordmark.svgvectorClean vectorized SVG of the 'STERLING ROW' wordmark, scalable for thumbnail and end-card placement.
teaser_youtube_1920x1080.mp4video30-90s podcast-cold-open teaser, hook in first 5s, hard-cut before resolution, 1920x1080 YouTube-native, cleaned voice-over, prestige tone, no transitions.
teaser_linkedin_1080x1080.mp4videoSame teaser reframed to 1080x1080 square for LinkedIn cross-posting, same length (no trim), cleaned voice-over.
endcard_1920x1080.mp4video10-15s branded end card reframed to exactly 1920x1080 with clear space for subscribe prompt, suggested-video card and CTA, consistent across videos.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 17 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Thumbnail base plate is exactly 1280x720 px (16:9)autothumbnail_base_1280x720.png is exactly 1280 px wide by 720 px tall (16:9 aspect ratio)K1mandatoryoutput
Thumbnail base plate is delivered as a PNG in sRGBautothumbnail_base_1280x720.png is a valid PNG file in the sRGB color spaceK1mandatoryoutput
The graded penthouse hero is outpainted to fill the full 1280x720 frame with no stretchingexpertOn thumbnail_base_1280x720.png the graded penthouse hero is cropped to the 16:9 frame and the canvas is AI-outpainted to fill the entire 1280x720 frame with no edge stretching, squashing, or empty/letterboxed regionsK3mandatorybrief
The penthouse hero shows a moody high-contrast luxury grade with restraintexpertRelative to the supplied penthouse_hero.jpg, thumbnail_base_1280x720.png shows deepened shadows, controlled/recovered window highlights, warmer prestige color temperature, and punchy local contrast while keeping luxury restraint (not over-saturated)K4qualitybrief
Isolated broker PNG has a fully transparent backgroundautobroker_portrait_isolated.png is a PNG with an alpha channel and a fully transparent background around the broker figure (no studio backdrop pixels remaining)K1mandatoryoutput
The broker figure is cleanly isolated with believable edges, straightened and auto-tonedexpertIn broker_portrait_isolated.png the broker is leveled/straightened, exposure/contrast/white-balance balanced, and the subject mask cuts the figure (including hair edges) cleanly with no backdrop fringing or chopped limbsK3mandatorybrief
Logo wordmark is delivered as a vector SVG (not embedded raster)autologo_wordmark.svg is a valid SVG file containing vector path data and no embedded raster bitmap imageK1mandatoryoutput
Vectorized logo faithfully reproduces the 'STERLING ROW' wordmarkexpertlogo_wordmark.svg renders the wordmark 'STERLING ROW' as a faithful vectorization of the supplied broker_wordmark_logo.png (correct serif letterforms, thin underline rule, monochrome charcoal) with crisp scalable edges and no regenerated or altered letteringK2dealbreakerinput
YouTube teaser is exactly 1920x1080 pxautoteaser_youtube_1920x1080.mp4 has a video frame size of exactly 1920x1080 pxK1mandatoryoutput
YouTube teaser duration is between 30 and 90 secondsautoteaser_youtube_1920x1080.mp4 runtime is >= 30s and <= 90sK1mandatorybrief
Teaser hooks in the first 5 seconds and hard-cuts before resolutionexpertteaser_youtube_1920x1080.mp4 opens with a compelling hook within the first 5 seconds and ends with a hard cut before the property/market resolution is revealed (podcast-cold-open structure)K4mandatorybrief
Teaser reads as prestige with no cheap transitionsexpertThe teaser cut reads as luxury/prestige and contains no cheap transitions (no flashy wipes, zooms, or stock effects); cuts are cleanK4qualitybrief
Teaser voice-over is denoised to broadcast-cleanexpertThe broker's lavalier voice-over on the teaser deliverables is denoised/enhanced so the spoken track is broadcast-clean (reduced room tone/noise, intelligible voice) versus the raw walkthrough audioK4mandatorybrief
LinkedIn teaser is exactly 1080x1080 px (square)autoteaser_linkedin_1080x1080.mp4 has a video frame size of exactly 1080x1080 pxK1mandatoryoutput
LinkedIn square teaser is the same length as the YouTube teaser with no trimautoteaser_linkedin_1080x1080.mp4 runtime equals teaser_youtube_1920x1080.mp4 runtime (reframe only, no trim)K1mandatorybrief
End card is exactly 1920x1080 px and 10-15 seconds long with no trimautoendcard_1920x1080.mp4 has a frame size of exactly 1920x1080 px and a runtime >= 10s and <= 15s, equal in length to the supplied endcard_backplate_1080p.mp4 (reframe only, no trim)K1mandatoryoutput
End card preserves clear space for subscribe prompt, suggested-video card, and CTAexpertAfter reframing to 1920x1080, endcard_1920x1080.mp4 retains the lower-third/negative-space zones so the subscribe prompt, suggested-video slot, and CTA sit correctly and stay consistent across videosK3qualitybrief

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-24   Open House Finance Flyer: property-photo retouch, brass/gold brand look, logo vectorize, and InDesign price-scenario data-merge for a Solano County mortgage lender

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 24)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Prep and grade the hero property photo and lender headshot into a black + brass/gold luxury look, vectorize the lender wordmark, then export the authored InDesign Open House Finance flyer (front/back) and data-merge the monthly-payment / down-payment / appreciation scenarios from a CSV into print-ready pages.

Full brief

I'm a mortgage lender working Solano County and the greater Bay Area, positioning myself as the local financing expert. I need the Adobe production pieces that go into my "Open House Finance Flyer" marketing package (front and back, print + digital). Style must read sophisticated, luxury-but-approachable, clean and modern, in black/white with brass/gold accents that appeal to Bay Area homebuyers. Assets I'm giving you: a hi-res exterior listing photo of the open-house property (shot slightly tilted, mixed afternoon light), my professional headshot, my brand wordmark as a flat PNG, my CLIENT-AUTHORED InDesign flyer template (front/back, with image frames + a tagged payment-scenario table carrying real <<merge fields>>), and a CSV of monthly-payment / down-payment / appreciation scenarios for several price points. What I need you to do: 1. Straighten and clean up the property exterior, fix the tilt and the mixed-light exposure, and crop it to the hero frame. 2. Give the property shot a consistent luxury grade — warm brass/gold-leaning, rich blacks — and make a banner-ratio version by extending the sky/foreground so it fills the wide hero band on the flyer without cropping the house. 3. Isolate the house from its background so I have a clean cut-out version for the digital/social variant. 4. Retouch and color-match my headshot to the same luxury look so it sits next to the property shot. 5. Vectorize my wordmark to a clean SVG so it stays crisp at any print size and on the QR-code/contact strip. 6. Export the finished front/back flyer from my authored InDesign template, and run the InDesign data-merge so each price scenario in my CSV produces its own print-ready page (purchase price, down-payment options, monthly payment, appreciation projection). Deliver the graded hero photo (print + banner), the cut-out, the matched headshot, the SVG wordmark, the exported flyer PDF, and the merged multi-page scenario PDF, plus a Firefly board collecting the final approved assets for my sign-off.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain real-estateDeliverable open-house-flyerWorkflow EditOperation O4Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 24Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[T]Brand Harlan Home Lending

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 24 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

outputkindspec
hero_property_print.jpgimageStraightened, exposure-corrected, luxury-graded (warm brass/gold cast, rich blacks) hero exterior at the flyer's print hero frame ratio, 300 DPI, sRGB JPEG
hero_property_banner.jpgimageWide banner-ratio version of the graded hero, canvas extended via generative outpaint on sky/foreground so the house is not cropped, sized to the flyer's full-width hero band
property_cutout.pngimageHouse isolated from background, transparent PNG, for the digital/social flyer variant
headshot_matched.jpgimageRetouched lender headshot color-matched to the same warm luxury grade, sRGB JPEG, sized for the flyer headshot frame
wordmark.svgvectorClean vectorized single-color wordmark, scalable SVG for print + contact strip
open_house_finance_flyer.pdfpdfFront/back Open House Finance Flyer exported from the authored .indd, US-Letter, print-ready PDF
payment_scenarios_merged.pdfpdfMulti-page print-ready PDF from the InDesign data-merge — one page per CSV scenario row (purchase price, down-payment options, monthly payment, appreciation projection, address)
approval_boarddataFirefly board deep-link collecting the final approved assets (graded hero, banner, cut-out, matched headshot, SVG wordmark) for client sign-off

wordmark.svg is a clean vectorized single-color SVG of the lender wordmarkautoA file named wordmark.svg exists and is a valid SVG containing vector path geometry (not just an embedded raster image)K1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized wordmark preserves the exact brand text 'HARLAN HOME LENDING' and the house-key monogram markexpertwordmark.svg renders the wordmark text 'HARLAN HOME LENDING' (correct spelling, high-contrast serif) with the small house-key monogram to the left, crisp at scale, traced from lender_wordmark.png rather than regenerated or alteredK2dealbreakerinput
open_house_finance_flyer.pdf is a front/back US-Letter print-ready PDF exported from the authored .indd templateautoA file named open_house_finance_flyer.pdf exists, is a valid PDF with exactly 2 pages (front/back), and each page is US-Letter sized (8.5 x 11 in)K1mandatoryoutput

AO-25   Premium, consistent HDR grade for a Caribbean vacation-rental villa listing set (interior + pool + sea/sky), delivered as web-ready JPEGs plus a client review board

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take a property's three raw Nodalview-HDR exports (luxury interior, pool deck, sea/sky terrace) and finish them into a consistent, natural, premium listing set — level horizons, balance exposure/white-balance, gently enhance pool water, sea color, sky and vegetation, brighten interiors and balance the bright window view, then deliver matched 2048px web JPEGs plus a 16:9 hero and a client review board.

Full brief

We manage ~100 luxury vacation rentals in Saint-Martin and need a consistent, premium visual identity across the whole portfolio. Photos come out of a Nodalview HDR workflow already roughly exposed; your job is to enhance and FINALIZE them while keeping a natural, realistic look — never a fake-HDR, over-saturated, or unrealistically-blue-pool result. For this batch, finish one representative villa's three hero frames: (1) a luxury living-room interior shot through to a bright sea-view window, (2) a pool deck with turquoise water, and (3) a wide terrace looking out to sea and sky. Required, in order: straighten any tilted horizon; apply a clean auto-tone baseline; do fine exposure and white-balance correction so the three frames read as one coherent set (no green/magenta casts, neutral whites indoors); lift interior shadows for brightness and clarity WITHOUT crushing the bright window view (balance the indoor/outdoor exposure); then do RESTRAINED, targeted enhancement of the exterior color story — pool water and sea color enhanced via selective hue/saturation of the cyans/blues only (not a global saturation pump), sky improved gently, vegetation greens enriched naturally. Keep overall vibrance gentle. Export all three at a matched 2048px long edge, sRGB JPEG q90, plus one 16:9 hero crop of the best exterior for the listing header. Finally assemble the finished frames into a single client review board so the property manager can approve the look before we roll it across the portfolio. Out of connector scope and to be flagged for the human finisher: converging-vertical / perspective warp and any cable/reflection/stain cloning — the automated pass does everything else.

4Planning2Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
interior_seaview_final.jpgimageFinished luxury interior, straightened + auto-tone + exposure/WB-corrected + shadow-lifted with the bright window view held, neutral whites, no green cast. sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, quality 90.
pool_deck_final.jpgimageFinished pool deck with naturally enhanced turquoise water (selective cyan/blue saturation, not global), enriched-but-natural vegetation greens, gently improved sky. sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, quality 90.
terrace_seaview_final.jpgimageFinished sea/sky terrace, leveled horizon, natural contrast, gently enhanced sea and sky color. sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, quality 90.
hero_16x9.jpgimage16:9 listing-header hero cropped from the finished terrace frame. sRGB JPEG, 1920x1080.
review_boardurlFirefly review board deep-link assembling the three finished frames + the hero so the property manager can approve the consistent look in one place.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All three finished frame JPEGs are delivered (interior_seaview_final.jpg, pool_deck_final.jpg, terrace_seaview_final.jpg)autoExactly three finished-frame JPEG files exist, named interior_seaview_final.jpg, pool_deck_final.jpg and terrace_seaview_final.jpgK1mandatoryoutput

All delivered images are sRGB JPEG formatautointerior_seaview_final.jpg, pool_deck_final.jpg, terrace_seaview_final.jpg and hero_16x9.jpg are all JPEG files encoded in the sRGB color spaceK1dealbreakeroutput
The three matched frames are exported at JPEG quality 90autointerior_seaview_final.jpg, pool_deck_final.jpg and terrace_seaview_final.jpg are each encoded at approximately JPEG quality 90K1qualityoutput

The hero crop is derived from the finished terrace (sea/sky) frame, not the interior or poolexperthero_16x9.jpg is visibly a 16:9 crop of the finished terrace_seaview sea/sky frame (the best exterior), matching its color treatment — not a crop of the interior or pool frameK1mandatoryoutput
A client review board deep-link is delivered assembling the finished frames plus the heroautoA review_board URL (Firefly board deep-link) is returned that assembles four assets: interior_seaview_final.jpg, pool_deck_final.jpg, terrace_seaview_final.jpg and hero_16x9.jpgK1mandatoryoutput
Tilted horizons are leveled on the interior and terrace framesexpertIn interior_seaview_final.jpg the ~1.5° tilt seen through the window is corrected, and in terrace_seaview_final.jpg the ~2° sea horizon is level/horizontalK3mandatorybrief
Interior white-balance is corrected to neutral whites with no green/magenta castexpertinterior_seaview_final.jpg shows neutral indoor whites with the warm tungsten cast and the green shadow tint removed, leaving no residual color castK4mandatorybrief
Interior shadows are lifted for brightness while the bright window sea view is held (indoor/outdoor balance)expertinterior_seaview_final.jpg has brighter, clearer interior shadows AND the bright window sea view is recovered/not blown out — both indoor and outdoor detail are readableK3mandatorybrief
Pool water and sea are enhanced via selective cyan/blue saturation, not a global saturation pumpexpertIn pool_deck_final.jpg the turquoise water reads cleaner via cyan/blue-specific enhancement while non-blue tones (loungers, deck) remain neutral — no global oversaturationK4mandatorybrief
Vegetation greens are enriched naturally and the sky is gently improved in the pool frameexpertpool_deck_final.jpg shows richer-but-natural palm/foliage greens and a gently improved (de-hazed) skyK4qualitybrief
The result avoids a fake-HDR / over-saturated / unrealistically-blue-pool look (DO-NOT-WANT list)expertNone of the finished frames show halo/fake-HDR artifacts, global over-saturation, or an unrealistically blue pool — overall vibrance stays gentle and naturalK4dealbreakerbrief
The three frames read as one coherent, consistent setexpertinterior_seaview_final.jpg, pool_deck_final.jpg and terrace_seaview_final.jpg share consistent exposure, white-balance and matched cyan/blue treatment so they read as one propertyK3mandatorybrief
The terrace frame has added natural contrast and gently enhanced sea/sky color consistent with the pool treatmentexpertterrace_seaview_final.jpg shows natural contrast (no longer flat) and the muted teal sea/sky is enhanced with the same restrained cyan/blue treatment as the pool frameK4qualitybrief
Out-of-scope items (converging-vertical/perspective warp and cable/reflection/stain cloning) are flagged for the human finisher rather than silently droppedexpertThe deliverable communication explicitly states that converging-vertical/perspective correction and cable/reflection/stain removal are out of automated connector scope and left for the human finisherK7mandatorybrief

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-26   High-Volume Real Estate Listing Retouch — HDR Tone-Map, Window Pull, Lawn Boost & Consistent-Style Delivery

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Edit one real-estate listing's frames into MLS-ready, consistently styled images: tone-map the wide-DR exterior (window pull), enhance the sky, pop the lawn, straighten and crop, then lock that look as a preset and push it across the interior frames for a uniform property set.

Full brief

You're the agency's go-to editor. We shot a listing and are sending you the frames; you handle the editing and return MLS-ready images with one consistent style across the whole property. For this batch, work the three representative frames we've attached (exterior hero + two interiors) end-to-end, then hand back a uniform set. EXTERIOR HERO (wide dynamic range, blown-out windows and bright sky over a shaded facade and front lawn): 1. Auto-straighten the frame — the verticals are leaning and the horizon is off. 2. Crop to clean bounds after straightening to remove the skewed edges (this is our lens-distortion/alignment cleanup). 3. Recover the overall tone so the shaded facade reads properly — apply auto-tone as the base. 4. WINDOW PULL: select the bright windows and pull their highlights down so the glass shows interior/structure instead of blowing out; also tame the overall highlights. 5. SKY: select the sky and enhance it — recover the blown highlights and deepen the blue (we are NOT swapping the sky, just making the existing one read well and MLS-safe). 6. LAWN ENHANCEMENT: select the front lawn/grass and lift its green saturation and luminance so it looks healthy and inviting. 7. Warm the white balance very slightly and add a touch of vibrance so the whole exterior feels bright and welcoming. STYLE LOCK + CONSISTENCY: 8. Pull the current Lightroom preset library so we can pick the house style. 9. Apply our chosen consistent "bright & airy real-estate" preset to the finished exterior — this is the look every frame in the property must match. 10-11. Apply that SAME preset to interior frame 1 and interior frame 2, then bright-window pull / highlight-tame each interior so the windows aren't blown and the rooms feel open — giving us one uniform style across the property. DELIVERY: return each finished frame as a high-quality PNG and show me previews of all three so I can confirm the set is consistent before you do the rest of the 20-40 on this listing. Turnaround 24-48h, bulk per-listing.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
exterior_hero_MLS.pngimageFinished exterior hero: straightened, cropped, auto-toned, window-pulled (windows + global highlights recovered), sky enhanced (masked highlight recovery + blue boost), lawn green lifted (masked), warmed WB + vibrance, then the locked bright-and-airy preset applied. High-quality PNG, MLS-ready.
interior_living_room_MLS.pngimageLiving-room interior with the SAME locked preset applied + window/highlight tame. High-quality PNG, style-matched to the exterior hero.
interior_kitchen_MLS.pngimageKitchen interior with the SAME locked preset applied + window/highlight tame. High-quality PNG, style-matched to the set.
listing_consistency_previewspreviewasset_preview_file display of all three finished frames so the agency can confirm one consistent style across the property before the remaining 20-40 frames are run.

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Three finished image deliverables are returned, one per frameautoExactly three finished output image files exist, named/derived as exterior_hero_MLS.png, interior_living_room_MLS.png, and interior_kitchen_MLS.png (one per attached frame)K1mandatoryoutput
All three finished frames are delivered as PNG filesautoEach of the three deliverables (exterior_hero_MLS, interior_living_room_MLS, interior_kitchen_MLS) is a valid PNG (PNG file signature), not JPEG/WebP/other — the brief requires 'high-quality PNG'K1dealbreakeroutput
A consistency preview of all three finished frames is shown for sign-offautoAn asset_preview_file step (output named listing_consistency_previews) is produced that displays all three finished frames (exterior_hero_MLS, interior_living_room_MLS, interior_kitchen_MLS) together so the agency can confirm one consistent style before the rest of the 20-40K1mandatoryoutput
The exterior hero is auto-straightened then cropped to clean bounds before tonal editsautoThe exterior pipeline runs image_auto_straighten (step 2) then image_crop_to_bounds (step 3), in that order, before image_apply_auto_tone — and in exterior_hero_MLS.png the leaning verticals/tilted horizon of exterior_hero_wideDR.png are corrected and the skewed edges removedK6mandatorybrief
Base tonal recovery (auto-tone) is applied to the exterior so the shaded facade readsautoimage_apply_auto_tone (step 4) is applied to the cropped exterior frame as the tonal base before the masked window/sky/lawn editsK6mandatorybrief
Window pull: the bright front windows are masked and their highlights pulled downexpertIn exterior_hero_MLS.png the previously blown-out front windows show recovered detail/structure rather than pure white, produced via image_select_by_prompt (windows) -> image_adjust_highlights with that maskK3mandatorybrief
Sky is ENHANCED, not replacedexpertThe original sky from exterior_hero_wideDR.png is retained in exterior_hero_MLS.png with recovered highlights and a deeper blue (via image_select_by_prompt sky -> masked image_adjust_single_color_saturation); no new/foreign sky is composited or swapped inK7dealbreakerbrief
Lawn enhancement: the front lawn/grass is masked and its green liftedexpertIn exterior_hero_MLS.png the front lawn shows increased green saturation and luminance (healthier/greener) vs the source, applied within a lawn mask via image_select_by_prompt -> image_adjust_hsl, without recoloring non-lawn areasK3mandatorybrief
Exterior white balance is warmed slightly and a touch of vibrance addedexpertexterior_hero_MLS.png shows a slightly warmer white balance and modestly increased vibrance vs the auto-toned exterior (via image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation), reading bright and welcoming without over-saturationK4qualitybrief
The Lightroom preset library is pulled before any preset is appliedautoimage_list_presets (step 13) is called to retrieve the preset catalog prior to any image_apply_preset callK6mandatorybrief
The SAME locked bright-and-airy preset is applied to all three framesautoimage_apply_preset is invoked on the exterior hero (step 14), the living-room interior (step 16), and the kitchen interior (step 19) using one identical preset identifier selected from the catalog (same preset across all three apply calls)K1mandatorybrief
Both interior frames receive a window/highlight tame after the presetexpertIn interior_living_room_MLS.png and interior_kitchen_MLS.png the bright/near-blown windows are tamed (highlights recovered via image_adjust_highlights applied after the preset) so the windows are not blown and the rooms read openK3mandatorybrief
The three finished frames share one consistent bright-and-airy property styleexpertexterior_hero_MLS.png, interior_living_room_MLS.png and interior_kitchen_MLS.png look like one cohesive set (consistent tone, brightness and color cast from the shared locked preset) — a uniform bright-and-airy MLS-ready look across the propertyK4mandatorybrief
No object removal or other generative cleanup was performedexpertThe finished frames contain the same physical objects/scene as their sources; 'general cleanup' was delivered as tonal/color + straighten/crop only, with no AI object removal, no gen-fill, and no compositingK7dealbreakerbrief
Masked exterior edits stay within their target regionsexpertThe window, sky, and lawn adjustments on exterior_hero_MLS.png are confined to their respective masked regions with clean edges (no halo/spill of the highlight pull, blue boost, or green lift onto adjacent areas)K3qualitybrief
Honest capability substitutions are disclosed to the clientexpertThe delivery message flags that the sky was enhanced (not swapped/replaced), that the HDR look came from single-file tone-mapping (auto-tone + highlight/shadow recovery) rather than a true bracket MERGE, and that 'general cleanup' excluded object removal — matching the spec's stated MLS-safe substitutionsK7mandatorybrief

AO-27   Streetline-style real-estate interior edit: straighten, HDR-look tone, white balance, window pull, outlet clean-up

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Edit a Dutch real-estate interior to a bright, clean, realistic Streetline-style finish — straighten verticals, recover an HDR-look exposure, fix white balance, pull the blown-out window view back, neutralize one distracting wall outlet, and deliver a high-res JPG.

Full brief

Kimberley is launching a real-estate photography business in the Netherlands and needs a reliable editing partner for an initial batch of 25-30 listing photos shot for the Dutch/European market (Streetline-style expectations). Using ONE representative hero frame of a living room (and the client's matching over-exposed bracket that still holds the exterior window detail), run the standard per-image edit recipe end-to-end on the Adobe connector so it can be looped across the whole set with a consistent style: 1) Load the client's two supplied frames into CC storage (the well-exposed hero interior + the brighter bracket reference that preserves the window-view exterior). 2) Auto-straighten the hero to fix tilted verticals/horizontals (walls must read straight), then crop to clean bounds removing the rotation wedge. 3) Apply auto-tone, then recover exposure / lift the dark portions / tame the highlights to build the bright-but-realistic, HDR-style interior WITHOUT an oversaturated or unrealistic HDR look. 4) Neutralize the warm/cool color cast: correct color temperature for accurate white balance, then gently lift vibrance (no oversaturation) so colors stay natural. 5) Window pull: select the windows by prompt, pull their highlights and exposure down on that mask so the outdoor view (garden/street) reads through the glass instead of blowing out white. 6) Clean one distracting element where necessary: select the small white wall power outlet and solid-fill it to match the surrounding wall color (a legal solid-fill clean-up, not AI object removal). 7) Apply a final consistent, realistic look and finalize the upload as the delivered high-resolution JPG, plus an inline preview for client approval. Deliverable: one high-resolution, Streetline-ready JPG of the interior — straight verticals, neutral white balance, bright realistic interior, intact through-window exterior view, distractor cleaned — that defines the repeatable editing style for the remaining images in the batch.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
livingroom_streetline_final.jpgimageHigh-resolution JPG (3:2, full sensor resolution preserved, sRGB), Streetline-style finish: straightened verticals, cropped clean (no rotation wedge), bright-but-realistic interior with recovered highlights/shadows, neutral white balance, natural non-oversaturated color, windows pulled so the exterior garden view reads through the glass, single wall outlet neutralized to wall color, consistent realistic final look. Delivered as the client-approved master that sets the editing style for the remaining 24-29 images.
livingroom_streetline_final_previewimageInline web preview (bounded JPEG/PNG render) of the finalized image for in-chat client sign-off before batch roll-out.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Final deliverable livingroom_streetline_final.jpg is a valid JPG fileautolivingroom_streetline_final.jpg is a valid JPEG (magic bytes FF D8 FF, extension .jpg, MIME image/jpeg)K1dealbreakeroutput
Final JPG is encoded in sRGB / RGB color space (not CMYK)autolivingroom_streetline_final.jpg channel mode is RGB and its embedded/assumed color profile is sRGB, not CMYK or another profile, per the output spec 'sRGB'K1dealbreakeroutput
Final JPG preserves a 3:2 aspect ratio at full hero resolution (no downscaling)autoWidth:height of livingroom_streetline_final.jpg equals 3:2 within rounding, and its pixel dimensions equal the supplied hero_livingroom_interior.jpg full resolution (allowing only the straighten/crop reduction), with no additional downscalingK1mandatoryoutput
An inline web preview of the finalized image is produced for client sign-offautolivingroom_streetline_final_preview exists as a bounded inline JPEG/PNG render derived from livingroom_streetline_final.jpg (asset_inline_preview output present)K1mandatoryoutput
Vertical wall lines in the final image read straight (tilt corrected)expertWall verticals appear plumb/upright in livingroom_streetline_final.jpg; the few-degree input tilt from hero_livingroom_interior.jpg is removed so walls read straightK3mandatorybrief
The image is cropped to clean rectangular bounds with no rotation wedgeexpertNo transparent/black triangular wedge or skewed corner from the straighten step is visible in livingroom_streetline_final.jpg; the frame is a clean rectangleK3mandatorybrief
Interior is bright but realistic with lifted shadows and tamed highlights, no unrealistic HDR lookexpertDark portions of the room are lifted and global highlights pulled back so the interior reads bright yet natural, with no HDR halo, crunch, or oversaturated HDR artifacts, per the brief 'bright but realistic interiors' and 'No oversaturated colors or unrealistic HDR effects'K4mandatorybrief
White balance is corrected so the warm/tungsten cast is neutralizedexpertThe slightly-warm input cast is neutralized; the white plaster walls read neutral white rather than warm/yellow in livingroom_streetline_final.jpg, per the brief 'Color correction and white balance adjustment' / 'Natural-looking colors'K4mandatorybrief
Colors are natural with only a gentle vibrance lift, not oversaturatedexpertColors read natural with only a gentle vibrance lift and no saturation push; no oversaturated greens/colors, consistent with the brief 'No oversaturated colors'K4mandatorybrief
The blown-out left garden windows are pulled so the exterior view reads through the glassexpertThe previously near-white left-hand windows now show the outdoor view (green Dutch garden, wooden fence, grey sky) through the glass, matching the exterior in window_bracket_reference.jpg, with highlights and exposure pulled down on the window regionK4mandatorybrief
The window pull is selective and does not darken the surrounding interiorexpertExposure/highlight reduction is confined to the window mask; the rest of the bright interior is unaffected, with no obvious masking halo at the window edgesK3qualitybrief
The small white wall power outlet is neutralized to the wall colorexpertThe distracting small white double outlet low on the right-hand wall is filled to match the surrounding wall color and no longer reads as a distractor in livingroom_streetline_final.jpgK3mandatorybrief
The outlet clean-up is a solid-color fill, not AI object removal or generative compositingexpertThe cleaned outlet area is a flat solid wall-matched fill (image_fill_area), with no synthesized texture, generative content, or composite artifactsK7dealbreakerbrief
Only connector-legal Adobe tools are used; no HDR-merge, generative-fill, AI object removal, compositing, upscaling, or Express/Canva tools appearautoThe executed tool set is a subset of the spec tools_used (asset_initialize_file_upload, asset_finalize_file_upload, image_auto_straighten, image_crop_to_bounds, image_apply_auto_tone, image_adjust_dark_portions, image_adjust_highlights, image_adjust_color_temperature, image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation, image_select_by_prompt, image_adjust_exposure, image_fill_area, image_apply_preset, asset_inline_preview); no true multi-bracket HDR-merge, generative-fill, AI-removal, compositing, upscaling, or Express/Canva tool is presentK7dealbreakerbrief
A single consistent realistic interior preset is applied as a repeatable batch lookexpertA neutral bright-clean look (image_apply_preset) is applied that reads as a realistic Streetline-style finish suitable to loop across the remaining 24-29 images, not a heavy stylized filterK4qualitybrief
Overall finish matches the Streetline reference: bright, clean, realistic, professionalexpertFinal master reads as bright, clean, realistic, and professional per the brief's style bar 'similar to Streetline: bright, clean, realistic, and professional', with straightened verticals, neutral white balance, intact through-window exterior view, and cleaned outlet all cohering into one believable real-estate listing photoK4qualitybrief

craft itemPassMinorMajor
Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost

Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic
Texture & detail preservedrealistic micro-detailslightly softdetail smeared or lost
Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-29   Continental 2026 Cup Stars Poster — Cinematic Grade, Flag-Colour Accents & Print/Web Master Delivery

Brand/vertical General / Cross-Industry Branding & GraphicsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 23)Tools 19 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take the client's supplied composited Continental-stars hero plate (Messi/Ronaldo/Mbappé + trophy already arranged) plus player cut-out and flag-pattern layers, and finish it into a cinematic, high-contrast, flag-colour-accented A2 300-dpi print master plus social-media versions using only Adobe connector tools.

Full brief

I am commissioning the FINISHING and COLOUR-GRADE pass on a 2026 Continental Cup hero poster. My photo team has already arranged the licensed shots — Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and Kylian Mbappé positioned dramatically around the Continental Cup trophy on a night-stadium background — into a single flattened HERO PLATE (5000×7000 px, slightly soft, flat ungraded log-style colour). I am also handing you each player isolated on its own cut-out plate (for selective per-player grading) and a single seamless flag-pattern swatch (a tasteful woven blend of Argentine sky-blue/white, Portuguese red/green, and French blue/white/red) to use as a subtle graphic accent. Your job, using Adobe tools only, is: 1. Straighten and auto-tone the hero plate, then build a punchy cinematic grade — lift the key/rim contrast so the figures carve out of the dark stadium, warm the floodlight highlights, cool the shadows for depth, and push overall high-contrast "stadium floodlights at night" mood. 2. Weave the national-flag COLOURS in tastefully, not as emblems: use prompt-masking to isolate each player region and nudge the dominant accent colour (Messi → sky-blue energy, Ronaldo → red/green, Mbappé → blue/white/red) via selective saturation, HSL and a soft directional colour overlay so the flag palette reads as rim-light and energy streaks. 3. Add believable night atmosphere — a gentle lens/atmospheric haze in the deep background only, plus fine film grain so it prints organically rather than digitally clean. 4. Turn the supplied flag-pattern swatch into a clean reusable VECTOR accent (SVG) for the studio's wider kit. 5. Outpaint the plate to a true A2 aspect bleed, crop to exact A2 at ≥300 dpi, and deliver: the layered/working master export, a flattened high-res print master, plus correctly-cropped 1:1 and 9:16 social versions — all carrying the same grade. Acceptance: (a) consistent high-contrast cinematic grade across the whole frame; (b) obvious-but-tasteful flag-colour integration per player, no literal flags; (c) A2 print-ready master at ≥300 dpi + web/social versions that match the grade; (d) a clean vector flag-accent asset. Please also assemble the graded stills onto a Firefly board so I can review the look before sign-off.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 23 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
continental_poster_print_master_A2.pdfpdfA2 (420×594 mm) press-ready PDF at ≥300 dpi, exported from the authored .indd with the fully graded full-bleed poster placed; flattened, CMYK-intent print master.
continental_poster_print_master_A2.tiffimageFlattened high-resolution TIFF of the finished graded A2 poster (≥4960×7016 px ≈ A2@300dpi) — the deliverable flattened master named in the brief.
continental_poster_social_1x1.jpgimage1080×1080 1:1 Instagram-feed crop of the graded poster, subject-aware crop keeping all three figures + trophy, same grade.
continental_poster_social_9x16.jpgimage1080×1920 9:16 story/reel crop of the graded poster, same cinematic grade.
flag_accent.svgvectorClean vectorised SVG of the flag-pattern swatch for reuse across the studio kit (scalable, no raster).
continental_review_boarddataFirefly board deep-link assembling the graded hero, the per-player accent-graded stills and the social crops for client look-review before sign-off.

Print master PDF is a flattened, CMYK-intent press-ready masterautocontinental_poster_print_master_A2.pdf is single-page, flattened, and carries CMYK output intent / colour space (not RGB-only)K1dealbreakeroutput
Flattened TIFF print master is delivered at A2 300-dpi pixel dimensionsautocontinental_poster_print_master_A2.tiff exists, is a flattened TIFF, and measures ≥4960×7016 px (≈A2 at 300 dpi)K1mandatoryoutput
1:1 Instagram-feed social crop is delivered at exact dimensionsautocontinental_poster_social_1x1.jpg exists and is exactly 1080×1080 px JPEGK1mandatoryoutput

Flag-accent asset is delivered as a true scalable vector SVGautoflag_accent.svg exists, is a valid SVG containing vector <path>/shape geometry, and embeds no raster image dataK1mandatoryoutput
A Firefly review board deep-link is delivered assembling the graded master, the three per-player accent stills and the two social cropsautocontinental_review_board is a resolvable Firefly board deep-link that assembles the graded A2 master/hero, the three per-player accent stills (left/centre/right) and the two social crops (1:1 and 9:16) for client sign-offK6mandatoryoutput
The 1:1 social crop is subject-aware and keeps all three figures plus the trophyexpertIn continental_poster_social_1x1.jpg all three footballer figures and the Continental Cup trophy remain fully visible and uncropped at the edgesK3mandatoryoutput
A consistent high-contrast cinematic 'stadium floodlights at night' grade is applied and carried across every deliverableexpertThe graded master shows punchy high contrast with figures carving out of dark stadium shadows, warm floodlit highlights and cooled shadows, and the SAME grade is visibly carried into the TIFF, the PDF print master and both social crops (1:1 and 9:16)K4mandatorybrief
Per-player flag-colour accents are integrated with the correct palette for each figureexpertLeft figure reads sky-blue (Argentina-style) accent, centre figure reads red/green (Portugal-style) accent, right figure reads blue/white/red (France-style) accent, appearing as rim-light/energy streaks rather than flat fillsK4mandatorybrief
Flag colours are woven in tastefully with NO literal flags or national emblemsexpertNo recognisable national flag, crest or emblem appears anywhere in the poster deliverables or in flag_accent.svg; colour accents read only as lighting/energy/graphic textureK2dealbreakerbrief
Believable night atmosphere is added — background haze/floodlight bloom plus fine film grainexpertThe deep background shows gentle atmospheric haze / floodlight bloom and the frame carries fine organic film grain so it prints organically rather than digitally clean, ideally without softening the three figures and trophyK3qualitybrief
The vectorised flag accent faithfully reproduces the supplied flag-pattern swatch with clean pathsexpertflag_accent.svg cleanly reproduces the woven sky-blue/white, red/green, blue-white-red bands of flag_pattern_swatch.png with smooth vector paths and no tracing artefactsK3qualityinput
Player likenesses, scene composition and the trophy come only from the client-supplied plates, not regeneratedexpertThe figures, trophy and night-stadium arrangement in all deliverables match the supplied hero plate, with no faces, trophy, or scene content invented or altered by generative tools beyond the A2 bleed outpaintK7dealbreakerinput
Generative expand is used only to extend the canvas to A2 bleed, not to add subject contentexpertGeneratively expanded margins appear only as background/bleed extension around the original framing; no new figures, trophy or subject content are introduced inside or around the original plate areaK7qualitybrief

AO-30   Daily Promo Spot — Color-Graded Key Frames, Thumbnail Pack, Cleaned VO & Sizzle Reel

Brand/vertical Music, Film, Publishing & MediaFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 19 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take today's exported promo key frames + the provided voice-over, apply one consistent lively color grade across the frames, build a YouTube thumbnail from the hero shot, source a royalty-free music bed, clean the VO, generate a sizzle cut and a content summary, and drop the finished, organized package back in the daily folder — same day.

Full brief

Fresh promotional footage and a voice-over land in the shared drive each afternoon and the finished, post-ready package must go back in the same folder within 24 hours. For today's spot the client exported five representative key frames from the raw clips (hero close-up, two mid-shots, an environment/wide, and a product/detail shot), all shot in mixed afternoon lighting so they look uneven and slightly cool. They also dropped a raw voice-over WAV (room tone + a little hiss) and want a music bed sourced for them. Deliver a same-day "promo finishing package": (1) all five key frames carrying ONE consistent, lively-but-tasteful grade — auto-tone baseline, warmer temperature, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, a touch more vibrance, plus the same Lightroom look-preset on every frame so the set feels edited together and "accessible, not corporate"; (2) a 1920x1080 YouTube/social thumbnail built from the graded hero frame (subject popped from the busy background via a blurred-background treatment, then cropped to 16:9 with the subject weighted left so a title can sit right); (3) a licensed royalty-free music-bed candidate from Adobe Stock (full-res licensed file, upbeat/accessible); (4) the voice-over denoised so it sits cleanly in the mix; (5) a 15-second vertical-friendly sizzle cut from the source clips for the social teaser, plus a short auto-generated content summary to seed the post caption/description. Package the graded frames, thumbnail, licensed music, cleaned VO and sizzle into a dated daily-delivery folder in CC and confirm done. Keep the turnaround fast and the look consistent across every frame.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
graded_keyframes/ (5 files)image5x 1920x1080 JPEG/PNG, each carrying the identical grade chain (auto-tone → warmer temperature → lifted shadows → recovered highlights → +vibrance → same Lightroom look-preset). Visually consistent 'edited-together' lively look.
youtube_thumbnail_1920x1080.pngimage1920x1080 (16:9) PNG thumbnail from the graded hero frame: subject popped via blurred background, cropped with subject weighted left, title-safe negative space on the right.
music_bed_licensed.{wav/mp3}audioFull-resolution LICENSED Adobe Stock royalty-free track (upbeat/accessible), delivered via presigned download URL after licensing.
voiceover_clean.wavaudioDenoised/de-hissed version of the provided voice-over, voice-only, ready to balance into the mix.
sizzle_15s_teaser.mp4video~15s AI engagement-cut sizzle reel assembled from the source clips for the social teaser (async-widget result in product).
content_summary.txtdataShort auto-generated scene/transcript summary of the source montage to seed the post caption + description.
daily_delivery_YYYY-MM-DD/dataDated CC asset folder containing all of the above, organized for same-day handback.

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom

The thumbnail pops the subject via a blurred background while the subject stays sharpexpertthe busy shelf/equipment background is blurred and the business-owner subject remains sharp, with clean subject-pop edge qualityK3mandatoryoutput

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-31   Daily-vlog brand kit: color-graded thumbnails, end-screen + lower-third plates, story-format still, reframed clip and cleaned VO

Brand/vertical Music, Film, Publishing & MediaFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Build the recurring per-episode still brand kit for a daily-life vlog — color-grade the supplied frame-grab and shot stills to one consistent "personal, upbeat" look, produce a subject-pop YouTube thumbnail plus a 9:16 story-format still, vectorize the channel wordmark into clean lower-third and end-screen plates, gather it all on a Firefly board, and ship a same-length social reframe of one clip with a denoised voice-over.

Full brief

You are the recurring post-production designer for "Princerrr," a daily-life vlog shot in 4K and delivered at 1080p. The video edit itself (trimming, jump-cuts, .srt captions, music, NLE project file) is handled elsewhere — your contract is the per-episode STILL BRAND KIT and the two media touches below, delivered consistently so every episode looks like the same channel. Assets the client uploads for this episode: (1) a 4K frame-grab pulled from the hero clip (the creator at their desk, window light), (2) a separate DSLR "shot" still of the creator used for the thumbnail, (3) a flat PNG of the channel wordmark / logo lockup, (4) the hero clip itself (a 12s 4K horizontal segment), and (5) the raw lavalier voice-over scratch track (one-take, roomy). What to do, as one chained job: 1. Normalize and color-grade BOTH stills to a single house look. Start from the frame-grab: auto-tone it, warm the white balance to match the creator's window-lit "personal, upbeat" feel, lift the shadows and tame the highlights, then push vibrance so it reads lively without going garish, and finally stamp a chosen Lightroom preset so the grade is repeatable across future episodes. Apply the SAME finishing preset to the DSLR shot still so the two match. 2. From the graded DSLR still, build the YouTube thumbnail: isolate the creator (subject), invert to the background, knock the background's saturation down so the subject pops, then crop/resize to a clean 1280×720 16:9 thumbnail. 3. From that finished 16:9 thumbnail, generate a 9:16 vertical "story / Shorts cover" still by outpainting the canvas top and bottom (no re-shoot) and exporting at 1080×1920. 4. Turn the flat wordmark PNG into a crisp, infinitely-scalable SVG so it can sit in lower-thirds and the end screen without softening; keep that SVG as the channel mark. 5. Assemble the graded stills, thumbnail, story still and vector mark onto a Firefly board as the episode's brand-kit deliverable / handoff link. 6. Media touches: reframe the supplied 12s horizontal hero clip to a 9:16 vertical of the SAME length for Shorts (no trimming), and clean/denoise the roomy scratch voice-over so the VO is broadcast-usable. Deliverables: graded frame-grab + graded DSLR still (matched look), a 1280×720 thumbnail, a 1080×1920 story still, the channel wordmark as SVG, a Firefly board link, a 9:16 same-length reframed clip, and a denoised VO track.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 18 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
framegrab_graded.pngimageColor-graded hero frame-grab, house look (warm WB, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, lively vibrance, finishing preset stamped), full-res PNG.
dslr_shot_graded.pngimageDSLR thumbnail still with the SAME finishing preset applied so it matches the frame-grab grade, full-res PNG.
thumbnail_1280x720.pngimageYouTube thumbnail, 1280x720 16:9, creator subject popped against a desaturated background.
story_still_1080x1920.pngimageVertical story / Shorts cover still, 1080x1920 9:16, canvas outpainted top+bottom from the thumbnail (no re-shoot).
channel_mark.svgvectorChannel wordmark vectorized to a clean scalable SVG for lower-thirds and end screen.
brand_kit_boardurlFirefly board deep-link gathering the two graded stills, thumbnail, story still and vector mark as the episode brand-kit handoff.
hero_clip_vertical_9x16.mp4videoHero clip reframed to 1080x1920 9:16, SAME length as source (no trim, no format change). [async]
vo_cleaned.wavaudioDenoised / de-reverbed voice-over scratch track, broadcast-usable. [async]

The graded frame-grab applies the house color-grade versus the flat ungraded source (warmer WB, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, lively vibrance)expertCompared to hero_framegrab_4k.png, framegrab_graded.png reads visibly warmer (the cool/green video cast removed), shadows lifted to open desk detail, window highlights tamed not blown, and vibrance pushed for a lively-but-not-garish 'personal, upbeat' lookK4mandatorybrief
dslr_shot_graded.png is delivered as a full-resolution PNG corresponding to creator_dslr_shot.pngautoA file named dslr_shot_graded.png exists, decodes as a valid PNG, and retains the full source resolution of creator_dslr_shot.png (6000x4000) rather than a downscaled or cropped imageK1mandatoryoutput
The graded DSLR still wears the SAME finishing preset as the frame-grab so the two stills read as one consistent channel gradeexpertdslr_shot_graded.png's color tone/look visibly matches framegrab_graded.png (same finishing preset stamped) so the two read as the same house grade, satisfying the brief's 'every episode looks like the same channel' consistency requirementK4mandatorybrief
thumbnail_1280x720.png is exactly 1280x720 pixels (16:9)autoImage dimensions read exactly 1280 wide by 720 high (16:9 aspect ratio)K1mandatoryoutput
In the thumbnail the creator subject is popped against a desaturated backgroundexpertThe creator (subject) retains full color/saturation while the busy background's saturation is visibly knocked down to make the subject pop; the subject itself is NOT desaturatedK3mandatorybrief
story_still_1080x1920.png is exactly 1080x1920 pixels (9:16)autoImage dimensions read exactly 1080 wide by 1920 high (9:16 vertical aspect ratio)K1mandatoryoutput
The 9:16 story still was produced by outpainting the thumbnail canvas top and bottom, not by re-shooting or stretchingexpertThe central content of thumbnail_1280x720.png is preserved undistorted, with new plausible image content generatively added above and below to fill the vertical canvas (no horizontal stretch, no new/different subject, no re-shoot)K3mandatorybrief
channel_mark.svg is delivered as a valid scalable SVG vector (not an embedded raster)autoA file named channel_mark.svg exists and parses as valid SVG containing vector path/shape elements, not merely a raster bitmap embedded in an SVG wrapperK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized channel mark faithfully reproduces the supplied wordmark lockup (PRINCERRR text, 'daily life, unfiltered' tagline, play glyph, brand colors) with clean letterformsexpertchannel_mark.svg renders the 'PRINCERRR' wordmark with the 'daily life, unfiltered' tagline, the play-button glyph, and the deep-teal and warm-coral brand colors, with crisp clean edges and legible correctly-spelled text matching channel_wordmark.png; the logo is vectorized, not regenerated or alteredK2dealbreakerinput
A Firefly board deep-link is delivered as the brand-kit handoffautobrand_kit_board is a resolvable Firefly board deep-link URL that opens a boardK1mandatoryoutput
The Firefly board gathers all five required brand-kit assetsexpertThe board displays exactly the five still/vector deliverables: framegrab_graded.png, dslr_shot_graded.png, the 1280x720 thumbnail, the 1080x1920 story still, and channel_mark.svg (all five present)K6mandatoryoutput
hero_clip_vertical_9x16.mp4 is a 1080x1920 MP4 of the SAME length as the ~12s source hero clip (no trim, no format change)autoOutput is an MP4 with frame dimensions 1080x1920 and duration equal to the ~12s source hero_clip_12s_4k.mp4 (no shortening, still an MP4 video)K1mandatoryoutput
The vertical hero clip is a meaningful reframe of the horizontal source keeping the speaking creator in frameexpertThe 9:16 output crops/repositions the 16:9 hero content so the creator stays framed across the clip (subject not cut off), not a letterboxed or stretched fillK3qualitybrief
vo_cleaned.wav is delivered as a WAV corresponding to the scratch voice-over with the same spoken introautoA WAV file vo_cleaned.wav exists corresponding to vo_scratch_track.wav, carrying the same spoken intro contentK1mandatoryoutput
The cleaned voice-over has the room reverb, HVAC hum and broadband hiss removed so the voice is broadcast-usableexpertCompared to vo_scratch_track.wav, vo_cleaned.wav has audibly reduced room reverb, hum and broadband hiss, the spoken line stays intelligible and broadcast-usable, and no new artifacts mangle the voiceK4mandatorybrief
No banned/out-of-scope capability was used and only the single allowed generative outpaint was performedexpertOutputs show no video timeline trimming/jump-cuts, no .srt captions or NLE project file, no background-replace-by-prompt, no object removal, no upscaling, and no layout composition; the only generative step is the top+bottom outpaint of the story still, and the channel mark is a faithful vectorization (not a regenerated or altered logo)K7dealbreakerbrief

AO-32   Modern House Listing Photography — Post-Production Color Grade & Listing Export

Brand/vertical Photo Editing, Retouching & RestorationFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 25)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Edit the client's three supplied room frames (living room, kitchen, bedroom) into clean, modern, naturally-lit real-estate listing photos with straight verticals and corrected color, then export each in web-optimized JPEG (<2 MB) and full-resolution TIFF for Airbnb/Zillow.

Full brief

A landlord listing a freshly renovated rental on Airbnb and Zillow has captured interior frames of the three key areas — living room, kitchen, and bedroom — during a 0700-1200 natural-light window, but the RAW-style frames are tilted, slightly under-exposed in the shadows, mixed-white-balance (warm tungsten vs cool window daylight), and carry minor wall scuffs and dust marks. They want the post-production handled to a "modern, clean, simple, ample lighting, natural" standard: straight verticals and distortion-free perspective, basic color correction, gentle shadow lift and highlight recovery so the rooms read bright and spacious, quick blemish spotting on wall scuffs/dust WITHOUT an over-processed look, and final files delivered in two formats per the brief — web-optimized JPEG under 2 MB each AND full-resolution TIFF — within three days. The freelancer must run an Adobe photo-editing chain per room: license/load the supplied frame, auto-straighten the verticals, crop out the resulting wedge to a clean distortion-free frame, apply auto-tone as a neutral baseline, neutralize the mixed white balance with a color-temperature correction, lift the dark portions and recover the blown window highlights, selectively brighten the room interior via a prompt-based mask so the space feels open without flattening the windows, spot-correct wall scuffs/dust by selecting the blemish and filling it with the matched neutral wall color (solid fill only — not generative), apply a final gentle vibrance/saturation lift for the "modern clean" feel, then crop-and-resize to the two delivery specs. Each room's edit is then assembled onto a Firefly Board so the client can review all three corrected listing shots in one deep-link before download. Across the three rooms the chain reuses the straighten→crop→tone→temperature→shadow/highlight→selective-brighten→spot-fix→vibrance→resize pattern, every step consuming the previous step's output.

4Planning1Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom
