3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 25 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
living_room_web.jpgimageWeb-optimized JPEG, longest edge ~2048px, sRGB, quality tuned to keep file under 2 MB; straight verticals, color-corrected, shadow-lifted, scuff/dust spotted, gentle modern grade.
living_room_full.tifimageFull-resolution TIFF (native capture resolution), same corrected/graded pixels as the web version, for print/archive delivery.
kitchen_web.jpgimageWeb-optimized JPEG under 2 MB, ~2048px long edge, sRGB; straightened, white-balanced, highlight-recovered, scuff-spotted kitchen frame.
kitchen_full.tifimageFull-resolution TIFF of the corrected kitchen frame for full-quality listing/print use.
bedroom_web.jpgimageWeb-optimized JPEG under 2 MB, ~2048px long edge, sRGB; straightened, exposure-balanced (window highlights recovered, interior brightened), spotted bedroom frame.
bedroom_full.tifimageFull-resolution TIFF of the corrected bedroom frame.
listing_review_boarddataFirefly Board deep-link assembling the three corrected listing shots (living room, kitchen, bedroom) for one-glance client review before download.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All three room frames are delivered in BOTH web-optimized JPEG and full-resolution TIFF (6 image files total: living_room_web.jpg, living_room_full.tif, kitchen_web.jpg, kitchen_full.tif, bedroom_web.jpg, bedroom_full.tif).autoExactly 6 image deliverables present, one .jpg + one .tif per room: living_room_web.jpg, living_room_full.tif, kitchen_web.jpg, kitchen_full.tif, bedroom_web.jpg, bedroom_full.tif; any missing file fails.K1mandatoryoutput
Each of the three web JPEG files is under 2 MB.autoFile size of living_room_web.jpg, kitchen_web.jpg, and bedroom_web.jpg is each < 2,097,152 bytes (2 MB); any file >= 2 MB fails.K1dealbreakerbrief
Each web deliverable is a valid JPEG container with longest edge approximately 2048px.autoliving_room_web.jpg, kitchen_web.jpg, bedroom_web.jpg each decode as JPEG and have a longest-edge dimension of ~2048px (tolerance 1900-2200px); wrong format or out-of-range dimension fails.K1mandatoryoutput
Each full-resolution deliverable is a valid TIFF at native capture resolution, larger than its ~2048px web JPEG counterpart.autoliving_room_full.tif, kitchen_full.tif, bedroom_full.tif each decode as a valid TIFF container whose longest edge exceeds 2048px (native/full resolution, not the resized web size); any that is not a TIFF or is <=2048px long edge fails.K1mandatoryoutput
Each web JPEG is encoded in the sRGB color space.autoEmbedded color profile / metadata of living_room_web.jpg, kitchen_web.jpg, and bedroom_web.jpg reports sRGB; a non-sRGB color space fails.K1qualityoutput
For each room, the full-res TIFF and the web JPEG are the same edited/graded master differing only in resolution (the web file is a resized version, not a separate edit).expertFor each room the .tif and .jpg show identical corrections (same straighten, crop, color/white-balance, exposure, spot-fix, grade), differing only in pixel dimensions; a visible mismatch in edit state between the two formats fails.K1mandatoryoutput
A Firefly Board deep-link (listing_review_board) is delivered that assembles all three corrected room masters (living room, kitchen, bedroom) for one-glance client review.autolisting_review_board is present as a Firefly Board URL/deep-link and references/contains the three corrected room shots (living room, kitchen, bedroom); a missing board or fewer than three shots fails.K6mandatoryoutput
Every room frame has straightened, level verticals correcting the input tilt (~2.5deg living room, ~2deg kitchen, ~3deg bedroom).expertVertical wall, cabinet, and window-frame lines read level/straight in all three outputs versus the visibly tilted inputs; noticeable residual tilt in any room fails.K3mandatorybrief
Each straightened frame is cropped to a clean rectangular frame with no empty wedge/triangle corners left by the rotation.expertNo transparent/blank/border wedge or triangle is visible at the corners of any of the three outputs; each frame is a full distortion-free rectangle.K3mandatorybrief
Mixed warm-tungsten vs cool-daylight white balance is neutralized in the living room and kitchen frames so whites/neutrals read natural.expertNo strong residual yellow/orange or blue cast remains in living_room or kitchen outputs; pale walls, stainless steel, and quartz read neutral-white.K4mandatorybrief
Underexposed shadows are lifted in the living room and bedroom interiors so they read bright and spacious without crushed detail.expertCorner/interior shadows in living_room and bedroom outputs show recovered detail and a brighter, open feel versus the dark inputs; fully crushed/blocked blacks fail.K4mandatorybrief
Blown-out window/highlight areas are recovered in the kitchen and bedroom frames so highlight detail is held, not clipped.expertThe bright window in the bedroom output and the can-light/window highlights in the kitchen output show recovered tonal detail rather than pure clipped white.K4mandatorybrief
The bedroom interior is selectively brightened via a prompt-based mask while leaving the recovered window untouched (window is not re-blown out).expertBedroom output shows the room interior opened/brightened while the window retains its recovered highlight detail; a re-blown window or globally flattened brightening fails.K3qualitybrief
Wall/cabinet scuff and dust blemishes are removed in each room via a solid matched-neutral fill (not generative healing) with no visible patch or halo.expertThe wall scuff/dust by the sofa (living room), cabinet-door scuff (kitchen), and headboard scuff (bedroom) are gone, covered with matched wall/cabinet color and showing no visible smudge, halo, or AI-generated artifact.K3mandatorybrief
The final grade is gentle and natural - clean and modern but NOT over-processed/over-saturated.expertOutputs show only a subtle vibrance/saturation lift and natural color; no HDR look, over-sharpening, blown saturation, or heavy stylization. An over-processed result fails.K4mandatorybrief
No generative AI object-removal, generative fill, compositing, background replacement, or upscaling was used; edits stay within color/tone/geometry/solid-fill corrections.expertOutputs contain no invented/added objects, no synthesized backgrounds, no generatively healed regions; spot-fixes are flat solid-color fills consistent with the brief's 'solid fill only, NOT generative' constraint. Any generative artifact fails.K7dealbreakerbrief

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-34   Informative explainer video: brand-graded sizzle cut, cleaned VO, and on-brand lower-third / call-out graphic plates

Brand/vertical Video Editing & Motion GraphicsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 25)Tools 21 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn my raw informative-explainer footage, scratch voiceover, brand logo, and color-reference still into a polished short cut: clean the VO, build a brand-locked color look plus a graded end-card, and produce on-brand lower-third, call-out, and vectorized-logo graphic plates — then deliver a 1080p sizzle and a vertical reframe, all assets organised on a board for the Premiere project.

Full brief

I have raw footage for an informative explainer video that needs to be shaped into a clear, engaging cut, plus the supporting brand assets to dress it. Source material I'm supplying: (1) a bundle of raw clips — a ~3-minute talking-head segment intercut with product/process b-roll; (2) a separate scratch voiceover WAV recorded in a noisy room that needs cleaning before it can carry the edit; (3) my company logo as a flat PNG; (4) a single "color reference" photograph that represents the warm, slightly cinematic brand look I want the whole piece graded toward; and (5) a loose narrative outline. I need the freelancer to handle the full edit and the supporting graphics, mapped to Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools, with every step honestly tagged for execution mode: A. AUDIO — clean my scratch voiceover so it can drive the cut: denoise / de-room the voice track (voice-only; this is not music mastering). B. VIDEO — (1) summarize the raw footage so we agree on the key talking points and a logical flow before cutting; (2) produce a punchy ~60-second highlight/sizzle cut from the raw clips that surfaces those key points; (3) reframe that cut to a 9:16 vertical version for social teasers, same length (no trimming). C. BRAND COLOR LOOK — from my color-reference still, build the locked "brand LUT" look the editor will match the master grade to: straighten and crop it clean, then run the full Lightroom-class grade (auto-tone baseline, exposure, highlights, dark-portion lift, warm color-temperature push, HSL + vibrance/saturation shaping, a gentle preset and fine grain) so I have one authoritative graded reference frame. D. GRAPHIC PLATES — the on-brand overlays the editor lays onto the timeline (delivered as standalone transparent PNG / SVG plates, NOT composited onto the video): (1) vectorize my logo to a clean SVG so it scales for the end-card and corner bug; (2) build a lower-third bar plate — solid brand-color bar generated by selecting a region and filling it; (3) build a call-out badge plate the same way; (4) grade the end-card hero frame to match the brand look. E. PACKAGING — gather the graded reference, the plates, the cleaned VO, and the video deliverables onto a Firefly board so the whole linked-asset set is organised for easy future tweaks, exactly as I want the Premiere project handed off. Deliverables on approval: cleaned voiceover; a ~60s 1080p H.264 sizzle cut and a 9:16 reframe (same length); one authoritative graded brand-reference frame; logo SVG; lower-third and call-out PNG plates; a graded end-card frame; and a Firefly board linking the whole organised asset set. Two rounds of feedback should be plenty.

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 25 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

callout_badge_plate.pngimageTransparent-background call-out badge plate — solid brand-color badge region, same selection-fill technique, for on-screen fact call-outs.
graded_endcard_frame.jpgimageEnd-card hero frame graded to match the locked brand look, with right-side negative space for the logo lockup.

graded_endcard_frame.jpg is graded to match the locked brand-look reference and retains right-side negative space for the logo lockupexpertEnd-card frame shows the same warm brand look as brand_look_reference_frame and preserves open negative space on the right for the logo lockupK4mandatoryoutput

AO-35   Restore a 1910 magician-troupe postcard: clarify the dark front photo and make the handwritten back legible, delivered as two book/social-ready files

Brand/vertical Photo Editing, Retouching & RestorationFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Digitize and restore one 1910 magician-troupe postcard — clarify and lighten the dark sepia front photo and sharpen the contrast of the handwritten back so the description is legible — and deliver each side as its own clean, deskewed, print-and-social-ready file.

Full brief

A family-history client is digitizing ~24 antique postcard photographs from 1910 documenting a famous stage magician and his traveling company touring Asia and abroad; the client's grandmother and aunt performed in the troupe and wrote a description of each scene on the BACK of the postcards. The client needs every postcard delivered as two separate restored files — the photographic FRONT as one picture and the handwritten BACK as another — that are clean enough to be pasted into a printed memorial book and posted to social media. Many of the front photos are dark, low-contrast, and tilted from flatbed scanning; the handwriting on the backs is faint cursive pencil/ink that is hard to read. This benchmark uses ONE representative postcard as the worked end-to-end example. The client supplies two raw flatbed scans: (1) the postcard front (a dark, slightly rotated sepia-toned albumen-style photograph of the magician and company on a theatre stage, with scanner-bed border) and (2) the postcard back (faded cursive handwriting plus a postmark and divided-back postcard printing, also slightly skewed). Required restoration pipeline, per side: ingest both raw scans into the workspace; auto-straighten each skewed scan and crop away the scanner-bed margins to the postcard edge; on the FRONT, recover the dark midtones and shadows, lift exposure and overall brightness/contrast, rein in any blown highlight on the bright stage lights, gently recover the crushed dark portions where the company stands in shadow, neutralize the scanner's cool cast and re-warm to a consistent archival sepia, and finish with a vintage-photo preset look that keeps the period feel while making faces and stage detail readable; on the BACK, push brightness and contrast hard and convert to a clean high-legibility monochrome/sepia so the faint cursive description reads clearly. Deliver a high-resolution restored FRONT and a high-resolution restored BACK, each deskewed and edge-cropped, color-consistent, copy-paste-ready for the book layout and for social posts. Document the exact per-card operation sequence so the same recipe can be re-run across the remaining 23 postcards.

postcard_back_scan_raw.tifimagegemini-3-pro-imageA raw flatbed-scanner capture of the BACK of the same 1910 photographic postcard: a divided-back postcard printing layou

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 18 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Magician_Postcard_01_FRONT_restored.tifimageRestored postcard FRONT: deskewed and edge-cropped to the card, dark midtones/shadows recovered, exposure and brightness/contrast lifted, highlights tamed, re-warmed to a consistent archival sepia with a vintage-look preset preserving period feel; long edge ~3000 px, 300 DPI, suitable for a printed book page. Plus a 1080 px JPEG social variant from the same pipeline.
Magician_Postcard_01_BACK_legible.tifimageRestored postcard BACK: deskewed and edge-cropped, brightness/contrast pushed and converted to a clean high-legibility monochrome/sepia so the faint cursive description and postmark read clearly; long edge ~3000 px, 300 DPI for the book caption, plus a 1080 px JPEG social variant.
restoration_recipe_notes.txtdataPlain-text log of the exact per-card operation sequence and parameters (straighten angle, crop bounds, tonal adjustments, color temperature shift, preset id, tint) so the identical recipe can be batch-re-run on the remaining 23 postcards.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Two separate restored deliverable files are produced — one for the postcard FRONT and one for the BACK — not a single combined imageautoOutput set contains exactly two restored master images, one identifiable as the FRONT (Magician_Postcard_01_FRONT_restored.tif) and one as the BACK (Magician_Postcard_01_BACK_legible.tif); FRONT and BACK are separate files, not one combined imageK1dealbreakeroutput
Both restored master files are delivered in TIFF format as the print-master deliverableautoBoth master deliverables are .tif/TIFF files named Magician_Postcard_01_FRONT_restored.tif and Magician_Postcard_01_BACK_legible.tifK1mandatoryoutput
Each restored master TIFF has a long edge of approximately 3000 px at 300 DPI for the printed book pageautoFRONT and BACK master TIFFs each have a long-edge dimension of ~3000 px (within ±10% tolerance) and carry 300 DPI resolution metadataK1mandatoryoutput
A 1080 px JPEG social variant is produced for each side from the same pipelineautoTwo additional JPEG outputs exist — one FRONT social variant and one BACK social variant — each with a long edge of 1080 px and JPEG formatK1mandatoryoutput
A plain-text restoration recipe log file is deliveredautoA plain-text file restoration_recipe_notes.txt exists as a deliverableK1mandatoryoutput
Both sides are deskewed (auto-straightened) so the postcard edges are square, with no residual scanner tiltexpertThe restored FRONT (originally ~4 deg rotated) and BACK (originally ~3 deg rotated) appear straightened — the postcard horizontal/vertical edges read level, not tiltedK3mandatorybrief
Both sides are edge-cropped to the postcard, removing the gray scanner-bed marginexpertNeither restored master shows the light-gray scanner-bed margin or edge shadow; the frame is cropped to the postcard edge on both FRONT and BACKK3mandatorybrief
The dark FRONT photo is visibly lightened and brightened as the client explicitly asked ('lightened and clear')expertThe restored FRONT is noticeably brighter and higher in exposure than the dark, low-contrast raw scan; overall the photo reads clearly lighterK4mandatorybrief
Crushed shadow detail is recovered on the FRONT where the company stands in stage shadowexpertDetail (figures/faces of the company in the dark areas) is visible in the recovered shadow regions of the restored FRONT, not left as solid blackK4qualitybrief
The blown bright stage-light highlight on the FRONT is reined in rather than further clipped by the brighteningexpertThe bright stage-light spot retains tonal detail and is not blown out to a larger pure-white region after brighteningK4qualitybrief
The cool/greenish scanner cast on the FRONT is neutralized and the image is re-warmed to a consistent archival sepia, preserving period feel (not converted to neutral B&W)expertThe restored FRONT shows a warm sepia tone with the cool/green cast removed, retaining the vintage period look rather than a neutral grayscaleK4mandatorybrief
The faint cursive handwriting on the BACK is rendered clearly legible via hard brightness/contrast and a clean monochrome/sepia tintexpertThe cursive description on the restored BACK separates cleanly from the paper and is readable; foxing color noise is suppressed by a monochrome/sepia conversionK4mandatorybrief
The original handwritten description text on the BACK is preserved and readable without being altered or rewrittenexpertThe restored BACK still shows the original cursive line 'Front row, my mother and Aunt Lillie with the Company at the Royal — Singapore, March 1910' and the 1910 postmark; the wording is not changed, fabricated, or substitutedK7mandatoryinput
The restoration alters only tone/contrast/color and geometry — no colorization, AI reconstruction, compositing, or upscaling was introducedexpertRestored FRONT and BACK remain the same monochrome/sepia photographs as the source content: no added color (colorization), no invented or regenerated image regions, no composited elements, and no resolution upscaling beyond the sourceK7dealbreakerbrief
The recipe log captures concrete reusable per-card parameter values, not just step names, so the recipe can be re-run on the remaining 23 postcardsexpertrestoration_recipe_notes.txt records the actual per-card operation sequence and values used (straighten angle, crop bounds, exposure/brightness/contrast amounts, color-temperature shift, preset id, monochromatic tint) sufficient to reproduce the look on the other cardsK6mandatoryoutput
FRONT and BACK restorations are color-consistent with each other for a cohesive book/social presentationexpertThe sepia/monochrome tone of the restored FRONT and BACK read as a matched, consistent archival look rather than clashing color treatmentsK3qualitybrief

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-36   Family Photo Restoration, Tonal Recovery & Canvas Extension for Large-Format Printing

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Restore one faded family-under-a-maple-tree photo: clip the two corner-intruding fingers out of frame, recover faithful colour/tone without touching faces, outpaint the canopy and scene for a balanced large-format composition, and deliver a print-ready PNG master plus high-res TIFF and a JPG preview.

Full brief

The client supplies ONE scanned family photograph (people seated/standing under a mature maple tree, blue sky, surrounding landscape) that has faded and slightly yellowed with age and has two fingers of the person who held the print intruding into two OPPOSITE corners. They want it digitally restored so it can be professionally printed in large format and proudly displayed. Hard rules from the client: faces, expressions, body shape, age and appearance must stay 100% faithful to the original (no alterations whatsoever); colours must read true to the original; the corner intrusions and any minor distractions must become invisible; and the canopy plus surrounding scene should be subtly EXTENDED to give a more balanced composition for larger-format printing. Mandatory deliverables: a high-resolution print-ready PNG, a high-resolution TIFF, and a smaller JPG preview for quick sharing. Honest Adobe-connector workflow (every step tagged with execution mode; all server="adobe" except the one final local packaging step): 1. Bring the client scan into Adobe via the file picker (asset_add_file) → presigned URL + assetId. [C] 2. Read the scan with asset_inline_preview to locate the two finger corners, the people, the canopy line and the horizon, and to judge the fade/cast. [C] 3. image_auto_straighten — correct the slight scan skew so horizon/tree are level. [C] 4. image_crop_to_bounds — geometric crop with normalized bounds that clip BOTH opposite finger-corners out of frame (no clone/heal/gen-fill; the fingers simply leave the frame). Leave generous margin so no person is cut. [C] 5. image_apply_auto_tone — recover the global tonal baseline from the de-skewed, de-fingered crop. [C] 6. image_adjust_color_temperature — neutralize the aged yellow/warm cast so colours read true to the original. [C] 7. image_adjust_exposure — lift the overall faded exposure to a faithful midtone. [C] 8. image_adjust_highlights — recover blown sky/highlight detail behind the canopy. [C] 9. image_adjust_dark_portions — open up the shaded foliage and the shadow side of the people. [C] 10. image_select_subject — select the people (the subjects) so faces can be PROTECTED. [C] 11. image_invert_selection — invert to the background-only mask (sky, tree, landscape — everything EXCEPT the people). [C] 12. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — restore faded colour, applied with the inverted background mask so the scene regains life while faces/skin are untouched. [C] 13. image_generative_expand — outpaint/extend the canopy, sky and surrounding landscape on all four sides to a balanced large-format composition (the client's explicit "subtly extend the scene" ask; the one allowed generative step). [C] 14. asset_inline_preview — verify the extended, colour-true, face-faithful result looks seamless before export. [C] 15. asset_preview_file — present the final print-ready PNG master to the client. [C] 16. Local packaging [L]: from the single connector PNG master, write the print-ready PNG deliverable and derive the high-res TIFF and the smaller JPG preview (TIFF/JPG are not connector render targets); zip the three for handoff. [L] Acceptance: both corner fingers are gone from frame, the extended canopy/sky/landscape read seamless and balanced for large format, every face/expression/body/age is identical to the original (only selective/background tonal work touched colour), colours are true to the original, and the PNG/TIFF/JPG set is delivered.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
family_restored_master.pngimagePrint-ready PNG master: de-skewed, both corner fingers cropped out of frame, auto-toned, neutralized white balance (true-to-original colour), exposure/highlights/shadows recovered, faded colour restored on a background-only mask (faces/skin untouched), and canvas outpainted on all four sides for a balanced large-format composition. Highest resolution the connector returns, RGB, no compression artifacts.
family_restored_print.tiffimageHigh-resolution TIFF derived locally from the PNG master for professional large-format printing: lossless, RGB, embedded sRGB profile, full resolution.
family_restored_preview.jpgimageSmaller JPG preview derived locally from the PNG master for quick sharing/approval: long edge ~2048px, quality ~85, sRGB.
family_restoration_deliverables.zipdataSingle zip bundling the print-ready PNG master, the high-res TIFF, and the JPG preview for client handoff.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All four mandatory deliverables are present: family_restored_master.png, family_restored_print.tiff, family_restored_preview.jpg, and family_restoration_deliverables.zip.autoAll four exact filenames exist (family_restored_master.png, family_restored_print.tiff, family_restored_preview.jpg, family_restoration_deliverables.zip); family_restoration_deliverables.zip bundles exactly those three image files (PNG master + TIFF + JPG preview).K1mandatoryoutput
family_restored_master.png is a PNG in RGB color mode with no compression artifacts.autofamily_restored_master.png: file format == PNG, color mode == RGB, lossless (no JPEG-style block/compression artifacts).K1dealbreakeroutput
family_restored_print.tiff is a lossless RGB TIFF with an embedded sRGB profile, at the same full resolution as the PNG master.autofamily_restored_print.tiff: file format == TIFF, compression == lossless, color mode == RGB, an sRGB ICC profile is embedded, and pixel dimensions equal those of family_restored_master.png.K1mandatoryoutput
family_restored_preview.jpg is an sRGB JPG with long edge ~2048px.autofamily_restored_preview.jpg: file format == JPG, color space == sRGB, and longest pixel edge is ~2048px (within ~5% tolerance). (JPEG quality target ~85 is a derive parameter, not a reliably readable property, so it is not asserted here.)K1mandatoryoutput
Both intruding fingers (top-left corner and bottom-right corner) are absent from family_restored_master.png.expertNeither the top-left finger nor the bottom-right finger intrusion is visible anywhere in family_restored_master.png; both of those corners are clean.K1dealbreakerbrief
The two fingers were removed by geometric crop (the opposite corners left the frame), not by clone/heal/generative-fill object removal.expertEach finger corner is cropped out of frame (the master's frame edges sit inboard of the original top-left and bottom-right corners); there is no painted, healed, cloned, or AI-rebuilt patch where a finger used to be.K6mandatorybrief
No person is cut off or partially clipped by the de-fingering crop.expertAll four family members (two adults and two children) remain fully within the cropped frame with generous margin; no person is sliced by the crop.K3mandatorybrief
Every face, expression, body shape, and apparent age is 100% faithful to the original scan with no alteration.expertEach of the four people's facial features, expression, body shape, and apparent age are identical to the input scan; no generative or identity-altering edit touched the people.K2dealbreakerbrief
Colour/vibrance restoration was applied only to the background via the inverted subject mask, leaving skin/faces untouched.expertSaturation/vibrance lift is visible in the background scene (sky, maple tree, landscape) while the people's skin tones match the original; the colour adjustment respected a background-only (inverted-subject) mask.K2mandatorybrief
The aged yellow/warm cast is neutralized so colours read true to the original.expertThe overall yellow/warm age cast is removed; whites/neutrals read neutral and colours are faithful to the original scene rather than orange/yellow-tinted.K4mandatorybrief
Fade is recovered through tonal correction: faded exposure lifted to faithful midtones, blown sky highlights recovered, and shaded foliage/shadow detail opened without crushing blacks.expertContrast/exposure look restored (not flat/faded), highlight detail in the sky behind/around the canopy is present, and shadow areas (shaded foliage, shadow side of the people) retain detail with no crushed blacks.K4qualitybrief
The ~2-degree scan skew is corrected so the horizon and maple tree are level.expertThe horizon line reads horizontal and the maple tree reads vertical; the original ~2-degree skew is gone.K3qualitybrief
The canopy, sky and surrounding landscape are extended (outpainted) on all four sides for a balanced large-format composition.expertfamily_restored_master.png shows added canopy/sky/landscape margin on all four sides relative to the crop, producing a more balanced composition suitable for large-format print.K1mandatorybrief
The outpainted canvas extension reads seamless with no visible seam, repetition, or texture break between original and generated pixels.expertThe boundary between the original photo and the outpainted margin is invisible; canopy leaves, sky gradient, and landscape continue naturally with no tiling, repetition, or seam.K3qualitybrief
The canvas extension is the single generative-expand (outpaint) step and the only generative operation in the workflow.expertScene extension is an outpaint of the existing margins; no full-scene regeneration, background-replace-by-prompt, clone/heal/generative-fill finger removal, or any other generative edit was applied to the original content.K7mandatorybrief
family_restored_master.png is delivered at the highest resolution the connector returns (full-resolution print-ready master, not downscaled).autoThe PNG master's pixel dimensions equal the full-resolution connector output (post-crop plus outpaint) and are not a downscaled copy.K1qualityoutput

AO-37   Restore Three Faded, Scratched Family Photo Scans for A4 Wall Prints

Brand/vertical Technology, SaaS & StartupsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 20)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Restore three damaged old photo scans — correct color fading, conceal scratches/tears over plain areas, and maximize clarity — then output each as a print-ready A4 (2480x3508 px, 300 DPI) file.

Full brief

I have several old family photo scans that I want to restore and print at A4 to mount on my wall. They suffer from three problems: color fading (the older color shots have gone yellow/orange and flat), scratches or tears (white scratch lines and a torn corner, mostly running across plain backdrops and sky), and blurriness — and the MAIN priority is improving overall clarity so they look crisp on a large print. Please work from the three supplied scans: 1. faded_color_portrait_1968.png — a colour studio portrait from the late 1960s, heavily yellowed, low contrast, slightly tilted on the scanner bed. 2. scratched_bw_studio_1955.png — a black-and-white studio photo with several fine white scratch lines crossing the plain grey studio backdrop behind the subject. 3. torn_outdoor_snapshot_1972.png — a colour outdoor snapshot with a torn top-left corner exposing white paper and a couple of scratches across the open sky. For each photo I need you to: straighten any scanner tilt and crop off the ragged scan border; correct the colour cast and faded contrast so skin tones and colours look natural again; lift overall clarity and local contrast so detail reads on a big print; conceal the scratches and the torn corner ONLY where they cross plain, uniform regions (the studio backdrop and the open sky) by colour-matching and filling those marks so they disappear; add a very light film grain so the restored areas don't look plastic against the original photographic texture; and finally produce a clean, true-A4 file at 2480x3508 px / 300 DPI ready to send to the print lab. Deliverables: three restored, A4 print-ready image files (one per scan), plus a single before/after contact-board so I can approve the restoration at a glance before printing.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
portrait_1968_restored_A4.pngimageRestored late-1960s color portrait: tilt-straightened, border-cropped, color-cast neutralized, faded contrast and clarity recovered, light grain re-added. Final A4 print size 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI, PNG.
bw_studio_1955_restored_A4.pngimageRestored 1955 B&W studio photo: backdrop scratches concealed by matched solid-grey fill, tonal range and clarity recovered, light grain re-added. Final A4 print size 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI, PNG.
snapshot_1972_restored_A4.pngimageRestored 1972 outdoor snapshot: torn corner and sky scratches concealed by matched solid sky-blue fill, color cast corrected, contrast/clarity recovered, light grain re-added. Final A4 print size 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI, PNG.
restoration_before_after_boardfirefly_boardA single Firefly Board deep-link assembling the three original scans next to their three restored A4 outputs as a before/after approval contact-board for the client to sign off before printing.

The 1955 B&W photo remains true monochrome with recovered tonal range and clarityexpertbw_studio_1955_restored_A4.png is neutral black-and-white (no colour cast introduced) with a recovered silver-gelatin tonal range and crisper local contrast versus the soft, faded originalK4mandatorybrief
The torn top-left corner and the sky scratches of the 1972 snapshot are concealed by a matched solid sky-blue fill over the plain skyexpertIn snapshot_1972_restored_A4.png the white torn-corner triangle and the two white scratch lines that crossed the plain sky are no longer visible, replaced by a sky-blue matching the surrounding sky; the two people are unaffectedK3mandatorybrief
The 1972 snapshot's aged magenta/cyan colour shift is corrected and faded contrast is recoveredexpertsnapshot_1972_restored_A4.png shows a neutralized colour balance (no residual magenta/cyan shift) and restored contrast versus the flat, faded originalK4mandatorybrief
Overall clarity / perceived sharpness is improved on all three restored outputs (the brief's stated MAIN priority)expertAll three A4 outputs read crisper with stronger local contrast and more readable detail than their originals at print scale, delivered via tonal/contrast/local-contrast shaping (not via an invented sharpen op, which the connector lacks)K4mandatorybrief
A very light film grain is present on all three restored outputs so concealment/regraded areas do not look plasticexpertEach of the three A4 outputs carries a subtle, uniform film grain that blends the solid-filled concealment patches and re-graded areas with the original photographic texture; grain is light, not heavy or distractingK3mandatorybrief
The before/after Firefly Board pairs the three original scans against the three restored A4 outputsexpertThe restoration_before_after_board deep-link opens a Firefly Board showing all three original scans (faded_color_portrait_1968.png, scratched_bw_studio_1955.png, torn_outdoor_snapshot_1972.png) alongside their three restored A4 counterparts as a before/after approval contact-board layoutK5mandatoryoutput
Damage concealment is confined to plain uniform regions only; no generative heal, clone, object-removal, or AI upscaling was usedexpertConcealment edits appear only over the uniform grey backdrop (PHOTO 2) and plain sky (PHOTO 3); no fabricated detail over textured/subject areas, and no evidence of generative fill, object-removal, or upscaling beyond the scans' native resolutionK7mandatorybrief

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-38   Logo + Four Challenge-Coin Marks: Concept Rasters to Coin-Ready Vector Artwork with Black/White/Gold/Silver Variants

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's five supplied concept rasters (one logo + four challenge-coin references) into clean, coin-ready vector marks, each delivered as transparent-PNG and SVG in black, white, gold, and silver.

Full brief

A challenge-coin / branding client (automotive-industrial sector) supplies five flat reference images: one primary logo concept and four symbolic coin-face concepts. They are pixelated mock-ups that cannot go to a mint. The task is to convert each into production-grade vector art and deliver a four-color family per mark. Pipeline per the connector chain: (1) upload all five client rasters to CC storage and preview them; (2) on the primary logo, remove the busy background so only the mark survives on transparency; (3) auto-straighten the de-bagged logo so the mark sits square (coins are minted on a true axis); (4) crop the straightened logo tight to its own bounds so the trace has no dead margin; (5) vectorize that clean, cropped, transparent logo into a scalable SVG — this is the editable master vector that also seeds EPS/PDF on packaging; (6) take the vector-master raster proof and select the mark's silhouette, then fill it with solid true-black to mint the BLACK variant; (7) on the same master, apply a flat color overlay in gold #C8A24B to mint the GOLD variant; (8) apply a monochromatic silver/gray tint to mint the SILVER variant (white #FFFFFF is the transparent-on-light case, documented from the same master). Then the four coin references are run through the same remove-background -> vectorize spine so all five marks are coin-ready vector. Finally, assemble every produced vector + variant asset into a single Firefly Board so the client can review the full logo+four-coin family in black/white/gold/silver before mint submission. Hard constraints honored: no from-scratch illustration (every mark is traced from a client reference), no generative fill / object removal / compositing, image_fill_area is used only for SOLID color minting of an isolated mask, and the only generative tool is avoided entirely (no outpaint needed). Deliverables map 1:1 to the brief: transparent PNGs, SVG vector source, and black/white/gold/silver color versions, coin-ready and readable at small sizes.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
logo_vector_master.svgvectorClean scalable SVG traced from the cropped, background-free, straightened primary logo; editable master vector (seeds EPS / vector-PDF on packaging); coin-ready, readable at <=15 mm.
logo_variant_black.pngimageTransparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silhouette filled solid true-black #000000.
logo_variant_gold.pngimageTransparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo flat gold #C8A24B color overlay.
logo_variant_silver.pngimageTransparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silver/gray #AAAAAA monochromatic tint (white #FFFFFF on-light case documented from same master).
coin_vectors_x4.svgvectorFour clean SVG coin-face vectors (eagle, gear-globe, anvil, tractor), each traced from its background-removed reference, coin-ready for mint review.
coin_review_boardurlFirefly Board deep-link assembling the logo master + four coin vectors + black/white/gold/silver variant proofs into one client-review moodboard.
ironline_coin_package.zipdataSingle ZIP bundling all SVG vector sources, transparent PNG exports, and the black/white/gold/silver variant set, organized per-mark for mint/manufacturer handoff.

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All seven deliverables are present and each is the correct kind: logo_vector_master.svg (SVG), logo_variant_black.png, logo_variant_gold.png, logo_variant_silver.png (PNGs), coin_vectors_x4.svg (SVG), coin_review_board (Firefly Board URL), and ironline_coin_package.zip.autoAll 7 named outputs exist; the SVG/PNG files open without error, the board URL resolves, and the ZIP is a valid archive. Missing or unopenable any one = fail.K1mandatoryoutput
logo_vector_master.svg is a genuine vector file (true SVG path/shape geometry), not a raster image wrapped in an SVG container.autoFile parses as SVG and contains vector geometry elements (<path>, <polygon>, <circle>, etc.); it is NOT merely a single embedded <image> bitmap. Raster-in-SVG = fail.K1dealbreakeroutput
coin_vectors_x4.svg delivers exactly four coin-face vectors (eagle, gear-globe, anvil, tractor), each genuine SVG vector geometry, none missing or duplicated.autoExactly 4 distinct coin-face vectors present (one per reference: eagle, gear-globe, anvil, tractor), each containing vector path/shape elements rather than an embedded bitmap. Fewer/more than 4, or any raster-in-SVG = fail.K1mandatoryoutput
The three minted color variants (logo_variant_black.png, logo_variant_gold.png, logo_variant_silver.png) are each a transparent PNG measuring exactly 2000x2000 px.autoEach of the 3 variant PNGs has an alpha channel (transparent background) and dimensions == 2000 x 2000 px. Any opaque background or any non-2000x2000 file = fail.K1mandatoryoutput
logo_variant_black.png fills the logo silhouette with true black #000000.autoSampled mark pixels read RGB(0,0,0) / hex #000000 within a small tolerance; not a near-black or dark gray.K1mandatoryoutput
logo_variant_gold.png applies the flat gold color #C8A24B (RGB 200,162,75) specified in the brief.autoSampled mark pixels read hex #C8A24B (RGB 200,162,75) within a small tolerance; not an arbitrary gold.K2mandatorybrief
logo_variant_silver.png applies the silver/gray monochromatic tint at #AAAAAA (RGB 170,170,170) specified in the brief.autoSampled mark pixels read hex #AAAAAA (RGB 170,170,170) within a small tolerance; tint is neutral gray, not colored.K2mandatorybrief
The full black/white/gold/silver four-color family is accounted for: black, gold, and silver are delivered as produced PNG assets, and the white #FFFFFF version is the transparent-on-light case derived from the same master and explicitly documented as such (not silently omitted).expertBlack, gold, and silver exist as produced assets AND the white #FFFFFF on-light case is documented/explained as derived from the same master per the brief. White claimed as a separately produced file when no white-fill output exists, or the white case omitted entirely = fail.K7mandatorybrief
The logo vector reproduces the client's IRONLINE FORGE concept (heraldic shield with piston-and-gear motif), traced from logo_concept_primary.png rather than illustrated or generated from scratch.expertlogo_vector_master.svg matches the supplied IRONLINE FORGE shield/piston-gear reference silhouette. Any invented, restyled, or substituted logo artwork = fail.K2dealbreakerinput
Each of the four coin vectors matches its corresponding client reference: eagle (eagle gripping wrench + lightning bolt), gear-globe (globe + gear ring + crossed hammers), anvil (anvil + crossed forging hammer + rope-border ring), tractor (side-profile tractor + wheat-wreath border + top star).expertEach coin vector reproduces the symbolic content of its source reference (coin_ref_01_eagle / 02_gear_globe / 03_anvil / 04_tractor). Any invented or substituted coin artwork = fail.K2dealbreakerinput
The primary logo's busy workshop background has been removed so only the mark survives on clean transparency before vectorization.expertThe logo master and its variants show the isolated mark on clean transparency with no residual workshop-background pixels or halo. Visible leftover background = fail.K3mandatorybrief
Each of the four coin references has had its non-transparent source background (off-white paper / mid-gray / beige / blue-gradient) removed before tracing.expertAll 4 coin vectors are isolated coin-face marks on transparency with no residual reference background. Any coin still carrying its source background = fail.K3mandatorybrief
The logo mark is auto-straightened (sits square/on-axis for coin minting) and cropped tight to its own bounds with no dead transparent margin.expertThe straightened logo is visually level (coin axis) and framed tight to the mark; obvious tilt or large empty margin = fail.K3qualitybrief
All five marks (logo + four coins) are coin-ready and remain legible at small coin proof size, including embedded micro-text such as the eagle banner text.expertWhen scaled to coin proof size (<=15 mm), silhouettes and fine details (gear teeth, rope border, wheat wreath, banner micro-text) stay clean and readable; broken or muddy detail at small size = fail.K4mandatorybrief
coin_review_board is a Firefly Board deep-link assembling the logo master + four coin vectors + the black/gold/silver variant proofs into one client-review moodboard.autoBoard URL resolves and the board contains the logo master, all 4 coin vectors, and the black/gold/silver variant proofs together in a single reviewable board.K6mandatoryoutput
ironline_coin_package.zip bundles all SVG vector sources (logo master + 4 coins), the transparent PNG variant exports, and the black/gold/silver color-variant set, organized per-mark for mint/manufacturer handoff.autoZIP opens and contains every SVG (logo_vector_master.svg + coin_vectors_x4.svg / 4 coin vectors), the transparent PNG exports, and the black/gold/silver variant PNGs, organized per-mark. Missing any class of asset = fail.K1mandatoryoutput
No banned capability was used: no from-scratch illustration, no generative fill / object removal / compositing, no text-to-image, no upscaling; image_fill_area is used only for solid-color minting of an isolated mask.expertEvery vector traces to a real client reference and color variants are pure solid-fill / color-overlay / monochromatic-tint outputs; any evidence of generative content, compositing, or invented elements = fail.K7dealbreakerbrief

K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)

K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-39   Apparel Shirt Isolation, Background Cleanup and Marketplace-Ready E-commerce Set

Brand/vertical Fashion & ApparelFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn a messy worn-shirt hero photo plus a flat-lay reference shirt into a clean, colour-true, marketplace-ready apparel product set (cleaned studio backdrop, isolated transparent packshot, vectorized logo, square + catalog crops).

Full brief

We sell a small line of cotton shirts and our marketplace listings look amateur. I'm sending you three things: (1) HERO_WORN.jpg — a model wearing our flagship olive-green button-down in a cluttered home studio (laundry, cables and a coat rack are visible behind her, and the shot is slightly tilted and a touch under-exposed); (2) SHIRT_FLATLAY.jpg — a clean-ish flat-lay of "the similar shirt" (the same garment laid flat on a light table) that we want isolated as a drop-in packshot for the listing thumbnail; (3) BRAND_LOGO.png — our small wordmark logo we want as crisp vector for the woven label and the listing banner. Please: straighten and tone-correct the hero so it looks professionally lit; cut the model cleanly off the messy background and put her on a clean solid-white studio backdrop (the clutter must be gone); make sure the olive-green of the shirt reads true and rich, not muddy; extend the cleaned hero outward so we have breathing room for a wide catalog banner; isolate the flat-lay shirt to a transparent PNG packshot; vectorize the logo to SVG; and hand back the hero as both a 1:1 Instagram square and a clean catalog frame, plus the transparent packshot and the SVG logo. Attention to detail on the edge of the collar and sleeves is essential.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain apparelDeliverable product-shotWorkflow EditOperation O2Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 21Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand NORTHGROVE
4Planning2Brand strictness5Tool diversity3Visual density2Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Technical Quality · Process · Honesty. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
HERO_WORN.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal documentary-style photo of a woman in her late 20s wearing an olive-green cotton button-down shirt, standing i
SHIRT_FLATLAY.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal top-down flat-lay of a single olive-green cotton button-down shirt laid flat and neatly arranged on a softly-l
BRAND_LOGO.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA clean minimalist apparel-brand wordmark logo reading 'NORTHGROVE' in a modern condensed sans-serif, deep charcoal lett

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_square_1x1.jpgimage1:1 Instagram-square (e.g. 2048x2048) JPEG of the straightened, auto-toned, colour-true model on a clean solid-white studio backdrop, derived from the cleaned/expanded hero.
hero_catalog_banner.jpgimageWide catalog/banner crop (e.g. 2400x1200, ~2:1) JPEG of the same cleaned hero after generative outpaint gave horizontal breathing room, white backdrop preserved.
shirt_packshot.pngimageTransparent-background PNG packshot of the isolated flat-lay olive shirt, clean collar/sleeve edges, ready to drop into a listing thumbnail.
northgrove_logo.svgvectorClean 2-color SVG vector of the NORTHGROVE wordmark + leaf glyph, suitable for the woven label and listing banner.
apparel_set.zipdataSingle ZIP bundling the four deliverables above plus a contact-sheet preview, for handoff to the client.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 15 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
hero_square_1x1.jpg is a 2048x2048 1:1 square JPEGautoFile hero_square_1x1.jpg exists, is JPEG format, with width == height == 2048 (2048x2048), aspect ratio exactly 1:1K1mandatoryoutput
hero_catalog_banner.jpg is a 2400x1200 wide ~2:1 catalog-banner JPEGautoFile hero_catalog_banner.jpg exists, is JPEG format, with dimensions 2400x1200 (width:height ratio exactly 2:1; accept within 1.9-2.1 if dims differ but width > height)K1mandatoryoutput
shirt_packshot.png is a PNG with a genuine transparent (alpha) backgroundautoFile shirt_packshot.png exists, is PNG format, contains an alpha channel, and background pixels outside the shirt are fully transparent (alpha=0)K1dealbreakeroutput
northgrove_logo.svg is delivered as a valid vector SVG fileautoFile northgrove_logo.svg exists and is a well-formed SVG containing vector path/shape elements (not a single embedded raster bitmap)K1dealbreakeroutput
apparel_set.zip bundles the four deliverables plus a contact-sheet preview (5 items total)autoapparel_set.zip exists and contains exactly hero_square_1x1.jpg, hero_catalog_banner.jpg, shirt_packshot.png, northgrove_logo.svg, plus a contact-sheet/preview image — 5 items totalK1mandatoryoutput
The SVG wordmark reads exactly 'NORTHGROVE' with correct spellingautoOCR/text extraction of northgrove_logo.svg yields the legible wordmark 'NORTHGROVE' (exact spelling, all caps, no misspelling or altered characters)K2dealbreakerinput
The vectorized logo preserves the supplied BRAND_LOGO.png 2-color palette: deep-charcoal letters and an olive-green leaf glyph to the left of the wordexpertnorthgrove_logo.svg renders as a flat 2-color mark with deep-charcoal letters and a small olive-green leaf glyph positioned to the left of the wordmark, matching BRAND_LOGO.png with no recolouring or restyling of the markK2mandatoryinput
The hero subject (model) is cleanly isolated and the cluttered original background is fully replaced by a clean solid-white studio backdropexpertIn both hero deliverables the coat rack with hanging clothes, the tangle of black cables, the laundry basket and the half-open closet from HERO_WORN.jpg are no longer visible; the model sits on an even solid-white backdropK3mandatorybrief
Collar and sleeve edges of the isolated model (hero) and isolated shirt (packshot) are clean with no haloing, fringing, or jagged cutoutexpertOn close inspection the collar and sleeve edges of the model in both hero deliverables, and the collar/sleeve edges of shirt_packshot.png, show clean masking with no leftover background halo, color fringing, or ragged edges (brief: attention to detail on collar and sleeves is essential)K3mandatorybrief
The hero is straightened (the ~4-degree tilt corrected)expertBoth hero deliverables are level/upright with the original ~4deg tilt corrected; vertical lines (coat rack, closet edges where retained, subject posture) read straight, not tiltedK3mandatorybrief
The hero is tone-corrected so it looks professionally lit, not under-exposed and flatexpertBoth hero deliverables show corrected exposure/contrast versus the deliberately dark, flat source and read as professionally lit, not under-exposedK4mandatorybrief
The shirt's olive-green reads true and rich, not muddyexpertThe olive-green of the shirt in both hero deliverables appears rich and true (mixed-light white balance neutralized, green saturation corrected), no longer the muddy in-camera olive of the sourceK4mandatorybrief
The catalog banner shows horizontal breathing room from generative outpaint with the white backdrop preservedexperthero_catalog_banner.jpg shows the cleaned hero extended horizontally with added white studio space on the sides, the solid-white backdrop continuing seamlessly into the expanded region with no visible seam or artifactK3qualityoutput
The packshot olive shirt is the same garment as the hero, keeping the set cohesiveexpertThe isolated flat-lay shirt in shirt_packshot.png is the same olive-green cotton button-down as the shirt worn in the hero, so the product set reads as one cohesive listingK3qualitybrief
The brief's out-of-scope generative asks (add-a-shirt compositing, AI clutter erasure) were honestly dropped, not fakedexpertNo shirt was AI-composited/added onto a person and no clutter was generatively erased/inpainted; background cleanup was achieved by solid-white fill behind the isolated subject, and any such limitation is disclosed honestly rather than passed off as doneK7mandatorybrief

craft itemPassMinorMajor
Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost

Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic

Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

AO-40   Cut Out a Designer Armchair to a Clean Transparent PNG and Prep a Catalog-Ready Asset Pack

Brand/vertical General / Cross-Industry Branding & GraphicsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Cut a studio-shot designer armchair out to a clean, high-res transparent PNG and deliver a small catalog-ready asset pack (color-corrected master, hero outpaint frame, platform crops, and a vector silhouette).

Full brief

I run a small furniture e-commerce brand and I need one of our studio product shots turned into a clean, professional cutout I can drop into any design. The supplied file is a high-resolution studio photo of a mid-century walnut-and-teal lounge armchair on a seamless light-grey backdrop. Deliverables: (1) the armchair cut out to a transparent-background PNG with a clean, halo-free edge — no grey fringe from the backdrop, attention to the legs and the gap under the armrests; (2) a color-corrected master (straightened, auto-toned, neutral white balance) so the walnut reads warm and the teal upholstery reads true; (3) a "hero" frame where the canvas is extended on both sides so the chair sits in a wider seamless studio sweep for banner use; (4) a 2048×2048 square crop and a 1080×1350 portrait crop of the cutout for catalog/social tiles; and (5) a clean two-tone vector silhouette (SVG) of the chair for use as a category icon and watermark. Bundle the PNG, the graded master, the hero frame, both crops and the SVG into one delivery folder. The cutout itself is the priority — it must be edge-accurate and design-ready; the rest is the prep we'd otherwise pay extra for.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain ecommerce-retailDeliverable product-cutoutWorkflow EditOperation O2Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 19Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[L]Brand generic
4Planning1Brand strictness5Tool diversity2Visual density3Creative freedom

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
armchair_cutout.pngimageTransparent-background PNG (RGBA), full source resolution (~4096px long edge), clean halo-free edge including the armrest gap and leg negative space. Primary deliverable.
armchair_master_graded.pngimageColor-corrected, auto-straightened, auto-toned, neutral-white-balanced master of the cutout; warm walnut, true teal. RGBA PNG, full res.
armchair_hero_frame.pngimageCanvas extended left+right via outpaint so the chair sits in a wider seamless studio sweep; ~3072×2048 landscape banner master, RGBA PNG.
armchair_tile_2048.pngimage2048×2048 square crop of the graded cutout, subject centered, transparent background. RGBA PNG.
armchair_tile_1080x1350.pngimage1080×1350 portrait crop of the graded cutout for social/catalog tiles, transparent background. RGBA PNG.
armchair_silhouette.svgvectorClean two-tone vector silhouette of the chair (SVG) for category icon / watermark use, derived from the cutout.
armchair_delivery_pack.zipdataSingle ZIP bundling all six outputs above plus the Firefly board deep-link, as the client-facing delivery folder.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
armchair_cutout.png is delivered as a transparent-background RGBA PNGautoFile armchair_cutout.png exists, is a valid PNG with an alpha channel (RGBA / PNG color type 6), and has fully transparent pixels (alpha==0) in the backdrop region outside the chair silhouetteK1dealbreakeroutput
armchair_cutout.png is at full source resolution (~4096px long edge)autoarmchair_cutout.png long edge is >= 4000px (spec: full source resolution, '~4096px long edge'; source is 4096x4096)K1mandatoryoutput
The cutout edge is clean and halo-free with no grey backdrop fringeexpertNo grey / light-grey fringe ring or matte halo from the seamless light-grey backdrop remains along the chair's silhouette; edges read crisp and design-readyK3mandatorybrief
The cutout preserves the open negative space (armrest gap and gaps between the splayed legs) as transparentexpertThe open gap between the seat and each armrest, and the negative space between the tapered splayed legs, are cut through to transparency rather than left filled with backdrop pixelsK3mandatorybrief
armchair_master_graded.png is delivered as a full-resolution RGBA PNG, distinct from the cutout (straightened / auto-toned / white-balanced)autoFile armchair_master_graded.png exists, is an RGBA PNG at full source resolution (long edge >= 4000px), and is pixel-distinct from armchair_cutout.png (tone/color/orientation differs)K1mandatoryoutput
The graded master shows correct color: warm walnut wood and true teal upholstery on neutral white balanceexpertThe walnut frame reads warm brown, the woven upholstery reads as true teal (not green/cyan-shifted), and there is no overall grey/blue/yellow color castK4mandatorybrief
armchair_hero_frame.png is a left+right outpainted landscape banner at ~3072x2048 RGBA PNGautoFile armchair_hero_frame.png exists, is an RGBA PNG, and dimensions are approximately 3072w x 2048h (landscape, width>height, each within ~5% of 3072 and 2048)K1mandatoryoutput
The hero frame seats the chair in a wider seamless studio sweep with the canvas extended on both sidesexpertCanvas is visibly extended left AND right of the original frame, the extended area continues the seamless studio backdrop with no visible seam, and the chair sits within the wider sweepK3qualitybrief
armchair_tile_2048.png is exactly 2048x2048 RGBA PNG with transparent backgroundautoarmchair_tile_2048.png is a PNG of exactly 2048x2048 px, RGBA with an alpha channel, and has fully transparent (alpha==0) background pixels around the subjectK1mandatoryoutput
The 2048x2048 tile has the chair centered in the squareexpertThe cutout chair is visually centered within the 2048x2048 square tileK3qualityoutput
armchair_tile_1080x1350.png is exactly 1080x1350 portrait RGBA PNG with transparent backgroundautoarmchair_tile_1080x1350.png is a PNG of exactly 1080px wide x 1350px tall, RGBA with an alpha channel, and has fully transparent (alpha==0) background pixels around the subjectK1mandatoryoutput
armchair_silhouette.svg is a valid vector SVG (not an embedded raster bitmap)autoFile armchair_silhouette.svg exists, parses as valid SVG containing vector path/shape elements, and is not merely a raster image wrapped in an <image> tagK1mandatoryoutput
The SVG silhouette is a clean two-tone silhouette whose outline matches the chairexpertThe vector is rendered in two tones and its outline is recognizably the cutout armchair (suitable for category-icon / watermark use), not a noisy or unrelated traceK3qualitybrief
armchair_delivery_pack.zip bundles all six deliverables plus the Firefly board deep-linkautoarmchair_delivery_pack.zip exists and contains armchair_cutout.png, armchair_master_graded.png, armchair_hero_frame.png, armchair_tile_2048.png, armchair_tile_1080x1350.png, armchair_silhouette.svg, plus a Firefly board deep-link (file or reference)K6mandatoryoutput
A single Firefly board review deep-link assembling all six produced assets is providedautoA Firefly board URL/deep-link is delivered and the board references all six produced assets (cutout, graded master, hero frame, 2048 tile, 1080x1350 tile, silhouette)K6qualityoutput
All raster deliverables depict the same single supplied source chair (armchair_studio_shot.png), not a regenerated or substituted chairexpertThe cutout, graded master, hero frame and both crops all depict the same mid-century walnut-and-teal lounge armchair from the supplied armchair_studio_shot.png, with no substituted or AI-fabricated chair (outpaint extends backdrop only, not the subject)K7dealbreakerinput

AO-41   Batch-clean and productize the Maple Ridge Home Services logo concepts into a scalable transparent/one-color/badge/vector brand-asset set

Brand/vertical Health, Wellness & MedicalFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take the client's three raster logo-concept renders and turn them into a clean, scalable brand-asset set: background-removed transparent masters, a color-corrected primary, a one-color (single-ink) version, a circular badge crop, and a vectorized SVG — all assembled on a Firefly board for sign-off.

Full brief

Maple Ridge Home Services is launching a premium-but-warm home-services brand (tagline: "Clean. Caring. Consistent.") and has early concept artwork it needs elevated into a polished, scalable asset system. The client supplies three raster files: (1) a photographed full-color primary lockup (maple-leaf icon + wordmark) shot on a cream studio sweep, (2) the maple-leaf-only icon photographed on a cluttered wooden workshop desk (busy background that must be stripped), and (3) a circular-badge concept render. The freelancer must NOT redraw the mark — the work is to clean, color-correct, and productize the existing concepts into the exact deliverables the brief lists. Pipeline: ingest the three client files into a working folder; straighten and tightly crop the primary lockup; auto-tone then warm/normalize its color toward the requested warm olive-sage / muted-terracotta / cream palette and lift the cream background to a clean even ivory; isolate the maple-leaf icon from its busy desk shot via prompt-driven subject selection and background removal to a transparent PNG; from that clean transparent icon, derive a one-color (single-ink terracotta) version using a flat color overlay for use on uniforms/yard signs where only one ink is available; build the circular badge by removing the badge render's background and cropping it square-to-round bounds; vectorize the cleaned icon to a scalable SVG for trucks and large-format signage; and finally assemble all the produced variants (color primary, transparent icon, one-color icon, badge, SVG preview) onto a Firefly board as the review/sign-off deliverable. Deliverables: transparent-PNG master set, a color-graded primary, a one-color version, a circular badge, an SVG, and the board deep-link.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
primary_logo_graded.pngimageColor-corrected, straightened, tightly cropped primary lockup; warm olive-sage/terracotta/ivory palette, even ivory background; high-res PNG for web headers and invoices.
maple_icon_transparent.pngimageMaple Ridge icon isolated from the busy desk shot, background fully removed, transparent PNG master for flexible placement.
maple_icon_onecolor.pngimageSingle-ink (muted terracotta) flat version of the transparent icon for one-color application on uniforms, yard signs and stamps; transparent PNG.
circular_badge_transparent.pngimageBackground-removed circular badge, cropped to its round bounds; transparent PNG for social avatars and truck doors.
maple_icon.svgvectorClean vectorized SVG of the maple-leaf icon for infinite-scale signage and embroidery (the source/scalable deliverable the brief requests alongside AI/EPS).
maple-leaf_brand_boarddataFirefly board deep-link assembling the color primary, transparent icon, one-color icon, badge and vector preview for client review/sign-off.
