Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All six required deliverables are present: primary_logo_graded.png, maple_icon_transparent.png, maple_icon_onecolor.png, circular_badge_transparent.png, maple_icon.svg, and the maple-leaf_brand_board Firefly board deep-link.autoExactly these 6 outputs exist: 4 PNGs (primary_logo_graded.png, maple_icon_transparent.png, maple_icon_onecolor.png, circular_badge_transparent.png), 1 SVG (maple_icon.svg), and 1 Firefly board deep-link (maple-leaf_brand_board); none missing.K1mandatoryoutput
The client's original maple-leaf mark is preserved (not redrawn, regenerated, or generatively altered) across every derived variant.expertThe maple-leaf icon shape and the 'Maple Ridge' / 'HOME SERVICES' lettering in every output match the supplied client concept renders (primary_lockup_concept.png, maple_icon_on_desk.jpg, circular_badge_concept.png); no new/invented mark and no generative change to the logo geometry, per the brief's explicit 'must NOT redraw the mark' requirement.K2dealbreakerbrief
maple_icon_transparent.png has a fully removed (transparent) background.autoPNG has an alpha channel and the desk/clutter background pixels are transparent (alpha=0), leaving only the isolated maple-leaf icon opaque.K1mandatoryoutput
The maple-leaf icon in maple_icon_transparent.png is cleanly isolated from the cluttered workshop-desk background.expertMask edges are clean and tight to the icon card; none of the maple_icon_on_desk.jpg clutter (wood desk, coffee mug-ring stain, wood shavings, metal ruler, pencil, paper scraps) remains, and there is no halo or fringe.K3mandatorybrief
maple_icon_onecolor.png renders in a single muted-terracotta ink only.expertThe icon is filled with one solid muted-terracotta color (no olive-sage, no second ink, no multi-tone gradients), suitable for one-color application on uniforms/yard signs per the output spec.K1mandatoryoutput
maple_icon_onecolor.png is a transparent PNG (single-ink fill on transparency).autoPNG has an alpha channel with a transparent background; only the terracotta-filled icon pixels are opaque.K1mandatoryoutput
circular_badge_transparent.png has its gray-tabletop background removed and is cropped to the badge's tight round bounds.autoPNG has an alpha channel; the neutral light-gray tabletop and cream-card margin from circular_badge_concept.png are transparent, and the opaque content is bounded tightly to the circular badge.K1mandatoryoutput
The circular badge is centered in the frame with the ring and its contents not clipped.expertThe round badge sits centered after the crop-to-bounds; the ring, the curved 'Maple Ridge · HOME SERVICES · EST 2026' text, and the centered maple-leaf mark are fully within frame, not cut off.K3qualitybrief
maple_icon.svg is a valid vector file produced by vectorizing the cleaned transparent icon.autoFile parses as valid SVG containing vector <path> elements (not an embedded raster bitmap) and represents the maple-leaf icon.K1mandatoryoutput
primary_logo_graded.png is straightened (wordmark baseline level) and tightly cropped to the artwork.expertThe 'Maple Ridge' / 'HOME SERVICES' lockup baseline reads level (the off-angle photographed rotation is corrected) and the surplus cream studio sweep is cropped away to the artwork bounds.K3mandatoryoutput
primary_logo_graded.png has an even, clean ivory background and a warm olive-sage / muted-terracotta / ivory palette.expertThe uneven photographed cream paper is cleaned to a flat even ivory background and the overall color reads warm with olive-sage and muted-terracotta accents, matching the requested brand palette.K4mandatorybrand
The Maple Ridge primary lockup text content is preserved and legible in primary_logo_graded.png.autoOCR of primary_logo_graded.png reads 'Maple Ridge', 'HOME SERVICES', and the tagline 'Clean. Caring. Consistent.' exactly as in the supplied lockup, with no characters dropped or altered by the cleanup.K2mandatoryinput
The maple-leaf_brand_board Firefly board assembles all five produced visual variants for client sign-off.autoThe board deep-link resolves and contains the 5 produced assets: primary_logo_graded, maple_icon_transparent, maple_icon_onecolor, circular_badge_transparent, and the maple_icon SVG preview.K6mandatoryoutput
primary_logo_graded.png is delivered as a high-resolution color PNG raster.autoFile is PNG format, high-resolution raster, and full-color (not single-ink and not JPG or another format), suitable for web headers and invoices per the output spec.K1qualityoutput
All three client raster inputs were ingested into a working CC folder before processing.autoA CC project folder exists holding primary_lockup_concept.png, maple_icon_on_desk.jpg, and circular_badge_concept.png as finalized asset URNs prior to any image edit.K6qualitybrief
image_fill_area is used only as a solid ivory fill of the selected background, not to invent or remove logo content.expertThe ivory cleanup is a flat solid-color fill of the selected cream-paper background region; the maple-leaf mark and wordmark are untouched by any generative-fill, text-to-image, or object-removal operation.K7qualitybrief

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-42   OVERDRIVE merch-brand logo finishing: variant set, transparent PNGs, SVG vector source, palette + font kit, usage board

Brand/vertical Nonprofit, Religious & CommunityFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 14)Tools 11 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take the client's supplied OVERDRIVE raster logo lockup and productionize it into the full merch brand-kit deliverable set — transparent primary/stacked/icon/avatar PNGs, social-PFP crop, black/white/mono variants, a clean SVG vector source, an extracted palette reference, font recommendations, and a usage moodboard — chaining every step in Adobe.

Full brief

OVERDRIVE is a new scholarship-focused merchandise brand (education support via merch sales + scholarship-style campaigns); the look must read bold, modern, youthful, collegiate and merch-ready — premium streetwear, not charity/nonprofit — with the name shown as OVERDRIVE in all caps and a clean, scalable graduation-cap element (no government/university seal, lottery, or generic-education-clipart vibe). The client has approved a rendered raster logo lockup (primary horizontal version on a flat background) and supplies a hoodie mock photo for usage context. Your job, executed entirely with the Adobe connector, is to finish that concept into the production brand kit the client requested: (1) upload the supplied logo lockup into Adobe storage; (2) strip its background to a clean transparent primary logo PNG; (3) derive a tight-cropped icon/avatar from the cap mark and a square social-profile-picture crop sized for PFPs; (4) generate the merch-critical single-color variants — a full BLACK version and a full WHITE version (for dark/light garments, stickers, and watermarks) using subject selection + solid fills, plus one monochromatic brand-tint alt; (5) vectorize the transparent logo into a clean, scalable SVG source for hoodies/hats/large print; (6) produce a flat palette/swatch reference for the color-palette deliverable; (7) recommend brand fonts that match the collegiate-streetwear tone; and (8) assemble the finished variants into a Firefly usage moodboard the client can review, then package everything as the delivered kit. Hard exclusions per Adobe capability ground truth: no from-scratch logo generation, no AI object removal, no compositing, no upscaling — the supplied lockup is the source of truth and every deliverable is derived from it by chaining. The brief's requested AI/EPS source and multipage PDF, and the written originality/commercial-rights confirmation, are noted as human/format-export finishing items layered on top of the connector-produced SVG + PNG masters.

outputkindspec
OVERDRIVE_primary_logo_transparent.pngimage/pngPrimary horizontal lockup, background removed, RGBA transparent, full source resolution (~2400x1400).
OVERDRIVE_icon_avatar_transparent.pngimage/pngCap-mark icon/avatar, tight-cropped to content bounds, transparent RGBA.
OVERDRIVE_social_pfp_1080.pngimage/pngSquare social profile-picture crop of the icon, 1080x1080, transparent RGBA, centered with padding.

OVERDRIVE_logo_white.pngimage/pngFull WHITE (solid #FFFFFF) single-color version of the lockup on transparency — for dark garments and headers.
OVERDRIVE_logo_mono_navy.pngimage/pngMonochromatic navy-tinted single-color brand alt of the lockup.
OVERDRIVE_logo_source.svgimage/svg+xmlClean vectorized SVG source of the transparent primary logo — scalable master for hoodies/hats/large print (stands in for the AI/EPS source deliverable).
OVERDRIVE_palette_swatches.pngimage/pngFlat color-palette reference (navy + varsity gold + neutrals) for the color-palette deliverable.

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-44   Instagram brand-suite asset prep: cutouts, unified grade, platform sizes + logo vectorize

Brand/vertical Technology, SaaS & StartupsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take the client's supplied product/founder shots, raster logo, and copy/hex palette and turn them into a cohesive, Instagram-ready brand asset pack: clean cutouts, one unified color grade across every image, exact platform sizes (1080×1920 reel + story, 1080×1350 feed), plus the logo as a scalable vector (AI/SVG) and transparent PNG.

Full brief

We are a B2B SaaS startup refreshing our Instagram presence and need the client-supplied source assets cleaned up, color-matched, and exported into a cohesive, platform-correct brand pack. At kickoff we hand you: (1) a square studio product shot of our app running on a phone-in-hand, (2) a founder/team candid for an 'about us' Reel cover, (3) our existing logo as a flat raster PNG that we've lost the vector for, and our brand palette — primary indigo #4F46E5, ink #0F172A, off-white #F8FAFC. Your job (production prep only — no new layout design, no type setting): license a clean gradient/studio backdrop from stock; cut our product and founder out of their busy original backgrounds; place each subject on the brand off-white; then push EVERY image through ONE identical brand grade (auto-tone baseline, then a cooler indigo-leaning temperature, lifted shadows, and a controlled vibrance bump) so the product shot, the founder cover, and the stock backdrop all read as one family. Because our hero product shot is square but the Reel cover must be a tall 1080×1920, generatively expand its short axis first so nothing important gets cropped, then deliver platform-exact exports: a 1080×1920 Reel/IGTV cover, a 1080×1350 feed version of the same hero, and a 1080×1920 story version. Finally, vectorize our raster logo into a clean, editable SVG (Illustrator-ready) and also give us a crisp transparent-PNG cutout of it. Everything must trace back to our supplied files and our hex palette so the feed feels instantly recognizable.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_reel_cover_1080x1920.pngimage1080×1920 PNG (9:16 Reel/IGTV cover). Product subject cut out, recomposited on brand off-white #F8FAFC, unified indigo brand grade applied, short axis generatively expanded before the 9:16 crop so the product is not clipped.
hero_feed_1080x1350.pngimage1080×1350 PNG (4:5 feed). Same graded hero, reframed subject-aware to the feed ratio.
hero_story_1080x1920.pngimage1080×1920 PNG (9:16 story). Same graded hero, story placement.
founder_cover_graded_1080x1920.pngimage1080×1920 PNG. Founder candid cut out, placed on brand off-white, SAME unified indigo grade as the hero (mixed-lighting corrected), reframed to 9:16 with founders kept in frame.
backdrop_graded.jpgimageLicensed stock gradient/studio backdrop pushed through the identical brand grade so it matches the family; full-res licensed asset.
logo_vector.svgvectorClean editable SVG traced from the raster logo (Illustrator-ready vector paths).
logo_transparent.pngimageTransparent-background PNG cutout of the logo for ready-to-post use.
instagram_brand_pack.zipdataAll deliverables zipped and clearly labelled into one organized pack (the only off-connector local step).

The founder candid subjects are cut out and placed on brand off-white #F8FAFCexpertIn founder_cover_graded_1080x1920.png both founders are cleanly cut from the busy co-working background (flyaway hair handled) and placed on a #F8FAFC off-white background with no halo/fringeK3mandatorybrief
One identical unified indigo brand grade is applied across product, founder, and backdrop so they read as one familyexperthero exports, founder_cover_graded_1080x1920.png, and backdrop_graded.jpg share a visibly consistent cooler indigo-leaning white balance, lifted shadows, and controlled vibrance — no image is an obvious tonal outlierK3mandatorybrief
The grade visibly corrects the founder candid's mixed tungsten/window lightingexpertfounder_cover_graded_1080x1920.png no longer shows the warm-tungsten vs cool-window color cast of the source founder_candid.jpg; whites read neutral-to-cool and consistent with the heroK4qualitybrief
The square hero short axis was generatively expanded before the 9:16 crop so the product is not clippedexpertIn hero_reel_cover_1080x1920.png and hero_story_1080x1920.png the phone-in-hand product is fully within frame with no important part cropped off; the top/bottom outpaint blends seamlessly with no visible seam or content distortionK3mandatorybrief
backdrop_graded.jpg is a licensed stock backdrop (licensed before editing), graded into the familyexpertbackdrop_graded.jpg is a clean gradient/studio backdrop sourced from licensed Adobe Stock (no watermark/comp marks) pushed through the same indigo brand grade so it matches the hero/founder familyK2mandatorybrief
All deliverables trace back to the client-supplied source files, not regenerated or newly designedexpertThe hero subject derives from product_hero_square.png, the founder subject from founder_candid.jpg, and logo_vector.svg/logo_transparent.png from logo_raster.png (the NIMBUSLY hexagon-arrow indigo wordmark) — no new logo designed and no poster/thumbnail layout or type composition introducedK7dealbreakerinput
Deliverables are organized into one neatly labelled packautoinstagram_brand_pack.zip is a single archive whose contents are named so each deliverable (reel cover, feed, story, founder cover, backdrop, logo SVG, logo PNG) is clearly identifiable by filenameK6qualitybrief

AO-53   Pop-Art Duotone Social Launch Kit from a Brand Logo + Hero Shot

Brand/vertical Video Editing & Motion GraphicsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O6 Stylized & duotoneHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 14)Tools 12 distinctCapability profile K4 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's hero shot and pop-art logo into a reusable duotone/halftone social launch kit: stickers, icons, carousel + reel-cover art, platform-sized posts, and data-merged post variations — all built on Adobe connector tools.

Full brief

Our brand identity is built on colorful pop-art, and we're launching across Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn and Pinterest. We already have our logo and brand identity (logo supplied as a layered Illustrator file with Variables; brand colors anchored on a hot-magenta + cyan duotone pair). Build the pop-art social launch kit from a single hero shot we provide.\n\nStart by isolating the hero subject (remove the background to a clean transparent PNG). Convert that cutout into our signature look: a bold halftone/duotone treatment (visible print-screen dots) recolored into the brand magenta/cyan pair, then push the pop with a punchy color overlay and an HSL boost so the magentas and cyans sing. From that same pop-art subject, derive a scalable sticker/icon asset by vectorizing it to clean SVG (so we can recolor and reuse it forever without quality loss). Produce a reel-cover canvas by extending the artwork to a tall 9:16 frame (outpaint the edges so nothing important is cropped), and then cut the finished pop-art hero into the four platform sizes we post at: 1:1 feed, 4:5 portrait, 9:16 story/reel, and 2:3 Pinterest. Export our supplied logo file to web-ready PNG/SVG/PDF so it drops into every template, and generate our launch post-variation set by data-merging our supplied caption/headline CSV against our authored Illustrator post template (one finished graphic per row). Recommend on-brand display + body fonts to pair with the kit, and finally assemble everything into a single shareable Firefly board so the team can remix and reuse the system for future campaigns. Deliverables must be reusable, not one-offs.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

outputkindspec
hero_cutout.pngimageTransparent-background PNG of the hero subject, full resolution, alpha-matted edges.
hero_popart_duotone.pngimagePop-art hero: halftone print-screen dots recolored to brand magenta (#E6007E) + cyan (#00AEEF) duotone, with punchy color overlay and HSL boost. PNG, sRGB.

platform_crops/image_setFour exports of the pop-art hero: 1:1 (1080x1080 feed), 4:5 (1080x1350 portrait), 9:16 (1080x1920 story/reel), 2:3 (1000x1500 Pinterest), subject-aware framing. PNG each.
brand_logo_export.{png,svg,pdf}vectorWeb-ready exports of the supplied logo .ai (transparent PNG, SVG, PDF) for drop-in across templates.
post_variations/image_set8 data-merged launch post variations (one finished graphic per CSV row) rendered from the authored post_template.ai + post_copy.csv. PDF/PNG.

launch_kit_boardurlA single shareable Firefly board deep-link assembling all kit assets for future remix/reuse.

The full deliverable set is present: hero_cutout.png, hero_popart_duotone.png, hero_sticker.svg, icon_set.svg, reel_cover_9x16.png, the platform_crops/ set, brand_logo_export in png+svg+pdf, the post_variations/ set, font_pairing.txt, and a launch_kit_board URLautoAll 10 named outputs exist; platform_crops/ contains exactly 4 image files, brand_logo_export exists as all three of {.png,.svg,.pdf}, and post_variations/ contains exactly 8 graphicsK1mandatoryoutput

hero_popart_duotone.png shows a visible halftone / print-screen dot patternexpertDiscrete, regularly spaced halftone dots are clearly visible across the subject (not a smooth continuous-tone image)K3mandatoryoutput

hero_sticker.svg is a true vector file (not a raster image embedded in SVG) so it is recolorable and scales without quality lossautohero_sticker.svg parses as SVG containing vector <path>/<polygon> geometry and no embedded raster <image> bitmap as its primary contentK1mandatoryoutput
icon_set.svg is a single true-vector SVG containing all 6 pop-art motifs: lightning bolt, starburst, heart, speech bubble, smiley, and arrowexperticon_set.svg parses as one SVG with vector path geometry (no embedded raster bitmap) and renders 6 distinct, recognizable motifs matching the named set (lightning bolt, starburst, heart, speech bubble, smiley, arrow)K1mandatoryoutput
reel_cover_9x16.png has a 9:16 aspect ratio with the original artwork extended (outpainted) rather than croppedautoreel_cover_9x16.png width:height equals 9:16; the original hero subject is fully present and uncropped, with new generated/extended canvas content above/below or to the sidesK1mandatoryoutput
The four platform_crops/ exports have the exact required pixel dimensionsautoplatform_crops/ contains PNGs at 1080x1080 (1:1), 1080x1350 (4:5), 1080x1920 (9:16), and 1000x1500 (2:3) — exactly one of eachK1mandatoryoutput
Each platform crop keeps the hero subject framed (not cut off) within its ratioexpertIn all four crops the hero subject's key area (face/upper body) remains within frame and reasonably composedK3qualityoutput
brand_logo_export is the exported supplied VOLT logo, unaltered — the wordmark, magenta/cyan colors and starburst accent match the input brand_logo.aiexpertThe PNG/SVG/PDF exports reproduce the supplied VOLT logo (hot-magenta wordmark, cyan offset, comic-style starburst accent) faithfully with no regeneration or redesign of the markK2dealbreakerinput
post_variations/ contains exactly 8 merged graphics, one per row of post_copy.csvautopost_variations/ holds exactly 8 finished graphics (PDF/PNG), matching the 8 CSV rowsK1mandatoryoutput
Each post variation displays the headline, subhead, and cta text from its corresponding post_copy.csv rowautoFor each of the 8 rows, the headline, subhead, and cta values appear (via OCR) on the matching merged graphic, with no missing or mismatched copyK1mandatoryinput
font_pairing.txt recommends an on-brand display font AND a separate body font with a written rationaleautofont_pairing.txt names at least one display font and at least one separate body font and includes a written rationale for the pairingK5mandatoryoutput

K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-70   Luxury-prize hero image for a UK raffle brand: stock-source a supercar, isolate it, AI-expand into a premium scene, bold luxury color grade, and export Instagram/Facebook ad crops

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Photo & ImageOperation O8 Stock-sourced heroHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K3 · K4 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Build a premium, bold luxury-car prize hero image for a UK raffle brand from a licensed stock supercar — isolate it, AI-expand the canvas into a wider hero scene, apply a bold premium color grade with cinematic depth, and export Instagram and Facebook ad-ready crops plus a clean vector of the brand logo.

Full brief

We are a fast-growing UK online raffle & competition company ("Velvet Wins") and our next campaign gives away a luxury supercar. We need a single premium, bold, attention-grabbing HERO image of the prize car — the kind of look luxury raffle / casino / giveaway brands use — that we can reuse across Instagram posts/ads, Facebook ad creatives, and a website competition banner. Starting point: we don't have our own studio shot of the car, so source a high-quality licensed stock photo of a luxury sports car (dark, glossy, dramatic studio or showroom lighting, three-quarter front angle, clean background). License it properly before any editing. What we need done to it: 1. Straighten and clean-crop the licensed plate so the car sits level and well-composed. 2. Isolate the car from its original background so we control the scene. 3. AI-expand the canvas wider and taller into a cinematic hero composition with breathing room around the car (room for our headline and CTA to be added later in our own template) — this is an outpaint/extend, not a composite. 4. Grade it to a bold, premium luxury look: punchy contrast, rich deep blacks, controlled highlights on the bodywork, a slightly cool/teal premium cast, and a saturated hero accent on the car's paint colour so it pops. 5. Add cinematic depth — a soft background blur / lens feel so the car is the unmistakable focus. 6. A subtle film grain so it reads premium/editorial rather than flat-digital. 7. Export the finished hero in the exact pixel sizes we post at: Instagram square 1080×1080, Instagram/Facebook story 1080×1920, and a wide website/Facebook banner 1920×1080 — each crop must keep the car as the focal subject. 8. Separately, take our supplied "Velvet Wins" wordmark logo (PNG) and give us a clean scalable VECTOR (SVG) version so it stays crisp at banner and billboard scale. Deliverables: one master graded hero plate, three platform-sized crops (1:1, 9:16, 16:9), and the vectorized logo. Modern, premium, bold — make the prize stand out. This will likely become ongoing work as we launch new competitions.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 18 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
velvet_wins_hero_master.pngimageMaster graded luxury-car prize hero plate, AI-expanded wide cinematic composition, full premium color grade + lens-blur depth + subtle grain baked in. Largest available resolution (~2400x1500+), RGB PNG. The source-of-truth plate the platform crops are cut from.
hero_instagram_1080x1080.pngimageInstagram square crop, exactly 1080x1080 px, car kept as focal subject, graded look preserved. RGB PNG.
hero_story_1080x1920.pngimageInstagram/Facebook story-and-reels vertical crop, exactly 1080x1920 px (9:16), car centered with headroom for later headline/CTA. RGB PNG.
hero_banner_1920x1080.pngimageWide website / Facebook banner crop, exactly 1920x1080 px (16:9), car offset with negative space for copy. RGB PNG.
velvet_wins_logo.svgvectorClean traced vector of the supplied wordmark, scalable SVG, solid color regions preserved, crisp at any banner/billboard scale.

All four image deliverables are RGB PNG files (not CMYK, not JPEG)autovelvet_wins_hero_master.png, hero_instagram_1080x1080.png, hero_story_1080x1920.png, and hero_banner_1920x1080.png are each valid PNG files in RGB color modeK1mandatoryoutput
The logo deliverable is a true scalable SVG vector, not a raster image embedded in an SVG wrapperautovelvet_wins_logo.svg is a valid SVG containing traced vector path/shape geometry (<path>/<polygon>/etc.) and contains no embedded raster bitmap (no <image> tag or base64 raster data)K1dealbreakeroutput
The vectorized logo is traced from the supplied Velvet Wins wordmark and preserves its contentexpertvelvet_wins_logo.svg clearly reproduces the supplied velvet_wins_logo.png 'VELVET WINS' wordmark (letterforms, the small ticket/diamond glyph to the left, purple-to-magenta fill with a thin gold keyline), with solid/flat color regions preserved and crisp edges at large scaleK2mandatoryinput
The hero car was licensed from Adobe Stock before any editing, not generated from scratchexpertThe hero subject is a licensed Adobe Stock luxury sports car obtained via a license-and-download step run BEFORE any edit step (license-first ordering), not a from-scratch generated or un-licensed carK6mandatorybrief
The hero canvas was AI-expanded (outpainted) into a wider/taller cinematic scene, not built by compositingexpertvelvet_wins_hero_master.png shows the car within an extended/outpainted wider-and-taller scene with breathing room around the car for a later headline/CTA, produced by canvas expansion (outpaint) rather than pasting the isolated car onto a separate backgroundK3mandatorybrief
A bold premium luxury color grade is applied: punchy contrast, deep blacks, controlled highlights, slightly cool/teal castexpertvelvet_wins_hero_master.png reads as a high-contrast premium grade with rich deep blacks, controlled (non-blown) highlights on the glossy bodywork, and a slight cool/teal castK4mandatorybrief
A saturated hero accent is applied selectively to the car's paint colour so the prize pops against the cool backgroundexpertThe car's paint hue is selectively more saturated/vivid than the surrounding scene (single-hue accent), making the car the standout color accent rather than a uniform global saturation boostK4qualitybrief
Cinematic shallow-depth background blur is present so the car is the focusexpertvelvet_wins_hero_master.png shows a soft/lens-blurred background relative to a sharp car, giving a shallow-depth-of-field cinematic hero feelK4qualitybrief
A subtle film grain is present so the plate reads premium/editorial rather than flat-digitalexpertvelvet_wins_hero_master.png carries a subtle, even film grain that is visible but not heavy or noisyK4qualitybrief
Each of the three platform crops keeps the car fully framed as the focal subjectexpertIn hero_instagram_1080x1080.png, hero_story_1080x1920.png, and hero_banner_1920x1080.png the supercar is fully framed as the dominant focal subject (story crop with headroom, banner crop offset with negative space for copy) and is not cut off or marginalizedK3mandatorybrief
All three platform crops are cut from the one master and carry the same graded lookexpertThe premium grade, depth blur, and grain of velvet_wins_hero_master.png are consistently preserved across all three crops (same colour, contrast, and treatment), with no crop showing a different or un-graded lookK1qualityoutput

AO-71   Refresh a VC firm's website visuals: brand-graded stock hero, outpainted to full-bleed, responsive section crops, and vectorized SVG icons

Brand/vertical Finance, Crypto & Professional ServicesFamily Photo & ImageOperation O8 Stock-sourced heroHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K4 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Take a licensed Adobe Stock business photograph plus three client icon sketches and produce a cohesive VC-website graphics set: a brand-graded full-bleed hero, responsive desktop/tablet/mobile section crops, a tinted section-divider variant, and clean SVG icons — all chained from the licensed source.

Full brief

Our venture-capital firm's website works structurally, but the graphics feel dated and no longer signal the credibility we want. Refresh the visual layer into a cohesive, web-ready set that matches our brand. (1) HERO: source a high-quality, forward-thinking business/finance photograph from Adobe Stock (modern glass-and-light office or abstract data-light scene, no recognizable faces or logos), license it at full resolution, then color-grade it to our brand palette — primary deep navy #0B1F3A, slate #2E3A4D, warm gold #C8A24B accent: auto-tone for a clean baseline, cool the white balance toward navy, pull the HSL so blues read as our navy and the gold accent stays warm, and lay a subtle navy color-overlay so the hero reads on-brand rather than generic. (2) FULL-BLEED: the licensed crop isn't wide enough for our 2560px hero band, so outpaint/extend the graded image to a 2560x1080 (≈21:9) full-bleed canvas using generative expand, keeping the new edges photographically consistent. (3) RESPONSIVE: from that master hero, produce the three breakpoint crops the brief requires — desktop 2560x1080, tablet 1536x1024 (4:3-ish), and mobile 1080x1350 (4:5 portrait) — each subject-aware so the focal area survives the reframe. (4) SECTION DIVIDER: derive one subtle monochromatic navy-tinted variant of the hero to use as a low-contrast section-background graphic. (5) ICONS: we'll supply three flat raster icon sketches (portfolio / growth / network); knock out their backgrounds to transparency and vectorize each into a clean, infinitely-scalable SVG so they stay crisp at any breakpoint and stay well under 50 KB. (6) HAND-OFF: recommend a brandable typeface pairing for headings/labels, assemble all produced assets into a single Firefly board the team can review, and upload the final set back to a shared CC folder. Deliverables: graded full-bleed hero (2560x1080 PNG, <200 KB target after web compression), 3 responsive hero crops (PNG), 1 navy section-divider variant (PNG), 3 transparent icon SVGs (<50 KB each), a font-pairing recommendation, and a review board link.

4Planning4Brand strictness4Tool diversity3Visual density2Creative freedom

The hero is sourced from a licensed Adobe Stock photograph rather than generated from scratchexpertThe graded full-bleed hero is recognizably derived from a licensed stock photo (modern glass/light office or abstract data-light scene, no recognizable faces or readable logos), not a from-scratch text-to-image illustrationK7dealbreakerbrief
Stock licensing occurred before any editing, using the full-resolution licensed download rather than an unlicensed watermarked compautoWorkflow log shows asset_license_and_download_stock executed before any image_* grading/edit step, and the edited hero derives from the full-resolution licensed download (no Adobe Stock watermark present in the graded hero)K6dealbreakerbrief
The hero is color-graded to the stated brand palette (deep navy #0B1F3A primary, slate #2E3A4D, warm gold #C8A24B accent)expertThe graded hero reads on-brand: shadows/blues pushed toward navy #0B1F3A with slate #2E3A4D midtones and the warm gold #C8A24B accent preserved rather than neutralized; image reads navy-on-brand, not warm-genericK4mandatorybrief
The full-bleed hero was created by generatively expanding/outpainting the graded crop to 2560x1080 with photographically consistent new edgesexpertThe 2560x1080 hero shows extended canvas beyond the original stock crop where outpainted regions match the source in lighting, perspective, grain, and content with no visible seam, stretch, or repeat artifactK3mandatorybrief

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-72   Modern Store Graphics Package — Hero Banner, PDP Assets & Store Decoration

Brand/vertical Party, Events & PromotionFamily Photo & ImageOperation O8 Stock-sourced heroHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 24)Tools 19 distinctCapability profile K3 · K4 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's product hero shot, stock backdrop, brand palette, and two authored source templates into a cohesive modern-storefront graphics package: a desktop + mobile hero banner, clean PDP image frames and vector icon/divider assets, and tied-together decoration — delivered as layered source exports plus optimized JPG/PNG.

Full brief

I'm refreshing my online shop and need a cohesive set of graphics that instantly tells shoppers they're in a sleek, modern space. Scope is three areas. (1) STORE BANNER — a hero that sets the tone the moment visitors land, sized for both desktop (1920x720) and mobile (1080x1350). I'll give you one clean product hero shot to build it from. (2) PDP ASSETS — template-ready image frames plus iconography and typography treatments that make features, specs and CTAs pop without clutter. (3) STORE DECORATION — background elements, section dividers and accent graphics that tie everything together page to page. Art direction: clean lines, bold but balanced color, plenty of negative space — nothing fussy or ornamental. I'm supplying brand colors (deep ink #14233B + warm sand #E7D3B1 accent), the product hero shot, a few supporting product crops, and two layered source templates my team authored in InDesign and Illustrator (the banner layout .indd with our logo + headline frames, and an .ai sheet of our icon/divider library) so you can produce the final layered source files and labeled exports we can tweak later. Please also pull one modern, negative-space lifestyle backdrop from stock for the secondary banner panel. Deliverables: layered PSD/AI/INDD source plus optimized JPG/PNG exports for immediate upload, clearly labeled, with desktop and mobile banner sizes, the PDP frame set, the vector icon + divider pack, and the decoration backgrounds. Lock the direction first, then banner, then PDP, then decoration.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 24 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_banner_desktop.jpgimage1920x720 JPEG, sRGB, optimized for web (<400KB), product on warm-sand with extended negative-space left third, auto-toned and brand-graded; primary store landing hero.
hero_banner_mobile.jpgimage1080x1350 JPEG, sRGB, vertically extended (outpainted) version of the same hero composition for mobile storefront, optimized <350KB.
secondary_banner_panel.jpgimage1920x720 JPEG built on the licensed stock backdrop, brand-temperature-matched to the hero, negative-space right side for headline overlay.
pdp_frame_set.pngimageSet of 1:1 PDP image frames (1200x1200 PNG) cropped from the product detail macros, consistently toned to match the hero look, clean edges, transparent-safe margins.
icon_pack.svgvectorClean vectorized icon + divider pack exported from the authored .ai plus one raster-derived accent vectorized to SVG; single-weight deep-ink strokes, transparent, scalable for PDP and decoration.
store_banner_source.indd_exportpdfPress/preview export (PDF + PNG) of the authored layered banner .indd source, delivered alongside the original layered .indd so the client team can swap text/images.
decoration_backgrounds.pngimageTwo tileable/section decoration backgrounds (2000x1200 PNG) in brand paper + accent tones with subtle grain, plus the exported section-divider graphics; ties pages together.
store_graphics_package.zipdataSingle labeled ZIP bundling all layered source exports and optimized JPG/PNG/SVG deliverables in clearly named folders (01_banner, 02_pdp, 03_decoration, 00_source).

The icon pack is delivered as a valid transparent SVG containing the authored .ai icon/divider set plus one raster-derived vectorized accentautoicon_pack.svg is a valid SVG with transparent background containing the icon+divider geometry exported from icon_divider_sheet.ai, plus at least one additional motif vectorized from a graded product crop (image_vectorize output)K1mandatoryoutput

Every deliverable is bundled into one labeled ZIP using the exact named folders specifiedautostore_graphics_package.zip contains all layered source exports and optimized JPG/PNG/SVG deliverables organized into folders named exactly 00_source, 01_banner, 02_pdp, 03_decorationK6mandatoryoutput
The brand palette is applied using the exact supplied hex values for ink, sand, and paperautoDeliverables use deep-ink #14233B and warm-sand #E7D3B1, and the decoration background solid fill uses brand paper #FAF6EF, exactly as given in brand_palette_copy.csvK2dealbreakerbrand
The secondary banner backdrop is a genuinely licensed Adobe Stock image, licensed before any editingexpertThe backdrop underlying secondary_banner_panel.jpg traces to an asset_license_and_download_stock result for the query 'minimal modern kitchen counter morning light negative space warm neutral lifestyle', not a fabricated or unlicensed imageK7dealbreakerbrief
The desktop hero is produced by generatively expanding (outpainting) the single client product hero shot, not by inserting a different product imageexperthero_banner_desktop.jpg shows the same matte-ceramic deep-ink-navy pour-over dripper from product_hero_shot.jpg, with the warm-sand negative space extended on the left third via image_generative_expand outpaint and no regenerated or substituted productK2dealbreakerinput
The authored .ai and .indd sources are EXPORTED faithfully, not re-drawn or re-composed by the agentexperticon_pack.svg/divider graphics match the authored icon_divider_sheet.ai icon library (8 thin-line icons + 3 section dividers + accent flourishes), and the banner source export matches the authored store_banner_layout.indd (logo lockup, headline/CTA frames, image frame), with no invented or altered layout contentK2dealbreakerinput
A single consistent master grade is propagated across the hero, secondary panel, and PDP macros so the package reads as shot togetherexperthero_banner_desktop.jpg, hero_banner_mobile.jpg, secondary_banner_panel.jpg, and pdp_frame_set.png share a cohesive warm-temperature grade consistent with the hero master lookK3qualitybrief
The package realizes the stated art direction: clean lines, bold-but-balanced color, plenty of negative space, nothing fussy or ornamentalexpertAcross all deliverables the design is minimal and uncluttered with generous negative space, restrained/balanced color, single-weight clean line work, and no fussy ornamentationK4qualitybrief

Layer 2 · Operation craft checklist — Photo & Image (expert marks Pass / Minor / Major vs reference images)

craft itemPassMinorMajor
Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost
Colour & white balanceneutral / on-brand; tones naturalslight castclear cast / wrong colour
Mask & edge qualityclean edges; fine detail (hair) keptsmall halo / jaggiesvisible halo / clipped subject
Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic
Texture & detail preservedrealistic micro-detailslightly softdetail smeared or lost
Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

AO-73   Lawn-Care Social Ad Pack: Grade + Outpaint a Stock Hero into 1080x1080 and 1200x628 FB/IG Ad Creatives

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O8 Stock-sourced heroHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K3 · K4 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Take the client's supplied lawn-care messaging, brand colors, and a licensed Adobe Stock hero photo, then grade it, pop the lawn green, AI-expand it into both 1080x1080 and 1200x628 ad canvases, build a brand-duotone carousel variant, vectorize the logo, and deliver the finished ad-creative image set for Facebook/Instagram.

Full brief

CLIENT: a small lawn-care / landscaping service running paid Facebook + Instagram ads. This is the $50 flat-fee TEST PROJECT from the "Social Media Ad Creative Designer — High-Volume, Fast-Turnaround" posting; nailing it earns ongoing $150–$500/project work. TURNAROUND: 72 hours. CHANNELS: Facebook feed, Instagram feed, Instagram carousel. REQUIRED DELIVERABLES (verbatim): three ad creatives delivered as PNGs in 1080x1080 square format AND 1200x628 landscape format. WHAT THE CLIENT SUPPLIES: ad messaging/headline copy, brand colors (a deep turf-green #1F5C2E and a sunny accent #F4C430), the company word-mark/logo, and the direction that the hero visual should be a clean, sunny, freshly-mowed-lawn/lawn-care-in-action stock photo. THE TASK (image craft a designer actually performs on those photos): (1) source and LICENSE a high-res lawn-care hero from Adobe Stock; (2) straighten and clean the frame; (3) apply a professional tonal grade — auto-tone baseline, exposure/contrast and highlight control, warm sunny color temperature, lifted shadows — so it reads as bright and premium; (4) selectively boost the LAWN green only (select the grass by prompt, invert, single-color saturation / HSL) so the turf looks lush without over-saturating the sky and skin; (5) reshape the SAME graded hero into BOTH required ad aspect ratios using AI outpaint (image_generative_expand) — extend the square stock frame outward to a true 1080x1080 and separately outpaint to a 1200x628 landscape — so neither format crops away the subject; (6) produce a second on-brand CAROUSEL variant by laying the deep turf-green brand color as a duotone/color treatment over the graded hero for a stylized slide; (7) vectorize the client word-mark to a crisp, scalable SVG for clean placement at any size; (8) assemble all working + final assets onto a Firefly Board as the client-facing review deliverable, and upload the final PNGs to a shared folder. The text/CTA lockup over each creative is the single finishing step the connector cannot do headlessly and is handed to the Express product / human. EVALUATION CARES ABOUT: correct final pixel dimensions (1080x1080 and 1200x628), a clean bright professional grade, lush-but-believable lawn green, outpaint that extends rather than crops the hero, faithful use of the supplied brand colors, and a tidy review hand-off.

4Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
ad_hero_square_1080imagePNG, 1080x1080, sRGB. Graded, lawn-green-popped, AI-outpainted-to-square lawn-care hero — the square Facebook/Instagram feed ad base.
ad_hero_landscape_1200x628imagePNG, 1200x628, sRGB. Same graded hero AI-outpainted to the 1.91:1 landscape ad canvas (subject preserved, not cropped) — the landscape FB/IG link-ad base.
ad_carousel_brand_duotoneimagePNG, 1080x1080, sRGB. On-brand carousel slide: graded hero with the #1F5C2E turf-green brand duotone/color treatment applied for a stylized branded slide.
greenblade_logo_vectorvectorSVG, scalable, single-color #1F5C2E word-mark + grass-blade icon vectorized from the client logo raster for crisp placement at any ad size.
review_board_and_folderdataA Firefly Board deep-link presenting all working + final assets for client review, plus a shared CC folder containing the three final ad PNGs and the logo SVG, ready for the Express/human text-lockup finishing step.

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Square ad hero deliverable (ad_hero_square_1080) is exactly 1080x1080 pixelsautoad_hero_square_1080 PNG measures exactly 1080 px wide x 1080 px tall (1:1)K1mandatoryoutput
Landscape ad hero deliverable (ad_hero_landscape_1200x628) is exactly 1200x628 pixelsautoad_hero_landscape_1200x628 PNG measures exactly 1200 px wide x 628 px tall (~1.91:1)K1mandatoryoutput
Carousel brand-duotone deliverable (ad_carousel_brand_duotone) is exactly 1080x1080 pixelsautoad_carousel_brand_duotone PNG measures exactly 1080 px wide x 1080 px tall (1:1)K1mandatoryoutput

All four required deliverable files are present (three ad PNGs plus the logo SVG)autoThe deliverable set contains all four files: ad_hero_square_1080 (1080x1080 PNG), ad_hero_landscape_1200x628 (1200x628 PNG), ad_carousel_brand_duotone (1080x1080 PNG), and greenblade_logo_vector (SVG)K1mandatoryoutput
The client word-mark is delivered as a scalable SVG vector, not an embedded rasterautogreenblade_logo_vector is a valid SVG file whose artwork is vector path/shape data (e.g. <path>/<polygon> elements), with no embedded raster bitmap (no <image>/base64 PNG-JPEG payload) standing in for the logoK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo faithfully reproduces the supplied client word-mark in single-color #1F5C2Eexpertgreenblade_logo_vector renders the 'GREENBLADE LAWN CARE' lettering and the grass-blade icon in the brand turf-green #1F5C2E, matching the supplied client_wordmark_logo art without altering the letterforms, spelling, or icon (no regeneration or restyling of the mark)K2dealbreakerinput
The carousel duotone is built on the supplied brand turf-green #1F5C2Eexpertad_carousel_brand_duotone shows a monochromatic/duotone treatment built on the deep turf-green brand color #1F5C2E (not an arbitrary or off-brand green)K2mandatorybrand
Both square and landscape ad bases are derived from the SAME graded hero photoexpertad_hero_square_1080 and ad_hero_landscape_1200x628 visibly depict the same lawn-care hero scene/subject (the same landscaper-mowing-a-lawn photo), not two different imagesK1mandatorybrief
The 1080x1080 and 1200x628 formats were produced by AI outpaint that EXTENDS rather than crops the hero subjectexpertIn both ad_hero_square_1080 and ad_hero_landscape_1200x628 the landscaper subject is preserved fully in frame and the added canvas extends the lawn/sky/scene outward with seamless continuation, rather than the subject being cropped to fit the aspect ratioK3mandatorybrief
The hero photo reads as a clean, bright, professionally graded imageexpertThe graded hero shows a balanced bright/premium tonal grade — warm sunny color temperature, controlled highlights, lifted shadows — with no blown-out sky or crushed blacksK4qualitybrief
The lawn green is selectively boosted to look lush without over-saturating sky or skinexpertThe grass/turf reads as lush and saturated green while the sky and the worker's skin tones remain natural and un-shifted, confirming the green pop is confined to the grass regionK3mandatorybrief
The outpaint edges and grade are seamless with no visible compositing artifactsexpertExtended canvas regions in the square and landscape creatives blend seamlessly with the original frame — no seams, repeated textures, warped grass stripes, or tonal mismatch at the outpaint boundaryK3qualitybrief
A shared CC delivery folder contains the three final ad PNGs and the logo SVGautoreview_board_and_folder includes a shared Creative Cloud folder holding the three final ad PNGs (ad_hero_square_1080, ad_hero_landscape_1200x628, ad_carousel_brand_duotone) plus greenblade_logo_vectorK6mandatoryoutput
A Firefly Board deep-link presents the produced final assets for client reviewautoreview_board_and_folder includes a valid Firefly Board deep-link assembling the produced final assets (square, landscape, carousel, logo) as the client-facing review surfaceK6mandatoryoutput
The text/CTA typographic lockup is flagged as the human/Express finishing step, not faked into the imagesexpertDelivered ad creatives contain no rendered headline/CTA text baked over the image, and the hand-off notes state the text/CTA lockup is handed to the Express product / human as the final finishing stepK7qualitybrief

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-45   Logo Vectorization + Brand Identity Kit — HaulAway (junk removal / vehicle-wrap-ready)

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Vectorize and clean up HaulAway' existing raster logo to print-ready quality, then build the full set of brand variations (master vector, horizontal, stacked, icon-only AB mark, one-color knockouts, a lime-on-black trailer-wrap hero) plus a font + color-usage reference and a presentation board — all wrap/shirt/print ready.

Full brief

HaulAway (junk removal & cleanout, Maitland FL / North Orlando) supplies ONE existing logo they love but only as a flat, low-resolution raster PNG (a bold "AB" monogram lockup with the company wordmark beneath, in their black / lime-green / gray palette). They need it turned into a professional, print-ready brand identity kit usable on t-shirts, vehicle & trailer wraps, car magnets, and print. The freelancer must drive the Adobe Creative Cloud connector end-to-end: 1) Ingest the client's raster logo and get a clean working copy (auto-tone + straighten the slightly skewed scan so edges are true). 2) Clean the color: neutralize any off-cast and crisp up the lime green and the blacks so the trace is sharp. 3) Clean-knock the background to transparent so the trace has no halo. 4) Vectorize the cleaned full lockup into a clean SVG = the MASTER vector (basis for AI/EPS/SVG/PNG exports). 5) Isolate the "AB" monogram for the ICON-ONLY mark: select the wordmark text by prompt, invert to keep just the monogram, solid-fill the rest away, then vectorize the isolated mark. 6) Produce the ONE-COLOR versions: a black-on-light knockout (solid black overlay) and a white-on-dark knockout (white fill) of the mark. 7) Build the layout LOCKUPS by cropping the cleaned master to its true bounds for the horizontal version and a tighter square for the stacked/vertical and the magnet. 8) Create the creative bonus "trailer-wrap hero": recolor the mark to a high-impact lime-on-black treatment the client can splash across a trailer. 9) Identify/recommend the brand font family + a usage pairing (font_recommend) as the typography guide. 10) Assemble all produced variations into a Firefly Board as the client-facing presentation / brand sheet (carrying the hex/CMYK/Pantone callouts in the board notes). Hard rules honored: ABSOLUTELY no generative fill, no AI object removal, no text-to-image, no compositing, no layout composition; image_fill_area is solid-color only; the master export step only EXPORTS genuine vector — the deliverable vector emerges from image_vectorize, not from an authored .ai. Every step after ingest consumes a prior step's output.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
ab_junk_master_logo.svgvectorClean vectorized master lockup (full AB monogram + wordmark + tagline) as scalable SVG with crisp paths, transparent background; the print-ready master from which AI/EPS/PNG exports derive. Black/lime/gray palette preserved.
ab_junk_icon_mark.svgvectorIcon-only 'AB' monogram, isolated (wordmark removed via select→invert→solid-fill) and vectorized; transparent background; for app icons, magnets, embroidery.
ab_junk_mark_oneclr_black_on_light.pngimageOne-color knockout: solid black AB mark on transparent/light, hi-res PNG for single-color print, stamps, light-shirt screen-print.
ab_junk_mark_oneclr_white_on_dark.pngimageOne-color knockout: solid white AB mark on transparent (for dark backgrounds), hi-res PNG for dark-shirt print and dark wrap panels.
ab_junk_horizontal_lockup.pngimageHorizontal layout version, cropped to true content bounds, transparent hi-res PNG sized for vehicle door / business-card horizontal use.
ab_junk_stacked_lockup.pngimageStacked/vertical (square-ish) layout version cropped tight, transparent hi-res PNG for car magnet and crew-shirt left-chest.
ab_junk_trailerwrap_hero.pngimageCreative bonus: high-impact lime-on-black 'trailer wrap hero' treatment of the mark, hi-res PNG meant to splash large across a trailer side.
ab_junk_font_usage_guide.jsondataTypography reference: recommended brand font family + heading/body pairing and usage notes from font_recommend, to accompany the kit.
ab_junk_brand_boarddataFirefly Board deep-link assembling all produced variations as the client presentation / brand sheet; board notes carry the black/lime/gray hex, CMYK and Pantone callouts and the font guidance.

All nine deliverables from expected_outputs are presentautoOutput set contains exactly these 9 named artifacts: ab_junk_master_logo.svg, ab_junk_icon_mark.svg, ab_junk_mark_oneclr_black_on_light.png, ab_junk_mark_oneclr_white_on_dark.png, ab_junk_horizontal_lockup.png, ab_junk_stacked_lockup.png, ab_junk_trailerwrap_hero.png, ab_junk_font_usage_guide.json, and ab_junk_brand_board (Firefly Board deep-link)K1mandatoryoutput
The master logo deliverable is a true vector SVG produced by image_vectorize, not a raster wrapped in SVGautoab_junk_master_logo.svg is a valid SVG containing actual <path>/vector geometry (not a single embedded <image> raster) with a transparent background; it is the output of image_vectorizeK1dealbreakeroutput
The icon-only mark deliverable is a true vector SVG of the isolated AB monogram onlyautoab_junk_icon_mark.svg is a valid SVG with vector <path> geometry and transparent background, containing the AB monogram only — no 'HaulAway SOLUTIONS' wordmark and no 'HAULING • CLEANOUTS • DEMOLITION' tagline pathsK1dealbreakeroutput
Master and icon are delivered as editable vector, satisfying the client's explicit rejection of JPG/PNG-only workautoab_junk_master_logo.svg and ab_junk_icon_mark.svg are both SVG files with editable vector paths; neither is delivered as a JPG or a flat PNGK1dealbreakerbrief
The vectorized logo preserves the supplied AB monogram and wordmark artwork rather than regenerating or redrawing itexpertThe master and icon vectors visually match the supplied ab_junk_logo_raw.png — same AB monogram shapes and the same 'HaulAway SOLUTIONS' wordmark forms; no generative-fill, object removal, redrawn, or AI-invented logo elements were introduced (workflow uses only trace/clean/mask tools)K2dealbreakerinput
Brand palette is restricted to black, lime green, and grayexpertAll produced color variations use only black, lime-green (~#9ACD32), and gray; no off-palette colors appear in the master, icon, lockups, knockouts, or trailerwrap heroK2mandatorybrand
Black-on-light one-color knockout is a single solid black markautoab_junk_mark_oneclr_black_on_light.png is a hi-res PNG showing the AB mark in solid black only (single color) on a transparent/light background, produced via a black color overlay on the isolated markK1mandatoryoutput
White-on-dark one-color knockout is a single solid white mark for dark backgroundsautoab_junk_mark_oneclr_white_on_dark.png is a hi-res PNG showing the AB mark in solid white only (single color) on a transparent background suitable for dark shirts/wrap panelsK1mandatoryoutput
The near-white #F4F4F2 background is knocked out to true transparency before vectorize, with no haloautoThe #F4F4F2 background is removed (via image_remove_background) before image_vectorize; ab_junk_master_logo.svg and the transparent PNG lockups have an alpha-transparent background with no white halo/fringe around the artworkK1mandatorybrief
Horizontal lockup is cropped tight to the true content bounds in landscape aspectautoab_junk_horizontal_lockup.png is a transparent hi-res PNG cropped to the artwork's true content bounds (minimal surrounding empty margin) in a horizontal/landscape aspect, produced via image_crop_to_bounds on the cleaned masterK1mandatoryoutput
Stacked/vertical lockup is a tight square-ish frame derived from the horizontal lockupautoab_junk_stacked_lockup.png is a transparent hi-res PNG in a square-ish (vertical/stacked) frame cropped tight, derived via image_crop_and_resize from ab_junk_horizontal_lockup.png, suitable for car magnet and left-chest useK1mandatoryoutput
The ~2.5 degree clockwise scan skew is corrected so logo edges are trueexpertThe straightened logo and downstream deliverables show level, true horizontal/vertical edges with no residual ~2.5° clockwise rotation (corrected via image_auto_straighten before vectorize)K3qualitybrief
Creative-bonus trailer-wrap hero is a high-impact lime-on-black treatment of the markexpertab_junk_trailerwrap_hero.png renders the AB mark in lime green (~#9ACD32) with a high-contrast black treatment, hi-res, reading as a bold large-scale trailer-wrap hero variationK4qualityoutput
Font/typography usage guide is delivered with a recommended brand font family plus a heading/body pairing and usage notesautoab_junk_font_usage_guide.json contains a recommended brand font family, a heading/body font pairing, and usage notes, produced via font_recommendK1mandatoryoutput
Brand color sheet carries hex, CMYK, and Pantone callouts for the black/lime/gray palette plus font guidanceexpertThe ab_junk_brand_board notes state hex, CMYK, and Pantone values for each of black, lime green, and gray, plus the recommended font guidanceK5mandatorybrief

AO-46   Recreate a Sports Jersey Font (A–Z, 0–9) as Clean Vector SVG / EPS / AI from a Reference Image

Brand/vertical Fashion & ApparelFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 24)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Turn two photographed jersey-font reference sheets (letters A–Z and numbers 0–9) into clean, print-ready vector artwork and deliver SVG + EPS + AI for both sets.

Full brief

We run a custom football/soccer kit shop and need our signature jersey numbering font rebuilt as proper vector artwork for DTF, Flex and Flock production. We are sending two reference images shot off one of our printed jerseys: one sheet showing the full uppercase alphabet A–Z, and a second sheet showing the digits 0–9. The phone shots are slightly tilted, unevenly lit, and have a faint fabric texture and color cast from the jersey weave, so they need cleanup before tracing. We also include a licensed close-up of the jersey collar so you can match the exact ink tone. Deliverables: clean vector recreations of the complete glyph set with consistent stroke widths and even spacing, exported as SVG + EPS + AI for the letter set AND separately for the number set. The vector paths must be tight enough to cut on a Flex/Flock plotter and to separate cleanly for DTF. We hold commercial rights to the original printed artwork; the recreation must be original vector geometry, not extracted from any proprietary font file. Please process the letters and the numbers as two parallel passes off the same cleanup recipe so the two sheets stay visually identical in weight and proportion, and hand back a single organized package.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
letters_AZ_clean.svgvectorClean traced SVG of the full uppercase A–Z glyph sheet, pure black geometry on transparent ground, tight continuous paths suitable for plotter cutting and DTF separation.
numbers_09_clean.svgvectorClean traced SVG of the 0–9 digit sheet, matched stroke weight and proportion to the letters SVG, transparent ground.
letters_AZ.aivectorAdobe Illustrator (.ai) master of the letter set, EXPORTED by the connector from the authored vector built off letters_AZ_clean.svg (single artboard, all glyphs).
numbers_09.aivectorAdobe Illustrator (.ai) master of the number set, exported from the authored vector built off numbers_09_clean.svg.
letters_AZ.eps / numbers_09.epsvectorEPS exports of each set for legacy DTF/Flex/Flock RIP workflows, derived from the same authored .ai vectors.
jersey_font_vector_package.ziparchiveSingle organized package: /letters and /numbers folders each containing the SVG + EPS + AI plus a contact-sheet preview PNG.

Vector glyphs are pure black geometry on a transparent groundautoletters_AZ_clean.svg and numbers_09_clean.svg render as solid black (#000000) glyph paths on a transparent background, with no white fill plate or opaque ground baked inK1mandatoryoutput
Vector paths are clean, tight, continuous geometry suitable for Flex/Flock plotter cutting and DTF separationexpertGlyph outlines are smooth continuous closed paths with no stray nodes, no leftover fabric-weave specks, and no jagged stair-stepping — tight enough to cut on a Flex/Flock plotter and to separate cleanly for DTFK3mandatorybrief
Stroke widths are consistent and inter-glyph spacing is even across each glyph setexpertWithin each set the glyphs share a uniform stroke/limb weight and the spacing between glyphs is even, as required for a coherent jersey fontK3mandatorybrief
The letter set and number set read as one coherent font family (matched stroke weight and proportion)expertnumbers_09 glyphs match letters_AZ glyphs in stroke weight, outline thickness, height and proportion so the two sheets read as one family — the result of applying the identical cleanup recipe to both branchesK3mandatorybrief
Fabric and photo artifacts from the reference shots are fully removed before tracingexpertNo knit-fabric texture, lighting gradient, cool color cast, or ~3-degree tilt survives into the vector output — glyph baselines are level and the ground is clean (straighten + auto-tone + temperature + contrast cleanup applied to both sheets)K3mandatorybrief
The recreation is original traced vector geometry, not extracted from a proprietary font fileexpertGlyph geometry is traced/authored from the two supplied reference sheets (raster -> vectorize -> authored .ai), with no embedded or copied outlines lifted from any commercial/proprietary OTF/TTF fontK7dealbreakerbrief
The optional OTF/TTF font file is honestly treated as out of scope and not falsely claimed as deliveredexpertNo installable OTF/TTF font is claimed as a produced deliverable; if the OTF/TTF bonus is mentioned, it is honestly flagged as out of connector scope per the briefK7qualitybrief

Stroke & fill consistencyuniform weights & fillsminor varianceinconsistent strokes / fills

Scalability & exportvalid vector; scales cleanlyminor export issuerasterized / invalid file

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-47   Redraw Swimming-Pool Photos into Clean Web-Ready Vector Graphics

Brand/vertical General / Cross-Industry Branding & GraphicsFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 29)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Turn three supplied swimming-pool photos into clean, detailed, flat-color vector graphics (SVG + transparent PNG) on a uniform canvas, packaged and shown on a moodboard for the client's website.

Full brief

A pool-design / pool-builder studio needs vector artwork of its swimming pools for the gallery and "Our Builds" pages of a new website. The client supplies three real photographs of completed pools — a freeform lagoon pool, a rectangular infinity-edge pool, and a kidney-shaped residential pool. Each must be "redrawn" as a high-quality, detailed vector image that accurately represents the pool, is visually appealing, and meets the site's clean, flat, modern standard. The freelancer's job per photo: (1) straighten the shot so the waterline and coping are level; (2) crop to a clean composition; (3) isolate the pool-and-deck subject from the busy backyard background; (4) knock the background out to a solid flat field so it does not bleed colored noise into the trace; (5) flatten exposure and contrast and normalize the water/coping/decking colors (so the blues read as one or two clean blues, not a gradient of speckled tones) — this is what lets the vectorizer emit clean, detailed shapes instead of a thousand tiny noisy paths; (6) extend the canvas so all three pieces share one uniform aspect ratio; (7) vectorize the cleaned raster into a crisp SVG; (8) export the genuine vector to web PNG + SVG. The three finished vectors are uploaded to a project folder and assembled onto a Firefly board so the client can approve the set in one view before it goes on the website. The deliverable emerges entirely from the connector chain: one supplied photo is straightened → cropped → subject-selected → background-inverted → solid-filled → tonally flattened → color-normalized → canvas-unified → vectorized → exported, with the same recipe applied to the freeform-lagoon hero piece, and the two remaining pools loaded and pushed through the equivalent prep so the whole gallery is consistent. Output is a uniform set of three flat-color pool vectors (SVG + transparent PNG) plus an approval moodboard.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 29 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
pool_freeform_lagoon_vector.svgvectorClean flat-color SVG of the freeform lagoon pool, detailed but path-economical (water, coping, decking, rock feature as distinct flat fills), on a uniform 4:3 canvas, web-optimized, scalable for the gallery hero.
pool_infinity_rectangular_vector.svgvectorFlat-color SVG of the rectangular infinity pool, same uniform 4:3 canvas and flat-fill style as the hero, grey+deep-blue palette preserved.
pool_kidney_residential_vector.svgvectorFlat-color SVG of the kidney-shaped pool, same uniform 4:3 canvas and style, light-blue+brick palette preserved.
pool_vectors_web_png_set.zipimageThree matching transparent-background web PNGs (one per vector), exported from the cleaned rasters/SVGs at a uniform canvas size for direct drop-in on the website gallery.
pool_gallery_approval_boarddataFirefly Boards deep-link assembling the three finished pool vectors (their uploaded asset URNs) into a single moodboard so the client can approve the gallery set in one view.
