checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Three vector SVG files are delivered, one per supplied pool photo, with the exact required namesautoExactly three .svg files exist named pool_freeform_lagoon_vector.svg, pool_infinity_rectangular_vector.svg, and pool_kidney_residential_vector.svgK1mandatoryoutput
Each delivered SVG is genuine vector artwork, not a raster wrapped in an SVG containerautoEach of the three .svg files parses as valid SVG and contains true vector path/shape elements (e.g. <path>, <polygon>) with no embedded raster bitmap (no <image> element and no base64 raster data URI)K1dealbreakeroutput
All three vectors share one uniform 4:3 canvas aspect ratioautoThe viewBox (or width:height) of all three SVGs is identical and equals a 4:3 ratio (within +/-1% tolerance)K1mandatoryoutput
Each vector is flat-color and path-economical rather than a noisy thousand-path traceexpertEach SVG renders as clean flat-color fills (water, coping, decking, and the pool feature read as a small number of distinct flat regions) with no speckled noise, gradient banding, or excessive fragmentary pathsK3mandatorybrief
Each vector accurately and recognisably represents its source pool's geometryexpertLagoon SVG reads as a freeform irregular-curved lagoon pool, infinity SVG as a rectangular infinity-edge pool, and kidney SVG as a kidney-shaped pool, each faithful to its source photo's shapeK2mandatorybrief
The freeform lagoon vector preserves the tan-travertine + turquoise-blue palette of its source photoexpertLagoon SVG fills read as tan/travertine coping-and-decking with clean turquoise-blue water (one or two blues), faithful to pool_freeform_lagoon.jpg and not a foreign paletteK2qualityinput
The infinity-pool vector preserves the grey + deep-blue palette of its source photoexpertInfinity SVG fills read as dark-grey coping with deep-blue water, faithful to pool_infinity_rectangular.jpg and distinct from the lagoon's tan/turquoiseK2qualityinput
The kidney-pool vector preserves the light-blue plaster + brick-red coping palette of its source photoexpertKidney SVG fills read as light-blue plaster water with brick-red coping, faithful to pool_kidney_residential.jpg and distinct from the other two pools' palettesK2qualityinput
The backyard background is knocked out to a solid flat field in every vectorexpertNone of the three vectors contain traced backyard clutter (fence, rooftop, hillside, sky, lawn, furniture, plants); the pool-and-deck subject sits on a clean flat solid fieldK3mandatorybrief
A ZIP of three web PNGs is delivered, one matching each pool vectorautopool_vectors_web_png_set.zip exists and contains exactly three .png files (one per pool vector)K1mandatoryoutput
Each delivered web PNG has a transparent backgroundautoAll three PNGs in pool_vectors_web_png_set.zip carry an alpha channel and the background region is fully transparent (alpha 0)K1mandatoryoutput
The three web PNGs share a single uniform 4:3 canvas sizeautoAll three PNGs have identical pixel dimensions and that size is 4:3 (within +/-1%)K1mandatoryoutput
A Firefly Boards approval moodboard is delivered as a deep-linkautopool_gallery_approval_board is a valid Firefly Boards deep-link URL that opens a boardK6mandatoryoutput
The approval board assembles all three finished pool vectors in one viewexpertThe Firefly board displays the three pool-vector assets (lagoon, infinity, kidney) together as a single reviewable gallery setK6mandatoryoutput
The three vectors are collected into a single delivery project folder in CC storageautoA delivery project folder exists in CC storage and contains the three finished pool-vector SVG assetsK6qualitybrief
No new pool content is invented; outputs derive only from the three supplied photos via permitted prepexpertEach vector traces back to its supplied source photo (lagoon/infinity/kidney) with no generatively-invented pool, object, or scene element; generative use is limited to outpainting the solid field to the uniform canvasK7dealbreakerbrief

AO-48   Convert an AI-Generated Logo into a Clean, Multi-Platform Vector Kit

Brand/vertical Technology, SaaS & StartupsFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Take the client's AI-generated raster logo, clean and de-skew it, isolate the mark on transparency, trace it into a crisp scalable SVG, and derive a full multi-platform vector + raster export kit that stays high-quality at every size and format.

Full brief

Northwind Analytics generated their logo in AI and only has a flat 1024x1024 PNG of it — a teal-and-charcoal wordmark-plus-icon (a stylized wind/data-flow glyph) sitting on a faint off-white textured card, with the soft anti-aliased edges, slight noise, and a baseline that tips a couple of degrees off level that AI image tools typically produce. They cannot use this raster on their website header, app icon, pitch deck, sign vinyl, or one-color merch because it does not scale and has no transparency. The freelancer must deliver a production vector kit: (1) auto-straighten and tonally clean the raster so edges are sharp and the background is even; (2) tighten the crop to the artwork; (3) isolate just the logo mark from its background card and flatten the background to pure white so the trace is clean; (4) produce a transparent cut-out of the mark; (5) VECTORIZE the cleaned mark into a crisp full-color SVG that scales to any size; (6) derive a single-color / knockout vector (logo silhouette filled solid for one-color print, vinyl, embroidery and small favicons) by selecting the mark, inverting, solid-filling the field, and re-vectorizing; (7) produce a halftone "print-screen" treatment of the mark for merch/poster use; (8) recommend a brand-safe font pairing that matches the wordmark so the client can extend the identity in decks and docs; and (9) export platform-sized PNG rasters (a 1024 app icon, a web header bug, a 512 social avatar) from the cleaned mark and assemble all generated deliverables into a single shareable presentation board with an organized asset folder. Everything must remain high-quality across formats — vectors stay editable/scalable, rasters are crisp at their target sizes.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
northwind_logo_fullcolor.svgvectorTransparent full-color vector trace of the cleaned logo mark, teal+charcoal, clean paths, scalable to any size; primary multi-platform deliverable.
northwind_logo_onecolor_knockout.svgvectorSingle-color knockout/silhouette vector of the mark (solid fill on flattened field) for one-color print, vinyl, embroidery and small-size favicons.
northwind_mark_transparent.pngimagePNG, transparent background, isolated logo mark with edges trimmed to bounds; source for raster exports.
northwind_mark_halftone.pngimageHalftone / print-screen duotone treatment of the mark for merch and poster use.
northwind_icon_1024.png / header_bug / avatar_512.pngimagePlatform-sized crisp PNG exports cropped/resized from the cleaned mark: 1024x1024 app icon, web header bug, and 512x512 social avatar.
font_pairing_recommendationdataRecommended brand-safe font pairing matching the wordmark for extending the identity in decks/docs.
Northwind_brand_kit_board + asset folderdataFirefly Board presentation deep-link assembling all generated deliverables, plus an organized CC asset folder containing every output for client hand-off.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
northwind_logo_fullcolor.svg is delivered as a valid SVG vector file with real paths (not an embedded raster)autoA file named northwind_logo_fullcolor.svg exists, parses as valid SVG markup, and contains vector path/shape elements; it must NOT be a single <image> tag wrapping the raster (i.e. it was genuinely traced via image_vectorize, not the raw PNG re-wrapped)K1dealbreakeroutput
The full-color SVG preserves the source teal-and-charcoal paletteautonorthwind_logo_fullcolor.svg contains fill colors approximating teal #178C8C and charcoal #2B2F33 (within a small tolerance); it is not a single-color or grayscale trace and does not introduce off-palette huesK2mandatorybrand
northwind_logo_onecolor_knockout.svg is a single-color solid charcoal knockout/silhouette vectorautoA file named northwind_logo_onecolor_knockout.svg exists, is valid SVG with vector paths, and uses exactly one solid fill color — a charcoal (~#2B2F33) silhouette with no teal or second hue presentK1mandatoryoutput
northwind_mark_transparent.png is the isolated mark on a transparent background trimmed to boundsautoA file named northwind_mark_transparent.png exists, is a PNG with an alpha channel, has fully transparent pixels at its corners (off-white card removed), and the opaque artwork is tightly cropped to the mark's bounding boxK1mandatoryoutput
northwind_mark_halftone.png is a halftone / print-screen treatment of the markexpertA file named northwind_mark_halftone.png exists and shows a recognizable halftone/print-screen (visible dot-pattern) duotone rendering of the Northwind Analytics mark suitable for merch/poster useK1mandatoryoutput
northwind_icon_1024.png is exported at exactly 1024x1024 pixels from the cleaned markautoA PNG app-icon export named northwind_icon_1024.png exists with pixel dimensions exactly 1024 x 1024, derived from the cleaned transparent markK1mandatoryoutput
avatar_512.png is exported at exactly 512x512 pixels from the cleaned markautoA PNG social-avatar export named avatar_512.png exists with pixel dimensions exactly 512 x 512, derived from the cleaned transparent markK1mandatoryoutput
A web header bug PNG export is delivered in addition to the 1024 icon and 512 avatarautoA third platform-sized PNG raster (the web header bug) is present alongside northwind_icon_1024.png and avatar_512.png, cropped/resized from the cleaned markK1mandatoryoutput
A brand-safe font pairing recommendation matching the wordmark is providedexpertA font_pairing_recommendation names a concrete font pairing (heading + body) that plausibly matches the modern semibold sans-serif Northwind Analytics wordmark for extending the identity in decks/docsK1mandatoryoutput
The supplied raster was de-skewed before tracing so the wordmark baseline is levelexpertThe cleaned mark in the SVG and transparent PNG shows the wordmark baseline horizontal — the ~2.5deg clockwise tilt from northwind_ai_logo_raw.png has been corrected, not still tiltedK3mandatorybrief
The off-white textured/noisy background was cleaned and flattened to even white before isolationexpertThe isolated mark shows no remnant of the faint off-white #F4F2EE textured card, luminance noise, or uneven lighting around the edges; the field behind the mark was flattened to even white before knockoutK3mandatorybrief
The vector trace edges are clean and crisp, not inheriting the AI soft/anti-aliased edges or noiseexpertThe full-color SVG paths read as sharp, smooth, deliberate vector outlines with no jagged, fuzzy, or noise-speckled edges carried over from the soft AI rasterK3qualitybrief
The full-color SVG stays high-quality and legible when scaled to any sizeexpertnorthwind_logo_fullcolor.svg renders crisp with no pixelation at both very small (favicon) and very large (sign/poster) sizes, satisfying the brief's 'remains high-quality across different formats' requirementK4mandatorybrief
The client's logo is faithfully reproduced, not regenerated or altered into a different markexpertThe vectorized mark matches the supplied Northwind Analytics artwork (three stacked curved wind/data-flow lines to the LEFT of the 'Northwind Analytics' wordmark); glyphs, letterforms, kerning, and icon shape are preserved, not redrawn or reinventedK2dealbreakerinput
All six rendered deliverables are collected into an organized CC asset folder for hand-offautoA CC asset folder named Northwind_Vector_Kit exists containing all six rendered outputs: northwind_logo_fullcolor.svg, northwind_logo_onecolor_knockout.svg, northwind_mark_transparent.png, northwind_mark_halftone.png, northwind_icon_1024.png, and avatar_512.pngK6mandatoryoutput
A shareable Firefly Board presentation deep-link assembling all deliverables is producedautoA Firefly Board deep-link (the Northwind brand kit board) is returned that assembles every generated vector and raster deliverable for client reviewK6mandatoryoutput

Layer 2 · Operation craft checklist — Vector & Print (expert marks Pass / Minor / Major vs reference images)

craft itemPassMinorMajor

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-49   Evergreen Cannabis Apparel Capsule — Screen-Print Separation & Vector Production Package

Brand/vertical Cannabis & DispensaryFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 20)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's hand-painted hero graphic and brand logo into a production-ready, screen-print-separable vector + halftone package locked to the Evergreen purple/black/pink palette, plus an exported tech-pack mockup, bundled for the decorator.

Full brief

Evergreen (a CT/OH/MO cannabis retailer) is producing a Fall/Spring 2027 elevated-streetwear capsule sold in-store and via loyalty redemption, and needs the hero front-print graphic handed off to a screen-print decorator in production-ready form. The client supplies three assets: (1) a high-res scan of a hand-painted "Evergreen crest" hero illustration (mountain + lotus motif) shot on textured paper, slightly rotated and with an off-white/warm cast from the scanner; (2) the Evergreen wordmark logo as a flat bitmap PNG; and (3) a subtle grunge paper texture from stock to give the print a worn, premium hand-feel. The contractor must: straighten and tightly crop the scanned hero, correct the warm scanner cast and lift it to clean neutral tone, force the artwork onto the locked brand palette (purple #4B2E83 dominant, black #0B0B0B, intentional accent pink #E8669E only) by collapsing stray colors, isolate the crest from its paper background to a clean transparent subject for separation, convert the cleaned crest to crisp print-ready vector art (the spot-color "separations" a screen shop burns), give a second halftone-screened raster variant of the crest for the distressed-overlay look the brand references (Higher Stitch / Supreme direction), vectorize the supplied wordmark to scalable line art, and finally export a genuine authored tech-pack mockup (.ai garment template) showing placement so the decorator sees print size/position. Everything is bundled into one handoff package for the manufacturer. Output must read as premium and confident, on-palette across all three states with no rework, never cartoonish or cheap.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain apparelDeliverable screen-print-sepsWorkflow EditOperation O7Family Vector & PrintModality vectorHorizon H4Est. calls 20Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[L]Brand Evergreen
4Planning5Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.

6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 20 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
crest_vector_seps.svgvectorClean print-ready vector of the hero crest (transparent), spot-color-friendly flat shapes locked to #4B2E83 / #0B0B0B / #E8669E — the screen-print separation art the decorator burns.
crest_halftone_distress.pngimageHalftone/duotone-screened raster variant of the crest (purple/black) for the distressed-overlay look, transparent PNG, print resolution.
wordmark_vector.svgvectorVectorized Evergreen wordmark, scalable solid-purple line art for embroidery/print handoff.
evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdfpdfExported tech-pack/placement mockup from the authored .ai garment template, showing chest print placement and color callouts.
Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.ziparchiveSingle decorator handoff bundle containing the two vectors, the halftone PNG, the cleaned transparent crest, and the tech-pack PDF.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All five expected deliverables are present in the handoff with their exact names: crest_vector_seps.svg, crest_halftone_distress.png, wordmark_vector.svg, evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf, and Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.zipautoAll 5 named files exist with exactly these filenames and extensions (crest_vector_seps.svg, crest_halftone_distress.png, wordmark_vector.svg, evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf, Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.zip); missing any one failsK1mandatoryoutput
Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.zip is a single archive containing exactly the five required handoff items: crest_vector_seps.svg, crest_halftone_distress.png, wordmark_vector.svg, the cleaned transparent crest, and evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdfautoContainer is a valid .zip; unzipping yields exactly 5 items — the two vectors (crest_vector_seps.svg, wordmark_vector.svg), the halftone PNG (crest_halftone_distress.png), the cleaned transparent crest, and the tech-pack PDF (evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf); fewer items or a non-zip container failsK1mandatoryoutput
crest_vector_seps.svg is a true vector file (SVG path/shape geometry, not an embedded raster)autoFile parses as SVG containing vector <path>/<polygon>/<shape> geometry with no embedded raster <image> bitmap standing in for the crest artworkK1dealbreakeroutput
crest_vector_seps.svg colors are locked to the Evergreen brand palette only: purple #4B2E83, black #0B0B0B, accent pink #E8669EautoEvery fill/stroke color in the SVG maps to #4B2E83, #0B0B0B, or #E8669E within tight tolerance; any off-palette color (e.g. the original hand-mixed magenta/teal accents) failsK2dealbreakerbrand
crest_vector_seps.svg has a transparent background (no opaque paper/cream fill behind the crest)autoSVG has no full-canvas opaque background rectangle; the area outside the mountain/lotus crest subject is transparentK1mandatoryoutput
crest_halftone_distress.png is a transparent PNG rendered as a purple/black halftone-screened variant of the crestautoFile is a PNG with an alpha channel (transparent background) and exhibits a halftone dot/screen pattern using only purple (#4B2E83) and black (#0B0B0B) tones; full-color or opaque-background output failsK1mandatoryoutput
crest_halftone_distress.png is at print resolutionautoImage pixel dimensions / DPI metadata indicate print-grade resolution (>=300 DPI at intended chest-print size), not a low-res screen previewK1qualityoutput
wordmark_vector.svg is a vector file with fill color solid purple #4B2E83 (scalable solid-purple line art)autoFile is SVG with vector <path> geometry (no embedded bitmap) and its fill color is solid purple #4B2E83 within tight toleranceK1mandatoryoutput
evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf is a valid PDF whose color-callout strip references the Evergreen palette hex values #4B2E83, #0B0B0B, and #E8669EautoFile is a valid PDF; OCR of the page recovers the color-callout strip containing the strings #4B2E83, #0B0B0B, and #E8669E (or 4B2E83 / 0B0B0B / E8669E); a PDF missing the color callouts failsK1mandatoryoutput
evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf shows the garment front view with the centered-chest print-placement box and print-size callout, exported from the authored .ai templateexpertPDF page shows the flat garment front view with a labelled centered-chest print-placement box and a print-location/size spec callout (per the authored ~11in-wide centered-chest placement); no placement box or no garment view failsK1mandatoryoutput
The wordmark in wordmark_vector.svg matches the supplied client 'Evergreen' wordmark (not a regenerated, retyped, or font-substituted logo)expertVectorized letterforms preserve the supplied condensed high-contrast serif 'Evergreen' wordmark shapes and proportions; redrawn, retyped, or font-substituted lettering failsK2dealbreakerinput
The scanned hero crest was straightened from its ~3-degree rotation and tightly cropped to the artwork boundsexpertThe crest in the cleaned transparent output and vector sits level with no residual tilt, and the uneven cream paper margins are removed (tight crop to artwork bounds); visible rotation or leftover margin failsK3mandatorybrief
The warm/yellow scanner cast was corrected to a clean neutral tone so the brand purples read trueexpertCleaned crest shows neutral tone with no yellow/warm cast and purples read true rather than muddy; residual warm cast failsK3mandatorybrief
Stray off-palette hand-mixed magenta/teal accents were collapsed onto the locked palette (filled with brand purple #4B2E83)expertNo magenta/teal or other off-palette colors remain in the cleaned crest or vector; the stray accent regions are collapsed to brand purple #4B2E83K2mandatorybrief
The crest is isolated from its paper background to a clean transparent subject suitable for separationsexpertThe cleaned transparent crest has clean, accurate edges with no paper texture, halo, or background fringe remaining around the mountain/lotus crestK3mandatorybrief
The final artwork reads as premium and confident elevated-streetwear, never cartoonish or cheapexpertHolistic review of the vector seps and halftone variant judges them premium and confident on the purple-and-black-forward brand direction; cartoonish, muddy, or cheap output failsK4qualitybrief

AO-51   Vanguard Athletic Logo — Spot-Color Separation, Vectorization & Multi-Format Screen-Print Package

Brand/vertical General / Cross-Industry Branding & GraphicsFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 23)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Refine my ChatGPT-made "Vanguard" athletic badge into clean, separated spot-color screen-print artwork (olive drab / khaki / black / dark grey + digital camo), vectorize it, and deliver it in multiple file formats with spot-color info.

Full brief

I run a youth/athletic "Vanguard" team and I already mocked up our logo set in ChatGPT, but the files are messy raster PNGs I can't actually use. I need them refined and turned into real production artwork I can hand to a screen printer and also post on social. The combined badge has several elements — the "VANGUARD" wordmark (UC), a subdued American flag, a star icon, chevrons, a soldier head mark, and the outer badge ring. For screen printing each of these has to print as a FLAT spot color, so I need every element reduced to a single clean ink from my military palette — olive drab, khaki, black, and dark grey — with the digital-camo swatch I made available as a fill/accent. Right now the ChatGPT export is dull, slightly crooked, has anti-aliased fuzzy color edges, and the colors aren't consistent enough to separate. So: straighten and tidy it, auto-tone/level it so the palette reads true, then go element-by-element and lock each named mark to one solid spot ink (so my printer gets clean separations), pull a true black plate and a knockout, vectorize the cleaned art into scalable vector, and give me a digital-camo accent swatch as a separate vector too. Final delivery: a genuine layered vector source (.ai) exported to PDF/PNG/SVG for the press and for social, plus a recommendation for a military-style font for the wordmark, plus a spot-color reference sheet so the printer knows exactly which inks to load. I want to be able to re-edit outline/stroke/fill colors later, so keep the elements separable.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain genericDeliverable screen-print-sepsWorkflow EditOperation O7Family Vector & PrintModality vectorHorizon H4Est. calls 23Feasibility partialExec mode [C]Brand Vanguard
5Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
vanguard_separations_source.aivectorGenuine layered Adobe Illustrator (.ai) vector source with each named mark (VANGUARD wordmark, subdued flag, star, chevrons, head, badge ring) as a separable element, each locked to a single spot ink from {olive drab, khaki, black, dark grey} so outline/stroke/fill stay re-editable. Authored .ai is the export target (render_vector exports it).
vanguard_badge_vector.svgvectorClean scalable SVG of the cleaned/spot-reduced badge (from image_vectorize), suitable for social and re-coloring.
vanguard_press_export.pdfpdfPress-ready PDF exported from the genuine .ai via document_render_vector (all artboards).
vanguard_social.pngimageHigh-res transparent-or-flat PNG of the finished badge for social media.
vanguard_black_plate.pngimageSingle-color black knockout/plate raster (badge filled flat black on transparent) for the press black separation.
digital_camo_accent.svgvectorVectorized digital-camo accent swatch (from image_vectorize) for use as a repeatable fill/accent.
spot_color_reference.txtdataSpot-color reference note: which solid ink each named element is locked to + recommended military font, packaged with the deliverables.

The digital-camo accent SVG uses only the four military palette colors and matches the supplied swatchexpertdigital_camo_accent.svg is built from olive drab green, khaki tan, dark grey, and black pixel clusters only, matching the supplied digital_camo_swatch.png pattern, with no colors outside that four-ink paletteK2qualityinput
A spot-color reference note named spot_color_reference.txt is delivered and maps each named element to its assigned spot inkautoA file named spot_color_reference.txt exists and explicitly maps each of the six named elements (VANGUARD wordmark, subdued flag, star, chevrons, soldier head, badge ring) to its assigned spot ink from {olive drab, khaki, black, dark grey}K1mandatoryoutput
The spot-color reference note includes a recommended military/stencil-style font for the VANGUARD wordmarkautospot_color_reference.txt names a specific military/stencil-style font recommendation for the VANGUARD wordmarkK1mandatorybrief
The VANGUARD wordmark reads as legible uppercase lettering preserved from the source badgeexpertIn the finished badge artwork the wordmark legibly reads 'VANGUARD' in uppercase, matching the supplied vanguard_badge_chatgpt_raster.png (not regenerated into different text or garbled)K2mandatoryinput
The badge is straightened and tonally corrected so the source tilt and dull contrast are resolvedexpertThe finished badge sits square/upright (the faint ~2-degree source tilt from vanguard_badge_chatgpt_raster.png is corrected) and the palette reads true and crisp with no residual dull low-contrast muddiness or anti-aliased fuzzy color edgesK3qualitybrief
The badge identity from the ChatGPT source is preserved — same six elements, not a regenerated different logoexpertThe finished badge depicts the same combined emblem as the supplied vanguard_badge_chatgpt_raster.png (outer ring, subdued American flag, single five-point star, three downward chevrons, left-facing soldier/minuteman head, VANGUARD wordmark) refined — not an AI-regenerated alternate designK2dealbreakerinput
All seven specified deliverables are present in the handoff packageautoThe delivery folder contains all of: vanguard_separations_source.ai, vanguard_badge_vector.svg, vanguard_press_export.pdf, vanguard_social.png, vanguard_black_plate.png, digital_camo_accent.svg, and spot_color_reference.txtK1mandatoryoutput

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-52   20th-Anniversary folk-art poster screen-print separation pack — approved master vectorized, white underbase + 4 spot-colour halftone plates with dot-gain choke, a grain-textured proof, the authored registration sheet rendered out of Illustrator, and a campus-reference Firefly board

Brand/vertical Nonprofit, Religious &amp; CommunityFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

From the approved folk-art 20th-Anniversary master, produce a print-ready 5-screen separation pack (white underbase + four spot-colour halftone plates with a dot-gain choke), a vectorized hard-edge lettering/line plate, a grain-textured client proof, the registration/separation sheet rendered out of the supplied Illustrator template, and a Firefly board of the campus reference photos for the screen printer.

Full brief

John Paul the Great Academy (a Catholic classical school in Lafayette, Louisiana) has APPROVED the final folk-art / festival-poster illustration celebrating the school's first twenty years — a handcrafted, story-driven composition weaving faith, academics, student life, community traditions, and campus landmarks, rich with discoverable details. The illustration phase is complete and signed off; this engagement is the screen-print PRODUCTION phase. You are given: (1) the approved master artwork as a high-resolution flattened RGB PNG (~24x36 in @300dpi, full-bleed), in which the figurative folk-art scene carries soft tonal passages and the commemorative title/year lettering is hard-edge solid; (2) the campus reference photos that accompanied the creative brief (chapel, oak quad, schoolhouse facade, founding-class photo); and (3) the print shop's authored Illustrator separation/registration sheet template (separations.ai) carrying real artboards, registration marks, and ink-name slots. Deliver a 5-screen spot-colour separation pack for a manual screen print on natural/cream stock: a WHITE UNDERBASE plate (solid fill behind all art), and four spot-ink plates — Marian Blue, Antique Gold, Brick Red, and Ink Black — where the continuous-tone folk-art passages are converted to a registered Ben-Day halftone dot field and CHOKED so dots do not plug on press, while the title lettering and key line-work stay hard-edge solid via a vectorized hold-out plate. Provide a single flattened grain-textured client proof that previews the screen-print "tooth" on cream stock, the registration/separation sheet rendered out of the supplied Illustrator template (PDF + PNG + SVG), and a Firefly Boards moodboard collecting the campus reference photos so the printer can match landmark colours. Every separation must share identical trim and registration so the five screens line up on the press.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain genericDeliverable screen-print-sepsWorkflow EditOperation O7Family Vector & PrintModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 17Feasibility partialExec mode [C]Brand John Paul the Great Academy
5Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom

See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

campus_oak_quad.jpgimagegpt-image-2A candid documentary photo of a school courtyard quad lined with large Southern live oaks draped in Spanish moss, brick
campus_schoolhouse_and_foundingclass.jpgimagegpt-image-2A candid documentary photo of a modest classical schoolhouse facade with a small founding-class group portrait framed an

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
plate_white_underbase.pngimageWhite underbase separation, 24x36in @300dpi, solid fill behind all printable art on transparency — first screen down on cream stock.
plate_marian_blue_halftone.pngimageMarian Blue spot plate: Ben-Day halftone dot field over the tonal folk-art passages, dot-gain choked, lettering held out solid; trim/registration identical to all plates.
plate_antique_gold_halftone.pngimageAntique Gold spot plate, same registered halftone geometry, choked, on its colour build.
plate_brick_red_halftone.pngimageBrick Red spot plate, same registered halftone geometry, choked.
plate_ink_black_lettering.pngimageInk Black plate carrying the vectorized hard-edge title/year lettering and key line-work as solid (no dots) plus the darkest tonal dots.
lettering_lineart.svgvectorVectorized clean SVG of the hard-edge commemorative lettering and key line-work — the hold-out master that keeps type crisp across screens and scales to wall size.
client_proof_grain.pngimageSingle flattened client proof of the assembled look with screen-print grain/tooth on cream stock, for sign-off before burning screens.
separation_registration_sheet.pdfpdfRegistration/separation sheet rendered from the authored separations.ai: all five labelled artboards with crop/reg marks + ink names, plus matching PNG and SVG exports for the printer.
campus_reference_boardurlFirefly Boards deep-link moodboard collecting the four campus reference photos so the printer can colour-match landmarks (chapel brick, oak greens, gold).

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The separation pack contains exactly five plate raster outputs named plate_white_underbase.png, plate_marian_blue_halftone.png, plate_antique_gold_halftone.png, plate_brick_red_halftone.png, and plate_ink_black_lettering.png.autoAll five PNG files are present and named exactly as in expected_outputs; any missing or extra plate file fails.K1mandatoryoutput
Every separation plate shares an identical trim of 24x36 in at 300 dpi (7200x10800 px) so the five screens register on press.autoAll five plate PNGs report identical pixel dimensions of 7200x10800 px (24x36 in @300dpi) and identical canvas extent; any plate differing in pixel size fails.K1dealbreakerbrief
plate_white_underbase.png is a solid (non-halftoned) white fill confined to the printable-art footprint over a transparent background.autoThe underbase plate shows a solid white fill confined to the art footprint with transparent alpha outside the art, no halftone dots, and no spot color; fails if it contains halftone dots, color, or fills the full rectangle including the cream ground.K1mandatoryoutput
plate_marian_blue_halftone.png, plate_antique_gold_halftone.png, and plate_brick_red_halftone.png each render the folk-art tonal passages as a Ben-Day halftone dot field (not continuous tone).autoEach of the three spot plates shows a regular halftone dot pattern across the figurative art region; a plate showing continuous-tone or no visible dot screen fails.K1mandatorybrief
The four spot plates carry their correct named ink builds: plate_marian_blue_halftone reads as deep Marian Blue, plate_antique_gold_halftone as Antique Gold, plate_brick_red_halftone as Brick Red, and plate_ink_black_lettering as Ink Black.expertplate_marian_blue_halftone reads as deep Marian blue, plate_antique_gold_halftone as antique gold, plate_brick_red_halftone as brick red, and the black plate as ink black; a plate keyed to the wrong spot color fails.K1mandatorybrief
The title/year commemorative lettering and key line-work are held out solid (no halftone dots) on each of the four halftone spot plates.expertOn the four spot plates the lettering and key line-work appear as solid holdout regions with no dot screen breaking up the type; lettering rendered as halftone dots fails.K1mandatorybrief
The halftone dot field is dot-gain choked so dots do not plug in shadow areas on absorbent cream stock.expertShadow/dark regions of the halftone field show discrete, slightly reduced dots rather than fully plugged solid black; an unchoked field where shadow dots merge into solid fails.K3qualitybrief
plate_ink_black_lettering.png carries the vectorized hard-edge title/year lettering and key line-work as solid (no dots) plus the darkest tonal halftone dots.expertThe black plate shows the commemorative lettering and line-work as crisp solid black with the deepest-shadow halftone dots present; fails if the lettering is dotted or if the black plate omits the held-out type.K1mandatoryoutput
lettering_lineart.svg is a valid vector SVG of the hard-edge commemorative lettering and key line-work that scales cleanly to wall size.autoFile is a valid SVG containing vector path data tracing the lettering/line-work and not merely an embedded raster; a raster-only or non-SVG output fails.K1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized lettering in lettering_lineart.svg reproduces the approved master's banner title and year copy crisply with correct spelling.expertThe traced lettering legibly matches the approved master's banner title 'JOHN PAUL THE GREAT ACADEMY' and year line '20 YEARS · 2006-2026' with clean hard edges and no misspelling or broken glyphs.K2mandatoryinput
client_proof_grain.png is a single flattened proof of the assembled look with screen-print grain/tooth on cream stock.expertOne flattened PNG shows the combined separations previewed together with visible print grain/tooth texture on a cream/natural ground, suitable for sign-off; fails if it is a bare single plate, lacks grain, or is on plain white.K4mandatoryoutput
separation_registration_sheet.pdf shows all five labelled spot-colour artboards (WHITE UNDERBASE, MARIAN BLUE, ANTIQUE GOLD, BRICK RED, INK BLACK) with crop/registration marks and ink-name slots.autoThe PDF contains five labelled artboards carrying the five exact ink names WHITE UNDERBASE, MARIAN BLUE, ANTIQUE GOLD, BRICK RED, and INK BLACK plus crop/registration marks; a sheet missing an artboard, an ink-name label, or registration marks fails.K1mandatoryoutput
The registration/separation sheet is also delivered as matching PNG and SVG exports alongside separation_registration_sheet.pdf.autoBoth a PNG and an SVG export of the registration sheet are present in addition to separation_registration_sheet.pdf; missing either format fails.K1mandatoryoutput
The registration sheet is derived from the supplied authored separations.ai vector template (exported, not recomposed from scratch).expertArtboard layout, registration marks, and ink-name slots match the supplied separations.ai structure (24x36in trim with 0.125in bleed); a freshly invented layout that ignores the authored template fails.K2dealbreakerinput
campus_reference_board is a reachable Firefly Boards deep-link moodboard collecting the three supplied campus reference photos (campus_chapel.jpg, campus_oak_quad.jpg, campus_schoolhouse_and_foundingclass.jpg).autoA reachable Firefly Boards URL is returned and the board contains the three supplied campus reference images campus_chapel.jpg, campus_oak_quad.jpg, and campus_schoolhouse_and_foundingclass.jpg; fails if fewer than the three campus photos are present or the link is dead.K6mandatoryoutput
The campus reference photos are used only inside the Firefly reference board and are NOT composited or edited into the artwork or any separation plate.expertNone of the five plates, lettering_lineart.svg, or client_proof_grain.png contain content from the campus photos; the photos appear only on the reference board. Any campus photo edited into a deliverable fails.K7mandatorybrief

AO-62   Variable-Data Event Promo Catalog + Price-Tag Merge (Overset-Safe Auto-Fit Template)

Brand/vertical Party, Events & PromotionFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Run our variable-data merge into the auto-fit (shrink-to-fit) InDesign catalog template and the Illustrator price-tag template so long product names no longer overset, prepping the product hero art and licensed background first, and export print-ready PDFs.

Full brief

We print a per-event promotional product catalog and matching shelf price tags, and our records come from a spreadsheet (one row per SKU: product name, blurb, price, event, SKU). Our recurring headache is that when a product name or blurb is long, the merged text overflows the frame and gets clipped on export — InDesign won't shrink it to fit on its own. We have already rebuilt our InDesign catalog template (catalog_template.indd) and our Illustrator price-tag template (pricetag_template.ai) with auto-size / "shrink text to fit frame" enabled on every variable-data frame and real merge fields (<<ProductName>>, <<Blurb>>, <<Price>>, <<SKU>>, <<EventName>>) wired up, so the layout itself is now overset-safe. We need you to: (1) stand up an asset workspace; (2) take in our authored templates, our catalog data (catalog_data.csv, ~24 SKUs), our product hero photo (hero_product.jpg) and our logo (logo_master.png); (3) prep the art that flows into the merge — isolate the product from its messy shot, clean the tone, build a clean bleed-safe background from a licensed stock texture, and vectorize the logo so it stays crisp at any print size; (4) run the data merge into BOTH the catalog .indd (one catalog card per row) and the price-tag .ai (Illustrator Variables, one tag per row); (5) export both as print-ready PDFs (catalog at A4 with bleed, tags at tag size); and (6) hand back a tidy package plus a font recommendation that pairs with our brand. The whole point: long records must render legibly via the template's auto-fit, never clipped.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain events-weddingsDeliverable catalogWorkflow Create+EditOperation O5Family Layout & DataModality documentHorizon H4Est. calls 18Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[T]+[L]Brand PartyWorks
5Planning3Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 18 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
catalog_merged_print.pdfpdfPrint-ready multipage PDF, one A4 portrait catalog card per CSV row (~24 pages), 210x297mm + 3mm bleed, exported from the merged .indd via document_render_layout. Long product names/blurbs rendered shrunk-to-fit (no overset clipping). Prepped product art and bleed-safe background placed.
pricetags_merged.pdfpdfPrint-ready PDF of merged shelf price tags, 70x40mm each, one per CSV row, exported from the merged .ai via document_render_vector. Vectorized logo placed; long names auto-sized.
logo_vector.svgvectorClean vector SVG of the brand wordmark produced by image_vectorize from logo_master.png, used inside the merge so the mark stays crisp at any print size.
product_art_prepped.pngimageBackground-removed, auto-toned, cropped-to-cell product hero PNG (transparent) used as the merge image for the hero SKU.
font_recommendation.txtdataAdobe Fonts pairing recommendation (display + body) that suits the event/party brand, from font_recommend.
merge_package.zipdataSingle zip bundling the two PDFs, the SVG, the prepped product PNG, and the font note for handoff (local packaging).

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 17 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
catalog_merged_print.pdf exists and is a valid PDFautoA deliverable named catalog_merged_print.pdf exists and opens as a valid PDF (kind=pdf)K1mandatoryoutput
Catalog PDF has one A4 portrait card per CSV row (~24 pages)autocatalog_merged_print.pdf page count equals the catalog_data.csv data-row count (~24 SKUs), exactly one page per rowK1mandatoryoutput
Catalog pages are A4 portrait 210x297mm with 3mm bleedautoEach catalog page trim box = 210x297mm portrait and the media/bleed box extends 3mm on all sides (216x303mm total)K1mandatoryoutput
Long merged product names/blurbs render shrunk-to-fit, never overset or clippedexpertOn the catalog page(s) carrying the deliberately long record (e.g. ProductName 'Deluxe Personalised Helium-Filled Foil Number Balloon Bundle (Gold)'), the full text is legible inside its frame with no clipping or overset, demonstrating the template's auto-fit shrink-to-fitK1dealbreakerbrief
Each catalog card's merged ProductName, Blurb, Price, SKU and EventName match the source CSV rowautoFor each catalog page, the ProductName, Blurb, Price, SKU and EventName text matches the corresponding catalog_data.csv row exactly (OCR vs CSV)K1mandatoryinput
pricetags_merged.pdf exists and is a valid PDFautoA deliverable named pricetags_merged.pdf exists and opens as a valid PDF (kind=pdf)K1mandatoryoutput
Price-tag PDF has one 70x40mm tag per CSV rowautopricetags_merged.pdf contains one tag per catalog_data.csv data row (~24), each tag sized 70x40mmK1mandatoryoutput
Price tags carry the correct merged ProductName, Price and SKU per rowautoEach tag's ProductName, Price and SKU text matches its catalog_data.csv row (OCR vs CSV)K1mandatoryinput
Price-tag long product names auto-size to fit without clippingexpertOn the tag carrying the long record (ProductName 'Deluxe Personalised Helium-Filled Foil Number Balloon Bundle (Gold)'), the name is fully legible inside the 70x40mm tag with no clipping or overset, showing the .ai template's auto-size area typeK1dealbreakerbrief
logo_vector.svg is a vector SVG (not an embedded raster) produced from logo_master.pngautoDeliverable logo_vector.svg exists, is valid SVG containing vector path data and does not merely embed a raster bitmap of logo_master.pngK1mandatoryoutput
Vectorized logo preserves the PARTYWORKS wordmark and confetti-burst mark fidelityexpertlogo_vector.svg reads as the 2-colour 'PARTYWORKS' wordmark with the confetti-burst mark, edges crisp and shapes faithful to logo_master.png, with no garbled glyphs or colour driftK2dealbreakerbrand
product_art_prepped.png is background-removed with an alpha channelautoproduct_art_prepped.png is a PNG with a transparency (alpha) channel where the original messy studio tabletop/backdrop is removed and only the foil-balloon product remainsK1mandatoryoutput
Prepped product art shows clean cutout edges and corrected toneexpertproduct_art_prepped.png has clean subject edges (no leftover tabletop/ribbon-background halo) and the gold foil tone is auto-tone corrected and vibrance-lifted versus the raw hero_product.jpgK3qualityoutput
Catalog background is a licensed Adobe Stock texture extended bleed-safeexpertThe catalog card background is the licensed festive bokeh/confetti stock texture, outpainted/cropped to fill the full 216x303mm bleed box with no edge gaps or repeated seamsK3mandatorybrief
Stock background was licensed before any editingexpertThe background asset is a properly licensed stock download (asset_license_and_download_stock performed before image_generative_expand), not an unlicensed comp or the bg_texture_ref reference imageK7mandatorybrief
font_recommendation.txt names an Adobe Fonts display + body pairingautofont_recommendation.txt exists and names a specific Adobe Fonts display typeface and a body typeface suited to the party/event brandK5mandatoryoutput
merge_package.zip is a valid zip bundling all required handoff deliverablesautomerge_package.zip opens as a valid zip and contains catalog_merged_print.pdf, pricetags_merged.pdf, logo_vector.svg, product_art_prepped.png and font_recommendation.txtK6mandatoryoutput

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-63   Maple Cricket League — a streetwear cricket league: vectorize the logo system, build the sublimation/screen-print kit artwork, and grade the brand photography into a production-ready package

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Turn Maple Cricket League's already-designed raster logo sketches and brand photos into a production-ready package: clean vector marks, a halftone sublimation/screen-print kit treatment, cohesive streetwear color-grade, a cut-out product shot, an outpainted banner, and an exported, packaged deliverable set.

Full brief

Maple Cricket League (Backyard Cricket League Canada) is a streetwear-forward cricket league for the South Asian and Caribbean diaspora in the GTA, with an aesthetic in the lane of Rastah, OVO, Aimé Leon Dore, and IPL. The concept is fully developed; the client needs execution and production-ready, organized, named files. You are the creative director executing the Adobe production-art thread. The client supplies: (a) a flat raster scan/mockup of the full logo system art (crest + monogram + hat badge laid out together) on a plain background; (b) an authored Illustrator master file (.ai) of the same three marks set up on three artboards, ready to export; (c) a hero brand photo of a street cricket scene shot in mixed/overcast GTA light; (d) a studio-style photo of a folded home jersey on a plain seamless backdrop for the e-commerce/lookbook cut-out; (e) a flat photo of the away-kit chest crest graphic to be turned into a single-color screen-print/sublimation separation. Produce, by chaining Adobe connector tools: (1) a clean, scalable VECTOR of the logo system from the raster scan (raster→SVG), then an exported vector master from the supplied authored .ai for the manufacturer; (2) a one-color HALFTONE screen-print / sublimation treatment of the away-kit crest, straightened, cropped to the print bounds, and converted to a print-ready halftone separation; (3) a cohesive streetwear COLOR GRADE of the hero brand photo (auto-tone baseline → exposure/contrast → split-tone-style monochromatic warmth → grain) so the photography matches the OVO/ALD reference; (4) a transparent-background PRODUCT CUT-OUT of the folded jersey for the lookbook; (5) an outpainted wide BANNER crop of the graded hero for the site/social header (canvas extended, not cropped). Finish by packaging all exported deliverables into one organized, named delivery folder/zip. Hard rules: stay production-faithful — no inventing new logo concepts, no from-scratch art, no text-to-image. The vector export must come from the genuine authored .ai the client supplies. Every step after each asset load must consume a prior step's output.

5Planning4Brand strictness4Tool diversity3Visual density2Creative freedom

See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

away_crest_graphic.jpgimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat, head-on photo of the MCL away-kit chest crest printed on light fabric: a single bold emblem — crossed cricket ba

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 18 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
MCL_logo_system_traced.svgvector/SVGClean vectorized trace of the supplied logo-system raster (crest + monogram + hat badge), flat paths, two ink colors preserved, infinitely scalable for manufacturer/embroidery digitizing.
MCL_logo_master_export.pdfvector-export/PDF+PNGExported render of the client's authored 3-artboard .ai master (all artboards together) as print-res PDF plus a PNG proof; this is the production logo handoff.
MCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.pngimage/PNGAuto-straightened, cropped-to-bounds, one-color halftone screen-print / sublimation separation of the away-kit crest, ready for the manufacturer's single-screen output.
MCL_hero_graded.jpgimage/JPEGStreetwear color-graded hero brand photo: auto-toned, exposure/contrast balanced, warm monochromatic split-tone, fine grain — matches the OVO/ALD/IPL reference look.
MCL_jersey_cutout.pngimage/PNG (transparent)Folded home-jersey product shot with background removed to transparency for the lookbook/e-commerce grid.
MCL_hero_banner_wide.jpgimage/JPEGWide site/social banner generated by AI-outpainting the graded hero to extended canvas (content extended, original not cropped).
MCL_brand_delivery.ziparchive/ZIPOne organized, named delivery folder zipping all exports above into the production handoff structure the brief requires.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All seven required deliverables are present in the packageautoThe delivery contains exactly these seven named files: MCL_logo_system_traced.svg, MCL_logo_master_export.pdf, MCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.png, MCL_hero_graded.jpg, MCL_jersey_cutout.png, MCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg, and MCL_brand_delivery.zipK1mandatoryoutput
Each deliverable is in the file format/colour-mode the spec namesautoMCL_logo_system_traced.svg is a valid SVG vector file; MCL_logo_master_export.pdf is a valid PDF accompanied by a PNG proof; MCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.png and MCL_jersey_cutout.png are valid PNG; MCL_hero_graded.jpg and MCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg are valid JPEG; MCL_brand_delivery.zip is a valid ZIP archiveK1dealbreakeroutput
The traced logo SVG is genuine scalable vector geometry derived from the supplied raster, not an embedded rasterautoMCL_logo_system_traced.svg contains real <path> vector geometry and no embedded raster <image>/bitmap element (the file is a true raster->SVG trace of logo_system_raster.png, not a wrapped bitmap)K1mandatoryoutput
The traced SVG reproduces all three marks from the source logo sheetexpertMCL_logo_system_traced.svg reproduces all three marks present in logo_system_raster.png: the circular MCL crest, the interlocking MCL monogram, and the simplified hat badgeK2mandatoryinput
The traced SVG preserves the two ink colours of the source logoexpertThe vectorized trace renders in two flat ink colours matching the source (deep forest green and cream) with clean flat paths, and introduces no extra colours or gradientsK2qualitybrand
The logo master PDF is an export of the client's authored 3-artboard .ai, not a regenerated or newly composed logoexpertMCL_logo_master_export.pdf renders all three artboards (MCL circular crest, interlocking MCL monogram, simplified hat badge) from logo_system_master.ai together, with no new/invented logo concepts and no from-scratch redrawing of the marksK2dealbreakerinput
The away-crest halftone output is auto-straightened and cropped tight to the print boundsexpertMCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.png shows the crest rotation-corrected (no longer ~3 degrees off straight, matching away_crest_graphic.jpg's intentional skew) and cropped to the crest graphic's bounds with the surrounding near-white jersey panel removedK1mandatoryoutput
The away-crest output is a single-colour halftone screen-print/sublimation separationexpertMCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.png is one-colour with a visible halftone dot pattern, ready as a single-screen print/sublimation separation, with no full-colour tones, gradients, or multi-inkK3mandatoryoutput
The graded hero photo received the full streetwear grade chain in orderexpertMCL_hero_graded.jpg reflects, over the ungraded hero_street_cricket.jpg, an auto-tone baseline, an exposure/contrast punch, a warm monochromatic split-tone tint, and fine film grain — reading visibly warmer, more contrasted, and less flat than the cool/underexposed inputK4mandatorybrief
The graded hero matches the named OVO/Aimé Leon Dore/IPL reference lookexpertThe grade reads as cohesive warm streetwear photography consistent with the OVO/Aimé Leon Dore/IPL aesthetic reference, not a neutral/flat result or an over-stylized filterK4qualitybrief
The jersey cut-out has a fully transparent backgroundautoMCL_jersey_cutout.png has a transparent alpha channel where the light-grey seamless backdrop of home_jersey_flat.jpg was, retaining only the folded jersey subjectK3mandatoryoutput
The jersey cut-out edge quality is clean enough for the e-commerce/lookbook gridexpertThe cut-out edge around the folded jersey is clean with no leftover grey backdrop halo, no clipped fabric, and intact knit/fold detailK3qualityoutput
The wide banner is produced by outpainting the graded hero (canvas extended, original content not cropped)expertMCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg is wider than MCL_hero_graded.jpg with the original framing fully retained and new generated content extending the scene at the sides, not a crop or re-frame of the heroK3mandatoryoutput
The banner is derived from the already-graded hero, preserving the gradeexpertMCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg carries the same warm graded look as MCL_hero_graded.jpg (the outpaint extends the graded frame, not the raw input), with the extended side region tonally matching the originalK3qualityoutput
No from-scratch art or text-to-image generation was used for the logo deliverablesexpertThe logo deliverables originate solely from the raster trace (image_vectorize) and the authored .ai export (document_render_vector); there is no invented new logo concept, no text-to-image-generated mark, and no compositing/gen-fill beyond the single permitted generative outpaint (the banner)K7dealbreakerbrief
Final ZIP is organized into the named production-handoff folder structureautoMCL_brand_delivery.zip contains all six exports sorted into named production-handoff subfolders (e.g. logos/, kits/, photography/), not a flat unnamed dumpK6mandatorybrief

AO-64   Premium skincare packaging: retouch hero shot, vectorize logo, render authored dieline + data-merge SKU labels, print-ready PDFs

Brand/vertical Beauty, Cosmetics & Personal CareFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Take Aurelia Botanics' supplied hero serum photo, logo, authored carton dieline (.ai) + label (.indd) and SKU data, and produce retouched/CMYK-graded imagery, a clean vector logo, data-merged print-ready label PDFs for all 3 SKUs, and high-res mockup assets.

Full brief

Aurelia Botanics is launching a premium 3-SKU facial-serum line (Rosehip Renewal, Niacinamide Clarity, Hyaluronic Hydra) and needs retail-ready packaging production. The client provides authored production files (they built the carton dieline in Illustrator and the label layout in InDesign with real merge fields) plus raw brand assets. Your job is the connector-doable production pipeline: 1) Upload all supplied assets (hero product photo, logo, carton dieline .ai, label .indd, brand-guide PDF, SKU CSV) into a working folder. 2) Clean up the hero serum-bottle photograph: auto-straighten, auto-tone, then lift exposure and warm the color temperature so the amber glass reads premium and consistent under the studio lighting. 3) Apply a Lightroom "Subject Pop" style preset to make the bottle separate cleanly from the seamless backdrop. 4) Knock the retouched bottle out of its background to a transparent PNG so it can drop onto any mockup backdrop. 5) License and download a marble-surface stock backdrop, and crop/resize it to the exact mockup canvas. 6) Build the botanical side-panel texture: take the supplied leaf-motif art and convert it to a 2-color halftone/duotone in brand green so it prints clean as a spot-style panel. 7) Vectorize the supplied raster logo into a clean SVG for the carton. 8) Export the client's authored carton dieline (.ai) to print PDF + a preview PNG (this is the structural cutting layout — connector EXPORTS it, does not author it). 9) Data-merge the 3 SKU rows from the CSV onto the client's authored label template (.indd with <<product_name>>, <<key_actives>>, <<volume>>, <<batch>> fields) to produce one print-ready label per SKU. 10) Export the merged label document to a CMYK-ready, bleed-correct print PDF. 11) Convert the supplied brand-guide PDF into an editable .indd so the studio can keep the spec sheet in InDesign. 12) Assemble the produced hero PNG, marble backdrop, duotone panel, vector logo, dieline preview and label previews onto a Firefly board as the client-facing concept presentation. Deliverables: retouched + background-removed hero PNG, marble mockup backdrop, brand-green duotone side panel, vector logo SVG, exported carton dieline PDF/PNG, 3 data-merged label print PDFs, an editable brand-guide .indd, and a Firefly concept board link.

5Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.

7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_bottle_retouched_cutout.pngimageBackground-removed transparent PNG of the straightened, auto-toned, exposure-/temperature-corrected, Subject-Pop-graded amber serum bottle; full hero resolution (~4000x5000 region), ready to drop onto any mockup.
marble_mockup_backdrop.jpgimageLicensed marble-surface stock backdrop cropped/resized to the 3000x3000px mockup canvas.
leaf_duotone_panel.pngimageBrand-green 2-color halftone/duotone side-panel texture derived from the supplied leaf motif, print-clean.
aurelia_logo.svgvectorClean vectorized SVG of the AURELIA BOTANICS wordmark + leaf mark, single solid spot color, scalable for the carton.
carton_dieline_print.pdfpdfPrint PDF exported from the authored carton dieline .ai with cut/fold lines, 3mm bleed, registration marks, CMYK; plus a preview PNG.
labels_merged_print.pdfpdfOne print-ready, 3mm-bleed, CMYK label page per SKU (3 pages) produced by data-merging skus.csv onto the authored label .indd.
brand_guide_editable.inddinddEditable InDesign document (.indd, delivered zipped) converted from the supplied 4-page brand-guide PDF.
aurelia_concept_boardboardFirefly Board deep-link assembling the hero cutout, marble backdrop, duotone panel, vector logo, dieline preview and label previews as the client-facing concept presentation.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The hero deliverable hero_bottle_retouched_cutout.png is a PNG with a real transparent (alpha) background, not a flat opaque imageautoFile hero_bottle_retouched_cutout.png is a valid PNG that contains an alpha channel with fully transparent pixels surrounding the serum-bottle subjectK1mandatoryoutput
The hero cutout shows the full retouch chain was applied: straightened framing, lifted exposure, warmer color temperature, and a Subject-Pop separation on the amber serum bottleexpertBottle reads visibly straightened/level, brighter than a neutral baseline with a warm amber cast, and pops cleanly off the original seamless light-grey sweep, consistent with auto_straighten -> auto_tone -> exposure -> warmer temperature -> Subject Pop presetK3qualitybrief
The background knockout edge quality on the hero cutout is clean around the amber glass, matte-black dropper cap, and refraction/specular edgesexpertCutout mask follows the bottle silhouette with no halo, no leftover grey backdrop, and no chewed glass edges; semi-transparent glass refraction is handled plausiblyK3qualitybrief
The marble mockup backdrop deliverable marble_mockup_backdrop.jpg is exactly 3000x3000 pixelsautomarble_mockup_backdrop.jpg has pixel dimensions width==3000 and height==3000K1mandatoryoutput
The marble backdrop is a genuinely licensed Adobe Stock marble-surface image (licensed before editing), not a generated or watermarked compexpertBackdrop depicts a real marble surface and carries no stock watermark, consistent with license-then-download of a licensed Adobe Stock assetK2mandatorybrief
The leaf side-panel deliverable leaf_duotone_panel.png is a 2-color halftone/duotone in brand forest-green, derived from the supplied leaf_motif.png artexpertPanel is a two-tone (forest-green + ground) halftone/duotone of the supplied eucalyptus/rosehip leaf motif with no full-color photographic content and no extra colorsK2mandatoryoutput
The logo deliverable aurelia_logo.svg is a valid vector SVG containing path/vector geometry, not an embedded raster bitmapautoaurelia_logo.svg parses as SVG and contains <path>/vector elements; it does not consist solely of an embedded <image>/base64 rasterK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo reproduces the AURELIA BOTANICS serif wordmark plus the leaf-sprig mark in a single solid spot color, faithful to the supplied aurelia_logo_raster.png and not redrawn or alteredexpertSVG renders the 'AURELIA BOTANICS' high-contrast serif wordmark and the leaf-sprig mark matching aurelia_logo_raster.png shape-for-shape in one solid color with clean closed paths; no new glyphs, ornaments, or color changes introducedK2dealbreakerinput
The carton dieline is delivered as a print PDF (carton_dieline_print.pdf) plus a preview PNG, exported from the client-authored aurelia_carton_dieline.ai and not authored from scratchautocarton_dieline_print.pdf exists alongside a preview PNG of the same dieline, and the PDF page corresponds to the supplied 40x40x120mm folding-carton dieline structureK1mandatoryoutput
The exported carton dieline PDF preserves the authored structural print elements: cut lines, fold/score lines, 3mm bleed and registration marks, in CMYKexpertDieline PDF shows distinct cut lines and fold/score lines, registration marks, and a 3mm bleed margin, and is CMYK, matching the authored aurelia_carton_dieline.ai it was exported fromK1mandatorybrief
The merged label deliverable labels_merged_print.pdf contains exactly 3 label pages, one per SKUautolabels_merged_print.pdf has exactly 3 pagesK1mandatoryoutput
Each of the 3 label pages carries the correct merged SKU data bound from skus.csv onto the product_name/key_actives/volume/batch fieldsautoThe 3 pages show, one per page: 'Rosehip Renewal Facial Serum' / 'Rosehip Oil + Vitamin C' / '30 ml' / 'AB-RR-0426'; 'Niacinamide Clarity Facial Serum' / '10% Niacinamide + Zinc' / '30 ml' / 'AB-NC-0426'; 'Hyaluronic Hydra Facial Serum' / '2% Hyaluronic Acid + B5' / '30 ml' / 'AB-HH-0426'K1dealbreakerinput
The label print PDF is a CMYK, 3mm-bleed print-ready file with no leftover literal <<field>> placeholdersexpertlabels_merged_print.pdf is CMYK with a 3mm bleed, and no unmerged '<<product_name>>'/'<<key_actives>>'/'<<volume>>'/'<<batch>>' placeholder text remains on any pageK1mandatoryoutput
The labels preserve the authored typographic hierarchy (product name large serif, actives medium, volume+batch small) and brand green/cream swatchesexpertOn each label the product name is the largest serif element, key_actives is medium, volume and batch are smallest, rendered with brand forest-green/cream swatches per the authored aurelia_label_template.inddK3qualitybrief
The brand-guide deliverable brand_guide_editable.indd is an editable InDesign document (delivered zipped) converted from the supplied 4-page brand_guide.pdfautoDeliverable is a ZIP containing an editable .indd (or .idml) document derived from the 4-page brand_guide.pdfK1mandatoryoutput
A Firefly concept board (aurelia_concept_board) deep-link is delivered assembling the six produced assets: hero cutout, marble backdrop, duotone panel, vector logo, dieline preview and label preview(s)autoA working Firefly Board link resolves to a board containing the 6 produced assets: hero_bottle_retouched_cutout, marble_mockup_backdrop, leaf_duotone_panel, aurelia_logo.svg, the carton dieline preview, and the label preview(s)K6mandatoryoutput

craft itemPassMinorMajor
