6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness ★A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-67   Tamil Traditional Service-Promotion Large-Format Banner Set — Print-Prep & Source-File Production (6 sizes)

Brand/vertical General / Cross-Industry Branding & GraphicsFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Take my supplied Tamil banner master files plus hero photography and brand logo, and produce the complete print-ready production package — graded hero image with print bleed, a clean vector logo, a traditional halftone texture motif, and CMYK 150-dpi PDF/JPEG exports of all six large-format banners — with editable source files alongside.

Full brief

I run a traditional Tamil service business and I'm rolling out a promotion across six printed banners (design already laid out by my designer): 10x10 ft (3 pieces), 10x20 ft (2 pieces), 10x6 ft (1 piece). All copy is in Tamil and the look must stay clearly traditional — warm, festive, gold/maroon tones, eye-catching from a distance. I will supply you: (1) my authored Illustrator master files for the banners with all Tamil typography already set correctly in licensed Tamil fonts (do NOT re-typeset or translate — typography is locked), (2) one wide hero photograph of my premises/service scene that needs heavy correction and a warm traditional grade, (3) my brand logo as a flat raster file that I need cleaned up into crisp vector for large print, and (4) a low-res PDF proof of last year's banner that I want converted back into an editable layout source. What I need from you, all on the Adobe production side: - Fix and grade the hero photo: straighten it, auto-correct tone, warm it up, push the gold/maroon vibrance, pull back blown highlights, then isolate the main service element so its colour stays controlled. - The hero photo prints right to the edge, so I need 1 inch of real bleed added around it for the largest 10x20 ft layout — extend the image content into the bleed, don't just pad it. - Turn my flat logo into a clean scalable vector so it stays sharp at 10-foot height. - Build me a traditional half-tone / screen-print texture motif derived from the graded hero so it reads as a printed-poster aesthetic up close. - Export each of the six authored banner masters as a flattened high-res raster and as a print PDF — CMYK intent, 150 dpi minimum at full scale, 1 inch bleed all around. - Convert my old PDF proof into an editable InDesign source so I can reuse last year's layout. - Hand back a single packaged folder with every final and source file organised. Acceptance: traditional consistent look across all sizes, no damage to the locked Tamil typography, and print-ready files meeting CMYK / 150 dpi / 1-inch-bleed specs. One round of minor revisions expected after I review the first draft.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_graded_bleed.pngimageCorrected + warm-traditional-graded hero photograph (straightened, auto-toned, highlights recovered, gold/maroon vibrance pushed, controlled subject color) with 1 inch of real outpainted bleed on all sides for the 10x20 ft layout. High-res PNG, sRGB working copy for placement into the authored master.
brand_logo_vector.svgvectorClean scalable SVG vectorization of the flat brand logo, sharp at 10-foot print height, solid maroon/gold fills, Tamil emblem preserved.
halftone_texture_motif.pngimageTraditional screen-print / halftone texture motif derived from the graded hero, for the close-up printed-poster aesthetic. High-res PNG.
banner_10x20_print.pdf + .pngpdfPrint export of the authored 10x20 ft master: PDF plus flattened high-res PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
banner_set_exports.zipdataPackaged folder containing print PDF + high-res PNG exports for all six authored banner masters (3x 10x10, 2x 10x20, 1x 10x6), the editable .indd converted from last year's proof, the vector logo, the graded hero, and the halftone motif — every final and source file organised in one deliverable.

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom

The traditional warm gold/maroon festive look is consistent across all six sizesexpertAll six banner exports and the graded hero read as a coherent traditional Tamil festive set — warm gold/maroon tones, ornamental treatment, eye-catching from a distance — with no inconsistent grading across sizesK4qualitybrief

AO-68   Roadside Coaching-Institute Banner: brand-graded hero, vector icon set, halftone geometry + print-ready CMYK PDF export

Brand/vertical Nonprofit, Religious & CommunityFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Prepare every visual asset for the DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES 10ft x 5ft roadside banner — brand-graded student hero, vector subject icons, brand-colour halftone geometry — and export the authored InDesign layout to a print-ready CMYK PDF for large-format flex printing.

Full brief

DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES needs a clean, premium, professional roadside banner (10 ft x 5 ft, 2:1) that stays readable from the road and presents the course/contact info without clutter. The brand palette is primary #046CB8 and secondary #02ADEF, headings/key numbers in bold Inter, with subject-hinting icons, a confident-but-minimal photo of students, and understated geometric elements. The freelancer has already authored the layered banner in InDesign with every line of verbatim copy (institute name, tagline "Where Learning Meets Success", the Pre-Foundation / 9th-10th / 11th-12th course list, "ADMISSIONS OPEN", the three phone numbers with 8103332901 most prominent, the "Railway Station Road, Multai" location, and the four feature bullets). That authored layout (.indd) plus a raw, slightly-tilted DSLR photo of students, a flat icon sheet, and the brand-color swatch file are the client inputs.\n\nYour Adobe production job, end to end:\n1. Stand up the project folders and load/preview the raw student photo and the icon sheet.\n2. Fix the photo geometry: auto-straighten the tilted horizon, then crop to the banner hero band aspect.\n3. Correct and grade it to the brand: auto-tone for a clean baseline, then warm/cool the white balance and push the dominant tones toward the #046CB8 / #02ADEF brand blues via a color overlay and a targeted single-colour saturation lift so the photo "matches the specs".\n4. Produce a clean placement plate: select the students as the subject and remove the background to a transparent cutout the banner can drop onto its geometric field.\n5. Extend the graded photo to the full 2:1 banner hero ratio with generative expand (outpaint) so there is no pixelation or awkward crop at full print size.\n6. Turn the flat icon sheet into crisp, infinitely-scalable print vectors (vectorize → SVG) so the subject icons stay sharp on a 10-foot banner.\n7. Build the "understated geometric element": take the brand-blue field and render a halftone/duotone texture in the brand colours for a modern, minimal accent.\n8. Export the client-authored InDesign banner layout to the final print-ready, high-resolution CMYK PDF for large-format flex printing, and also export a JPEG proof.\n9. Assemble the graded hero, the cutout plate, the vector icons, the halftone texture and the PDF/JPEG proof onto a single Firefly board so the client can sign off that the file opens cleanly, the colours match, and the hierarchy is crystal clear at full size.\n\nThe client signs off once the PDF opens cleanly, colours match #046CB8/#02ADEF in CMYK, and the text hierarchy is legible at full 10ft x 5ft size.

Domain professional-servicesDeliverable bannerWorkflow EditOperation O5Family Layout & DataModality documentHorizon H4Est. calls 18Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand Dynamic Solution Classes
4Planning5Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Brand Alignment · Technical Quality · Process. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 18 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
students_hero_brandgraded_2x1.pngimageStraightened, cropped, auto-toned, white-balanced and brand-graded student hero photo pushed toward #046CB8/#02ADEF, outpainted to the full 2:1 (10ft x 5ft) banner hero band, full print resolution, no pixelation.
students_cutout_transparent.pngimageBackground-removed transparent-PNG cutout of the student group for clean placement onto the banner's geometric field.
subject_icons.svgvectorBrand-blue subject icon set vectorized to clean scalable SVG paths that stay crisp at 10-foot print size.
brand_halftone_texture.pngimageModern, minimal halftone/duotone geometric texture in the #046CB8/#02ADEF brand colours for the understated background accent.
DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdfpdfPrint-ready, high-resolution CMYK PDF of the authored banner layout, 10ft x 5ft, exported for large-format flex printing — the primary client deliverable.
DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_proof.jpgimageFlattened JPEG proof of the banner layout for quick on-screen client review.
DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_signoff_boarddataFirefly board deep-link assembling the graded hero, cutout plate, vector icons, halftone texture and the PDF/JPEG proof for client sign-off.

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The print-ready CMYK PDF deliverable DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdf exists and is a valid PDFautoA file named DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdf is delivered and opens cleanly as a valid PDF documentK1mandatoryoutput
The print PDF colour space is CMYKautoDYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdf reports a CMYK (DeviceCMYK / ICC CMYK) colour space, not RGB, matching the brief's 'Print-ready PDF in CMYK' requirementK1dealbreakerbrief
The banner uses the 10ft x 5ft (2:1) aspect ratio at print scaleautoDYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdf page geometry is 2:1 (width:height = 10ft:5ft), within a small toleranceK1mandatorybrief
The print PDF is high-resolution suitable for large-format flex printing with no pixelationexpertAt full 10ft x 5ft size the exported PDF shows no visible pixelation, blurring or upscaling artefacts in the hero photo, icons or texture, satisfying the brief's 'high resolution suitable for large-format flex printing (no pixelation)'K4mandatorybrief
The brand-graded student hero deliverable students_hero_brandgraded_2x1.png is delivered as a 2:1 PNGautostudents_hero_brandgraded_2x1.png is delivered as a PNG whose aspect ratio is 2:1 (the full 10ft x 5ft banner hero band produced by the outpaint/generative-expand step)K1mandatoryoutput
The student hero photo is graded toward the brand palette #046CB8 / #02ADEFexpertThe dominant tones of students_hero_brandgraded_2x1.png are visibly pushed toward the brand blues #046CB8 (primary) and #02ADEF (secondary) without unnatural skin tonesK2mandatorybrand
The student hero horizon is straightened and the crop is cleanexpertThe tilted horizon from the raw handheld DSLR photo is corrected to level and the hero is cropped to the banner hero band with no awkward cuts of the studentsK3qualitybrief
The student cutout deliverable students_cutout_transparent.png is a background-removed transparent PNGautostudents_cutout_transparent.png is delivered as a PNG with a real alpha channel and the original background fully removed to transparencyK1mandatoryoutput
The student cutout has clean mask edges with no background fringingexpertEdges of the student group in students_cutout_transparent.png (hair, shoulders, notebook edges) are cleanly masked with no halo, fringe or leftover backgroundK3qualityoutput
The subject icon set deliverable subject_icons.svg is true scalable vector outputautosubject_icons.svg is delivered as a valid SVG containing vector <path> geometry (not an embedded raster image)K1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized set contains the six subject icons, stays crisp at 10-foot print size, and is in brand blueexpertsubject_icons.svg contains the six subject icons (beaker, sigma/maths, globe, open book, DNA helix, balance scale) as clean closed brand-blue (#046CB8) line shapes that stay sharp when scaled to large-format print, with no raster blurK2qualityinput
The halftone texture deliverable brand_halftone_texture.png exists in the brand coloursautobrand_halftone_texture.png is delivered as a PNG rendering a halftone/duotone geometric texture using the #046CB8 / #02ADEF brand coloursK1mandatoryoutput
The halftone texture reads as an understated, modern, minimal accentexpertbrand_halftone_texture.png reads as a subtle understated geometric accent (not a loud or busy pattern), consistent with the brief's 'modern, minimalistic feel' and 'understated geometric elements'K4qualitybrief
The JPEG proof deliverable DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_proof.jpg is a flattened proof of the banner layoutautoDYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_proof.jpg is delivered as a flattened JPEG showing the full banner layout for on-screen reviewK1mandatoryoutput
All verbatim banner copy is present and legible in the exported layoutexpertThe exported PDF/proof shows, legibly, the institute name DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES, tagline 'Where Learning Meets Success', the Pre-Foundation / 9th-10th / 11th-12th course list, 'ADMISSIONS OPEN', the three phones (8103332901, 8989871223, 9589110600), 'Railway Station Road, Multai', and the four feature bullets (Small Batch Size / Personal Attention / Regular Tests / Doubt Sessions)K1dealbreakerbrief
Text hierarchy is crystal clear at full size with 8103332901 most prominent and headings in bold InterexpertAt full 10ft x 5ft size the headings/key numbers read in bold Inter, the phone number 8103332901 is the most prominent of the three phones, and the overall hierarchy is legible from a distance (per the client sign-off bar 'text hierarchy is crystal clear at full size')K3mandatorybrief
The Firefly sign-off board deliverable DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_signoff_board assembles all review assetsautoDYNAMIC_SOLUTION_signoff_board is a Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded hero, cutout plate, vector icons, halftone texture and the JPEG proof for client sign-offK6mandatoryoutput

craft itemPassMinorMajor

Image placement & cropaligned, well-cropped, correct aspectslightly loose cropstretched / mis-cropped
Balance & whitespacebalanced; whitespace used wellslightly heavy / sparsecramped or empty

checkPassMinorMajorfeeds

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness ★A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-69   TitanSeal Sealants Launch — Logo-System Extension, Swappable SKU Label/Carton Data-Merge, and Print-Ready + Digital Asset Package

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 22)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Take the approved TitanSeal master logo plus the client's authored InDesign label .indd and Illustrator carton .ai mechanicals, derive the full logo-variant system, data-merge the three SKUs into swappable print-ready label/carton artwork, and output the print PDFs plus a correctly-sized digital-asset pack.

Full brief

TitanSeal is a Dubai-based B2B private-label sealants brand launching three SKUs in January 2027. The master logo is approved and supplied as a high-res transparent PNG; the client also supplies two desktop-authored print mechanicals carrying real merge fields/variables (an InDesign wrap label .indd and an Illustrator carton .ai) and a sku_variants.csv with the three SKUs (600ml MS Polymer grey, 600ml PU Construction grey, 300ml Neutral Silicone white). Using ONLY the Adobe connector, the freelancer must: (1) upload the supplied logo and mechanicals into a project workspace; (2) build the LOGO-VARIANT SYSTEM from the one master logo — a clean vector SVG (for scalable lockups), a single-color BLACK mono version and a single-color WHITE reversed/knockout version (for the black-on-cardboard carton and dark backgrounds), and a brand-blue color-overlay version — without redrawing the mark; (3) build the wide HORIZONTAL LOCKUP working canvas by outpainting the square logo composition to a 3:1 banner area; (4) run the swappable-SKU LABEL data-merge: the authored .indd + sku_variants.csv produce one print-ready label mechanical per SKU so SKU codes/volumes swap by editing the CSV, then export those merged labels to press-ready PDF; (5) run the CARTON data-merge from the authored .ai Variables + CSV for the 20-cartridge and 24-tube single-color cartons and export to PDF/SVG; (6) assemble the DIGITAL pack — license a construction/jobsite background from Adobe Stock, color-grade it on-brand, then crop-and-resize it to the exact platform specs (1080x1080 social tile, 1920x1080 web banner, 350x120 email signature, and a 1:1 WhatsApp catalog card); and (7) present the finished logo-variant system and key assets as a Firefly board deep-link for client review. OUT OF SCOPE for the connector (flag as human/desktop finishing): authoring the 5–8pp brand-standards PDF body copy and typography pages, drawing any new layout from scratch, and the Amazon UAE listing page composition. The connector's job is the asset/variant system, the merged swappable SKU mechanicals, the export-ready print PDFs, and the correctly-sized digital tiles.

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-54   RoadWise — Branded Top-Down Traffic Shorts: Reusable Asset Kit, Color-Graded Frames, Vertical Cover + VO/Reel Prep

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Motion & AudioOperation O3 Video & audioHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K6 · K7

Build the RoadWise branded source-asset kit for the top-down traffic shorts: vectorize reusable road/vehicle/arrow icons to clean SVG, apply one consistent color grade across the reference frames, produce a 1080x1920 vertical cover/end-card and a stock-backed road texture, then clean the scratch VO and reframe the rough preview clip to vertical — all chained so the deliverable kit emerges from the supplied plates.

Full brief

RoadWise™ is a modern driving-education brand (beginner, nervous, teen, and defensive drivers) producing mobile-first vertical shorts (1080x1920) of clean, top-down animated traffic explainers — lane changes, turns, parallel parking, highway merging, 4-way stops, hazard recognition. We are standing up a 10-template content system and need the reusable, brand-consistent SOURCE-ASSET PACKAGE that our After Effects animator will build the templates from. (We are NOT asking the connector to animate — keyframing stays with our AE artist. We need the upstream art kit + downstream delivery prep.) Supplied by us: (1) a flat-rendered top-down reference frame of a 4-lane road junction (PNG, on a neutral asphalt background); (2) a separate "icon plate" PNG holding our reusable top-down car body, turn-arrow, and yield/stop-sign glyphs laid out on white; (3) our RoadWise wordmark/logo PNG (transparent); (4) a scratch voice-over WAV (a presenter explaining a 4-way-stop, recorded on a noisy laptop mic); (5) one rough ~12s After Effects preview render (16:9, 1920x1080) of a lane-change move that we want test-reframed to the vertical short canvas. We need, as one chained job: - A clean, scalable SVG icon set (cars, arrows, signs) extracted from the icon plate so the AE artist can drop infinitely-scalable vectors into every template — this is the heart of the "reusable" requirement. - ONE locked brand color grade (cool, high-clarity, slightly desaturated asphalt, punchy lane-line yellow) applied identically to the reference junction frame AND the cover frame so all 10 templates read as one brand. - A 1080x1920 vertical COVER / END-CARD frame: take the graded junction frame, extend it top and bottom to the vertical short ratio so there is clean road headroom for the title and the logo lockup, with the wordmark color-matched in. - A licensed, subtly-grained seamless asphalt texture (from stock) the animator can tile as the road base. - The scratch VO denoised to broadcast-clean voice. - The rough AE preview reframed to 1080x1920 (same length) so we can sanity-check the vertical crop before committing the template. - Everything assembled onto a single shareable Firefly board as the kit hand-off, and a font recommendation for the on-road label/caption type.

4Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity3Visual density2Creative freedom

See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

driving_sense_wordmark.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA transparent-background PNG logo lockup: the wordmark 'RoadWise' in a friendly modern geometric sans-serif, with a smal
scratch_vo_4way_stop.wavaudiogpt-4o-mini-ttsA warm, clear instructional voice-over, ~25 seconds: 'At a four-way stop, the first vehicle to arrive has the right of w
ae_preview_lanechange_16x9.mp4videoveo-3.1-generate-previewA 12-second flat top-down 2D motion-graphics animation, 1920x1080, of a single car smoothly changing from the right lane
caption_label_strings.jsondatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a small JSON array of 10 short on-road caption/label strings for driving shorts, e.g. {"scenario":"4-way stop",

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
drivingsense_icons.svgvectorClean auto-traced SVG icon set (top-down cars, turn-arrows, road signs) with closed solid-fill paths, transparent background, scalable for the AE template library.
junction_frame_graded.pngimageThe top-down junction reference frame with the locked RoadWise brand grade (cool temp, reduced asphalt saturation, lifted clarity/contrast, punchy lane-yellow), 1024x1024 PNG.
cover_endcard_1080x1920.pngimageVertical 1080x1920 cover / end-card: graded junction frame outpainted top+bottom to 9:16 with clean road headroom, brand-matched solid title band, wordmark color-consistent. PNG.
asphalt_texture_grained.pngimageLicensed seamless asphalt stock with subtle film grain added, tileable road-base texture for the templates. PNG/JPEG full-res from the licensed presigned URL.
vo_4way_stop_clean.wavaudioDenoised broadcast-clean voice-over (hiss/hum/reverb removed by media_enhance_speech), same content/length as the scratch take.
ae_preview_1080x1920.mp4videoThe rough AE lane-change preview reframed to 1080x1920 vertical, SAME ~12s length, for vertical-crop sanity check.
drivingsense_kit_boardfirefly_boardFirefly board deep-link assembling the produced kit assets (icons SVG preview, graded frame, cover, texture) as the hand-off moodboard, plus the font recommendation noted alongside.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom

junction_frame_graded.png is delivered at exactly 1024x1024 PNGautoFile junction_frame_graded.png is PNG format with pixel dimensions exactly 1024 x 1024K1mandatoryoutput
The locked RoadWise brand grade (cool temperature, reduced asphalt saturation, lifted clarity/contrast, punchy lane-line yellow) is visibly applied to the graded junction frameexpertjunction_frame_graded.png reads cooler and higher-clarity/contrast than the source topdown_junction_frame.png with desaturated asphalt grays while the lane-line yellow stays punchy/saturatedK4qualitybrief

The cover/end-card is the graded junction frame outpainted top and bottom to 9:16 with clean road headroom, not a stretched or cropped squareexpertThe vertical cover shows the graded junction at correct aspect with newly generated road area extended above and below leaving clean headroom for title and logo; the original 1024x1024 frame is not distorted, stretched, or letterboxedK3mandatoryoutput
The cover/end-card carries a solid brand-charcoal title band region for the title/wordmark lockupexpertcover_endcard_1080x1920.png shows a solid-color (brand charcoal) filled band in the top headroom region to seat the title/wordmark, color-consistent with the RoadWise wordmark paletteK2qualityoutput
The same locked color grade is applied identically to both the junction reference frame and the cover frame so all templates read as one brandexpertThe road/asphalt and lane-yellow grade in cover_endcard_1080x1920.png matches that of junction_frame_graded.png (same cool / desaturated-asphalt / punchy-yellow treatment), not two different looksK4qualitybrief
asphalt_texture_grained.png is delivered as a full-res licensed Adobe Stock asphalt textureautoFile asphalt_texture_grained.png (PNG or JPEG) exists at full resolution and was sourced from the licensed Adobe Stock presigned download (asset_license_and_download_stock), not a generated or screenshotted imageK1mandatoryoutput
The asphalt stock texture was licensed before any edit was applied to itautoasset_license_and_download_stock was called and completed before image_add_grain in the workflow orderK6dealbreakerbrief

No 2D animation, keyframing, or compositing is claimed or produced — only the source-asset kit and delivery prepexpertDeliverables contain no animated/keyframed/composited output authored from scratch; connector output is limited to the vectorized icons, graded junction frame, vertical cover, asphalt texture, cleaned VO, reframed preview, and board, with animation explicitly left to the AE artistK7mandatorybrief

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-55   Multi-Channel Meal-Prep Ad Package: Product-Showcase Video + Social Cutdowns + Retouched Hero Still + Vectorized Merch Logo

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Motion & AudioOperation O3 Video & audioHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 25)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K6 · K7

From the client's raw chicken-bowl hero photo, raw 4K product-showcase footage, raw phone-recorded voiceover, and flat logo PNG, produce a retouched website-banner hero still, a vertical + square + landscape cutdown set of the product-showcase video, a denoised voiceover, a screen-print-ready vector logo, and a moodboard of the package.

Full brief

Scale My Meal Prep has delegated this week's creative package for the client "Fuel'd Kitchen", who is launching a High-Protein Chicken Bowl. The client uploaded four raw assets: (1) a hand-held studio hero photograph of the plated chicken bowl (slightly tilted, mixed lighting, flat color, shot wider than the banner crop), (2) ~70 seconds of raw 4K horizontal product-showcase b-roll of the bowl being assembled and steaming, (3) a phone-recorded founder voiceover ("Real food, prepped for real life…") with room hiss and HVAC hum, and (4) a flat full-color PNG of the Fuel'd Kitchen wordmark logo for the meal-prep container labels. Deliverables for the package: - ONE retouched, on-brand hero still for the website banner and Facebook/Instagram feed ad: leveled horizon, cleanly cropped, auto-toned and color-corrected, warmer appetizing temperature, the bowl made to pop against a softened background, then canvas-extended at the top so it can also run as a tall hero banner. - ONE supporting steam/texture still licensed from Adobe Stock, color-matched to the hero look, for the email header and landing-page background. - A THREE-format social cutdown of the product-showcase video — 9:16 for Reels/TikTok, 1:1 for the feed, and the original 16:9 — all the same runtime (reframe only, no trimming) plus an AI sizzle/quick-cut highlight version for the paid short-form ad. - A cleaned founder voiceover (denoise/de-hum) and a short text content-summary of the showcase footage for the post caption / scheduling sheet. - A screen-print-ready vectorized (SVG) version of the wordmark logo for the branded meal-prep container labels. - A Firefly Board deep-link assembling the finished package for client review. Work fast and to spec — the agency measures this role on turnaround. Use only Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools; every editing step must build on the previous one.

PNG, sRGB, ~2400px wide.

Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-56   Comedy Gameplay YouTube Edit: Sizzle Cut, Reframed Shorts Variant, Meme Cutaway Graphics + Clickbait Thumbnail

Brand/vertical Music, Film, Publishing & MediaFamily Motion & AudioOperation O3 Video & audioHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn raw comedy gameplay footage plus client notes, custom graphics and a music folder into a punchy 1080p H.264 YouTube cut with a vertical Shorts variant, polished meme/text-callout overlay graphics, a denoised commentary track, and a clickbait thumbnail PNG — all organized into one reusable project package.

Full brief

You are editing a comedy gameplay channel's next upload. The client hands you: a ~22-minute raw 1080p gameplay capture (ranked match, lots of dead air between highlights), a voice-memo commentary/ad-lib track recorded on a cheap mic, a time-stamped notes sheet flagging the funny moments ('3:12 clutch fail', '7:48 teammate rage', '14:02 accidental teamkill', etc.), a folder of custom graphics (a raw 'POV:' meme reaction screenshot and a hero gameplay still earmarked for the thumbnail), and a royalty-free music folder. Goal: a fully edited, fast-paced comedy YouTube piece that lands a laugh every few seconds — rapid reaction zooms, jump cuts, text call-outs, and meme-style cutaways timed to the music — PLUS a matching reusable project package and a clickbait thumbnail. Concretely: 1. Pull the client's footage, commentary, meme screenshot, hero still and music into a managed Creative Cloud project folder, and license one extra royalty-free 'explosion/impact' stock sound or sting if the supplied folder is thin. 2. Profile the music folder for beat/energy so cuts can be timed to it; clean the commentary track so the ad-libs sit cleanly over the gameplay. 3. Generate the on-screen comedy graphics from the supplied raw assets: straighten + auto-tone + crop the hero frame into a punchy 1280x720 thumbnail base; punch its contrast/saturation and apply a clickbait color overlay; turn the raw 'POV:' meme screenshot into a high-contrast duotone meme tile; and vectorize two text-callout cards to clean SVGs so they stay crisp when zoomed. 4. Cut the gameplay down to a tight comedy edit driven by the client's funny-moment notes, then produce a vertical Shorts teaser of the same energy. 5. Hand back: the edited 1080p H.264 master, the vertical Shorts cut, the thumbnail PNG, the overlay graphic set (duotone tile + 2 SVG callouts), and a project board/folder that ties every source + produced asset together so the client can tweak details later. Stay within YouTube guidelines; humor tasteful; graphics crisp at zoom.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain music-entertainmentDeliverable youtube-edit-packageWorkflow Create+EditOperation O3Family Motion & AudioModality videoHorizon H4Est. calls 18Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[A]Brand generic
5Planning1Brand strictness5Tool diversity3Visual density3Creative freedom

See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

hero_thumbnail_frame.pngimagegpt-image-2A dramatic high-action gameplay still earmarked for a YouTube thumbnail: explosion/muzzle flash, character mid-dive, viv

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 18 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
comedy_edit_master_1080p_h264.mp4videoEdited fast-paced comedy cut, 1920x1080, H.264/MP4, ~3-4 min target, produced by an AI Quick Cut driven by the funny-moment notes — the YouTube master deliverable.
comedy_edit_shorts_1080x1920.mp4videoVertical reframed teaser of the master cut, 1080x1920, H.264/MP4, SAME length as its source clip (reframe only, no trim) — YouTube Shorts variant.
youtube_thumbnail_1280x720.pngimageClickbait thumbnail: straightened, auto-toned, contrast/saturation-punched hero frame with clickbait color overlay, cropped/resized to 1280x720 PNG.
meme_cutaway_tile.pngimageHigh-contrast duotone/halftone meme reaction tile derived from the 'POV:' screenshot, ready to drop in as an on-screen cutaway.
text_callout_card_1.svgvectorCrisp vectorized text-callout graphic (SVG) for on-screen punchline call-outs, stays sharp under reaction zooms.
text_callout_card_2.svgvectorSecond vectorized SVG text-callout card, matching style, for a different beat.
cleaned_commentary.wavaudioDenoised/cleaned commentary ad-lib voice track (speech-only enhancement) for layering over gameplay.
project_packagedataA CC project folder containing every source + produced asset plus a Firefly Board deep-link that ties them together as the reusable 'project file' reference; plus a music-energy summary note for beat timing.

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The edited comedy master video is delivered in H.264/MP4 formatautocomedy_edit_master_1080p_h264.mp4 is a valid MP4 file whose video stream is H.264-encodedK1dealbreakeroutput

The vertical Shorts variant is delivered at the 1080x1920 portrait dimensions in H.264/MP4autocomedy_edit_shorts_1080x1920.mp4 is a valid H.264/MP4 file with frame dimensions exactly 1080x1920K1mandatoryoutput
The Shorts variant is a reframe of the master with the same length (reframe only, no trim)autocomedy_edit_shorts_1080x1920.mp4 duration equals the duration of comedy_edit_master_1080p_h264.mp4 within one-frame toleranceK1mandatoryoutput
The YouTube thumbnail is delivered as a PNG at the exact spec dimensionsautoyoutube_thumbnail_1280x720.png is a PNG file with dimensions exactly 1280x720K1mandatoryoutput
The thumbnail is built from the supplied hero_thumbnail_frame.png (not a regenerated/substituted image)expertyoutube_thumbnail_1280x720.png is recognizably the supplied hero gameplay still (same explosion/muzzle-flash mid-dive scene), processed rather than replaced by a different generated imageK2mandatoryinput
The thumbnail shows the straighten + auto-tone + contrast/saturation-punch + clickbait color-overlay treatmentexpertyoutube_thumbnail_1280x720.png is leveled (not crooked), brighter/contrastier than the underexposed source, with punched saturation and a clickbait orange-teal color overlay appliedK3mandatoryoutput
The meme cutaway tile is a high-contrast duotone/halftone treatment of the supplied 'POV:' screenshotexpertmeme_cutaway_tile.png is recognizably derived from the supplied pov_meme_screenshot.png and shows a high-contrast duotone or halftone effect, ready as an on-screen cutawayK2mandatoryinput
Two text-callout cards are delivered as valid SVG vector filesautotext_callout_card_1.svg and text_callout_card_2.svg both exist and are well-formed SVG filesK1mandatoryoutput
The two SVG callout cards stay crisp/sharp when scaled up (true vector paths, not embedded raster)experttext_callout_card_1.svg and text_callout_card_2.svg render as resolution-independent vector paths that stay clean at zoom, not just a bitmap wrapped in an SVG elementK3qualityoutput
The cleaned commentary track is delivered as a WAV with speech-only denoise/enhancement appliedexpertcleaned_commentary.wav is a WAV of the commentary ad-libs that audibly has the cheap-mic hiss/reverb/plosives reduced versus commentary_adlibs.wav while preserving the speechK1mandatoryoutput
One royalty-free 'explosion/impact' stock sound is licensed (not preview) and added to the projectautoAn Adobe Stock 'explosion/impact' audio asset is licensed (not a watermarked preview) and its full-res asset is present in the project packageK6qualitybrief
A music summary is produced from the supplied royalty-free music stems and included in the project packageexpertproject_package includes a media_summarize-derived content/energy summary of the supplied music stems, present as the beat/timing reference noteK1qualitybrief
A Creative Cloud project folder named 'Comedy_Gameplay_Edit' holds the source and produced assetsautoA CC project folder named exactly 'Comedy_Gameplay_Edit' exists and contains the ingested source files plus the produced deliverablesK6mandatorybrief
A Firefly Board deep-link ties the produced assets together as the reusable 'project file' referenceautoproject_package contains a Firefly Board deep-link aggregating the thumbnail (youtube_thumbnail_1280x720.png), meme tile (meme_cutaway_tile.png), both SVG callouts, the master cut, the Shorts cut, and the cleaned commentaryK6mandatoryoutput
All eight produced deliverables are present in the handoffautocomedy_edit_master_1080p_h264.mp4, comedy_edit_shorts_1080x1920.mp4, youtube_thumbnail_1280x720.png, meme_cutaway_tile.png, text_callout_card_1.svg, text_callout_card_2.svg, cleaned_commentary.wav, and project_package all existK1dealbreakeroutput
The comedy edit is a tight fast-paced cut driven by the client's funny-moment notes (dead air trimmed, comedy-sketch pacing)expertcomedy_edit_master_1080p_h264.mp4 is noticeably shorter than the ~22-min source (roughly a 3-4 min cut), removes dead air, retains the flagged funny beats from funny_moment_notes.csv, and has rapid comedy pacingK4qualitybrief

AO-57   Phonk-Energy 9:16 Reel/TikTok Edit Package — Sizzle Cut, Reframe, Cleaned VO, and Scroll-Stopping Thumbnail

Brand/vertical Music, Film, Publishing & MediaFamily Motion & AudioOperation O3 Video & audioHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 30)Tools 18 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn one batch of raw vertical clips plus a hero still, brand logo, and a voiceover scratch track into a post-ready 9:16 phonk-energy short: an AI sizzle cut reframed to 1080x1920, a cleaned VO track, an on-screen caption sheet, and a fully color-graded exported thumbnail with a vector logo lockup.

Full brief

I run a fast-growing Instagram + TikTok presence and need a short-form edit shipped post-ready for both platforms. For this assignment I'm sending: (a) three raw vertical clips from a night drive / street shoot, (b) one hero still grabbed from the footage to become the cover/thumbnail, (c) my brand wordmark logo as a flat PNG, (d) a rough voiceover scratch take with room noise that needs cleaning, and (e) a captions script as data. The concept is phonk-style energy but with a professional, polished finish — clean, current, on-brand color (cool teal shadows, magenta highlight bite). Deliverables: 1x final ~30s 9:16 MP4 ready to post on Instagram and TikTok (HD/Full HD); a matching on-screen captions/text asset; a cleaned voiceover track; and an exported, fully color-graded 1080x1920 thumbnail frame with my logo locked up in the corner as a crisp vector so it scales. Source assets stay in my Creative Cloud folder so tweaks are easy. Turnaround 24-48h, lean budget — efficient workflow over flashy effects. NOTE up front: frame-accurate timeline cuts, animated motion graphics, hard transitions, and beat-synced music are a manual Premiere finish and are out of scope for the automated connector pass; the automated pass delivers the AI sizzle assembly, the 9:16 reframe, the VO cleanup, the captions asset, and the graded thumbnail — the human editor does the final timeline polish on top.

4Planning3Brand strictness5Tool diversity3Visual density3Creative freedom

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
phonk_sizzle_9x16.mp4video1x vertical 1080x1920 (9:16) MP4, ~30s, H.264, AI sizzle assembly of the three night-drive clips reframed to full-frame vertical, Instagram & TikTok ready. Produced by the [A] async video chain (quick_cut then resize); frame-accurate timeline cuts / transitions / motion graphics / beat-sync are the flagged manual finish.
voiceover_clean.wavaudioCleaned voiceover track: isolated speech stem from media_enhance_speech (room hiss, HVAC rumble and reverb suppressed), WAV, ready to lay under the reel. Background/reverb stems also returned by the tool.
reel_captions.jsondataOn-screen captions/text cue sheet (6 timed cues) stored alongside the project in the client's Creative Cloud folder for the editor to apply as text animations.
thumbnail_cover_1080x1920.pngimageExported, fully color-graded vertical cover/thumbnail, PNG 1080x1920: straightened, auto-toned, phonk-graded (cool teal shadows + magenta highlight bite), subject-popped, halftone-grain texture, cropped to exact 9:16. Carries the brand wordmark as a crisp corner lockup.
NOCTURNE_logo.svgvectorClean vectorized brand wordmark (SVG) produced from the flat logo PNG, used as the scalable corner lockup on the thumbnail and reusable for future covers.
reel_package.ziparchiveSingle zip bundling the deliverables (MP4, cleaned VO WAV, captions JSON, graded thumbnail PNG, logo SVG) plus the kept-in-CC source references, for one-click handoff.

Brand wordmark vector deliverable NOCTURNE_logo.svg is delivered as an SVGautoNOCTURNE_logo.svg exists and is a valid SVG file containing vector path dataK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo preserves the original 'NOCTURNE' wordmark and is not regenerated or restyledexpertNOCTURNE_logo.svg reproduces the supplied brand_wordmark_logo.png 'NOCTURNE' wordmark (same glyphs, tight geometric sans-serif, solid white) with clean crisp edges and no redesigned or restyled letteringK2dealbreakerinput
The deliverable package bundles the five produced assets together for handoff (MP4, cleaned VO WAV, captions JSON, graded thumbnail PNG, logo SVG)autoreel_package.zip (or the dated CC handoff folder produced by asset_copy_assets) contains all five files: phonk_sizzle_9x16.mp4, voiceover_clean.wav, reel_captions.json, thumbnail_cover_1080x1920.png, and NOCTURNE_logo.svgK1mandatoryoutput
Out-of-scope timeline work (frame-accurate cuts, hard transitions, motion graphics, beat-synced music, caption burn-in, logo lockup compositing) is NOT falsely claimed as delivered by the automated connector passexpertThe handoff honestly flags frame-accurate cuts/hard-transitions/motion-graphics/beat-synced-music, caption burn-in onto video, and the logo-lockup placement on the thumbnail as the manual human-finish steps and does not present them as completed automated outputK7dealbreakerbrief
The thumbnail PNG is the graded cover WITHOUT the logo burned in — the logo lockup placement is left as the flagged human-finish stepexpertthumbnail_cover_1080x1920.png is the color-graded/cropped cover with no NOCTURNE wordmark composited onto it; the SVG logo is delivered separately for the human editor to placeK7mandatorybrief

  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-58   Repeatable Adobe pipeline turning one raw exercise clip into a branded vertical coaching reel (subtitles cue, cleaned VO, watermark, muscle-group callout)

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Motion & AudioOperation O3 Video & audioHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 15)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K6 · K7

Run our repeatable exercise-reel pipeline on one raw 'Goblet Squat' clip: summarize it for subtitle/cue text and naming, clean the coaching voiceover, build the reusable white-watermark and branded muscle-group arrow overlays, reframe to vertical, cut a 30s reference reel, and file everything under our naming convention.

Full brief

We run a personal-training business with a library of 5,000+ exercises and want to turn raw footage into short, consistent educational reference videos for coach education and client support — today each one takes 20-30 min in Premiere and we want a repeatable, AI-assisted production pipeline. This task is ONE run of that pipeline on a single representative exercise, the Goblet Squat, so we can validate the repeatable unit before scaling it across the library. Supplied assets: (1) a raw ~45s 4K landscape clip of a coach demoing the goblet squat with spoken form cues recorded in a slightly echoey gym (camera mic); (2) our gym's square logo PNG (dark logo on transparent-ish background); (3) a flat hand-drawn red 'muscle-activation' arrow/callout PNG we use to point at the working muscle groups. What we need produced this run: 1. SUBTITLE & CUE SOURCE — an AI content summary / transcript-style scene summary of the raw clip we can paste into Premiere as the subtitle + on-screen coaching-cue source text, and which we'll also use to name the clip consistently. 2. CLEAN VOICEOVER — the coach's spoken cues de-echoed / denoised into a clean voice track to drop back under the reel (voice only — leave the music/ambience decision to us). 3. REUSABLE WATERMARK — our logo turned into a transparent, corner-sized, white version that reads on dark gym footage (this becomes a reusable overlay asset for the whole library). 4. REUSABLE MUSCLE-GROUP CALLOUT — the hand-drawn arrow cleaned up into a crisp scalable vector recolored to our brand accent, sized as an overlay element (also reusable library-wide). 5. VERTICAL REEL — the raw clip reframed to 1080x1920 vertical (full length, no trimming at this step) and then cut down by AI into a punchy ~30s highlight 'reference reel' of the key reps. 6. ORGANIZED LIBRARY ENTRY — a folder following our naming convention (Exercise / LowerBody / GobletSquat) with the finished reel + the reusable overlay assets copied in, ready to template across the next 5,000 clips. Honesty note for the vendor: we understand the actual frame-by-frame compositing (burning subtitles in, placing the watermark and arrow onto the video, transitions) stays in Premiere on our side — what we want from this automated pipeline is every input asset and the reframed/cut reel prepared and organized so that final Premiere step is fast and identical every time.

5Planning4Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

muscle_arrow_callout_raw.pngimagegemini-3.1-flash-imageA single flat hand-drawn-style curved red arrow with a small 'GLUTES' tag at the tail, drawn with a marker texture, soli

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 15 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
goblet_squat_subtitle_cue_source.txttext/summaryAI scene/transcript-style summary of the raw clip (media_summarize output) capturing the spoken form cues + rep structure, usable as Premiere subtitle + on-screen cue source text and as the consistent clip-naming basis.
goblet_squat_VO_clean.wavaudioCoach's spoken cue track de-echoed/denoised (voice only) via media_enhance_speech, ready to drop under the reel.
watermark_white_corner.pngimageTransparent PNG of the gym logo, background removed, recolored solid white, cropped/resized to a corner-badge overlay size (~400x400) — reusable library-wide watermark.
muscle_arrow_accent.svgvectorClean vectorized muscle-group callout arrow recolored to the brand accent and sized as an overlay element — reusable library-wide.
goblet_squat_vertical_full.mp4videoRaw clip reframed to 1080x1920 vertical, full original length (no trim) via video_resize.
goblet_squat_reference_reel_30s.mp4video~30s AI highlight cut (video_create_quick_cut) of the vertical clip — the punchy key-reps reference reel.
Exercise_LowerBody_GobletSquat_folderasset-folderCC asset folder following the naming convention containing the finished reel + reusable watermark + arrow overlay, copied in and ready to template across the library.

The reference reel is vertical 1080x1920, derived from the reframed vertical clipautogoblet_squat_reference_reel_30s.mp4 has frame dimensions 1080x1920 (portrait), consistent with being cut from goblet_squat_vertical_full.mp4K1mandatoryoutput

AO-60   Packaging-Portfolio Sizzle Reel: Grade the Stills, Cut the Hero Video

Brand/vertical Video Editing &amp; Motion GraphicsFamily Motion & AudioOperation O3 Video & audioHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take my mixed-lighting packaging photos, color-grade them into one cohesive polished hero set, then assemble a fast-paced 16:9 website sizzle reel plus a square social cut and a moodboard.

Full brief

I run a small packaging design studio and I'm launching a new website. I have a folder of finished work — six photographs/mockups of packaging projects I've shipped (a tea carton, a cosmetics jar, a coffee bag, a craft-beer can, a perfume box, and a chocolate bar sleeve), all shot in my studio over the past year so they don't match: some are warm, some are cool, a couple are slightly crooked, exposure drifts shot to shot. I also have one clean photo of a plain kraft box I want to use as the title card, but it's framed too tight — I need the canvas extended so I can overlay my logo on negative space. I want all of this pulled together into an eye-catching HERO sizzle reel for the top of my homepage: fast-paced, energetic, and making my design work look its absolute best. Please do the following, in order, so the look is cohesive across every shot: 1) Bring all my supplied photos in and get them straight and uniformly exposed (auto-straighten the crooked ones, auto-tone, lift the dark portions a touch). 2) Give the whole set one consistent grade — neutralize the mixed white balance toward a clean studio look, push vibrance so the print colors pop, apply a single cinematic Lightroom preset across every frame, and add a light film grain so they read as one family. 3) For the title card: extend the kraft-box canvas to a wide full-bleed frame (outpaint the empty studio sweep), and also make a clean "cut-out" hero variant of the cosmetics jar — remove its background and drop it onto a solid charcoal card. 4) Crop/resize every graded frame to a uniform 1920x1080 so they sequence cleanly. 5) Pull in one licensed studio-texture stock plate to use as the intro/outro background. 6) Assemble the graded frames into a fast, energetic ~20-second sizzle reel at 1920x1080 for the website hero, and also give me a 1080x1080 square cut for Instagram. 7) Finally, collect the graded stills into a moodboard so I can sign off on the look before launch. Quality bar: every shot must look like it belongs to the same set; print colors must be punchy but not blown out; the title-card extension must be seamless.

The 16:9 website hero sizzle reel deliverable packaging_sizzle_hero_16x9.mp4 is present and playable.autoA file named packaging_sizzle_hero_16x9.mp4 exists and is a valid, playable MP4 video.K1mandatoryoutput
The hero reel resolution matches the spec (1920x1080, 16:9).autopackaging_sizzle_hero_16x9.mp4 is exactly 1920x1080 px (16:9 aspect ratio).K1mandatoryoutput
The hero reel runs approximately 20 seconds.autopackaging_sizzle_hero_16x9.mp4 duration is between 18 and 22 seconds (~20s as specified).K1mandatoryoutput
The square Instagram social cut packaging_sizzle_social_1x1.mp4 is present and exactly 1080x1080.autopackaging_sizzle_social_1x1.mp4 exists, is a valid MP4, and is exactly 1080x1080 px (1:1).K1mandatoryoutput
The square social cut is the same length as the hero reel (video_resize is same-duration, never trims).autopackaging_sizzle_social_1x1.mp4 duration equals packaging_sizzle_hero_16x9.mp4 duration within ~0.5s.K1mandatoryoutput
The graded still set contains exactly six packaging frames at the uniform target size as JPEGs.autograded_frames_1920x1080/ contains exactly 6 JPEG stills, each exactly 1920x1080 px.K1mandatoryoutput
The six graded frames correspond to the six supplied packaging subjects, one per frame.expertAll six distinct supplied packaging products (tea carton, cosmetics jar, coffee bag, craft-beer can, perfume box, chocolate bar sleeve) appear across the graded frame set, one per frame, none missing or duplicated.K2mandatoryinput
The six graded frames read as one cohesive set with a single unified grade.expertAcross all 6 frames the mixed white balance is neutralized toward one clean studio white point, one consistent cinematic Lightroom preset look is applied, and film grain is uniform; no frame looks visibly warmer/cooler or stylistically off from the rest.K3mandatorybrief
Print colors are punchy but not blown out (vibrance pushed without clipping).expertPrinted/illustrated package colors read as saturated and vivid while highlights and saturated channels retain detail and are not clipped or blown out.K4qualitybrief
The crooked source frames are straightened and the underexposed/dark frames are corrected.expertThe tea-carton (~2 degree tilt) and beer-can (~1.5 degree tilt) horizons read level with no residual tilt, and the previously crushed-shadow coffee bag shows lifted, legible dark print detail.K3mandatorybrief
The kraft-box title card titlecard_kraft_fullbleed.png is outpainted to a wide full-bleed 1920x1080 plate with clean negative space.autotitlecard_kraft_fullbleed.png exists, is a valid PNG exactly 1920x1080 px, and shows the kraft box with extended empty studio sweep (negative space) for a logo overlay.K1mandatoryoutput
The title-card canvas extension is seamless (outpaint blends invisibly with the original sweep).expertThe boundary between the original kraft-box frame and the generated extended studio sweep shows no visible seam, tone break, texture mismatch, or repetition artifact.K3mandatorybrief
The cosmetics-jar cut-out hero variant cosmetics_jar_cutout_charcoal.png is on a solid charcoal card of the exact specified color.autocosmetics_jar_cutout_charcoal.png exists, is a valid PNG, and its background is a solid fill of charcoal #2B2B2B with the cosmetics jar seated on top.K1mandatoryoutput
The cosmetics-jar background removal has clean edges.expertThe jar is cleanly cut out with no background halo, fringing, or chopped edges against the charcoal card; the frosted glass and gold-lid silhouette are intact.K3qualitybrief
A licensed Adobe Stock studio-texture plate is used as the intro/outro background (license-before-use, no watermarked/unlicensed plate).autoA studio-texture stock plate was licensed via asset_license_and_download_stock and appears as the intro/outro background of the hero reel with no visible Adobe Stock watermark.K7mandatorybrief
The look-signoff moodboard is delivered as a valid Firefly Board deep-link assembling the graded frames, title card, and cut-out.autolook_signoff_moodboard is a valid Firefly Board deep-link whose board contains the 6 graded frame URNs plus titlecard_kraft_fullbleed and cosmetics_jar_cutout_charcoal.K1mandatoryoutput

Text & lower-thirdslegible; on long enoughslightly fast / tightunreadable / mistimed

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness ★A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-61   Fun YouTube Podcast: clean-audio cut, reframed 1080p sizzle, and a branded graphics kit (lower-third + end-screen + vector logo)

Brand/vertical Music, Film, Publishing &amp; MediaFamily Motion & AudioOperation O3 Video & audioHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 20)Tools 19 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the raw podcast footage into a clean-voiced, reframed 1080p YouTube master plus a fun branded graphics kit (vector logo + lower-third + end-screen as PNG-transparent/vector), delivered separately.

Full brief

You are editing one episode of a casual, fun YouTube podcast. The client supplies the raw multi-cam-ish recording (a single 1080p+ master with two speakers), a separate isolated voice scratch track that has room hiss and uneven levels, their existing brand logo lockup (a flat PNG on a busy background), and a hand-shot lower-third texture plate. They want three things delivered: (A) a fully edited 1080p YouTube-ready video that flows smoothly with clear, balanced voices; (B) the working hand-off so they can tweak later; and (C) any custom graphic assets you create, delivered SEPARATELY in PNG/transparent or vector format, with their logo worked in tastefully in the intro / lower-third / end screen without overpowering the content. Concretely: clean the voice track so the two hosts are clear and balanced (denoise/de-hiss only — no elaborate sound design). Produce a punchy highlight/sizzle cut from the raw footage for the cold-open, and reframe the full episode to a clean 1080p YouTube frame. Pull a short content summary to seed the chapter markers and description. For the graphics kit: lift the client's logo off its busy background to a clean transparent PNG, color-correct and brand-tint it, then vectorize it to an infinitely-scalable SVG for the end screen. Build a lower-third "name plate" from the supplied texture plate: knock out the background, fill a clean solid brand-colored bar, tint it to match, crop it to a broadcast lower-third strip, and recommend a friendly display font for the host name. License one subtle on-brand background texture from stock for the end-screen card. Present the whole graphics kit as a Firefly board the client can review. The vibe is light-hearted and casual — clean and consistent branding, never stiff or corporate.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain music-entertainmentDeliverable sizzle-reelWorkflow EditOperation O3Family Motion & AudioModality videoHorizon H4Est. calls 20Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[A]Brand The Long Way RoundMixed-modality yes
5Planning3Brand strictness5Tool diversity2Visual density3Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Brand Alignment · Technical Quality · Creative Quality · Process · Honesty. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over
